chunks.heading: README
chunks.body:
# Marketing Cloud — help PDF extracts

Markdown exports of Salesforce Help PDFs for use as local documentation in AI workflows.

Each product group is a top-level subfolder; within it each source PDF becomes its own section.
chunks.productLabel: Marketing Cloud Engagement (MCE; Email Studio, Journey Builder, Automation Studio, Content Builder, Mobile Studio)

chunks.heading: Regenerating
chunks.body: Run `python _scripts/pdf_to_mce_md.py` from the `mce/` folder after adding new PDFs.
chunks.productLabel: Marketing Cloud Engagement (MCE; Email Studio, Journey Builder, Automation Studio, Content Builder, Mobile Studio)

chunks.heading: Notes
chunks.body:
- Text and **tables** are extracted with [pdfplumber](https://github.com/jsvine/pdfplumber); detected grid tables are emitted as Markdown pipe tables. Line-only layouts may still be plain text.
- The PDF **table of contents** uses *printed* page numbers; those are mapped to **PDF file** page indices using footer page numbers where possible.
- Very long TOC sections are split into `part-NN.md` files inside a numbered subfolder (roughly **2500 lines** per part) so individual files stay usable in AI context windows.
chunks.productLabel: Marketing Cloud Engagement (MCE; Email Studio, Journey Builder, Automation Studio, Content Builder, Mobile Studio)

chunks.heading: README
chunks.body:
# Loyalty Management

| Section | Source PDF |
|---------|------------|
| [loyalty-management](./loyalty-management/) | `loyalty_management_4-7-2026.pdf` |
| [referral-marketing](./referral-marketing/) | `referral_marketing_4-7-2026.pdf` |
chunks.productLabel: Loyalty Management (Salesforce Loyalty Management; loyalty programs, referral marketing, member engagement)

chunks.heading: 01-loyalty-management
chunks.body:
---
title: "Loyalty Management"
source_pdf: "loyalty_management_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "5-7"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

|  | Loyalty Management |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

Loyalty Management, built on the Salesforce platform, helps organizations deliver innovative programs
for customer recognition, reward, and retention. Loyalty Management is a unified, cross-industry solution
that offers a host of features that enable you to plan and design loyalty programs, manage members, and
partners. You can also track members' activities, reward members, and manage promotions.
Loyalty Management is an end-to-end solution that helps you create intelligent, personalized
engagements that increase customer loyalty. Loyalty Management is versatile and can cater to B2B and
B2C business needs in any industry. With the Loyalty Management app, create and set up loyalty
programs. Track members' activities and reward them with points or vouchers. With Loyalty Program
Partners, build a strong network of brand advocates for your organization. Boost customer engagement
and get a better return on your investment with promotions. Let members track their loyalty benefits
from the Loyalty Member Portal.
Control Who Sees What in Loyalty Management
Your Salesforce admin enables Loyalty Management in your org. When enabled, users have edit access
to loyalty objects and records. Users can create profiles and assign permissions to access loyalty
application as appropriate.
Manage Loyalty Programs
A loyalty program is your starting point for setting up Loyalty Management in your Salesforce org. It
helps to design, organize, and customize the loyalty building blocks with loyalty programs.
Manage Clubs
Clubs are interest-based membership initiatives designed to provide customers with exclusive benefits
and privileges. Customers can join a club through a subscription model or based on their current
loyalty program tier. By offering unique benefits and exclusive promotions, clubs can enhance
customer loyalty.
Manage Loyalty Program Partners
Loyalty Management enables the onboarding and managing of cross-industry program partners to
increase member engagement with the loyalty program. Partnerships give your members more
options to engage members and help them earn and redeem points for complementary products or
services.
Vouchers
Vouchers are offered to customers and loyalty program members as rewards for engaging with the
brand and as compensation for service recovery actions. Your company and its loyalty programs can
offer different types of vouchers based on your business requirements. You can also choose from a
range of different options for how vouchers are issued to customers and members.
Manage and Work with Promotions
Promotions are a way to engage loyalty program members by providing them valuable offers at a

Loyalty Management
specific time, often tied to a seasonal sales cycle. Promotions can play a significant role in increasing
product sales and generating business revenues. Promotions help loyalty program members get a
better return on their investment with attractive offers in the form of vouchers or loyalty points.
Offer Games to Members
Brands can keep its customers and loyalty program members engaged by offering them games as
rewards. Use games to reward purchase-specific activities such as placing an order and non-purchase
activities such as completing a survey.
Settings for Loyalty Processes
You can set Loyalty Management to update non-qualifying points balance of members in real time.
Turn on another setting to add template Data Processing Engine definitions to your org and use them
to aggregate and expire members' fixed-expiration-model, non-qualifying points. Customer service
representatives can engage your loyalty program members better with Loyalty Service Excellence.
Einstein for Loyalty Management
Reduce the amount of time that various departments across your company spend to manage and run
your company's loyalty programs with Einstein for Loyalty Management.
Set Up Loyalty Processes
You can provide an efficient process for customer recognition, rewards, and experiences by setting up
end-to-end Loyalty Processes.
Create Loyalty Program Processes by Using Promotion Setup
Loyalty program managers can create loyalty program processes to define how to run business
processes in Loyalty Management.
Loyalty Program Members
Loyalty program members are the members enrolled in a loyalty program that you create. A member
can be part of multiple loyalty programs in an organization.
Member Engagement Widgets
Engage with members of your loyalty program and your customers on your company's website and
external portals by using member engagement widgets. Create widgets by using widget templates
provided by Loyalty Management and customize the widgets based on your company's branding and
design requirements. You can configure your widgets by using either the Widget Designer within the
Loyalty Management app or the OmniStudio's Flexcard Designer.
Manage and View Transaction Journals
View transaction journals to track member activities, member behaviors, and make manual
adjustments to points. Transactions happen when members accrue points, redeem points, adjust
points manually, and reverse redemptions.
Loyalty Service Excellence
Use the Identity Verification flows and the service console for Loyalty Program Members so that your
customer service representatives (CSRs) can engage loyalty program members better. CSRs can use
Identity Verification flows to verify the identity of your loyalty program members or their authorized
member representatives. Then, CSRs can use the service console to resolve issues, clarify questions,
manage requests, and more of loyalty program members or authorized member representatives.
Set Up Experiences for Loyalty Program Members
Create a feature-rich portal for your loyalty program members using the Loyalty Member Portal
template. Keep your loyalty members engaged by letting them track their progress in the reward

Loyalty Management
program with the Loyalty Member Portal. With a few clicks, admins can set up the loyalty member
portal where program members can view their program details, including recent transactions, current
tiers, benefits, points associated with each transaction, and profile details. You can customize the
portal based on your company's requirements.
Integrate Loyalty Management with External Systems
Loyalty Management comes with a set of developer resources, such as business APIs, invocable
actions, and standard objects that you can use to integrate your loyalty programs with external
systems, such as point of sales, reservations, booking, billing, service, and operations. For example, to
create a transaction journal and credit points to members for online purchases, you can integrate your
external website with a business API that can run the relevant loyalty program process.
Integrate Loyalty Management With Google Wallet
Drive customer engagement by integrating your loyalty program with Google Wallet. This integration
allows loyalty program managers to issue and manage dynamic digital passes, such as loyalty cards
and vouchers, directly within customers' Google Wallets.
Integrate Loyalty Management with Marketing Cloud Engagement
Keep your loyalty program members informed and engaged with content blocks and journeys.
Integrate Loyalty Management with Service Cloud
Your service team members can create a case and populate the fields right on a loyalty member
profile.
Deploy and Use Analytics for Loyalty
The Analytics for Loyalty app gives you actionable insights into loyalty programs that help you devise
strategies to increase membership, retain customers, improve processes, and grow your business.
Loyalty Management Limits and Considerations
Loyalty Management provides a set of features and capabilities that enable companies to manage and
run business processes related to their loyalty programs. Each of your business processes can use
features such as Promotion Setup and Salesforce Flows, and different services such as business APIs,
Data Processing Engine, and Business Rules Engine. When planning your implementation of Loyalty
Management, make sure that you account for the Loyalty Management limits and considerations.
Data Protection and Privacy for Loyalty Management
When companies use Loyalty Management to run their loyalty programs, they must comply with local
and global data protection and privacy regulations. As part of your company's loyalty program strategy,
decide how you want to delete, export, archive, restrict access, manage consent, and restrict the
processing of customer data stored as part of the loyalty programs.
chunks.productLabel: Loyalty Management (Salesforce Loyalty Management; loyalty programs, referral marketing, member engagement)

Get Started with Loyalty Management
Get Started with Loyalty Management
Loyalty Management helps organizations design and run loyalty programs that recognize, reward, and
engage your customers. Run B2B and B2C loyalty programs tailored to your industry's requirements.
Explore a trial org and then learn about what's included, what setup we recommend, and how to get
ready for your implementation.

| Get Oriented What is Salesforce Loyalty Management? Video: Watch a Demo Create a Loyalty Management Learning Org | Dive In: Learn About the Recommended Core Features Design Your Loyalty Program Along with Its Tiers and Currencies Add Partners to Your Loyalty Program Watch: Design a Loyalty Program | Go Deeper: Define Rewards for Members Add Tier Benefits and Personalised Benefits Get Your Voucher Definitions Ready Design Badges for Your Members Configure Your Promotions and its Rewards Watch: Add a Loyalty Program's Rewards |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Extend Further: Learn About Additional Capabilities & Add- Ons Segment Members for Promotions using Data Cloud Stay Connected with Your Members using Marketing Cloud Offer Promotions in Real Time with Interaction Studio | Get Ready for Your Implementation Turn on Loyalty Management Settings Provide Users Access to the Loyalty Management App Create Processes to Manage Member Events Configure a Tier Assessment | Know Your Resources & Get Help Trailhead: Loyalty Programs and Loyalty Management Loyalty Management Developer Guide Watch: Launch a Loyalty Program |

Loyalty Management Get Started with Loyalty Management

| Add Widgets on Your Website Watch: Design Loyalty Engagement Widgets As Per Your Company's Branding Watch: Embed Loyalty Engagement Widgets Mobile SDK for Loyalty Management Watch: Experience Loyalty Management on Your Mobile Devices Loyalty Management Point of Sale API Watch: Loyalty Management POS API for In-Store and E- Commerce Unlocked Package Watch: Loyalty Management POS API for In- Store and E-Commerce Powered by MuleSoft | Process Watch: Take a Tour of Promotion Setup Watch: Process Member Redemptions using Promotion Setup in Loyalty Management Watch: Use Loyalty Management Promotion Setup to Reward Members for Purchases Integrate with External Systems Give Partners Permissions to Edit Their Program Data Analyze Your Loyalty Program's Performance Watch: Track the Performance of Your Promotions with CRM Analytics |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

Loyalty Management Implementation Guide
The implementation guide outlines the steps to set up and use Loyalty Management.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management
The guide contains a reference implementation of how:
•
Admins can:
- Set up Loyalty Management
- Import loyalty program-specific data
- Provide users permissions to work with Loyalty Management
- Set up automated process for the company's loyalty program
- Set up CRM Analytics for the company's loyalty program

Loyalty Management Get Started with Loyalty Management
- Set up a member site
- Integrate the company's loyalty program with external sites, mobile apps and external systems
•
Loyalty program managers can:
- Design a loyalty program
- Manage program partners
- Create member experiences
- Configure loyalty program processes
•
Service representatives can:
- Review membership profiles
- Manage member requests
To review and use the implementation guide, see Implement Loyalty Management for Retail and
Consumer Goods.
Create a Loyalty Management Trial Org
Sign up for a Loyalty Management trial org, and get a firsthand experience of creating and managing a
loyalty program.
Note Trial orgs are intended for proof of concept and guided self-exploration. Trial orgs expire in 30
days.
11.. Go to the sign up page of the trail org that you want to create.
••
Loyalty Management Learning Trial Org-This fully configured org includes rich sample data and lets
you see all the Loyalty Management capabilities in action. Use these orgs to see what a
comprehensive implementation looks like.
••
Loyalty Management Base Trial Org-This unconfigured org is a blank slate with only the necessary
licenses and permissions. Use these orgs for quick proof of concept projects or general testing.
••
Loyalty Management Pre-Release Trial Org-This org provides a sneak peek at upcoming release
features. You can create these orgs only during sandbox preview windows. If the page doesn't
include a sign-up form, the preview window is closed.
Bookmark the Loyalty Management Developer Free Trials page to easily find the sign up pages for the
Loyalty Management trial orgs.
22.. Enter your details.
33.. Read and agree to the main service agreement.
44.. Click Submit.
After your org is created, look for an email with login details. Your trial org includes Loyalty Management,
Experience Cloud, and Tableau CRM for Loyalty Management.
Explore and Set Up Customer Loyalty Features With Salesforce Go
Explore, set up and configure, and monitor usage of customer loyalty features in one streamlined setup
location. Access content resources, such as videos, online tours, Trailhead, and Salesforce Help articles,

Loyalty Management Get Started with Loyalty Management
to learn about features, and get help with configuration. Discover more loyalty features and add-on
licenses that are available based on your Salesforce edition. And if you've turned on the Your Account
app, you can quickly buy add-on licenses for some products directly from Salesforce Go.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management -
Growth or Loyalty Management - Advanced
You can set up Global Promotions Management effortlessly with Salesforce Go. Enable marketing
managers to design promotions with the global promotions wizard. Deploy rule library and context
service to empower marketing managers to design pricing promotions tailored to unique company
requirements. Provide marketing managers the capability to leverage powerful features like Promotion
Evaluation.
See Also
Salesforce Help: Feature Set Up and Discovery with Salesforce Go
Loyalty Management on Hyperforce
Loyalty Management is available on Hyperforce. With the Hyperforce architecture, you can store your
Loyalty Management data locally by using Salesforce's public cloud partners.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Developer, and Unlimited Editions that
have Loyalty Management enabled.
Loyalty Management components like Data Processing Engine, Batch Management, and Decision Tables
are also available on Hyperforce. If you need to integrate Loyalty Management with other Salesforce
products and services, you can learn about their availability on Hyperforce here.
See Also
Knowledge Article: Introducing Hyperforce - General Information & FAQ
External Link: Unified Cloud (Hyperforce) Security, Privacy and Architecture
Languages Supported by Loyalty Management
The Loyalty Management app is localized in languages that Salesforce fully supports and languages in
which end users are supported. Your global users can choose the language in which they want to view
and use the Loyalty Management app.

chunks.heading: 03-control-who-sees-what-in-loyalty-management
chunks.body:
---
title: "Control Who Sees What in Loyalty Management"
source_pdf: "loyalty_management_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "15-29"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Control Who Sees What in Loyalty Management

| Learn About the Core App Features | Explore Member Rewards | Achieve More With Loyalty Management |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Video: Watch a Demo Watch: Design a Loyalty Program Watch: Launch a Loyalty Program | Watch: Add a Loyalty Program's Rewards Watch: Take a Tour of Promotion Setup Watch: Process Member Redemptions using Promotion Setup in Loyalty Management Watch: Use Loyalty Management Promotion Setup to Reward Members for Purchases Watch: Track the Performance of Your Promotions with CRM Analytics | Watch: Design Loyalty Engagement Widgets As Per Your Company's Branding Watch: Embed Loyalty Engagement Widgets Watch: Experience Loyalty Management on Your Mobile Devices Watch: Loyalty Management POS API for In-Store and E- Commerce Unlocked Package Watch: Loyalty Management POS API for In- Store and E-Commerce Powered by MuleSoft |

Control Who Sees What in Loyalty Management
Your Salesforce admin enables Loyalty Management in your org. When enabled, users have edit access to
loyalty objects and records. Users can create profiles and assign permissions to access loyalty application
as appropriate.
Loyalty Management Permission Sets
Give users in your org the required access to Loyalty Management features. Use the permission sets
provided by Salesforce, or clone the default permission sets to create role-based custom permission
sets for the users.
Loyalty Management Permissions
Provide permissions to invocable actions, user interface actions, and connect APIs. Use sharing rules
to share and control access of loyalty records for users.
Implement Field-Level Security in Loyalty Management
Field-level security settings let you restrict users' access to view and edit specific fields.
Loyalty Management Permission Sets
Give users in your org the required access to Loyalty Management features. Use the permission sets
provided by Salesforce, or clone the default permission sets to create role-based custom permission sets

Loyalty Management Control Who Sees What in Loyalty Management
for the users.
Loyalty Management Permission Set
The Loyalty Management permission set provides users access to the Loyalty Management app. Here's
how the app is used by different user groups:

| Role | Action |
| --- | --- |
| Loyalty program managers | • Create a loyalty program and complete its one- time setup. • Add partners to the loyalty program. • Add program rewards and run promotions. • Track member details and member transactions. |
| Customer service representatives | • Track the details of members and their activities. • Manage service resolution tasks such as adjusting member points, viewing member benefits, and issuing vouchers. |

Manage Loyalty Program Permission Set
The Manage Loyalty Program permission set includes Read, Create, Edit, Delete, View All Records, and
Modify All Records permissions for all Loyalty Management objects. You can clone the permission set to
create role-based custom permission sets.
Example An admin clones the permission set to create two custom permission sets. The first
custom permission set enables loyalty program managers to manage their loyalty program but not
delete member records such as transaction journals, vouchers, and badges. The second custom
permission set enables customer service representatives to view member information such as
membership number and to issue vouchers, or adjust points without being able to edit the loyalty
program setup.
Permission Set License for Loyalty Management
Each type of Loyalty Management license provides a specific number of Loyalty Management
permission set licenses. When you assign a Loyalty Management permission set to a user, the number
of Loyalty Management permission set licenses that are available in your org reduces by one.
See Also

To view the total number of Loyalty Management permission set licenses that are available in your org, in

count of Loyalty Management permission set licenses remaining in the org decreases by one.
If you need more permission set licenses in addition to the ones that the Loyalty Management license
enabled in your org provides, contact your Salesforce representative.

chunks.body:

title: "Manage Loyalty Programs"
source_pdf: "loyalty_management_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "30-73"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Manage Loyalty Programs

| Object | Field Name | Read Access | Edit Access |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Promotion | Current Liability Amount |  |  |
| Promotion | Transaction Journal Type |  |  |
| Promotion | Is Points With Factor |  |  |
| Promotion | Point Factor |  |  |
| Benefit | Priority Sequence |  |  |
| Benefit | Unit of Measure |  |  |
| Benefit | Value |  |  |
| MemberBenefit | Primary Reference Object Record |  |  |
| MemberBenefit | Primary Reference Object Record Title |  |  |
| MemberBenefit | Source Type |  |  |

11.. From Setup, in the Quick Find box, enter Permission Sets, then select Permission Sets. Or from
Setup, in the Quick Find box, enter Profiles, then select Profiles.
22.. Select a permission set or profile.
Depending on which interface you're using, complete one of these actions:
••
Permission sets or enhanced profile user interface-In the Find Settings box, enter the name of the
object that you want, and select it from the list. Click Edit, then scroll to the Field Permissions
section.
••
Original profile user interface-In the Field-Level Security section, click View next to the object that
you want to modify, and then click Edit .
33.. Specify the field's access level.
44.. Save your changes.
Manage Loyalty Programs
A loyalty program is your starting point for setting up Loyalty Management in your Salesforce org. It helps
to design, organize, and customize the loyalty building blocks with loyalty programs.
The Loyalty Management app lets you create and set up loyalty programs for your Salesforce
organization. You can start by creating a loyalty program and then add tiers, tier groups, program
currencies, members, partners, voucher definitions, benefits, and promotions for your organization. A
loyalty program tracks and manages members' activities, measures tier points, and assesses members for
tier upgrades. The program rewards and recognizes members with points or vouchers.
Key Elements of Your Loyalty Program

Loyalty Management Manage Loyalty Programs
To build an effective loyalty program, understand the aspects that matter to your customers.
Create a Loyalty Program by Using Simplified Setup
Configure and activate your loyalty program in a few easy steps. The simplified setup enables program
managers to create a loyalty program, and add redeemable currencies, define tier groups and tiers,
map benefits, and track eligible activities.
Create a Loyalty Program Using Record Pages
The Loyalty Management app lets you create and set up loyalty programs for your Salesforce
organization. You can configure each component of your loyalty program from the respective record
page.
Loyalty Program Home Page
The loyalty program home page allows you to view the primary details of a loyalty program and
manage the day-to-day activities such as creating promotions, and managing games, vouchers,
partners and processes with a single click.
Loyalty Management Home Page
Start the day with the Loyalty Management Home page. Determine what needs your attention, and
make better decisions to improve member engagement.
Key Elements of Your Loyalty Program
To build an effective loyalty program, understand the aspects that matter to your customers.
A loyalty program consists of these key elements:
•
Currencies
•
Tier Groups
•
Tiers
•
Benefits
•
Activities
Loyalty Program Currencies
Loyalty Program Currencies refer to qualifying and non-qualifying currencies associated with the
program. Qualifying currency refers to the engagement of a member with the program and is
associated with a tier group. Qualifying currency is used for a member's tier evaluation. Non-qualifying
currency refers to the points that the member earns for redemption.
Loyalty Tier Groups
Loyalty tier groups help you define tiers and the associated tier model for member recognition.
Loyalty Tiers
Tiers categorize loyalty program members based on their loyalty activities. As members engage more
with the program, they're recognized to higher tiers and get more exclusive benefits.
Benefits
Use benefits to reward and motivate loyalty customers when they increase their engagement with the
loyalty program.
Events in Loyalty Management

Loyalty Management Manage Loyalty Programs
A loyalty event constitutes any member activity that creates a transaction journal. For example,
member purchases, social media posts, app downloads, voucher redemptions are all tracked and
processed in Loyalty Management as events. Loyalty events also include events performed by
automated processes or service reps and not directly performed by members like point expiration,
qualifying points, and manual points adjustment.
Loyalty Program Currencies
Loyalty Program Currencies refer to qualifying and non-qualifying currencies associated with the
program. Qualifying currency refers to the engagement of a member with the program and is associated
with a tier group. Qualifying currency is used for a member's tier evaluation. Non-qualifying currency
refers to the points that the member earns for redemption.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions that
have Loyalty Management enabled.
You can set up the loyalty currencies for a loyalty program so that members accrue points when they
perform certain activities in the loyalty program.
The Loyalty App offers two types of currencies that you can incorporate in your program structure:
•
Qualifying Points (QPs): Measures a member's level of engagement in a loyalty program, and helps in
assessing a member's tier for upgrade or downgrade. Qualifying points can be associated with only
one tier group. A tier group can have more than one qualifying point.
•
Non-Qualifying Points (NQPs) : Points that members earn for their loyalty program-related activities,
which they can redeem for rewards.
Expiration models for non-qualifying points come in one of two types:
- Fixed: Each non-qualifying point in a member's balance expires after a fixed period specified for this
loyalty currency.
- Activity: If there's no member activity for the duration specified for this point type, the member's
entire non-qualifying points balance expires. A member's inactivity is determined by using the Last
Activity Date in the member record.
Important If you rename an existing currency, ensure that you manually update the currency name
in the associated Loyalty Member Currency records. If a currency is created after Loyalty Program
Member records are created, you've to set up a custom process to create Loyalty Member Currency
records for existing members. You can set up a flow or a batch process to create member currency
records for existing customers whenever a new currency is added to the loyalty program.
Negative Points
Members can now use more points than their current balance for redemptions. You can enable a
negative balance for the required non-qualifying currencies, and define the negative balance limit for
each tier. Enable a negative points balance in a loyalty program to increase member engagement,

Loyalty Management Manage Loyalty Programs
boost sales, and enhance customer loyalty.
Escrow Points
You can wait for a period before you credit points to members after they complete a transaction. You
can also specify the number of days that you want to hold members' points in escrow and
automatically release the points after the escrow period ends. Qualifying and nonqualifying points can
be held in escrow.
Points Accounting with Traceability
Points that members accrue for loyalty activities are associated with a liability. For better control over
liability, enforce how members redeem points by using currency subtypes. Create currency subtypes
for fixed-type non-qualifying currencies and restrict how those points are used during redemption.
Negative Points
Members can now use more points than their current balance for redemptions. You can enable a
negative balance for the required non-qualifying currencies, and define the negative balance limit for
each tier. Enable a negative points balance in a loyalty program to increase member engagement, boost
sales, and enhance customer loyalty.
Set Negative Points Limits
Define a negative balance limit for each tier in your loyalty program.
How Negative Points Work
Find out how to manage negative points and settle negative balances.
Set Negative Points Limits
Define a negative balance limit for each tier in your loyalty program.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management -
Growth or Loyalty Management - Advanced
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create a loyalty program currency tier:
Loyalty Management permission set
AND
Manage Loyalty Programs permission set
11.. In the Loyalty Programs tab, open your program name.
22.. On the Setup tab, select a non-qualifying currency that has negative points enabled.
33.. Click Related.

Loyalty Management Manage Loyalty Programs
44.. In the Loyalty Program Currency Tier related list, click New.
55.. Select a loyalty tier and enter a negative points balance limit for the tier.
66.. Save your changes.
Example The Cloud Kicks Inner Circle loyalty program has a non-qualifying currency called Member
Points. It also has a tier group, Inner Circle, with four tiers. The loyalty program manager sets a
negative limit for each tier in the Loyalty Program Currency Tier:
•
Silver: -1000
•
Gold: -2000
•
Diamond: -3000
•
Platinum: -4000
All the tiers are associated with Member Points.
How Negative Points Work
Find out how to manage negative points and settle negative balances.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management -
Growth or Loyalty Management - Advanced
What Happens When a Transaction Journal is Processed?
The Debit Points action in a loyalty program process or flow deducts points from a member's balance
when the action processes transaction journals. A negative adjustment is made if the member doesn't
have sufficient balance, and if the value doesn't exceed the negative limit of the member's tier. Members
can continue to redeem points up to their tier's negative limit.
Example Adria Darby, a new Cloud Kicks Inner Circle member, belongs to the Silver tier. On March
3, 2024, she purchases a pair of shoes for which she receives 2000 points, which brings her balance
to 2000 points. Later, she finds a shoe care kit that's available for 3000 points. On March 6, 2024,
Adria redeems her points for the kit. To process this transaction the program needs 3000 points.
The program debits 3000 points, which brings Adria's point balance to -1000.
How are Negative Points Settled?
When a member has a negative points balance, the date that the balance first went negative is updated
in the Redemption Settlement Pending From Date Time field in the Loyalty Member Currency object.
When a subsequent accrual transaction occurs, the newly credited points are used to adjust the negative

Loyalty Management Manage Loyalty Programs
balance. The Redemption Settlement Pending From Date Time field is updated with the date of the
accrual transaction.
Example As of March 6, 2024, Adria Darby's balance is -1000. On March 15, 2024, she purchases a
pair of socks and accrues 500 points. Her updated points balance is -500, and the Redemption
Settlement Pending From Date Time field is updated to March 15, 2024.
What Happens When Accruals Are Canceled?

chunks.body:
---

source_pdf: "loyalty_management_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "74-82"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Manage Clubs
Loyalty Management Home Page
Start the day with the Loyalty Management Home page. Determine what needs your attention, and make
better decisions to improve member engagement.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Developer, and Unlimited Editions that
have Loyalty Management enabled.
By default, the Home page displays these components:
•
The list of recently updated promotions and loyalty program partners prompts you to continue from
where you left off.
•
The recently updated promotions and partners belong to the primary loyalty program. You can make a
loyalty program the primary one by checking the Primary checkbox on the Loyalty Program details
page.
•
The Today's Tasks component lists your next five tasks due for the day.
•
The Items to Approve list allows you to quickly assess and address approval process requests.
•
Your Chatter feed and posts indicate where you're @mentioned.
Note What you see on the Home page depends on your permissions, edition, layout, and
customizations. Use the Lightning App Builder to customize your Home page. You can also include
the Loyalty Program Overview dashboard on your Home page from the Lightning App Builder .
Manage Clubs
Clubs are interest-based membership initiatives designed to provide customers with exclusive benefits
and privileges. Customers can join a club through a subscription model or based on their current loyalty
program tier. By offering unique benefits and exclusive promotions, clubs can enhance customer loyalty.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management -
Advanced
When you create a club, you can either link it to an existing loyalty program or create it as an
independent club. An independent club is internally linked to a dummy loyalty program named Club
Program.
Note You can create a maximum of 20 clubs under a single loyalty program.

Loyalty Management Manage Clubs
Key Roles in Clubs
Understand the key positions that drive the operations and engagement within clubs.
Clubs Data Model
Clubs are interest-based membership initiatives designed to provide exclusive benefits and privileges
to customers.
Club Setup
Learn about the necessary prerequisites for clubs and the audience of clubs.
Design a Club
To design a club, enter the basic information about your club, define various membership plans, and
establish clear eligibility criteria.
Club Management
Managing clubs involves a range of tasks, such as enrolling new members and leveraging business
APIs for streamlined operations.
Key Roles in Clubs
Understand the key positions that drive the operations and engagement within clubs.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management -
Advanced
Club Admin
•
Defines the club and determine if the club will be independent or part of a Loyalty program.
•
Defines member eligibility criteria.
•
Establishes subscription rules for joining the club.
•
Assigns benefit types and benefits to club members.
•
Assigns promotions and offers to club members.
System Admin
•
Sets up the clubs related list.
•
Sets up the club membership fees duration.
•
Sets up the payment model for club membership.
Clubs Data Model
Clubs are interest-based membership initiatives designed to provide exclusive benefits and privileges to
customers.

Loyalty Management Manage Clubs
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management -
Advanced
Clubs are modeled as loyalty tier groups. For each club, there is an associated loyalty tier that has the
same name as the loyalty tier group. This loyalty tier is then associated with various objects that store
eligibility criteria, benefits, and promotions.
Clubs are either independent or associated with existing loyalty programs. For independent clubs, a
dummy loyalty program is set up.
Note To access the objects that are part of the Clubs data model, user must be assigned the
Manage Loyalty Programs permission set.
These objects are associated with the clubs data model.
•
Loyalty Program: Stores the loyalty program that the club is a part of.
• ̌Loyalty Tier Group: Groups all your company's clubs together.
•
Loyalty Tier: Stores detailed information about each club, including its name, description, and specific
attributes.
•
Loyalty Tier Eligibility Source: Determines the eligibility criteria for club membership. The criteria can
be based on:
- Loyalty Tiers: Loyalty program members from specific tiers.

Loyalty Management Manage Clubs
- Segments: Customers who are part of specific Data Cloud segments.
- Campaigns: Customers who are part of specific campaigns.
- Actionable Lists: Customers who are part of specific actionable lists.
•
Loyalty Tier Membership Fee Option: Stores information about the subscription fees for each club,
including the amount and payment models, such as:
- Cash
- Loyalty Currency
- Cash and Loyalty Currency
•
Loyalty Tier Benefit: Associates club to a benefit that the club offers.
•
Loyalty Tier Promotion: Associates exclusive promotions with the club, which you can offer to
members.
•
Loyalty Program Member: Stores information about a club member.
•
Loyalty Member Tier: Tracks the membership tenure of each member, including the start and end
dates of their club membership.
•
Loyalty Program Member Promotion: Associates a club member with the promotion they're eligible
for.
•
Loyalty Membership Lifecycle: Manages the stages of a member's subscription, from initial enrollment
to ongoing engagement, and any changes in status.
Note These objects and fields are shared between loyalty programs and clubs of your organization.
Club Setup
Learn about the necessary prerequisites for clubs and the audience of clubs.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management -
Advanced
Prerequisites for Creating Clubs
Before you set up a club, work with your admin to complete the prerequisites.
•
Permissions: Assign users the Loyalty Management and the Manage Loyalty Programs permission sets
to give them access to the Clubs Management app and permissions to create clubs.
•
Clubs related list: Turn on the Interest-Based Clubs setting to view the Clubs related list under a loyalty
program. You can update the related list on the page layout from the Lightning App Builder.
For more details on customizing page layouts, see Customize Page Layouts with the Enhanced Page
Layout Editor
You can add flexibility and control by using a dynamic related list. When you configure a dynamic
related list, configure these options.

Loyalty Management Manage Clubs
- Parent Record: Loyalty Program
- Related List: Loyalty Tier Groups
- Related List Label: Clubs
- Related List Fields: Add fields such as Name, Description, Start Date, End Date, and Active.
For more details dynamic related lists, see Create Dynamic Related Lists in Lightning App Builder
•
Duration: Set up the values for the timeframe within which a customer must pay the club membership
fees. Duration is a dynamic enum field and you can configure the picklist values such as monthly,
quarterly, and yearly. For more details about adding picklist values using Object Manager, see Add or
Edit Picklist Values. Use the Membership Duration Type picklist field under the Loyalty Tier
Membership Fee Option Object.
•
Payment model: Set up the values for the type of payment that a customer can use for club
membership. Payment Model is a dynamic enum field and you can configure the picklist values such
as cash, loyalty currency, or cash and loyalty currency. For more details about adding picklist values
using Object Manager, see Add or Edit Picklist Values. Use the Fee Type picklist field under the Loyalty
Tier Membership Fee Option Object.
Club Audience
•
Customers who are a part of specific Data Cloud segments, campaigns, and actionable lists
•
Members of specific tiers within a loyalty program
Design a Club
To design a club, enter the basic information about your club, define various membership plans, and
establish clear eligibility criteria.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management -
Advanced
You can create clubs either from the Clubs Management app or from the home page of a loyalty
program.
From the home page of a loyalty program, navigate to Clubs > New Club.
From the App Launcher, open Clubs Management. On the Club Setup page, click Create Club.
Enter Primary Information
To get started with your club, enter the basic details, including the name, description, and the start
and end dates.
Define Club Eligibility
After you create a club, add membership plans and configure eligibility criteria for customers to enroll.

Loyalty Management Manage Clubs
Add Benefits and Promotions
After you configure the eligibility criteria of the club, you can add the benefits that your club offers,
and the promotions that are exclusive to your club members. You can create promotions for club
members or offer the company's already running promotions.
Enter Primary Information
To get started with your club, enter the basic details, including the name, description, and the start and
end dates.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience

chunks.heading: 06-manage-loyalty-program-partners
chunks.body:
---
title: "Manage Loyalty Program Partners"
source_pdf: "loyalty_management_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "83-106"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Manage Loyalty Program Partners
44.. Save your changes.
Note You can use the Club Member Enrollment API to enroll members in your club from an external
system such as your website. The integration user who is executing the API must be assigned the
Loyalty Management and Manage Loyalty Programs permission sets.
Business APIs to Manage Clubs
You can use business APIs to manage your clubs effectively.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management -
Advanced
To manage the enrollment of new members, use the Club Member Enrollment business API.
To renew the membership of existing members of the club, use the Club Membership Renewal business
API.
Manage Loyalty Program Partners
Loyalty Management enables the onboarding and managing of cross-industry program partners to

Loyalty Management Manage Loyalty Program Partners
increase member engagement with the loyalty program. Partnerships give your members more options
to engage members and help them earn and redeem points for complementary products or services.
You can associate partners from different industries with loyalty program partners. Members can benefit
from the partner and organization. Partners can be an accrual partner, a redemption partner, or both. An
accrual partner rewards members with points for doing business with them. A redemption partner is
where members spend their points.
You can allow a partner to link with a loyalty program membership to seamlessly access co-branded
rewards. For more information about linking partners, see Link Partners With Loyalty Program
Membership.
You can also add product or product categories to the loyalty program that the partner deals with. There
are two types of products:
•
Generic products are modeled into product categories and assigned on a category level.
•
Specific products are modeled into product tables. They allow you to dive into product categories and
assign selective products.
Add a Program Partner
You can add program partners to the loyalty programs. The program partners can only be an account
in the organization.
Link Partners With Loyalty Program Membership
Members can link their membership with partner accounts or partner loyalty memberships to
seamlessly access co-branded rewards, such as bonus rewards or free upgrades to a higher tier. The
loyalty program member and partner must belong to the same loyalty program. Linking memberships
provides members with additional benefits in the home loyalty program. For example, a member
staying at a hotel can get a discount on a car rental of a partner brand during the stay.
Add Products or Product Categories
Before you add products or product categories for your program partner, create products in the
Product object and categories in the Categories object.
Experience Cloud Site Access for Partners
Partners often require access to view the details of a loyalty program and its rewards and members. In
some cases, partners need access to update the information in the loyalty program. For example, you
can provide partners access to view the active promotions that they're part of and to create
transactions where members have purchased a partner product. Based on the requirements of your
partners, you can provide them with access to Loyalty Management objects from your Experience
Cloud site.
Expanded Partner Management
With Expanded Partner Management, brands can bill partners for the liability that the loyalty program
has accrued when members of the program purchase a partner product or service. Brands can set up
a prepaid or a postpaid billing system for partners, review the value that the partner is bringing to the
program, and track partner transaction history. Brands can also run joint promotions along with
parnters to increase the program's appeal to members.

Loyalty Management Manage Loyalty Program Partners
Add a Program Partner
You can add program partners to the loyalty programs. The program partners can only be an account in
the organization.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions
Available in: Orgs that have the B2C - Loyalty, B2C - Loyalty Plus, B2B - Loyalty, Loyalty Management -
Growth, or Loyalty Management - Advanced license enabled
11.. In the Loyalty Programs tab, select your loyalty program.
22.. On the Members and Partners tab, under Loyalty Program Partners, click New.
33.. Enter a name for the program partner.
44.. Select an account for the program partner.
55.. Select the type of program partner.
••
If the partner rewards member with points for doing business with them, select Accrual.
••
If the members redeem their points with the partner, select Redemption.
••
If the partner is accrual and redemption, select Both.
66.. Select the program partnership start date and end date.
77.. Select the type of industry.
Picklist values for Industry aren't provided by default. Based on the requirements of the organization,
admins must add picklist values for the field in Object Manager.
88.. Select Active.
99.. Select Billing Type of the partner.
For prepaid billing, the partner buys point packs from the loyalty program. For postpaid billing, the
partner pays the bill amount after offering their products or services to members. This is available only
for Expanded Partner Management.
1100.. Select the category for the program partnership.
1111.. Enter the accrual cost per unit and the redemption cost per unit.
••
The Accrual Cost per Unit is the cost per point associated with the transactions of the accrual
partner.
••
The Redemption Cost per Unit is the cost per point associated with the transactions of the
redemption partner.
Note The Accrual Cost per Unit and the Redemption Cost per Unit fields are not available by
default. Navigate to the Object Manager > Loyalty Program Partner > Page Layouts and add the
fields to the custom UI screen.
1122.. In the Partner Linkage Details section, enter the following information:
aa.. To allow linking of the partner with other loyalty program memberships, select Allow Linking.
bb.. Select a date and time from which you can link the partner with other loyalty program
memberships.
cc.. Select a date and time after which you can't link the partner with other loyalty program

Loyalty Management Manage Loyalty Program Partners
Note Make sure the linking window falls within the program partnership start and end dates.
1133.. Save your changes.
See Also
Salesforce Help: Billing Type Options for Partners
Salesforce Help: Partner's Conversion Factor for Currencies
Link Partners With Loyalty Program Membership
Members can link their membership with partner accounts or partner loyalty memberships to seamlessly
access co-branded rewards, such as bonus rewards or free upgrades to a higher tier. The loyalty program
member and partner must belong to the same loyalty program. Linking memberships provides members
with additional benefits in the home loyalty program. For example, a member staying at a hotel can get a
discount on a car rental of a partner brand during the stay.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management -
Advanced
When partner accounts are linked, segmentation and targeting become more powerful because you can
combine loyalty data with partner-specific attributes. For example, a loyalty program can target members
who have linked a specific credit card type but haven't made a purchase in six months with a special
promotion.
Example Sarah is a frequent traveler and a member of the Coral Cloud Resorts (her home program).
She also regularly uses taxi services from Ursa Major Solar Transit (the partner program). Sarah links
her Coral Cloud membership with her Ursa Major account. When Sarah books a premium ride to
the airport via Ursa Major, she automatically earns Coral Cloud loyalty points for the transaction.
Because her memberships are linked, Coral Cloud analyzes her travel patterns to send her
personalized offers for her upcoming hotel stays or bundled travel packages.
Link and Unlink Partners by Using APIs
A loyalty program member or a customer service representative can link and unlink partners. The
partner linking process begins when a customer initiates a linkage from either a partner app or the
loyalty ecosystem.
Link Partners by Using Loyalty Management
The loyalty program member and partner must belong to the same loyalty program. A loyalty program
manager or a customer service representative can initiate the linking. Make sure that you initiate
linking within the linkage window that the partner allows.
Unlink Partners by Using Loyalty Management
Unlinking a partner marks the linkage status as Inactive and stops the cross-program benefits. When

Loyalty Management Manage Loyalty Program Partners
you link the same partner back, the system considers it as new linkage and creates new records.
Link and Unlink Partners by Using APIs
A loyalty program member or a customer service representative can link and unlink partners. The partner
linking process begins when a customer initiates a linkage from either a partner app or the loyalty
ecosystem.
Depending on the starting point, the user is authenticated and authorized via the respective platform
before the system calls the Link Program Member Partner connect API. This API call utilizes specific
inputs, including the loyalty program ID, member ID, partner ID, and an external reference number, to
process the request. Finally, Salesforce creates a new linkage record and sets the status to Active,
enabling seamless cross-program rewards. For more information see Link Program Member Partner.
The partner unlinking process initiates when a customer requests to disconnect their accounts. This
request triggers a call to the Unlink Program Member Partner connect API, terminates the connection
between the loyalty program and the partner. The system automatically marks the linkage status as
Inactive to stop cross-program benefits and updates the unlink date to maintain an accurate historical
audit trail. For more information, see Unlink Program Member Partner.
The Link Member and Partner and Unlink Member and Partner connect APIs support custom
parameters. You can add custom fields to the Loyalty Program Member Linked Partner object and those
fields will be available to use in the Get Member Partner Linkage Status rule action. For more information
on the data model of partner linking, see Loyalty Program Member Linked Partner.
Link Partners by Using Loyalty Management
The loyalty program member and partner must belong to the same loyalty program. A loyalty program
manager or a customer service representative can initiate the linking. Make sure that you initiate linking
within the linkage window that the partner allows.

Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management -
Advanced

chunks.body:
---
title: "Vouchers"
source_pdf: "loyalty_management_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "107-134"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Vouchers
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Developer, and Unlimited Editions
Available in: Orgs that have the B2C - Loyalty Management Plus or the Loyalty Management - Advanced
license enabled
11.. From the Loyalty Program Promotion Partners related list of a joint promotion record, click Add.
The Add button is available for joint promotions only.
22.. The joint promotion name defaults in the Name field.
33.. Select a partner from the same loyalty program.
44.. Enter the partner's contribution percentage.
55.. Save your changes.
Review the partners and their contribution in the Loyalty Program Promotion Partners list. Activate the
joint promotion only if the total contribution of the partners is 100 percent.
See Also
Salesforce Help: How Can I Change a Partner in an Active Joint Promotion?
FAQs for Joint Promotion
Some of the common and frequently asked questions and responses in Joint Promotion are as follows.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Developer, and Unlimited Editions
Available in: Orgs that have the B2C - Loyalty Management Plus or the Loyalty Management - Advanced
license enabled
How Can I Change a Partner in an Active Joint Promotion?
You can't change or delete a partner of an active joint promotion. To change a partner, you first need to
deactivate the joint promotion and then delete the partner. Now, you can add another partner from the
same loyalty program to the joint promotion.
Vouchers
Vouchers are offered to customers and loyalty program members as rewards for engaging with the brand
and as compensation for service recovery actions. Your company and its loyalty programs can offer
different types of vouchers based on your business requirements. You can also choose from a range of
different options for how vouchers are issued to customers and members.

Loyalty Management Vouchers
Voucher Design and Strategies in Loyalty Management
Learn about the different types of vouchers, the ways in which you can design vouchers, and how
vouchers are issued.
Add Your Company's Voucher Definitions
Voucher definitions are templates that are used to issue vouchers. You set up a voucher definition one
time and then use the voucher definition to issue vouchers on demand or by configuring flows and
loyalty program rules. Based on your requirements, createas many voucher definitions as needed and
use each of them for different purposes.
Automatic Generation of Voucher Codes
You can configure a voucher definition to ensure that codes of the vouchers issued by using the
voucher definition are automatically generated. The codes that are automatically generated contain a
prefix and a suffix that you specify. The codes also consist of alphanumeric characters that make the
voucher code unique and you can specify the length of the alphanumeric characters in the voucher
definition.
Issue Vouchers as a Reward
There are various ways in which you can issue vouchers as a reward to customers and members of your
loyalty program. For example, vouchers are issued to customers and loyalty program members for
completing a purchase or an experiential activity. Either loyalty program managers or admins can set
up automated processes that check for a customer's or a loyalty program member's eligibility for a
voucher and issue it to them. Vouchers can also be issued to contacts, accounts, and loyalty program
members as a promotion reward.
Offer Vouchers as a Service Recovery Action
Vouchers are a great way to gratify customers who have had a negative experience while engaging with
your company or the loyalty program. When interacting with members or while working on a
member's case, service reps can issue vouchers to members. In case members don't receive their
eligible vouchers, service reps, and loyalty program managers can issue the vouchers to the impacted
members.
Ways to View Issued Vouchers
Customer and loyalty program members often reach out to customer support to learn about the
vouchers that are available to them. Program managers also want to understand how issued vouchers
are being used and are performing.
Ways to Reserve and Redeem Vouchers
On most commerce and shopping sites, members redeem vouchers either when adding products to
the cart or when checking out a cart during payment. Often, customers abandon their carts or their
orders aren't successfully placed. In these scenarios, you can lock the vouchers that customers want to
redeem until customers have successfully placed their order. In retail scenarios, vouchers can be
reserved until orders are fulfilled or until the order return period is over.
Ways to Cancel Vouchers
There are scenarios where issued vouchers must be canceled. For example, vouchers that are issued
after members place an order must be revoked if members cancel or return their order. In other
scenarios, duplicate vouchers or incorrectly issued vouchers must be canceled.
Automated Management of Vouchers Issued for Transactions
Loyalty program members are issued vouchers when they complete a purchase or nonpurchase

Loyalty Management Vouchers
activities. For purchase-related activities, there are scenarios where members aren't eligible to redeem
an already issued voucher. For example, members aren't eligible to use a voucher that was issued for
an order that is canceled. Loyalty Management automatically takes care of changing the status of
vouchers status based on the status of their related transaction journals.
Automated Voucher Expiration
The status of unredeemed or partially redeemed vouchers changes to Expired after the vouchers pass
the expiration date. Loyalty Management automatically changes the status of up to 2 million vouchers
to Expired hourly.
Provide Members Access to Vouchers on Your Company's Site
With Experience Cloud sharing sets, admins can provide contacts and loyalty program members
access to view their vouchers on the company or the loyalty program's site.
Developer Resources for Vouchers
Developers can manage and integrate vouchers with external systems such as point of sales (POS) and
order management systems. Learn about the API resources that are available to developers to manage
vouchers across their lifecycle.
Voucher Design and Strategies in Loyalty Management
Learn about the different types of vouchers, the ways in which you can design vouchers, and how
vouchers are issued.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management
Voucher Types

| Voucher Type | How It Works | Examples |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Fixed Value | Offer customers and loyalty program members a fixed amount of discount on their next purchase. The discount can be either on a complete order or a specific product. | • Get US $10 off on your next order • Get US $25 off on Cloud Kicks shoes |
| Discount Percentage | Offer customers and loyalty program members a percentage discount on their order or on the price of a specific product. | • Get 10% off on your next order • Get 25% off on Cloud Kicks shoes |

Loyalty Management Vouchers

| Voucher Type | How It Works | Examples |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Free Product or Service | Offers customers a free product or service in exchange for the voucher. | • Get a free pair of socks • Get a free spa |

Voucher Design Options

| Business Requirement | How to Achieve It |
| --- | --- |
| Voucher recipients can't redeem vouchers after a specific date or time | Issue vouchers that expire on a fixed date or date time. |
| Voucher recipients can't redeem vouchers a specific period after the voucher is issued | Issue vouchers that expire after a fixed period of days or minutes. |
| Voucher recipients can redeem the voucher to get a partner product or service | Issue vouchers that are associated with partners. |
| Voucher recipients must use their unique voucher code to redeem their voucher | Turn on the setting that automatically generates a unique code for each issued voucher. |

Voucher Recipients and Distribution Strategy

| Recipient | Business Requirement | How To Achieve It |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Customers of the company | Issue vouchers to contacts as part of an automated process set up by the admin | Use the Issue Voucher action in a flow |
| Members of the loyalty program | Issue vouchers to members as part of an automated process set up by the admin | Use the Issue Voucher action in a flow |
|  | Issue vouchers to members as part of an automated process set up specifically for a loyalty program by the program manager | Issue Voucher action in a loyalty process rule |
|  | Issue vouchers to members instantly as a resolution for customer cases or to resolve a dispute | Issue Voucher quick action |
|  | Issue vouchers to members as part of a promotion | Issue Voucher reward type on the Quick Promotion guided |

Loyalty Management Vouchers

| Recipient | Business Requirement | How To Achieve It |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  | setup |

Voucher Validity

| Business Requirement | How It Works |
| --- | --- |
| Voucher recipients can redeem vouchers as soon as they're issued. | The voucher definition that's used to issue a voucher consists of an effective date. If the date on which a voucher is issued is before the effective date of the voucher definition then the voucher's effective date is the same as the voucher definition's effective date. If the date on which a voucher is issued is after the effective date of the voucher definition then the voucher's effective date is the same as the current date. When issuing a voucher, you can change the effective date of the voucher based on your requirement. |
| Voucher is redeemable starting vouchers from a specific date. |  |
| Voucher recipients must not be able to redeem vouchers after they've expired | Loyalty Management removes expiring unused vouchers that are past their expiration date with an automated process behind the scene. |

Add Your Company's Voucher Definitions
Voucher definitions are templates that are used to issue vouchers. You set up a voucher definition one
time and then use the voucher definition to issue vouchers on demand or by configuring flows and
loyalty program rules. Based on your requirements, createas many voucher definitions as needed and
use each of them for different purposes.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create a voucher definition:
Loyalty Management permission set

Loyalty Management Vouchers
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
AND
Manage Loyalty Programs permission set
Here's how you can map your business requirements to key voucher definition fields when you create a
voucher definition record:

| Business Requirement | Voucher Definition Configuration | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Voucher definition issues vouchers that offer one-time discounts worth only specific amounts | Select Fixed Value as the type and enter a face value. In orgs that have multiple currencies set up, you can select the currency ISO code of the fixed value. | Get US $10 off on your next purchase |
| Voucher definition issues vouchers that offer only percentage discounts | Select Discount Percentage as the type and enter the discount in percentage. | Get 25% off on your next purchase |
| Voucher definition issues vouchers that offer free products or services | Select Product or Service as the type and select the product that the vou
