chunks.heading: part-01
chunks.body:
---
title: "Manage and Work with Promotions (part 1/4)"
source_pdf: "loyalty_management_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "135-530"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Manage and Work with Promotions

| Task | Resource |
| --- | --- |
|  | • Cancel a Voucher |

Manage and Work with Promotions
Promotions are a way to engage loyalty program members by providing them valuable offers at a specific
time, often tied to a seasonal sales cycle. Promotions can play a significant role in increasing product
sales and generating business revenues. Promotions help loyalty program members get a better return
on their investment with attractive offers in the form of vouchers or loyalty points.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management -
Growth or Loyalty Management - Advanced
Promotions give you the ability to define the right offers for various customer segments at the right time.
Promotions can be of these types:
Standard Promotions: Promotions that provide immediate benefits to members. These promotions are
based on a single transaction and don't require member behavior tracking for promotion execution. For
example, to clear out stock before winter, you offer 20% off for jacket purchases on October 25.
Cumulative Promotions: Promotions that reward members based on their longer-term behaviors, such
as participation frequency or the amount of money spent to qualify for a reward. Cumulative promotion
is based on multiple customer transactions and requires member behavior tracking for the promotion
execution. For example, to boost overall sales, your company instructs all stores in a store group to offer
1000 credit points to consumers. The points are credited only on purchases above $1000 during the
month of January.
Joint Promotions: Promotions involving one or more partners who jointly contribute and engage their
members better and capture partner liability at the promotion level. For example, Cloud Kicks and NTO
contribute 40% and 60% respectively for a joint promotion. The liability is also 40% for Cloud Kicks
and 60% for NTO as liability distribution is based on contribution percentage. When a new loyalty
program member uses this promotion for a partner's products or services and accrues 1,000 points,
Cloud Kicks is liable for 400 points and NTO is liable for 600 points.
Joint promotion is available only for Expanded Partner Management .
In Loyalty Management, promotions use Data Cloud or Salesforce Reports to identify members for
promotion and use Marketing Cloud Engagement to send the campaigns to the members.

Loyalty Management Manage and Work with Promotions
Note Loyalty Management and Data Cloud must be installed in the same Salesforce org.
Explore and Learn
Watch this video to understand how to add rewards for your loyalty program.
Watch the video: https://play.vidyard.com/P3k3wpwapNABrQVdBnWRmq
If you aren't able to watch the video in full screen, open the video on a new tab: Add a Loyalty
Program's Rewards.
Global Promotions Management
With Global Promotions Management, marketing managers and loyalty program managers can design
both pricing and non-pricing promotions. They can design the promotions to run as part of the
company's loyalty program or outside the loyalty program as part of the company's 360-degree
marketing strategy. Companies can use Global Promotions Management to run granular promotions
with in-built limits and promotion launch emails. Also, your company's customers get a real-time view
of the promotions that are applicable for their cart and can apply the eligible promotions of their
choice during checkout.
Create a Promotion
You can create loyalty promotions and use reward vouchers and loyalty points to acquire new
customers or engage existing customers. You can define the type of the promotion, the loyalty
program associated with the promotion, whether enrollment is required for the promotion, and the
promotion's start and end dates. You can also define how to reward the members upon fulfilling the
requirement of promotion by vouchers or credit points, and the status of the promotion.
Add Partner Products
You can associate products or product categories that are applied to a particular promotion.
Set Up Promotions with Data Cloud
To identify and create the right promotions for the right customers, associate promotions with Data
Cloud segments.
Set Up Promotions With Campaigns
You can set up and manage promotions with campaign members when you don't have Data Cloud in
your Salesforce org. Use campaigns to identify the right customer segments and send promotional
campaigns to these customers. The segments and members are created using Salesforce Reports. Use
data extensions with loyalty data and send promotional campaigns to efficiently create and track
customer journeys through Journey Builder.
Decide Eligible Promotion Audience by Using Actionable Segmentation
Actionable Segmentation provides your company an easy-to-configure lightweight solution to find
loyalty program members who are eligible for promotions. Program managers provide admins the
criteria to filter the eligible loyalty program members for promotions. Admins create an actionable
segmentation list definition based on the requirements. Program managers create the eligible
members actionable list from the definition and associate the actionable list to the promotion the
actionable list belongs to.

Loyalty Management Manage and Work with Promotions
Apply Promotions and Update Members' Progress
When processing transaction journals, use Flow actions to find promotions that are applicable for a
transaction and the transaction's associated member. Use the Apply Promotion template flow to apply
selected promotions to members. The flow template can credit points or issue a voucher.
Promotion Calendar
Loyalty program managers can visualize all promotions of a loyalty program on the promotion
calendar. The promotion calendar lists events, so program managers can check whether promotions
are planned during company and social events. On the calendar, program managers can select a date
range and view the active and inactive promotions available during the period. Program managers can
view promotions in groups or filter promotions to easily find the promotions that they're looking for.
The promotion calendar helps program managers easily find white spaces where new promotions can
be added. And, view the details of multiple promotions in a single view helping you identify
duplicates.
Clone Promotions
Loyalty program managers can clone their seasonal or repetitive promotions. Instead of creating a
promotion from scratch, loyalty program managers can clone a similar promotion, edit the cloned
promotion if necessary, and activate the new promotion. Loyalty program managers can clone
promotions that are created either using the standard Promotion page layout or using the Quick
Promotion guided flow.
View and Track Member Promotions
View the promotions offered to a member in the loyalty program. For Cumulative type promotions,
you can track the member's progress towards achieving the promotion's target usage.
Personalized Promotion Recommendations
Use the Promotion Recommendations API to recommend personalized promotions to your
customers. The API leverages Salesforce Personalization's machine learning capabilities to recommend
promotions based on the products each customer is most likely to purchase. Show these
recommended promotions on your website by using the configurable Recommended Promotion
widget.
Promotion Integration with Marketing Cloud Personalization
Use Marketing Cloud Personalization (formerly Interaction Studio) to provide targeted promotions to
members in real time. Configure your Salesforce org and Marketing Cloud org to ensure that your
promotion-related data is available in Marketing Cloud Personalization. This configuration requires you
to create segments by using Data Cloud.
Flow Actions for Promotions
Use the three Flow actions to find eligible promotions for members and track members' progress
toward availing cumulative promotions.
Global Promotions Management
With Global Promotions Management, marketing managers and loyalty program managers can design
both pricing and non-pricing promotions. They can design the promotions to run as part of the
company's loyalty program or outside the loyalty program as part of the company's 360-degree
marketing strategy. Companies can use Global Promotions Management to run granular promotions with

Loyalty Management Manage and Work with Promotions
in-built limits and promotion launch emails. Also, your company's customers get a real-time view of the
promotions that are applicable for their cart and can apply the eligible promotions of their choice during
checkout.
Learn About Global Promotions Management
Learn about the features of Global Promotions Management and the key roles involved. Explore the
workflow to design and run promotions with Global Promotions Management.
Set Up Global Promotions Management
Turn on Global Promotions Management in your org and provide marketing managers the permission
to design promotions by using the Global Promotions wizard. Connect Global Promotions
Management with a Marketing Cloud Engagement business unit to automatically create promotion
launch Engagement journeys when marketing managers activate promotions.
Design Promotions
With Global Promotions Management, companies can run two types of promotions. Companies can
run promotions as part of their loyalty program and run promotions for their complete customer base
outside their loyalty program. Marketing managers can use the Promotion Console app to create
promotions for the company's loyalty program or outside the loyalty program. Marketing managers
can also manage their day-to-day promotion management tasks from the Promotion Console app.
Loyalty program managers and marketing managers can create loyalty promotions from the Loyalty
Management app.
Evaluate Promotions
After promotions are defined, marketing managers can configure how the promotions are evaluated
and applied to customer carts.
Run Promotions
After you activate your promotions, you can run the promotions for your customers. Eligible customers
can use the promotion from the promotion's start date time until the promotion's end date time. If
your promotion doesn't have an end date time, then customers can use the promotion until you
deactivate the promotion.
Manage Promotions
After promotions are activated and running, marketing managers can track the usage of promotions
among customers and loyalty program members. Marketing managers can also easily replicate
seasonal promotions and promotions that run multiple times a year.
Learn About Global Promotions Management
Learn about the features of Global Promotions Management and the key roles involved. Explore the
workflow to design and run promotions with Global Promotions Management.
Global Promotions Management Availability with Other Salesforce Products
Learn about the Global Promotions Management capabilities that are available with other Salesforce
products.
Map Your Business Requirements to Global Promotions Management Features
Companies that run promotions for their customers have varied business requirements. Learn how
Global Promotions Management supports your business and customer needs.

Loyalty Management Manage and Work with Promotions
Key Roles in Global Promotions Management
Learn about the different user personas involved in a typical Global Promotions Management
implementation and their responsibilities.
Quick Promotions Without Global Promotions Management
Companies can run promoti

program that the promotion belongs to.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management

Loyalty Management Manage and Work with Promotions
Promotion's Primary Details (1)
Define the key aspects of the promotion:

| Attribute | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Promotion's name and | Enter the promotion's internal name and customer-facing display name. |

Loyalty Management Manage and Work with Promotions

| Attribute | Function |
| --- | --- |
| objective | Also, enter the promotion's use case and marketing objectives. |
| Promotion's priority | Enter the promotion's priority. If a customer is eligible for multiple promotions, the company's shopping site lists the eligible promotions based on the priority number. |
| Promotion's code | Enter the promotion's unique code. Customers use the promotion code to select the promotion that they want to apply on their cart. |
| Promotion's duration | Select the start date and end date for the promotion. Customers can apply active promotions on their cart only between the promotion's start and end date. To run an evergreen promotion, leave the end date blank. |
| Promotion's business unit | Select the Marketing Cloud business unit that the promotion belongs to. After you design the promotion's launch email and activate the promotion, a journey is automatically created in the selected business unit. If you don't select a business unit, you can't design the promotion's launch email in the Quick Promotion wizard. If you can't find the business unit that the promotion belongs to, request your Salesforce admin to connect the business unit with Global Promotions Management in Setup. |
| Promotion's currency | Select the currency for the promotion. All financial values related to the promotion use the selected currency. |
| Promotion's rule library | Select the active Global Promotions Management type rule library that contains the custom tag mappings for the promotion's context definition. Here are a few scenarios when you must select a rule library for your promotion: • If promotion rules that you want to create must use the Build Your Own Promotion template. • If there are multiple Global Promotions Management type rule libraries active in the org. The rule library that you select decides that context tags that are available for you to create promotion eligibility criteria and promotion rule eligibility criteria. If you aren't sure about which rule library to use for your promotion, check with your Salesforce admin. |

Loyalty Management Manage and Work with Promotions

| Attribute | Function |
| --- | --- |
|  | If you don't select a rule library, you can still go ahead and create promotion rules using templates such as Buy X, Get Discount + Rewards and Spend X, Get Unit Price Discount on Y + Rewards. |

Promotion's Loyalty Program (2)
If the promotion belongs to a loyalty program, select the loyalty program that the promotion belongs to.
When you launch the Quick Promotion wizard from a loyalty program record, the loyalty program is
automatically selected.
After you select a loyalty program for a promotion and proceed to the next steps of the wizard, you can't
come back and change the promotion's loyalty program.
Promotion's Additional Fields (3)
Apart from deciding the promotion's primary attributes, define other promotion attributes that are key to
your promotion's strategy, such as the promotion's branding image. The additional fields list also includes
custom fields that the admin adds in Salesforce.
What Happens After Moving to the Next Step?
After you enter the primary information about the promotion and go to the next steps, a Promotion
record is created in the org.
Define Promotion Eligibility
On the Define Promotion Eligibility step in the Quick Promotion guided flow, marketing managers decide
the products, channels, customer groups, and customer events that are eligible for the promotion.
Marketing managers also decide whether customers must sign up for the promotion before they can use
it. They can also choose to add coupons to the promotion.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management

Loyalty Management Manage and Work with Promotions
Eligible Products or Categories (1)
Marketing managers can decide the products or product categories that are eligible for the promotion.
They can select a promotion's products or categories from these options.

| Option | What Marketing Managers Can Select | What Happens in the Next Steps |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Products and categories | Marketing managers can select the products and categories eligible for the promotion. | If the marketing manager selects products and categories, then they can't select the Accrual |

Loyalty Management Manage and Work with Promotions

| Option | What Marketing Managers Can Select | What Happens in the Next Steps |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  | Events template on the Select Promotion Template step. |
| Partner products form loyalty program | Marketing managers can select the partner products of the promotion's loyalty program that are eligible for the promotion. | If the marketing manager selects partner products, then they can't select the pricing templates on the Select Promotion Template step. |

Tip If you can't find a product or category, work with the person who manages the company's
products in the org to add the products or categories. If partner products are missing, work with the
loyalty program manager to add the missing products as loyalty partner products.
If the marketing manager doesn't select the eligible products and categories, the products or categories
aren't available for selection in promotion rules. To easily identify the products that you're looking for,
search using their SKU or code.
Note You can't search for products using code and SKU if Product Catalog Management is enabled
in the org.
Eligible Channels (2)
Marketing managers can choose the channels that customers must shop from to be eligible for the
promotion. Marketing managers can select multiple channels for the promotion.

| Channel Type | what Can Be Selected |
| --- | --- |
| Online | Marketing managers can select one or more of these online channels. • Web • Mobile • App |
| Offline | Marketing managers can select the retail stores or retail store groups that they want to run the promotion for. |
| Social | Marketing managers can select one of these social channels. |

Loyalty Management Manage and Work with Promotions

| Channel Type | what Can Be Selected |
| --- | --- |
|  | • Facebook • WhatsApp • Instagram • Twitter |

Tip If you can't find a specific store or store group, work with the person who manages the
company's stores in the org to add the stores and store groups. For more online and social channels,
create an Engagement Channel Type record for each new channel and select the Delivery value that
corresponds to that channel's type. The Delivery field isn't available on the standard page layout of
Engagement Channel Type and admins must add the field to the page layout.
If the marketing manager doesn't select an eligible channel, then all channels are considered as eligible
channels of the promotion.
Eligible Customer Groups (3)
Marketing managers can select the target audience of the promotion. Customers who are part of the
target audience receive the promotion launch email and are the only people who are eligible to use the
promotion.
Marketing managers can decide the target audience for promotions in these ways.

| Audience Type | When the Audience Type is Available | What Marketing Managers Can Select |
| --- | --- | --- |
| All loyalty members | These audience types are available only when the promotion belongs to a loyalty program | The marketing manager isn't required to select any other option. When they select All loyalty members as the audience type, all members of the promotion's loyalty program are eligible for the promotion. |
| Loyalty tier members |  | The marketing manager must select the tiers of the promotion's loyalty program whose members are eligible for the promotion. |
| Campaign members | These audience types are available for promotions that belong to a loyalty program and | The marketing manager must select a campaign whose |

Loyalty Management Manage and Work with Promotions

| Audience Type | When the Audience Type is Available | What Marketing Managers Can Select |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | for promotions that run outside the loyalty program. | members are eligible for the promotion. To make sure that a customer or loyalty program member is part of a campaign, the customer or loyalty program member's Contact record must be a member of the campaign. If your company uses person accounts to manage customers, ensure that the campaign's members are the contacts associated with the eligible person accounts. |
| Segment members |  | The marketing manager must select the Data Cloud segments whose audience is eligible for the promotion. To select a Data Cloud segment, the org must have Data Cloud enabled. |

Tip If you can't find a specific tier, campaign, or Data Cloud segment, ask your Salesforce admin to
provide you with the object permissions to select the target audience.
If you don't select an eligible customer group, then all contacts are considered eligible for the
promotion.
Eligible Events (4)
If the company wants customers to use the promotion on special occasions, the marketing manager can
select the occasion that customers are eligible to use the promotion on.
Here's the list of events that you can create promotions for.

| Event Type | Event Period | How It Works | How The Date is Found |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Customer's birthday | Birthday | Customers can use the | You can find the |

Loyalty Management Manage and Work with Promotions

| Event Type | Event Period | How It Works | How The Date is Found |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  |  | promotion on their birthday. If the customer's birthday is on Feb 29, then the customer can use the promotion on February 29 of leap years and on March 1 of non-leap years. | birthday from the customer's Contact record. |
|  | Birthday Week | Customers can use the promotion during their birthday week. The birthday week is from Sunday through Saturday of the week that the customer's birthday falls in. |  |
|  | Birthday Month | Customers can use the promotion during their birthday month. The birthday month is the month that the customer's birthday falls in. |  |
| Member's birthday | Birthday | This event is available only for the promotions that belong to a loyalty program. Members of the promotion's loyalty program can use the promotion on their birthday. If the member's birthday is on Feb 29, then the member can use the promotion on February 29 of leap years and on March 1 of non-leap years. | You can find the birthday from the member's Contact record. |
|  | Birthday Week | This event is available only for the promotions |  |

| Event Type | Event Period | How It Works | How The Date is Found |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  |  | that belong to a loyalty program. Members of the promotion's loyalty program can use the promotion during their birthday week. The birthday week is from Sunday through Saturday of the week that the member's birthday falls in. |  |
|  | Birthday Month | This event is available only for the promotions that b

chunks.body:
---
title: "Manage and Work with Promotions (part 3/4)"
source_pdf: "loyalty_management_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "135-530"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Loyalty Management Manage and Work with Promotions
•
Eligible members get the Top Shopper badge but the badge expires on September 15, 2024.
•
Eligible members get the Social Star badge but can get the badge only up to three times.

Loyalty Management Manage and Work with Promotions
The dates for the badges that you select must meet these requirements:
•
The badges are active between the start date and end date of the promotion.
•
The start dates of the badges are on or before the promotion start date.
•
The validity end dates of fixed date badges are on or after the end date of the promotion.
Add Promotion Limits
On the Add Promotion Limits step of the wizard, marketing managers decide the limits of the promotion.
You can set limits for the promotion and each customer's individual limits for the promotion. The
promotion limits keep the promotion within the approved budget and protect the promotion from
malicious activities. This step is available for marketing managers only when they use the pricing
promotion templates in the promotion.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management
Promotion Limits
Here are the limits that you can set for the promotion.

| Callout | Limit Type | What It Does | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Points | Marketing managers | The promotion can't credit more than 500,000 points across |

Loyalty Management Manage and Work with Promotions

| Callout | Limit Type | What It Does | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  |  | decide the maximum number of points that a promotion can credit across all eligible loyalty program members. Marketing managers set limits for non-qualifying currencies and qualifying currencies. | eligible loyalty program members. |
| 2 | Voucher | Marketing managers decide the maximum vouchers that a promotion can issue. | The promotion can't issue more than 1,000 vouchers to eligible customers. |
| 3 | Liability | Marketing managers decide the maximum financial liability that the promotion can incur across all the rewards. | The promotion can't incur a liability of more than US$1 million across all the rewards offered by the promotion. |
| 4 | Total Use | Marketing managers decide the maximum number of times the promotion can be redeemed | The promotion can be used up to 200 times by all customers. |

Loyalty Management Manage and Work with Promotions
Customer Limits
Here are the limits that you can set for each customer.

| Callout | Limit Type | What It Does | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Points | Marketing managers decide the maximum number of points that each loyalty program member gets from the promotion. Marketing managers set limits for non-qualifying currencies and qualifying currencies. | The promotion can't credit more than 100 tier points to a single customer. |
| 2 | Voucher | Marketing managers decide the maximum number of vouchers that a promotion can issue to each customer and loyalty program member. | The promotion can't issue more than 2 vouchers to a single customer. |

Loyalty Management Manage and Work with Promotions

| Callout | Limit Type | What It Does | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 3 | Liability | Marketing managers decide the maximum financial liability that the promotion can incur across all the rewards for each customer and loyalty program member. | The promotion can't incur a liability of more than US$400 across all issued rewards for a single customer. |
| 4 | Per Cart | Marketing managers decide the maximum number of times each customer or loyalty program member can apply the promotion to an individual cart. | If a promotion offers a 10% discount with a limit of 3 uses per cart, the discount can be applied to the total cart value up to three times. |
| 5 | Per Line Item | Marketing managers decide the maximum number of times each customer or loyalty program member can apply the promotion on a line item. | If a promotion offers buy one, get one free with a per line item limit of 5, you can apply it up to five times per eligible item. If a customer has 10 eligible items in their cart, the promotion applies to the first five items. |
| 6 | Use per Customer | Marketing managers decide the maximum number of times each customer or loyalty program member can use the promotion. | A customer can use the promotion only 2 times. |

Important You can add the overall points limit and customer points limit for the same loyalty
program currency. However, you can't set the limits for more than one loyalty program currency.
When you set limits for a promotion, some limits are automatically managed whereas others require you
to set up processes to manage them. See Transaction Journal Execution API.
Design Distribution Assets
On the Design Distribution Assets step of the wizard, marketing managers can design and activate the

Loyalty Management Manage and Work with Promotions
launch email for pricing promotions. Marketing managers get a predefined email and they can customize
the email based on the promotion's branding requirements. To personalize the email for customers,
marketing managers can add promotion and contact merge fields.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management
The Design Distribution Assets step is only available if you:
•
Select a Marketing Cloud Engagement business unit for the promotion
•
Create promotion rules using one of these types of templates:
- Cart Promotions
- Line Level Promotions
- Unit Price Promotions
- Combo Promotions
- Custom Promotions

Loyalty Management Manage and Work with Promotions
Organization-Wide Email Address for Sender (1)
The promotion is sent from an organization-wide email address that the admin sets up. If you don't find
the email address that you want to use, ask your Salesforce admin to add the email address as an
organization-wide email address.
Merge Fields for Email Body (2)
The email includes a list of Contact object and Promotion object merge fields that you can add to the
email body. The merge fields resolve to the value of the fields in the context of the customer who
receives the email and in the context of the promotion that the email belongs to.
Email Activation (3)
After you design the promotion, activate the email. Only activated promotion launch emails are sent to
customers. The email is automatically sent using the Engagement feature Journey Builder. After you
activate the promotion, the Launch Promotion journey is automatically created in the promotion's
Engagement business unit.
The journey sends emails to only the customers or loyalty program members that you select as the
promotion's target audience in the Define Promotion Eligibility step of the guided flow.
Important Engagement journeys aren't created for promotions that use Loyalty Promotion type
templates to create promotion rules. These promotions don't have a launch email. Loyalty program
managers can decide how they want to announce loyalty promotions.
See Also
Salesforce Help: Organization-Wide Email Addresses
Review and Activate Promotion
On the Review and Activate step of the wizard, marketing managers can review the promotion's
complete configuration before they activate the promotion. If the promotion needs a business
executive's approval before activation, marketing managers can save the promotion.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management
To change the promotion after reviewing its details, marketing managers can go back and update the
steps that they want to change. After a promotion is activated, the promotion is ready for customers to
use. Activate the promotion only when you want customers to start to use the promotion.

Loyalty Management Manage and Work with Promotions
Design Promotions Programmatically
Use the Promotion Creation API to create, configure, and manage promotions. System integrators can
seamlessly integrate the API with external systems where users such as store managers can create
promotions.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management
To create a promotion, specify the primary details, promotion rules, and rewards. When you specify the
details of the rules and rewards, map the details that you specify with the type of promotion template
that you want to use in the promotion. You can also specify eligibility criteria and limits for the
promotion.
Important You can't use Promotion Creation API to create promotions that use the Engagement
Trail promotion template.
You can also use the API to update and delete promotions, and to retrieve promotion details. To
understand how to design a promotion, see Quick Promotion Wizard steps.
Example The marketing team at Cloud Kicks is planning their summer marketing campaign for the
'Cloud Kicks Loyalty' loyalty program. To boost sales and reward their loyal customers, they want to
create a promotion called Summer Sale. This promotion will run during the month of June and will
offer a 10% discount on purchases over $50. The promotion is specifically for the Summer
Sneakers product category on their app and is targeted towards their Loyal Customers segment. To
ensure the promotion is effective, they want to limit its usage to 1000 times overall and 5 times per
customer.Here's what the Promotion Creation API's request looks like when the API is executed:

Translate Promotions
Show promotions to in-store employees, sales representatives, and customers in their preferred
language. Translate promotions in the local languages of the customers and users that the promotions
are intended for.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To translate promotion details Promotion Viewer

chunks.heading: 09-offer-games-to-members
chunks.body:
---
title: "Offer Games to Members"
source_pdf: "loyalty_management_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "531-544"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Offer Games to Members

| Input | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Loyalty Program Member ID | ID of the member whose usage you want to update. |
| Promotion ID | ID of the Cumulative type promotion you're updating the member's usage for. |
| Usage Increment for Cumulative Promotion | Positive whole number to add to the member's usage. For example, if a member has completed two units toward the cumulative usage for a promotion, enter 2. |

1111.. Click Done.
1122.. Save your changes, and then activate the flow.
The action updates the fields of the relevant Loyalty Program Member Promotion record and also
provides these outputs:

| Output | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Cumulative Usage Target | Target value to attain the promotion. |
| Updated Cumulative Usage Completed | Value member has achieved so far. The formula is: previously achieved value + increment in value specified in the input. |

Example A member has to achieve a target of purchasing 10 products to attain a promotion. The
member purchases 2 items. In the Usage Increment for Cumulative Promotion input parameter,
enter 2. The action shows 2 as the value of the Updated Cumulative Usage Completed output
parameter. In the member's next transaction, the member purchases 4 items. Now enter 4 as the
value of the Usage Increment for Cumulative Promotion input parameter. The action shows 6 (2+4)
as the value of the Updated Cumulative Usage Completed output parameter.
Offer Games to Members
Brands can keep its customers and loyalty program members engaged by offering them games as
rewards. Use games to reward purchase-specific activities such as placing an order and non-purchase
activities such as completing a survey.
Watch this video to understand how to design and run games.
Watch the video: https://play.vidyard.com/gAWPGS6ZfmfPtNnjvc44Dy

Loyalty Management Offer Games to Members
If you aren't able to watch the video in full screen, open the video in a new tab: Gamify Member
Engagement with Loyalty Management.
Create Games and Add Rewards
Program managers can create games such as spin the wheel and scratchcard, and decide the rewards
that customers receive after they play the games.
Workflow for Offering Games to Members
After a program manager creates a game and adds its rewards, the game can be offered to customers
in several ways. Admins can use Business APIs to enable customers to play games on external systems
such as mobile apps or shopping sites. Program managers can also set up processes that assign games
to members based on their loyalty activities. Customer service reps can assign games to customers
and members as part of service recovery actions.
Create Games and Add Rewards
Program managers can create games such as spin the wheel and scratchcard, and decide the rewards
that customers receive after they play the games.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create games:
Loyalty Management
AND
Edit access on Game Definition
To create game rewards:
Loyalty Management
AND
Edit access on Game Reward
A loyalty program can offer these types of games to its customers and loyalty program members:

| Game Type | Customer Experience | How the Game Is Designed In Salesforce |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Spin The Wheel | Customers can spin a virtual wheel and the wheel segment | A game definition record represents the game. Game |

Loyalty Management Offer Games to Members

| Game Type | Customer Experience | How the Game Is Designed In Salesforce |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | where the pointer stops shows the reward that the player receives. | reward records represent the rewards offered by each segment of the wheel. You can add only one reward for each wheel segment. |
| Scratchcard | Customers can scratch a virtual card and the card reveals the reward that the player receives. | A game definition record represents the game. Each reward that the scratchcard offers is represented as game reward records. |

Tip Select the reward type in the game reward record as No Reward for instances where a wheel
segment or a scratchcard outcome doesn't offer a reward to participants.
Create a Game
Here's how you can map your business requirements to key game definition fields when you create a
game definition record:

| Business Requirement | Game Definition Configuration | Purpose | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Ensure that only select customers can play the game and the game isn't available to all customers. | In the game definition record, select Game Assignment Required. | The Game Assignment Required checkbox ensures that only the customers who are assigned the game are eligible to play the game. Use this option if you want to offer the game to customers after they complete a specific eligible activity. | • A game that's assigned to customers when they become a loyalty program member. • A game that's assigned to loyalty program members when they reach the Gold tier. • A game that's assigned to loyalty program members when they shop for US$50 or above. |

To design an evergreen game that can be assigned to customers at any time, select a start date but not an end date. | A game that's assigned to customers between Black Friday and Cyber Monday. |

Add Game Rewards

chunks.heading: 10-settings-for-loyalty-processes
chunks.body:
---
title: "Settings for Loyalty Processes"
source_pdf: "loyalty_management_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "545-589"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Settings for Loyalty Processes
After the API provides the result, the game participant reward record that was provided in the input is
updated with these details:
•
Game Reward-Associates the game reward provided to the member.
•
Status-Changes to Rewarded.
•
Issue Reward Reference-Associates the voucher that was issued to the member or the transaction
journal that credited the points to the member.
•
Expiration Date-Gets updated with the date when the issued voucher expires or the credited points
expire.
•
Source Activity-Associates the transaction journal that represents the member activity that made the
member eligible to play the game.
Note To automatically generate voucher codes, turn on the Generate Codes for Vouchers setting.
For more information on enabling the setting, see Enable Automatic Generation of Voucher Codes.
Settings for Loyalty Processes
You can set Loyalty Management to update non-qualifying points balance of members in real time. Turn
on another setting to add template Data Processing Engine definitions to your org and use them to
aggregate and expire members' fixed-expiration-model, non-qualifying points. Customer service
representatives can engage your loyalty program members better with Loyalty Service Excellence.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions that
have Loyalty Management enabled.
Enable Real-Time Update of Non-Qualifying Points Balance
To update non-qualifying points balance of members in real time, turn on real-time balance update
setting in your Salesforce org.
Enable Real-Time Update of Qualifying Points Balance
To update the qualifying points balance of members in real time, turn on the real-time balance update
setting in your Salesforce org.
Consolidation of Fixed-Type, Non-Qualifying Points for Expiration
Automate the consolidation and expiry of fixed-type, non-qualifying points of members. You can
consolidate members' points in the Loyalty Aggregated Point Expiration Ledger object in real time or in
batches. By aggregating non-qualifying points in the Loyalty Aggregated Point Expiration Ledger
object, you can decouple the points expiration process from the Loyalty Ledger records.
Enable Automatic Calculation of Points Expiration Date for Canceled Redemptions
Enable a setting that automatically calculates the expiration date of fixed-type non-qualifying points
that are credited back to members for canceled redemptions.
Data Cloud for Loyalty Management

Loyalty Management Settings for Loyalty Processes
Gather insights about your company's loyalty program and its members with Data Cloud. A prepacked
data bundle enables admins to set up the connection that sends data from Loyalty Management to
Data Cloud. To create targeted member segments, users must be assigned the Data Cloud Activation
Specialist permission set.
Enable Automatic Generation of Voucher Codes
Automate the process of generating unique codes for vouchers that are issued to members.
Get Access to Loyalty Service Excellence
To access the service console, enable the Service Excellence for Loyalty Program Member setting for
your org.
Allow Members to Join and Leave Loyalty Program and Promotions from Experience Cloud Site
Give contacts, accounts, and person accounts in your company the permission to join or leave your
loyalty program from the loyalty program's Experience Cloud site. You can also give loyalty program
members the permission to join or opt out of promotions from the Experience Cloud site.
Select Members for Tier Upgrade Automatically
You can select members automatically for tier upgrade when their qualifying point balance changes.
The setting helps you avoid setting up a custom process to find the members eligible for tier upgrade
assessment. In your tier upgrade process, you can assess and upgrade the tier of members who have
been automatically selected for tier upgrade assessment.
Enable Negative Point Balance for Non-Qualifying Currencies
Enable currencies to accommodate negative points balance for members. Turn on the negative point
balance setting in Salesforce. When you enable negative point balance, members can redeem points
beyond their existing points balance.
Enable Processes and Rules to Inherit Status in Target Orgs
Deploy loyalty program processes and their rules in the same status as defined in the
LoyaltyProgramSetup metadata type. This means that if a process and its rules are active in the source
org, they are in active status in the target org. Transferring processes and rules between orgs in their
original status reduces post-deployment effort.
Enable Trace Usage of Points
Enable a setting to create currency subtypes for fixed-type non-qualifying currencies, restricting the
use of points for particular activities, such as promotions. You can also use the setting to identify the
origin of points during redemption. When tracing is enabled, the relationship between debit and
credit ledgers is stored in the Traceability Loyalty Ledger object.
Enable Clubs
Enable a setting to design and manage clubs for customers with similar interests and affinities. Create
clubs that engage club members with personalized benefits and promotions.
Automatically Transfer Points to a Member Group
Automate the process of transferring points from members to their associated membership groups in
real-time when points are credited to them. The automation calculates and transfers points to each
group based on the member's contribution percentage for each group. This automation ensures that
there is no duplication in the transfer of points.
Track Member Progress in Engagement Trails
You can reduce your data storage requirements by using the Promotion Party Usage object to track
members' progress towards achieving milestones for Engagement Trail promotions. The Promotion

Loyalty Management Settings for Loyalty Processes
Party Usage object uses fewer records to store attributes compared to engagement attributes. When
you enable this setting, the attributes for Engagement Trail promotions are automatically stored in
Promotion Party Usage records JSON format. You can use the Member Engagement Trail API to
retrieve the record details.
Enable Real-Time Update of Non-Qualifying Points Balance
To update non-qualifying points balance of members in real time, turn on real-time balance update
setting in your Salesforce org.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions that
have Loyalty Management enabled.
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To enable real-time points balance update:
•
System Administrator
•
Loyalty Management
Before you turn on the setting to update members' non-qualifying points balance in real time, you must
run the Credit Non-Qualifying Points to Members Data Processing Engine definition. This ensures that
the members' points balance is updated for all the transactions processed until the current date.
Member points balance is updated in real time only for Accrual type transaction journals. When you turn
on this setting, you don't have to use the Credit Non-Qualifying Points to Members Data Processing
Engine definition in a flow to update points balance. When you make a credit type points adjustment for
members or for transaction journals, points are automatically credited to the associated members'
points balance.
Enabling real-time balance update doesn't impact real-time point movement to escrow.
11.. From Setup, in the Quick Find box, enter Loyalty Management, and then select Loyalty
Management Settings.
22.. Turn on Update non-qualifying point balance of loyalty program members in real time.
After you turn on this setting, create loyalty program processes or flows to process Transaction Journal
records and use the Credit Points action to credit non-qualifying points in real time.
Enable Real-Time Update of Qualifying Points Balance
To update the qualifying points balance of members in real time, turn on the real-time balance update
setting in your Salesforce org.

Loyalty Management Settings for Loyalty Processes
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with
Loyalty Management
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To enable real-time points balance update:
•
System Administrator
•
Loyalty Management
Before you enable the setting to update members' qualifying points balance in real time, run the Credit
Qualifying Points to Members Data Processing Engine definition. This ensures that the members' points
balance is updated for all the transactions processed until the current date.
Member points balance is updated in real time only for Accrual-type transaction journals. When you turn
on this setting, you don't have to use the Credit Qualifying Points to Member Data Processing Engine
definition in a flow to update points balance. When you make either a Credit-type points adjustment for
members or for transaction journals, points are automatically credited to the associated members'
points balance.
The setting checks the Next Reset Date field on the member's related loyalty member currency before
crediting points to the member for a qualifying currency. If the member's next reset date is in the past,
the setting resets the member's points balance for the currency to zero and then credits points to the
member.
11.. From Setup, in the Quick Find box, enter Loyalty Management, and then select Loyalty
Management Settings.
22.. Turn on Update qualifying point balance of loyalty program members in real time.
After you enable this setting, create loyalty program processes or flows to process Transaction Journal
records and use the Credit Points action to credit qualifying points in real time.
Consolidation of Fixed-Type, Non-Qualifying Points for Expiration
Automate the consolidation and expiry of fixed-type, non-qualifying points of members. You can
consolidate members' points in the Loyalty Aggregated Point Expiration Ledger object in real time or in
batches. By aggregating non-qualifying points in the Loyalty Aggregated Point Expiration Ledger object,
you can decouple the points expiration process from the Loyalty Ledger records.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Developer, and Unlimited Editions that
have Loyalty Management enabled

Loyalty Management Settings for Loyalty Processes
Tip Salesforce recommends consolidating and expiring member points. Some advantages of
consolidating points are:
•
The expiration date of points credited back to members for canceled redemption is automatically
calculated.
•
The points that are debited from members for canceled accruals are deducted based on the
activity date of the canceled transaction and points aren't deducted using the first-in, first-out
model.
•
Members can be provided a simple break-up of their points by their expiration date.
Records are created in the Loyalty Aggregated Point Expiration Ledger object based on:
•
Loyalty program member
•
Currency associated with the member
•
Expiration date of the points
A record is created for each member when their points for a currency expire on a particular date. A
record is updated when the associated member is credited points that are of the associated currency and
have the same expiration date.
Note The Loyalty Aggregated Point Expiration Ledger object can't be customized. The object isn't
available in the Object Manager or in the Loyalty Management app.
Here are the scenari

---

Einstein for Loyalty Management
For example, if you create an engagement trail with three milestones, tracking a member's progress
through engagement attributes creates a minimum of three records. In contrast, the Promotion Party
Usage object tracks a member's progress with just one record, thereby reducing data usage.
11.. From Setup, in the Quick Find box, enter Loyalty Management, and then select Loyalty
Management Settings.
22.. Turn on Track Member Progress for Engagement Trails Using Promotion Party Usage.
The Track Member Progress for Engagement Trails Using Promotion Party Usage setting is enabled by
default from the Summer '25 release onwards. To use engagement attributes for tracking a member's
progress in engagement trails, turn off the setting.
Einstein for Loyalty Management
Reduce the amount of time that various departments across your company spend to manage and run
your company's loyalty programs with Einstein for Loyalty Management.
Agentforce for Loyalty Management
Marketing managers can leverage the power of Agentforce to efficiently manage everyday tasks related
to the company's loyalty programs and promotions. Customers can use Agentforce to self-serve and
resolve their promotion-related queries without contacting a customer service representative.
Einstein Summary for Loyalty Management
Securely generate summaries for your loyalty programs and loyalty promotions with Einstein Summary.
Agentforce for Loyalty Management
Marketing managers can leverage the power of Agentforce to efficiently manage everyday tasks related
to the company's loyalty programs and promotions. Customers can use Agentforce to self-serve and
resolve their promotion-related queries without contacting a customer service representative.
Note Work with your Salesforce representative to ensure the licenses and permissions required to
work with Agentforce are available in your org.
Agentforce Topics for Loyalty Management
Marketing managers can use Agentforce to efficiently create and update promotions with Accrual and
Spend X templates. Agentforce in self-service channels empowers customers to easily retrieve details
about their profile and eligibility for loyalty promotions. The topics are available in the Agentforce
(Default) agent.
Set Up Agentforce
Admins must set up Einstein Generative AI and Agentforce to enable marketing managers and other
users to work with the standard Agentforce topics provided by Loyalty Management.
Assign Loyalty Users Permissions to Work with Agentforce
Assign marketing managers and other users the permission they require to create promotions using

Loyalty Management Einstein for Loyalty Management
Agentforce.
Agentforce Topics for Loyalty Management
Marketing managers can use Agentforce to efficiently create and update promotions with Accrual and
Spend X templates. Agentforce in self-service channels empowers customers to easily retrieve details
about their profile and eligibility for loyalty promotions. The topics are available in the Agentforce
(Default) agent.
Agent Topic: Promotion With Loyalty Accrual Rule
Marketing managers can use the Promotion With Loyalty Accrual Rule topic to create and update
promotions that reward loyalty program members for completing eligible accrual activities. They can
also draft loyalty promotion emails for effective communication.
Agent Topic: Promotion With Spend X Rule
Marketing managers can use the Promotion With Spend X Rule topic to create and update promotions
that reward customers for placing orders worth a specific amount.
See Also
Salesforce Help: Agent Topics
Agent Topic: Promotion With Loyalty Accrual Rule
Marketing managers can use the Promotion With Loyalty Accrual Rule topic to create and update
promotions that reward loyalty program members for completing eligible accrual activities. They can also
draft loyalty promotion emails for effective communication.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management,
and Foundations or Agentforce 1 Editions.
Topic Details

| API Name | PromotionWithLoyaltyAccrualRule |
| --- | --- |
| Included Agent Actions | Create or Update Promotion Using Accrual Template Draft or Revise Email |

Loyalty Management Einstein for Loyalty Management
Agent Action: Create or Update Promotion using Accrual Template
Gathers information about the loyalty accrual promotion that the marketing manager wants to create or
update. The output includes the details of the promotion that was created or updated.
Here are a few example utterances for the action:
•
Can you create a loyalty promotion for me called 'Cloud Kicks December Delights'? It should start on
2025-01-05, be part of the 'Cloud Kicks' program, and give 100 fixed 'Reward Points'. Customers need
to buy at least 5 items and spend at least 100 to qualify.
•
Set up a promotion named 'Summer Fun' for the 'Cloud Kicks Loyalty Program'. Members in the 'Gold
Tier' should earn double points for purchases over $50.
Agent Action: Generate Loyalty Promotion Email
Creates the subject and content of a draft launch email for a promotion.
Here are a few example utterances for the action:
•
Generate an email for the promotion you just created.
•
Generate an email for the promotion you just updated.
Example A marketing manager at Cloud Kicks wants to create a loyalty promotion named 'Holiday
Season Promotion' starting on March 1, 2025. This promotion is associated with the 'Cloud Kicks
Insider' program and rewards 200 'Reward Points' for purchases above $250. The manager also
wants to generate an email for the promotion.The marketing manager launches Agentforce and
enters the following utterance: Create a loyalty promotion with accrual rule type
named 'Holiday Season Promotion' starting on March 1, 2025. This promotion is
associated with the program named 'Cloud Kicks Insider' and rewards 200
'Reward Points' fixed points. It requires a minimum spend amount of $250. The
promotion description is 'Get 200 Reward Points on all purchases above $250'.
Generate an email for the promotion.The agent generates the promotion with the
specified details. The agent generates the content for the promotion's launch email, including the
primary information and reward details.
Agent Topic: Promotion With Spend X Rule
Marketing managers can use the Promotion With Spend X Rule topic to create and update promotions
that reward customers for placing orders worth a specific amount.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience

Loyalty Management Einstein for Loyalty Management
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management,
and Foundations or Agentforce 1 Editions.
Topic Details

| API Name | PromotionWithSpendXRule |
| --- | --- |
| Included Agent Actions | Create or Update Pricing Promotion Create or Update Promotion Using a Spend X Template Add or Update Limits in Pricing Promotion |

Agent Action: Create or Update Pricing Promotion
Gathers information about the pricing promotion that the marketing managers wants to create or
update. The output includes the details of the promotion that was created or updated.
Here are a few example utterances for the action:
•
Make a Spend X promotion called Wardrobe Refresh Sale. It's for all customers, starting Feb 5, 2025,
at midnight, and ending Dec 31, 2025, at noon.
•
Create a Buy X promotion 'Active wear Attack', set the eligible campaign to 'Black Friday Deals and
eligible segments to 'New Subscribers.
•
Create a promotion titled Carry More Saver. Add the description 'Buy Mocha for $194.89 from the
Beverages catalog and receive a 9.99% unit price discount on Mocha'. Set the eligible customer
events to Customer Birthday.
Agent Action: Create or Update Promotion using a Spend X Template
Gathers information about the promotion that the marketing manager wants to create or update for
rewarding customers based on their order value. The output includes the details of the promotion that
was created or updated.
Here are a few example utterances for the action:
•
Create line promotion 'Spring Promotion'. Promotion starts on March 1, 2025 and rewards 10% off on
apparel category when total spend is between $100 and $200. Promotion description is 'Get 10% off
on apparel when total spend is between $100 and $200 this Spring'.
•
Create unit promotion 'Spring Promotion Bonanza'. Promotion starts on 1st March this year and
rewards 10% off on Red Shirt when total spend is greater than $100.
•
Run a cart promotion named 'March Madness Discount' that starts on March 1, 2025 and offers a
10% discount on all purchases over $100.

Loyalty Management Einstein for Loyalty Management
Agent Action: Add or Update Limits in Pricing Promotion
Gathers information about the limits that the marketing managers wants to add or update in a pricing
promotion. The output includes details of the limits added or updated for the pricing promotion.
Here are a few example utterances for the action:
•
Update the 'Season Buy Out Sale',to add an overall limit of 500 vouchers and a per-customer limit of 1
voucher.
•
I need to add limits to the 'Winter Sale' promotion. The promotion should have an overall limit of 500
redemptions and a per-customer limit of 1 redemption.
Example A marketing manager at Cloud Kicks wants to create a promotion named 'Summer
Savings' starting on June 1, 2025 that rewards customers for placing orders worth a specific
amount. This promotion offers a 20% discount on all items in the cart when the total spend is $100
or more.The marketing manager launches Agentforce and enters the following utterance: Create a
promotion named Summer Savings using Spend X template. This promotion is
eligible for the cart and offers a 20% discount when the total spend is $100
or more.The agent generates the promotion with the specified details. You can enhance your
promotion by adding additional detailed eligibility criteria, limits, and rewards.
Set Up Agentforce
Admins must set up Einstein Generative AI and Agentforce to enable marketing managers and other
users to work with the standard Agentforce topics provided by Loyalty Management.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management,
and Foundations or Agentforce 1 Editions.

See Give Users Access to the Agentforce (Default).
66.. Activate the default Agentforce agent.
See Activate or Deactivate Your Agent.

chunks.heading: 12-set-up-loyalty-processes
chunks.body:
---
title: "Set Up Loyalty Processes"
source_pdf: "loyalty_management_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "599-649"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Set Up Loyalty Processes

| Page | Component Name | Summary Outline | Collaterals Used to Generate Summary | Limitations |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  |  | period • List of eligible activities and eligibility criteria • List of rewards offered | Promotion Summary | • Five Data Cloud segments associated with the promotion The Einstein Summary component generates summaries for promotions that are created using the Quick Promotion guided setup and associated with a loyalty program. |

Set Up Loyalty Processes
You can provide an efficient process for customer recognition, rewards, and experiences by setting up
end-to-end Loyalty Processes.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions that
have Loyalty Management enabled.
Set up Loyalty Processes that include these actions.

| Loyalty Processes | Actions |
| --- | --- |
| Member Enrollment Bonus | Awards points for joining the program or referring new members to the program. |
| Accruals | Awards points based on the eligibility criteria. |
| Redemptions | Deducts points when members redeem points for a service. |

Loyalty Management Set Up Loyalty Processes

| Loyalty Processes | Actions |
| --- | --- |
| Point Expiry | Deducts points based on the expiration model. |
| Reset Qualifying Points | Resets a member's qualifying points on the reset date. The reset date can be a fixed or an anniversary date. |
| Points Balance | Updates the points of accrual, redemption, or expiry in the points balance. |
| Tier Assessment | Measures the tier points and changes the tier. |

You can also create business processes specific to your organization in Flows and Process Builder. To
trigger business processes in real time, create flows or processes that run whenever a record is created or
updated. For example, you can trigger a flow or a process when a Transaction Journal is created.
Key Components
Get to know the key components of loyalty processes.
Flow Builder Actions for Loyalty Management
Loyalty Management provides a list of Flow Builder actions that you can use to automate processes for
your loyalty program.
Member Enrollment Bonus
To increase the engagement with the loyalty program, you can define an enrollment bonus for all the
members who join the loyalty program of your organization. You can also reward points to members
for referring members to your organization. The work flow for enrollment bonus is defined using
Flows.
Accrual Process
Accrual transactions occur when a member performs an activity that earns them points. For example,
an activity can be a product purchase from a host business or partner, a bonus, or any other business
interaction. The business is responsible for payment when the member redeems or makes a claim for
rewards.
Redemption Process
A redemption transaction occurs when a member redeems non-qualifying points for an award. It's a
real-time process, where the member's points are deducted from their point balance. You can define
redemption processes in Flow using Loyalty Actions or in Apex using Loyalty APIs.
Data Processing Engine Definitions for Loyalty Management
Loyalty App provides out-of-the-box templates that can process high-volume jobs. These templates
help calculate the point balance, reset qualifying points, and expire points for your loyalty program
members. The out-of-the-box job templates are enabled through Data Processing Engine.
Orchestration of Loyalty Processes
You can use batch jobs and data processing engine jobs that appear as invocable actions in the Flow
and connect all the loyalty processes (Accrual, Redemption, Point Balance, Qualifying Point Reset,
Point of Expiry) together. And you can schedule them to run at regular intervals.
Tier Assessment

Loyalty Management Set Up Loyalty Processes
Review the tier assignment of members at regular intervals and at the end of the tier period to check
whether members are eligible for a tier upgrade based on their current qualifying points balance. You
can either use a loyalty program process or a flow to upgrade the tier of the eligible members.
Get Member Details from Transaction Journals When Loyalty Management and Rebate
Management Are Enabled
In Salesforce, if Loyalty Management and Rebate Management both are enabled, the Program
Member Id field of the Transaction Journal object works as a polymorphic field. In Flows, you can
retrieve the record ID only from polymorphic fields.
Mobile SDK for Loyalty Management
Enhance brand engagement by providing Loyalty Management features on your iOS and Android
mobile devices. Use the iOS and Android Mobile Software Development Kit (SDK) for Loyalty
Management to build custom mobile applications with immersive member experiences. Elevate
member experience and loyalty by providing personalized offers, rewards, and checkouts on mobile
devices.
Key Components
Get to know the key components of loyalty processes.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Developer, and Unlimited Editions that
have Loyalty Management enabled.
You can configure Loyalty Processes using one or more of these components:
•
Decision Table: Helps to define complex business rules in a simple tabular form. You can use a
decision table when you want to define rules to generate output based on multiple inputs. Decision
tables read business rules and decide the outcome for records in your org or for the values that you
specify.
To define business rules for loyalty programs and processes, you can only use decision tables of the
type Standard. For more details, refer to the Decision Table help.
•
Batch Management: Processes large volumes of data in your Salesforce org. Batch Management
determines which records to process using batch jobs. It also tracks the status and health of batch jobs
using Monitor Workflow Services.
For more details, refer to the Batch Management help.
Tip We recommend that you limit the size of your batch job parts to 200. You can explore larger
batch job parts to optimize the performance of long-running processes.
•
Data Processing Engine: Provides out-of-the-box job templates to calculate point balances, reset
qualifying points, and expire points for your loyalty program members. You can customize any of the
Data Processing Engine job templates, or you can create a job template that suits your business needs.
You can also schedule any template to run at defined intervals.
For more details, refer to the Data Processing Engine help.

Loyalty Management Set Up Loyalty Processes
Note The Loyalty Processes can be defined in Flows using Loyalty Actions or Loyalty APIs in Apex.
Flow Builder Actions for Loyalty Management
Loyalty Management provides a list of Flow Builder actions that you can use to automate processes for
your loyalty program.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management

| Action | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Assign Member Tier Benefits | Assign to members the benefits that are associated with their current tier. |
| Change Tier | Change the tier of members. |
| Credit Points | Credit points to members for Accrual-type transaction journals that are in Pending status. You can specify the number of points to credit and set an expiration date for fixed-type non-qualifying points. If you specify an expiration date for the points, the expiration date that's automatically calculated based on the currency's expiration period is overwritten. |
| Debit Points | Debit points from members for Redemption-type transaction journals that are in Pending status. You can specify the number of points to debit. |
| Get Loyalty Promotions for Customer Data Platform | Get a list of promotions that are associated with the Data Cloud segment to which members belong. This action is available only for instances in which Data Cloud is enabled. |
| Get Promotions for Transactions | Get a list of promotions that are applicable for transaction journals. |

Loyalty Management Set Up Loyalty Processes

| Action | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Get Member's Active Segment | Get a list of active Data Cloud segments to which members belong. This action is available only for instances in which Data Cloud is enabled. |
| Get Points Balance | Get the points balance of members for a selected currency. |
| Get Tier | Get a member's current tier. |
| Issue a Voucher | Issue vouchers to members based on a selected voucher definition. You can select to overwrite the voucher value and the effective date that's specified in the selected voucher definition. You can also associate promotions and benefits with the voucher. |
| Run Program Process for Transaction Journal | Run the applicable loyalty program process for either an Accrual-type or Redemption-type transaction journal that's in Pending status. |
| Transfer Member Points to Groups | Transfer members' points from their points balance to their groups' points balance for Processed transaction journals. |
| Update Progress For Cumulative Promotion Usage | Update members' progress towards achieving a Cumulative-type promotion's target. |
| Merge Loyalty Program Memberships | Merge two loyalty program memberships and transfer the points and vouchers to one membership. |
| Unmerge Loyalty Program Memberships | Unmerge a loyalty program membership. |
| Run Program Process | Run a loyalty program process based on the specified process rules and process parameters. |
| Cancel Accrual Transactions | Revert an accrual transaction and deduct points from the member's points balance. |
| Cancel Redemption Trasactions | Revert a redemption transaction and credit points back to the member's points balance. |
| Generate Member Referral Code | Generate referral codes for members. If members already have referral codes, use the action to |

Loyalty Management Set Up Loyalty Processes

| Action | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | generate new referral codes. |

Get Members' Active Segments
Use the Get Member's Active Segments action in flows to get the list of active Data Cloud segments to
which members belong. You can use this action in flows that reward members based on their
segment.
Cancel an Accrual Transaction in Flows
Use the Cancel Accrual Transaction action in flows to revert an accrual transaction and deduct points
from a member's point balance. When you specify the transaction journal ID and cancellation reason,
the action picks up the ledgers from the transaction journal and adds another transaction journal
entry of the Accrual Cancellation journal type.
Cancel a Redemption Transaction in Flows
Use the Cancel Redemption Transaction action in flows to revert a redemption transaction and credit
points back to a member's point balance. When you specify the transaction journal ID and
cancellation reason, the action picks up the ledgers from the transaction journal and adds another
transaction journal entry of the Redemption Cancellation journal type.
Run Loyalty Program Processes by Using a Flow Action
Loyalty program processes can be created for various transactional, nontransactional, and tier-
processing tasks in loyalty management. Processes are run based on defined parameters and rules.
After you activate a process and the process's rules, you can find the processes in the Flow Builder
actions in the Loyalty category.
Get Members' Active Segments
Use the Get Member's Active Segments action in flows to get the list of active Data Cloud segments to
which members
