Create Loyalty Program Processes by Using Promotion Setup
•
Enroll in the loyalty program.
•
Enroll for active promotional offers, or opt out of a promotion.
•
View your membership details, points summary, and tier details.
•
View your transaction history.
•
View and redeem your active vouchers with the voucher code.
•
View the benefits of the loyalty program.
Watch and Learn about the Loyalty Management Mobile SDK
Watch this video to understand how you can elevate member experience by using an effective mobile
application for your loyalty program.
Watch the video: https://play.vidyard.com/2kUw7fnfRjUqa6P5zWQaCN
If you aren't able to watch the video in full screen, open the video on a new tab: Experience Loyalty
Management on Your Mobile Devices.
Watch and Learn about the Loyalty Management Mobile SDK's Receipt
Scanning Capabilities
Watch this video to understand how you can enable members to earn rewards by purchasing your
company's products from multibrand and third-party stores.
Watch the video: https://play.vidyard.com/9XxKtNBrduNofws9rgA6Do
If you aren't able to watch the video in full screen, open the video on a new tab: Reward Members for
Shopping at Multibrand (Third-Party) Stores.
See Also
Loyalty Management Developer Guide: Loyalty Management Mobile SDK
Create Loyalty Program Processes by Using Promotion Setup
Loyalty program managers can create loyalty program processes to define how to run business processes
in Loyalty Management.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management

Loyalty Management Create Loyalty Program Processes by Using Promotion Setup
Explore and Learn
Watch this video to understand how to design processes that are required to launch your loyalty
program.
Watch the video: https://play.vidyard.com/gt9LBKShrC1VU4JUsJSQMr
If you aren't able to watch the video in full screen, open the video on a new tab: Launch a Loyalty
Program.
Key Terms of Promotion Setup
Learn about the key terms and the workflow of Promotion Setup.
Create a Loyalty Program Process
A loyalty program can have multiple program processes. A program process can process either Accrual
or Redemption-type transaction journals. You can also filter transaction journals further by specifying
the subtype of transaction journals that you want to process.
Create a Loyalty Program Process Parameter
Parameters store static values, dynamic values, and formulas. You can use parameters to define
conditions or actions. Parameters can store multiple value types depending on the type and data type
of the parameter.
Create a Rule
Define a business scenario to process transaction journals in a rule. You can associate a rule with a
promotion to ensure that transaction journals are only processed by the rule during the promotion
period. A rule contains conditions and actions that help you design your business scenario.
Create a Formula in Loyalty Program Process
You can use a formula to determine either the value of a parameter, the resource or value of a
condition, or an input field of an action.
Transaction Journal Processing
Promotion Setup can process incoming and new Acrrual and Redemption-type transaction journals
either in real time or in batches. You can also use both methods simultaneously for a loyalty program
process.
Considerations for Using Promotion Setup
Here are a few things to keep in mind while using Promotion Setup.
Examples of Promotion Setup
Here are two examples of loyalty program processes. One of them processes Accrual-type transactions
and the other one processes Redemption-type transactions.
Key Terms of Promotion Setup
Learn about the key terms and the workflow of Promotion Setup.

Loyalty Management Create Loyalty Program Processes by Using Promotion Setup
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management
Key Terms

| Term | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Loyalty program process | Contains the parameters and rules that you can use to define how and what to process. |
| Parameters | Contains the static and dynamic values that you can use in the conditions and actions of a rule. |
| Rules | Contains the conditions and actions that you can use to define a business scenario. To apply a rule during a promotion period, associate the rule with the promotion. |
| Conditions | Contains the conditions that determine the eligibility for one or more actions. |
| Actions | Contains the various types of actions that you can initiate. |
| Process Templates | Contains predefined parameters and rules that you can use to create loyalty program processes for transactional and nontransactional tasks. |

Sample Workflow for Transactional Processing
Explore and Learn
Watch this video to learn about the key components of Promotion Setup.
Watch the video: https://play.vidyard.com/YXB7WBCq9DinUMjQTrsDnr
If you aren't able to watch the video in full screen, open the video on a new tab: Take a Tour of
Promotion Setup in Loyalty Management .
Watch this video to understand how you can design a process using Promotion Setup.
Watch the video: https://play.vidyard.com/zmFvZAKWMmKn8MwV2y6PD5

Loyalty Management Create Loyalty Program Processes by Using Promotion Setup
If you aren't able to watch the video in full screen, open the video on a new tab: Use Loyalty
Management Promotion Setup to Reward Members for Purchases.
Create a Loyalty Program Process
A loyalty program can have multiple program processes. A program process can process either Accrual or
Redemption-type transaction journals. You can also filter transaction journals further by specifying the
subtype of transaction journals that you want to process.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create a loyalty program process:
Loyalty Management permission set
AND
Edit access on Loyalty Program and other required
objects
AND
View Setup and Configuration
You can associate a combination of journal type and journal subtype with only one program process. This
ensures that a particular type and subtype of transaction journals aren't processed by multiple loyalty
program processes.
11.. In the Loyalty Management app, open a Loyalty Program record.
22.. Click the Loyalty Processes tab.
33.. On the Program Processes related list, click New.
If you don't find the Program Processes related list on the Loyalty Processes tab, add the related list on
the Loyalty Program page layout.
44.. Specify values for these fields.

| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Program Process Name | Enter the name of the loyalty program process. |
| Journal Type | Select whether you want the loyalty program process to process Accrual or Redemption-type transaction journals. |

Loyalty Management Create Loyalty Program Processes by Using Promotion Setup

| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Journal SubType | Select the journal subtype the process uses to filter transaction journals. This field is optional. Journal subtype records aren't provided with Loyalty Management. Before you select a journal subtype for a process, create Journal Subtype records. |
| Status | Select the status of the process. Only active processes provide outcomes for incoming transaction journals. |
| Execution Type | Select the mode to process transactions. •• Real Time: Incoming transactions are processed as soon as they are generated. •• Batch: New transactions are grouped and processed in batches based on a schedule that the admin decides. •• Both: Transactions can be processed in real time and in batches. The value that you select in this field doesn't impact how you decide to trigger the process. This information helps provide added context about the process. |

Example A loyalty program manager creates a process that processes Accrual-type transactions in
real time. The manager keeps the status of the process in Draft until the manager creates the
parameters and rules for the process.
After you create a Loyalty Program Process record, add parameters and rules for the process.
Tip Change the status of a process to Active only after you have set up all your parameters and rules.
A process can start to process transaction journals as soon as it's active. If necessary, you can add
rules to active processes for a new promotion or for new business scenarios.
Define Eligibility Conditions for a Process
Define the conditions that determine the transaction journals that are eligible for a process. The
process eligibility conditions that you define filter ineligible transactions before the transactions are
processed by the rules of the process. Process eligibility conditions help ensure that the conditions

Loyalty Management Create Loyalty Program Processes by Using Promotion Setup
that are applicable for all rules of the process, are defined at the process-level and not at the rule-level.
For example, you can add eligibility conditions to filter out inactive member transactions that are less
than a particular amount. Or, you can set a transaction value limit beyond which transaction journals
aren't processed to ensure fraudulent activities aren't rewarded.
See Also
Loyalty Management Developer Guide: LoyaltyProgramSetup
Define Eligibility Conditions for a Process
Define the conditions that determine the transaction journals that are eligible for a process. The process
eligibility conditions that you define filter ineligible transactions before the transactions are processed by
the rules of the process. Process eligibility conditions help ensure that the conditions that are applicable
for all rules of the process, are defined at the process-level and not at the rule-level. For example, you can
add eligibility conditions to filter out inactive member transactions that are less than a particular
amount. Or, you can set a transaction value limit beyond which transaction journals aren't processed to
ensure fraudulent activities aren't rewarded.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create a loyalty program process:
Loyalty Management permission set
AND
Edit access on Loyalty Program and other required
objects
AND
View Setup and Configuration
You can add process eligibility conditions for TransactionJournal type processes.
To define your conditions, you can use:
•
Transaction journal fields
•
Member tier and currency parameters
•
Transaction journal, partner, and member aliases
Also, you can use formulas in your conditions.

Loyalty Management Create Loyalty Program Processes by Using Promotion Setup
11.. On a loyalty program process, click Define Process Eligibility.
22.. Select Enable process eligibility conditions.
33.. Add conditions that decide the transactions that are eligible for the process.
44.. Save your changes.
After you activate the process, transaction journals whose journal type and subtype match that of the
process are filtered by the process eligibility conditions. The transaction journals that match the process
eligibility conditions are then processed by the active rules of the process.
Example The loyalty program manager has set up a process to process transactions where members are
rewarded for purchasing flight ticke

chunks.heading: 14-loyalty-program-members
chunks.body:
---
title: "Loyalty Program Members"
source_pdf: "loyalty_management_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "701-754"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Loyalty Program Members

| Step Type | Name | Parent Step | Input Field Values | Output Field Values |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  | (parameter) |  |

Loyalty Program Members
Loyalty program members are the members enrolled in a loyalty program that you create. A member can
be part of multiple loyalty programs in an organization.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions that
have Loyalty Management enabled.
A member can be one of two types: individual or corporate. Individual members are represented by a
contact in your organization and corporate members are represented by an account in your organization.
Individual and corporate members can exist in the same loyalty program.
A set of members can also form a Group type membership. Individual or Corporate members who are
part of a group can pool non-qualifying points together.
Create a Loyalty Program Member
You can create members who are enrolled in a loyalty program.
Member Groups
You create groups and add members to the groups. Members can contribute a percentage of their
own points to a group. A member can be a part of multiple groups and can also decide the percentage
of points to contribute to each group.
Transfer Member Points to Other Members
Members can transfer their redeemable points to other members of the loyalty program or to a group
they are associated with. To ensure effective use of member points, members can share them with
friends and family who are willing to make purchases. Service representatives can help transfer
members' nonqualifying points to other members by using the Transfer Points action in a new or
existing flow.
Member Engagement Attributes
With member engagement attributes, you can track long-term member behavior. You can create
engagement attributes and track a member's progress in achieving the attribute's target. Also, you can
reward members for achieving the engagement attribute target.
Member Badges
Assign badges to members based on members' interaction with your loyalty program. You can assign

Loyalty Management Loyalty Program Members
badges to recognize member milestones or other specific activities.
Member Referral Code
Members can use referral codes to invite their friends and family to join the loyalty program. When a
member joins the loyalty program, you can automatically generate a referral code for the member or
specify a referral code for the member when they join the loyalty program.
Manage Loyalty Program Members
You can manage and view the program details of members, the tier that members belong to, the
program currencies that members hold, member transactions, and benefits.
View Member Benefits
You can view member benefit details, the status of benefits, and the records created or updated by
Benefit Actions.
Create a Loyalty Program Member
You can create members who are enrolled in a loyalty program.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Developer, and Unlimited Editions that
have Loyalty Management enabled.
11.. In the Loyalty Programs tab, select your loyalty program.
22.. On the Setup, select Loyalty Program Members, and click New.
33.. Select a type for the member.
••
If your member belongs to a business, select Corporate.
••
If your member doesn't belong to a business, select Individual.
44.. Select a Contact record or an Account record.
••
For an Individual type member, select a contact or a person account in the Contact field.
••
For a Corporate member, select a business acount in the Account field.
55.. If your member is corporate and associated with an account, select Account.
66.. Enter the membership number.
Tip To automatically generate membership numbers based on the format that your company
uses, configure a custom logic.
77.. Select a loyalty program member who referred the member to the program.
88.. Select a date for enrollment.
99.. Select the enrollment channel.
1100.. Select the loyalty program member associated with the corporate.
1111.. Select the member status.
Note The member status has two values: active and inactive. The default member status is active.
The active and inactive values can't be edited or deleted. However, you can add new values to the
member status field.
Note Last Activity Date and Statement Last Generated Date can be updated in the Edit mode. The

Loyalty Management Loyalty Program Members
dates are updated based on the member transactions.
1122.. Save your changes.
After a Loyalty Program Member record is created, a set of related records are automatically created for
the member. A Loyalty Member Currency record is created for each active loyalty program currency. The
Loyalty Member Currency records enable the loyalty program to track and manage the member's points
balance. A Loyalty Member Tier record is created for each active tier group in the loyalty program. The
Loyalty Member Tier records assign the member to the lowest tiers of the active tier groups.
To add a member to a group, go to the member's Loyalty Member record and then click Add Member on
the Associated Groups related list.
Note Group Members is another related list that's added to the member's Loyalty Member record
by default. This related list is for Group type Loyalty Program Member records. To remove the Group
Members related list for Individual or Corporate type members, see Update the Layout of Loyalty
Program Member Record Page.
Member Groups
You create groups and add members to the groups. Members can contribute a percentage of their own
points to a group. A member can be a part of multiple groups and can also decide the percentage of
points to contribute to each group.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management -
Growth or Loyalty Management - Advanced
Create Member Groups
Individual and corporate members of a loyalty program can be added to a group. As part of a group,
members can pool points together. Based on a member's contribution percentage towards a group,
the points earned by a member are transferred to the member's groups.
Add Members to a Group
After you create a group, you can add Individual or Corporate type loyalty program members to the
group. Members of a group can contribute nonqualifying points to the group's point balances.
Learn How a Group's Point Balances Are Managed
Loyalty Management automates the process that manages group balances. Here's a summary of how
you can use Loyalty Management to manage a group's point balances.
Transfer Member Points to Groups
You can transfer points to a member's groups only after the points are credited to the member. If the
Transfer Points to Member Group Automatically setting is turned on, the points are transferred
automatically. If the setting is turned off, use the Transfer Member Points to Groups action in a new or

Loyalty Management Loyalty Program Members
existing flow to transfer members' nonqualifying points to their associated groups.
Update the Layout of Loyalty Program Member Record Page
The page layout for Individual, Corporate, and Group type Loyalty Program Member records are the
same. But, some of the related lists on this page layout don't apply to Individual and Corporate
members, or to groups. Customize the layout to ensure that the most relevant related lists appear for
each member type.
Things to Consider While Working with Member Groups
There are a few points to remember before you add member groups to your loyalty program and
transfer points to groups.
Create Member Groups
Individual and corporate members of a loyalty program can be added to a group. As part of a group,
members can pool points together. Based on a member's contribution percentage towards a group, the
points earned by a member are transferred to the member's groups.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Developer, and Unlimited Editions
Available in: Orgs that have the B2C - Loyalty Management Plus or the Loyalty Management - Advanced
license enabled
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create a member group: Loyalty Management
11.. On the Loyalty Program tab of the Loyalty Management app, select the program for which you want to
create a member group.
22.. On the Loyalty Program Members related list, click New.
33.. In the Member Type field, select Group.
44.. Complete these required fields for a Group type member:

| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Membership Number | The unique membership number of the group. |
| Group Name | The name of the group. |
| Enrollment Date | The date on which the group enrolled into the loyalty program. |

55.. In the Group Created By field, specify the loyalty program member who is either creating the group or
has requested for the creation of the group.
66.. Save your changes.
On the group's Loyalty Member record, click Add Members to Group on the Group Members related list

Loyalty Management Loyalty Program Members
to add Individual or Corporate type members to the group.
Note Associated Groups is another related list that's added to the group's Loyalty Member record by
default. This related list is for Individual and Corporate type members. To remove the Associated
Groups related list for Group type members, see Update the Layout of Loyalty Program Member
Record Page.
After a group is created, members can view the details of the group on the loyalty program's site created
using the Loyalty Member Portal template. To review the details of a group, a member must open the
group's Loyalty Program Member record.
Add Members to a Group
After you create a group, you can add Individual or Corporate type loyalty program members to the
group. Members of a group can contribute nonqualifying points to the group's point balances.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions
Available in: Orgs that have the B2C - Loyalty, B2C - Loyalty Plus, B2B - Loyalty, Loyalty Management -
Growth, or Loyalty Management - Advanced license enabled
To add an Individual or a Corporate type member to a group, use these ways:
•
Click Add Member on the Group Members related list on a Group type Loyalty Program Member
record.
•
Click Add Member to Group on the Associated Groups related list on an Individual or a Corporate
type Loyalty Program Member record.
•
From the App Launcher, find and select Loyalty Program Group Member Relationships. Click New.
When adding a member to a group, provide these details:

| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Group | The group to which you intend to add the Individual or Corporate member. |
| Loyalty Program Member | The Individual or Corporate member you intend to add to a group. Ensure that the member and the group are part of the same loyalty program. |
| Contribution Percentage | The percentage of points that a member intends to transfer to the group. Members can contribute up to 100% of their points across all groups that they're a part of. |

Loyalty Management Loyalty Program Members

| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Member can redeem group points | This field is optional and not used. Admins can customize your company's instance of Loyalty Management to use this field to indicate which members can redeem points from the group. |

A member can be added to a maximum of 100 groups.
Members can view the details of the groups they're a part

…

chunks.heading: part-01
chunks.body:
---
title: "Member Engagement Widgets (part 1/2)"
source_pdf: "loyalty_management_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "755-834"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Member Engagement Widgets
View Member Benefits
You can view member benefit details, the status of benefits, and the records created or updated by
Benefit Actions.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Developer, and Unlimited Editions that
have Loyalty Management enabled.
11.. On the Loyalty Programs tab, open the loyalty program record.
22.. On the Setup tab, click Loyalty Program Members.
33.. Select a loyalty program member, and click View Member Benefits.
It lists the benefits available to a member based on the member's tier assignments.
44.. Click Close.
Member Engagement Widgets
Engage with members of your loyalty program and your customers on your company's website and
external portals by using member engagement widgets. Create widgets by using widget templates
provided by Loyalty Management and customize the widgets based on your company's branding and
design requirements. You can configure your widgets by using either the Widget Designer within the
Loyalty Management app or the OmniStudio's Flexcard Designer.
Prerequisites for Working with Member Engagement Widgets
The member engagement widget templates in Loyalty Management are based on Omnistudio
Flexcards. You can deploy widgets on your company's website or external portals by using OmniOut.
Install Omnistudio and OmniOut in your org before loyalty program managers create and customize
widgets.
Loyalty Widget Designer
Loyalty Widget Designer is a point and click canvas that lets you customize the widgets that you've
cloned by using the widget templates. After you customize your widgets, you can preview, activate,
and download the widgets.
OmniStudio FlexCard Designer
Customize Loyalty Member Engagement widget templates by using OmniStudio FlexCard Designer. To
add components to or remove components from a widget, use FlexCard Designer.
Learn About Member Engagement Widgets
The Member Engagement Widgets templates in Loyalty Management can be cloned and customized
by using Loyalty Widget Designer within the Loyalty Management app or by using OmniStudio's
FlexCard Designer. Each widget template has personalized content and design. Modify the content
and the design as per your requirements.

Loyalty Management Member Engagement Widgets
Customize Widgets with Components and Data of Your Choice
Use Loyalty Widget Designer to customize the look and feel, and text of a widget. To use custom
components or data of your choice, use the Apex classes of the widget templates based on your
requirements. To add a custom icon or a new merge field in the widget, customize the widget's Apex
class to update the widget.
Considerations for Using Member Engagement Widgets
Keep these points in mind when working with Member Engagement widgets.
Prerequisites for Working with Member Engagement Widgets
The member engagement widget templates in Loyalty Management are based on Omnistudio Flexcards.
You can deploy widgets on your company's website or external portals by using OmniOut. Install
Omnistudio and OmniOut in your org before loyalty program managers create and customize widgets.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with
Loyalty Management

| Install | Help |
| --- | --- |
| Omnistudio | • Install Omnistudio Managed Package • Omnistudio Release Summary for Installation and Upgrade |
| OmniOut | • Download the OmniOut Static Resource • Install OmniOut Dependencies |

Loyalty Widget Designer
Loyalty Widget Designer is a point and click canvas that lets you customize the widgets that you've cloned
by using the widget templates. After you customize your widgets, you can preview, activate, and
download the widgets.
Explore the Loyalty Widget Designer
The Loyalty Widget Designer lets you customize the branding and content of your widgets.
Clone Member Engagement Widget Templates
Loyalty Management provides 11 member engagement widget templates. Clone a widget template,
and then customize the widget to suit your use case and your company's requirements.
Preview Widgets in Loyalty Widget Designer
After you customize widgets, preview them by using data from your org, before you embed the

Loyalty Management Member Engagement Widgets
widgets on websites and external portals. The preview lets you verify whether a widget is working as
expected and also to check if the widget style is as per your branding requirements.
Activate or Download Widgets in Loyalty Widget Designer
Embed member engagement widgets on Experience Cloud site pages or on external portals. After
you've customized your widget and tested it in preview, activate the widget or download it based on
your use case. Download the widget to use OmniOut to embed the widget on external portals.
Activate the widget to embed the widget on Experience Cloud site pages as a FlexCard.
Explore the Loyalty Widget Designer
The Loyalty Widget Designer lets you customize the branding and content of your widgets.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with
Loyalty Management
Get to know the Loyalty Widget Designer canvas and its capabilities before you start working with it.

| Section | Use |
| --- | --- |
| Widget State (2) | Select the state of the widget that you want to customize. All widgets have a default state and an error state. In the default state, you can customize the text and the design of the widget. In the error state, you can customize the message that appears when the widget doesn't load on your website or portal. Some widgets have additional states. |
| Form Factor (3) | Choose the form factor that you want to customize the widget in. All widgets are responsive to the desktop, tablet, and mobile form factors. |
| Widget Properties (4) | Select the sections of the widget and customize the text and design of each component within the sections. The widget sections vary between the default and error states. |
| Widget Details (5) | Update the name or the description of the widget. Also, specify the visibility criterion for the widget: |

Loyalty Management Member Engagement Widgets

| Section | Use |
| --- | --- |
|  | • All members • Members of a particular tier • Members whose points balance is more than the number that you specify Some widgets have additional visibility criteria. For details, see the help for each widget. |
| Buttons (1) | • Activate-To embed the widget on a Lightning Web Component or Experience Cloud site page, activate the widget after you've customized it. • Download-To embed the widget on a site or page outside Salesforce by using OmniOut, download the widget after you've customized it. • Save & Preview-To have a preview of your customized widget. • Save-To save your customized widget and come back to it later. |

Explore and Learn
Watch this video to understand how to customize widgets based on your company's branding on the
Widget Designer.
Watch the video: https://play.vidyard.com/HgpYEnntaoKTTCGtnyXPEu
If you aren't able to watch the video in full screen, open the video on a new tab: Design Loyalty
Engagement Widgets As Per Your Company's Branding.
Clone Member Engagement Widget Templates
Loyalty Management provides 11 member engagement widget templates. Clone a widget template, and
then customize the widget to suit your use case and your company's requirements.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with
Loyalty Management

Loyalty Management Member Engagement Widgets
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To clone member engagement widget templates: Loyalty Management permission set
11.. On the Loyalty Program tab in Loyalty Management, select the program that you want to clone a
member engagement widget template for.
22.. Click Setup.
33.. In the Loyalty Program Widgets related list, click New.
44.. Search for and select the desired widget template.
55.. Click Next.
66.. Enter the name and description of the widget.
77.. Save your changes.
The cloned widget opens in Loyalty Widget Designer.
Customize the widget as per your requirements.
Preview Widgets in Loyalty Widget Designer
After you customize widgets, preview them by using data from your org, before you embed the widgets
on websites and external portals. The preview lets you verify whether a widget is working as expected
and also to check if the widget style is as per your branding requirements.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with
Loyalty Management
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To preview member engagement widgets: Loyalty Management permission set
11.. On the Loyalty Program tab in Loyalty Management, select the program that you want to clone a

To continue with the customization of the widget, switch to the Design view.

Loyalty Management Member Engagement Widgets

widgets:

A zip file is downloaded to your local system. The zip file contains files that you require to embed the

After you activate the widget, you can't customize the widget anymore. To customize the widget,

Component to an Experience Cloud Page.

websites.

chunks.heading: 16-manage-and-view-transaction-journals
chunks.body:
---
title: "Manage and View Transaction Journals"
source_pdf: "loyalty_management_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "835-844"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Manage and View Transaction Journals
See Also
Salesforce Help: Remind Members About Expiring Points Widget Template
Considerations for Using Member Engagement Widgets
Keep these points in mind when working with Member Engagement widgets.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with
Loyalty Management
Images
•
Check the size of images and ensure that the images fit in the real estate available on the widget.
•
Ensure that the image URLs that you use aren't placed behind authentication.
Widgets on Experience Cloud Site Pages
•
Provide customers and members permission to view widgets on site pages.
•
Provide customers and members the permission to view and update data site pages.
For example, customers need the Create permission on the Loyalty Program Member object and the
Person Account object to join a loyalty program by using the Join Program widget template. Members
need the View permission on the Transaction Journal object to view their recent transactions on the
View Transaction History widget.
Benefit Selection on Select Member Benefits Widget
Currently, members can't select benefits on widgets that are cloned by using the Select Member Benefits
widget template.
Manage and View Transaction Journals
View transaction journals to track member activities, member behaviors, and make manual adjustments
to points. Transactions happen when members accrue points, redeem points, adjust points manually,
and reverse redemptions.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions that
have Loyalty Management enabled.

Loyalty Management Manage and View Transaction Journals
As an admin, you can view transaction journals and perform manual actions such as adjusting the points
for member transactions or activities. You can also cancel accrued and redeemed transactions. You can
perform bulk actions for up to 200 journal entries.
The member's earned points are debited when an accrual transaction is reversed. The member's earned
points are credited when a redemption transaction is reversed.
The Loyalty App includes these key transaction objects.

| Loyalty Transaction Objects | What Does it Store? |
| --- | --- |
| Transaction Journal | All customer transactions in a loyalty program. The transactions can be accruals, redemptions, or adjustments. |
| Loyalty Ledger | The details of every transaction: • The transaction types performed by program members. • Points accrued for each member that were processed in the Loyalty Ledger. • The expiry date for each point. |
| Loyalty Member Currency | Every detail of a member's currency: • A member's points balance • Total points accrued • Total points expired • Total points redeemed • Total points eligible for expiration |

Use the Loyalty Accrual Transactions and Loyalty Redemption Transactions content blocks in Marketing
Cloud Engagement to send personalized emails to members.
Learn About Journal Types and Subtypes
Journal types and subtypes help categorize transaction journals. Specifying the journal type and
subtype of a transaction journal helps you to design processes that process transaction journals based
on their type and subtype. For example, you create transaction journals of the type Accrual and
subtype Member Enrollment to credit points to members for joining a loyalty program. Now you can
design a process to credit points to new members when the member's transaction type is Accrual and
subtype is Member Enrollment.
Prerequisites for Managing Member Transactions
You must complete certain prerequisites before you set up processes that involve managing points
and transactions.

Loyalty Management Manage and View Transaction Journals
Adjust Points for a Transaction Journal
Use Adjust Points to manually adjust a member's credited or debited points. The system makes these
adjustments in real time.
Adjust Points for Multiple Transaction Journals
You can manually adjust points for multiple transaction journals for loyalty program members in real
time.
Cancel an Accrual Transaction
You can cancel an accrual transaction for various reasons such as a product return, an order
cancellation, and when a member requests to cancel an accrual. After you reverse an accrual
transaction, the points that were credited to the member for the transaction are debited from the
member's points balance.
Cancel a Redemption Transaction
You can reverse a redemption transaction for various reasons, such as a product return, a canceled
order, and when a member wants to cancel a redemption. After you reverse a redemption transaction,
the points redeemed by the member are credited back to the member.
Manage Transaction Journal and Loyalty Ledgers
Learn how to manage Transaction Journal and Loyalty Ledger records.
Learn About Journal Types and Subtypes
Journal types and subtypes help categorize transaction journals. Specifying the journal type and subtype
of a transaction journal helps you to design processes that process transaction journals based on their
type and subtype. For example, you create transaction journals of the type Accrual and subtype Member
Enrollment to credit points to members for joining a loyalty program. Now you can design a process to
credit points to new members when the member's transaction type is Accrual and subtype is Member
Enrollment.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Developer, and Unlimited Editions that
have Loyalty Management enabled
Important To use journal types and subtypes that are provided with Loyalty Management in your
sandbox, you need a full sandbox. The records aren't available in partial copy sandboxes.
Loyalty Management provides these journal types and subtypes:

| Journal Type | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Accrual | Provided for transaction journals for which points are credited to members. | A transaction journal that's created to credit points to a member for purchasing a product. |

Loyalty Management Manage and View Transaction Journals

| Journal Type | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Two journal subtypes are provided with the Accrual journal type: • Member Enrollment: Provided for transaction journals that are created to credit points to a member for joining the loyalty program. • Member Referral: Provided for transaction journals that are created to credit points to a member for referring another member to the loyalty program. |  |
| Accrual Reversal | Provided for transaction journals that are created to debit points from members because they canceled an Accrual type transaction journal for which members were credited points. | A transaction journal that's created to debit points from a member when the member cancels a product for which the member was credited points. |
| Manual Points Adjustment | Provided for transaction journals that are created to adjust points in the member's points balance or the points that were debited or credited for a transaction journal. | A transaction journal that's created to credit points to a member as a token of apology because their product wasn't delivered on time. |
| Points Expiration | Provided for transaction journals that expire member points that are past the expiration date. | A transaction journal that's created on December 31 to debit the points that expired in December 30 from the member's points balance. |
| Qualifying Points Reset | Provided for transaction journals that reset the qualifying points balance of members to zero after the member's qualifying period for a tier is over. | A transaction journal that's created to reset a member's Qualifying currency points balance to zero on the qualifying points reset date. |
| Redemption | Provided for transaction journals for which points are debited | A transaction journal that's created to debit points that the |

Loyalty Management Manage and View Transaction Journals

| Journal Type | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | from members. | member redeemed to purchase a product. |
| Redemption Reversal | Provided for transaction journals that are created to credit points to members because they canceled a Redemption type transaction journal for which points were debited from the members. | A transaction journal that's created to credit points to the member after the member cancels a product for which the member redeemed points. |

Note The Journal Type and Journal SubType records that are provided with Loyalty Management
aren't localized.
Prerequisites for Managing Member Transactions
You must complete certain prerequisites before you set up processes that involve managing points and
transactions.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management
Journal Types
Before you set up processes that involve crediting points, debiting points, and canceling transactions,
make sure that these journal type records are available in the org.
•
Accrual
•
Accrual Reversal
•
Manual Points Adjustment
•
Points Expiration
•
Qualifying Points Reset
•
Redemption
•
Redemption Reversal
See Journal Types and Subtypes.
Journal Reason Types
Make sure that these journal reason type values are available for one or more picklist values in the

Loyalty Management Manage and View Transaction Journals
Journal Reason field on the Transaction Journal object.

| JOURNAL REASON TYPE | BUSINESS REQUIREMENT |
| --- | --- |
| RedemptionReversal | Used in transactions that credit points back to members for canceled redemptions. |
| AccrualReversal | Used in transactions that debit points from members' point balance for canceled accruals. |
| ManualPointsAdjustment | Used in transactions that credit points to or debit points from members for points adjustments. |

If the journal reason types aren't available in the org, admins can't configure automated processes that
cancel transactions or adjust member points. The journal reason types also enable sales reps to manage
member requests related to transaction cancellations and points adjustments.
If any of these Journal Reason Types aren't available as a picklist value in the Journal Reason field, add
the picklist value with the corresponding API name. See JournalReason.
Adjust Points for a Transaction Journal
Use Adjust Points to manually adjust a member's credited or debited points. The system makes these
adjustments in real time.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions that
have Loyalty Management enabled.
When you adjust points for multiple transaction journals to debit points from associated members, the
system updates the members' points balance in real time. When you adjust to credit points, points are
credited in real time but the points balance can be updated in these ways.

| Scenario | Action |
| --- | --- |
| Qualifying points are credited. | Run the Credit Qualifying Points to Members Data Processing Engine definition. |
| The org has real-time non-qualifying points balance update enable, and non-qualifying points are credited. | The points automatically credit to the associated members' points balance as soon as you make the adjustment. |
| The org credits non-qualifying points in batches. | Run the Credit Non-Qualifying Points to Members Data Processing Engine definition. |

chunks.heading: 17-loyalty-service-excellence
chunks.body:
---
title: "Loyalty Service Excellence"
source_pdf: "loyalty_management_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "845-854"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Loyalty Service Excellence

| Scenario | Action |
| --- | --- |
|  | • If users aren't required to view Transaction Journal records from the Loyalty Program record, remove the Transaction Journal related list from the Loyalty Program page layout. • Provide standard users access to the Loyalty Program Member records of members whose related Transaction Journal records you want the user to view. Users automatically have access to the related Transaction Journal records of a member whose record they have access to. |
| You want users to experience optimal performance when they open the Transaction Journals list view page. | The user experience is optimal when users view Transaction Journal records by using: • Recently Viewed list view. • Custom list views created by admins. Admins can add filters in custom list views to ensure that users can view the most relevant records. |
| You want users to experience optimal performance when they open the Loyalty Ledger list view page. | The user experience is optimal when users view Loyalty Ledger records by using: • Recently Viewed list view. • Custom list views created by admins. Admins can add filters in custom list views to ensure that users can view the most relevant records. |

Loyalty Service Excellence
Use the Identity Verification flows and the service console for Loyalty Program Members so that your
customer service representatives (CSRs) can engage loyalty program members better. CSRs can use
Identity Verification flows to verify the identity of your loyalty program members or their authorized
member representatives. Then, CSRs can use the service console to resolve issues, clarify questions,
manage requests, and more of loyalty program members or authorized member representatives.

Loyalty Management Loyalty Service Excellence
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Developer, and Unlimited Editions
Available in: Orgs that have the B2C - Loyalty Management Plus or the Loyalty Management - Advanced
license enabled
Prerequisites for Setting Up the Service Console for Loyalty Program Member
Review these prerequisites and considerations to set up and use the Service Console for Loyalty
Program Member.
Identity Verification
Customer service representatives verify the identity of loyalty program members or authorized
member representatives by using the prebuilt Verify Customer Identity or the Verify Identity of Loyalty
Program Member flows. The Verify Identity of Loyalty Program Member flow is a customized flow that's
provided by Loyalty Management. You can also create custom flows in Flow Builder by using the Verify
Customer Identity Flow template.
Service Console for Loyalty Program Members
Customer service representatives (CSRs) can get a comprehensive view of members' details and
activities in one page by using the service console for loyalty program members. CSRs can use the
console to proactively engage loyalty program members and resolve their questions, and address their
requests, search for relevant knowledge articles, get alerts for related records that need attention, and
run actions to resolve member requests. CSRs can also track previous interactions on a timeline and
check related records.
Member Profile Card
Customer service representatives can view a loyalty program member's basic information in the
Member Profile card. A member is an individual represented by a contact in your organization.
Loyalty Member Timeline
Timeline provides a comprehensive, chronological, and interactive view of member-related activities in
one place. For example, when a member calls in about their points balance, the customer service
representative can create an engagement interaction that gets included in the Timeline, making the
interaction viewable for other service representatives.
Member Activity Details
Show relevant details of loyalty program members' activity in your service console so that your
customer service representatives (CSRs) can quickly view the details and assist members efficiently.
You can show four tabs out of the seven options in any order: Overview, Transactions, Promotions,
Vouchers, Benefits, Badges, and Associated Groups.
Actions
Configure actions by using flows, OmniScripts, or quick actions so that your customer service
representatives can use Action Launcher to quickly find and run service actions, such as issuing a
voucher, and changing member's tiers, and resolve queries.
Alerts
Show alerts on the Loyalty Program Member page so that your customer service representatives,
loyalty program admins, and the program managers can stay on top of changes related to your loyalty
program members. For example, show alerts on a member's expiring points or vouchers so that the

Loyalty Management Loyalty Service Excellence
members can take immediate action and avoid issues. You can also alert customer service
representatives. You can create alerts manually or automatically.
Cases
Create cases for loyalty program members and associate the cases with the respective loyalty program
member records. Manage cases in the service console for loyalty program members.
Loyalty Member Lifecycle
Use the Loyalty Membership Lifecycle component to track a member's change of status. You can track
changes, such as the duration of a purchased subscription and the status of the subscription. If your
loyalty program has paid tiers, then you can track the duration for which members are required to be
part of the paid tier.
Prerequisites for Setting Up the Service Console for Loyalty
Program Member
Review these prerequisites and considerations to set up and use the Service Console for Loyalty Program
Member.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Developer, and Unlimited Editions
Available in: Orgs that have the B2C - Loyalty Management Plus or the Loyalty Management - Advanced
license enabled
Setting
To work with the Service Console for Loyalty Program Member, turn on the Service Excellence for Loyalty
Program Member in Setup.
Timeline
•
To use the Timeline component, enable Timeline in Setup.
•
The console has a preconfigured Loyalty Member Timeline component that shows the engagement
interaction records for a loyalty program member.
•
To create a custom timeline that shows other loyalty object records, configure the Timeline
component included with Service Excellence, and add the component to the Loyalty Program Member
record page.
Record Alerts
Configure record alerts as per your business needs.

Loyalty Management Loyalty Service Excellence
Actions
You must configure actions as per your business needs. See Create an Actions & Recommendations
Deployment.
Identity Verification
Use a flow to verify the identity:
•
Verify Customer Identity flow from Industry Service Excellence
•
Verify Identity of Loyalty Program Member flow
To add the Verify Identity of Loyalty Program Member flow to your component, use the Edit Page and
select the flow and activate the page. You can also create your own flow by cloning the Verify Customer
Identity flow. In Flow Builder, create a flow using the Verify Customer Identity flow template or Verify
Identity of Loyalty Program Member template. Next, create a Voice Call Lightning record page. Finally,
make the flow available to the Customer Service Representative by adding it to the Voice Call record
page or Engagement Interaction in Lightning App Builder.
To customize the Verify Identity of Loyalty Program Member flow, modify the Loyalty Member Verification
Process Definition process details and fields. For example, in the Verify Identity of Loyalty Program
Member flow, Membership ID is a required field and Email is an optional field. If you want to make Email
as a required verifier, you can modify it as a Required Verifier in the Identity Verification Process Field
page.
Engagement Interaction
To create engagement records, configure computer-telephony integration (CTI) systems and Service
Cloud Voice for partner telephony (BYOT). See Salesforce Open CTI and Set Up Service Cloud Voice with
Partner Telephony.
Identity Verification
Customer service representatives verify the identity of loyalty program members or authorized member
representatives by using the prebuilt Verify Customer Identity or the Verify Identity of Loyalty Program
Member flows. The Verify Identity of Loyalty Program Member flow is a customized flow that's provided
by Loyalty Management. You can also create custom flows in Flow Builder by using the Verify Customer
Identity Flow template.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Developer, and Unlimited Editions
Available in: Orgs that have the B2C - Loyalty Management Plus or the Loyalty Management - Advanced

Loyalty Management Loyalty Service Excellence
license enabled
You can verify the identity of a loyalty program member or an authorized representative for engagements
initiated through OpenCTI and Omni-Channel. Customer service representatives can access the
verification flow from an Engagement Interaction or a Voice Call record page. Add the flow to the
Engagement Interaction and to the Voice Call record pages in Lightning App Builder.
Verify Identity of Loyalty Program Member
Here are the pages in Flow Builder that are customized for Loyalty Management:
In the Verify Identity of Loyalty Program Member flow, the page where the reason for engagement is
specified:
In the Verify Identity of Loyalty Program Member flow, the participant type is specified:
In the Verify Identity of Loyalty Program Member flow, search for a loyalty program member:

Loyalty Management Loyalty Service Excellence
In the Verify Identity of Loyalty Program Member flow, search for a loyalty program member:
In the Verify Identity of Loyalty Program Member flow, the required and optional attributes for
verification information for the loyalty program member:
Service Console for Loyalty Program Members
Customer service representatives (CSRs) can get a comprehensive view of members' details and activities
in one page by using the service console for loyalty program members. CSRs can use the console to
proactively engage loyalty program members and resolve their questions, and address their requests,
search for relevant knowledge articles, get alerts for related records that need attention, and run actions
to resolve member requests. CSRs can also track previous interactions on a timeline and check related
records.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Loyalty Management -
Growth or Loyalty Management - Advanced
The service console brings together the Service Excellence components and platform capabilities to a

Loyalty Management Loyalty Service Excellence
unified console. The console comes with these preconfigured components:
•
Identity Verification flow
•
Member Profile Card
•
Timeline component that shows Member Engagement Interaction records
•
Record Alerts
•
Actions
•
Member and member activity details
•
Cases
•
Member Lifecycle
Note The Call Controls, Conversation, Knowledge, and Notes components can be used in the
console for Service Cloud license holders.
You can configure these components to individually work with other standard navigation apps too. For
example, you can track the different attributes of a contact by creating a timeline for the Contact object
and then adding the Timeline component to the Contact record page.
In Lightning App Builde

Set Up Experiences for Loyalty Program Members
Loyalty Member Lifecycle
Use the Loyalty Membership Lifecycle component to track a member's change of status. You can track
changes, such as the duration of a purchased subscription and the status of the subscription. If your
loyalty program has paid tiers, then you can track the duration for which members are required to be
part of the paid tier.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Developer, and Unlimited Editions
Available in: Orgs that have the B2C - Loyalty Management Plus or the Loyalty Management - Advanced
license enabled
Set Up Experiences for Loyalty Program Members
Create a feature-rich portal for your loyalty program members using the Loyalty Member Portal template.
Keep your loyalty members engaged by letting them track their progress in the reward program with the
Loyalty Member Portal. With a few clicks, admins can set up the loyalty member portal where program
members can view their program details, including recent transactions, current tiers, benefits, points
associated with each transaction, and profile details. You can customize the portal based on your
company's requirements.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Developer, and Unlimited Editions that
have Loyalty Management enabled.
The Loyalty Member Portal is available for all the Experience Cloud Site licenses and user types except for
guest users. The Experience Cloud site admins with the loyalty management license can set up the
loyalty member portal with out-of-the-box components provided by the Experience Cloud site.
Loyalty Member Portal Workflow
Here's a quick summary of all the steps an admin must complete to set up a loyalty member portal.
Enable the Experience Cloud Site in Your Org
Enabling the Salesforce Experience Cloud site is the first step to create your Loyalty Member Portal.
Create Your Loyalty Member Portal
Use the Loyalty Member Portal Lightning template to create a portal for your loyalty members. You can
customize the portal using out-of-the-box components provided by the Experience Cloud site.
Members can use the portal to view their profile details, including recent transactions, point balance,
and current tiers.
Associate the Experience Cloud Site with a Loyalty Program
You can associate a member with multiple loyalty programs. The Loyalty Member Portal shows the

Loyalty Management Set Up Experiences for Loyalty Program Members
member's loyalty programs that are associated with the Experience Cloud site. You can associate a
Loyalty Member Portal only to one loyalty program member.
Customize the Loyalty Member Portal
The Loyalty Member Portal home page is ready to brand and personalize your member experience.
You can use the out-of-the-box components provided by Experience Cloud site to customize the
portal.
Which Objects Are Available for Experience Cloud Users in Loyalty Management?
Users and guest users of your Experience Cloud site can access data from the loyalty program
associated with the site.
Enable Experience User
Enable a user to log in to the Loyalty Member Portal on the Experience Cloud site.
Activate Your Loyalty Member Portal
Activate your Loyalty Member Portal to make it accessible to the loyalty program members.
Launch Your Loyalty Member Portal
Keep your loyalty members engaged by letting them track their own progress in the program with the
Loyalty Member Portal. Members can view their profile details, loyalty program details, membership
details, recent transactions, points balance, and current tiers. Members can also view the program
currencies that members hold, the credited vouchers, promotions, and the benefits.
Loyalty Member Portal Workflow
Here's a quick summary of all the steps an admin must complete to set up a loyalty member portal.

chunks.body:

Integrate Loyalty Management with External Systems
Integrate Loyalty Management with External Systems
Loyalty Management comes with a set of developer resources, such as business APIs, invocable actions,
and standard objects that you can use to integrate your loyalty programs with external systems, such as
point of sales, reservations, booking, billing, service, and operations. For example, to create a transaction
journal and credit points to members for online purchases, you can integrate your external website with
a business API that can run the relevant loyalty program process.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions
Available in: Orgs that have the B2C - Loyalty, B2C - Loyalty Plus, B2B - Loyalty, Loyalty Management -
Growth, or Loyalty Management - Advanced license enabled
Business APIs for Automating Loyalty Processes
Use Business APIs to integrate external systems with Loyalty Management for performing everyday
processes of your loyalty program, such as updating member details, crediting points, or issuing
vouchers.
Configurable Business APIs
For repetitive and complex loyalty tasks, Loyalty Management comes with process templates that you
can run using the configurable business APIs. Each process template contains a set of predefined
process parameters and rules. You can clone a process template to create a program process.
Static Business APIs
The simpler tasks that don't require customization are left as Business APIs. They have a fixed payload
of parameters. You can change the parameters but not the structure.
Actions
You can create and manage loyalty programs for your organization using the standard and custom
invocable actions. For example, change member tier based on their loyalty points, assign and process
benefits based on their tier, manage loyalty points for a member, issue voucher for a member, and
cancel redemption transactions for a member. You can also use Actions to integrate data in third-party
systems with Loyalty Management.
Standard Objects
Loyalty Management uses objects on the Salesforce core platform and the SObject APIs are available
for all Loyalty objects. SObject APIs are defined on top of the Salesforce Objects. These APIs retrieve
basic metadata for a specified object, or create a record for the specified object. For example, this
resource can be used to retrieve the metadata for the Account object using the GET method, or create
an Account object using the POST method.
Loyalty Management Point of Sale API
Integrate Loyalty Management with a Point of Sale system to manage customer loyalty programs and
transactions with ease. You can integrate Loyalty Management with a POS system through Loyalty
Management POS API Unlocked Package or through a combination of the unlocked package and
Loyalty Management POS API Powered by MuleSoft. A Point of Sale (POS) system unifies in-store and

Loyalty Management Integrate Loyalty Management with External Systems
online operations and provides the best experience for an existing or new member by using secure
API requests. Use the APIs to get member profile details, enroll individual members in a loyalty
program, manage vouchers, and create accrual and redemption orders.
Business APIs for Automating Loyalty Processes
Use Business APIs to integrate external systems with Loyalty Management for performing everyday
processes of your loyalty program, such as updating member details, crediting points, or issuing
vouchers.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions
Available in: Orgs that have the B2C - Loyalty, B2C - Loyalty Plus, B2B - Loyalty, Loyalty Management -
Growth, or Loyalty Management - Advanced license enabled
Loyalty Management comes with these types of Business APIs:
Configurable APIs
Use this API type to create processes from loyalty program process templates and to extend the
processes based on your requirements.
Static APIs
Use this API type to process simple tasks, such as to view member details, to redeem vouchers,
and to enroll members. These APIs are used out of the box.
Important You can execute 500,000 Post requests and 500,000 Get requests per hour for the
Loyalty Management Business APIs.
Configurable Business APIs
For repetitive and complex loyalty tasks, Loyalty Management comes with process templates that you can
run using the configurable business APIs. Each process template contains a set of predefined process
parameters and rules. You can clone a process template to create a program process.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions
Available in: Orgs that have the B2C - Loyalty, B2C - Loyalty Plus, B2B - Loyalty, Loyalty Management -
Growth, or Loyalty Management - Advanced license enabled
In a cloned process, you can customize the predefined process parameters and rules based on your

Loyalty Management Integrate Loyalty Management with External Systems
requirements. For example, after you clone the Update Member Details process template, you can
update the predefined rule to also update members' email addresses.
Here are the loyalty program process templates that are provided with Loyalty Management.

| Process Template Category | Process Template Name | Description | URL of Business API |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Tier Processing | Change Member Tier | Upgrades or downgrades members' tiers. | /connect/ loyalty/ programs/ DemoProgram/ program- processes/ updateTier |
| Promotion | Enroll in Promotion | Enrolls members to promotions that are associated with members' loyalty programs. | /connect/ loyalty/ programs/ DemoProgram/ program- processes/ enrollInProm otion |
| Promotion | Get Member Promotions | Gets loyalty promotions based on the member eligibility category. | /connect/ loyalty/ programs/ DemoProgram/ program- processes/ GetMemberPro motions |
| Member | Update Member Details | Updates the details of the Member and Contact records. For example, Membership Number, preferences, and custom fields of the Member record. And first name, last name, email ID, and mobile phone number of the Contact record. | /connect/ loyalty/ programs/ DemoProgram/ program- processes/ updateMember Details |
| Member | Unenroll Member | Changes members' status to inactive in a loyalty program. | /connect/ |

Loyalty Management Integrate Loyalty Management with External Systems
Explore and Learn
Watch this video to understand how to customize a loyalty program process template.
Watch the video: https://play.vidyard.com/BwfEPhWYVxujEuebNNcR4o
If you aren't able to watch the video in full screen, open the video on a new tab: Process Member
Redemptions Using Promotion Setup in Loyalty Management .
Change Member Tier Process Template
To upgrade or downgrade a member's tier from an external system, you can use the Change Member
Tier process template to create processes for changing the tier of the members in the loyalty tier
group.
Enroll in Promotion Process Template
When loyalty members of a loyalty program enroll in loyalty promotions associated with loyalty
program from an external system, you can use the Enroll in Promotion process template to process the
enrollment of members for a promotion.
Get Member Promotions Process Template
Use the Get Member Promotions process template to create a process for showing member
promotions on an external system.
Opt Out of Promotion Process Template
Use the Opt Out of Promotion loyalty program process template to let members leave the promotions
that they've enrolled in.
Update Member Details Process Template
Use the Update Member Details process template to update an Individual type member's details in
their Loyalty Program Member record and the related Contact record. You can update standard fields,
such as member address or custom fields, such as shopping preferences. You can also credit points to
members for completing or updating their membership profile.
Unenroll Member Process Template
If your loyalty members are leaving the loyalty program and when you want to disengage those
members from the loyalty program from an external system, you can use the Unenroll Member
process template to create processes for inactivating the status of the member in the loyalty program.
Credit Points to Members Process Template
When loyalty members gain points for transactions in external systems, you can use the Credit Points
to Members process template to create processes for crediting the precalculated points to the
member.
Debit Points from Members Process Template
If a redemption catalog doesn't exist in external systems and when loyalty members redeem points for
transactions, you want the Loyalty Management application to calculate the points and redeem the
points from the member's points balance. Use the Debit Points from Members process template to
create a process for processing the member's transaction journals and debit points from the member's
points balance.
Issue Voucher Process Template

Loyalty Management Integrate Loyalty Management with External Systems
To issue vouchers to loyalty program members or contacts, especially from an external system, you can
use the Issue Voucher process template to create processes for issuing vouchers as per the specified
voucher definition.
Cancel Voucher Process Template
To discard the unused vouchers or to withdraw the issued vouchers, you can cancel the vouchers
especially from an external system. You can use the Cancel Voucher process template to create
processes for c
