chunks.heading: 20-integrate-loyalty-management-with-google-wallet
chunks.body:
---
title: "Integrate Loyalty Management With Google Wallet"
source_pdf: "loyalty_management_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "906-914"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Integrate Loyalty Management With Google Wallet
See Also
Loyalty Management Developer Guide: Loyalty Management Integrations API
Loyalty Management Developer Guide: Install Loyalty Management POS API Unlocked Package
What to Expect from Loyalty Management Point of Sale API
This scenario study explains what you can achieve with the Loyalty Management with POS integration
through an unlocked package or through a combination of MuleSoft and unlocked package.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: all editions that have Loyalty Management enabled
Enroll an individual member
Imagine Adam Smith shopping at the NTO Insider store. At the checkout counter, the operator enrolls
Adam as a loyalty program member by using the Member Enrollment API.
Place orders for items purchased at the store
Adam purchases items at the store and the operator bills them through the POS system. The order is
processed by using the Order Placement API.
Get member details
The operator enters the membership number in the POS system and gets member details such as tier,
member currency, tier group, and the redemption cost per unit by using the Member Profile API.
Get voucher details
The operator enters the membership number in the POS system and gets the details of vouchers issued
to the member.
Integrate Loyalty Management With Google Wallet
Drive customer engagement by integrating your loyalty program with Google Wallet. This integration
allows loyalty program managers to issue and manage dynamic digital passes, such as loyalty cards and
vouchers, directly within customers' Google Wallets.

Loyalty Management Integrate Loyalty Management With Google Wallet
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Developer, Enterprise, Performance, and Unlimited editions that have Loyalty
Management enabled.
Loyalty program members can use digital passes to access their membership profile and the linked
vouchers from their mobile devices. For example, a member may be enrolled in three different loyalty
programs, and can see all of them in the Google Wallet app. This ease of use encourages members to
use their loyalty rewards and boosts program participation. If a member holds a loyalty membership pass
in their Google Wallet, the system automatically links the newly issued vouchers to that existing pass. The
system supports linking up to 50 vouchers to a single loyalty membership pass.
Important Google Wallet Integration with Loyalty Management is a third-party service provider
which is integrated with Salesforce Loyalty Management system and Salesforce is enabling its
customers to use this at their own option and discretion. Customers will have the option to pass-on
the information it opts for its own customers so that the customers experiences can be powered.
Customers can invoke the Google Wallet API and the API would consume the information through
Loyalty Management system which will be shown on the Google Wallet Digital Pass.
Example A prospective customer navigates to a brand's website and completes the sign-up process
for the loyalty program. Upon successful sign-up, the system creates a new contact record and a
loyalty program member record. The website presents the new member with an option to add a
digital pass to Google Wallet, which then redirects them to the Google Wallet app. Within the
Google Wallet app, the member confirms the action to finalize the creation of their new digital
loyalty pass. The member's digital loyalty pass containing their membership ID, tier, and point
balance is now instantly available in their Google Wallet app for convenient use and display.
Cross-Platform Setup
To successfully issue digital passes, you must complete specific configuration tasks across both the
Google Cloud and Salesforce environments.
Integration Prerequisites and Setup
Integrate Loyalty Management with Google Wallet, allowing a loyalty program to generate digital
passes and provide real-time membership updates to your members.
Digital Pass Template Setup and Dynamic Pass Generation
A loyalty program manager can design loyalty classes on the Google Pay and Wallet Console. When the
program manager creates digital pass templates, they can fetch the classes created to generate the
digital pass.
Create a Digital Pass Template
Create a digital pass template to connect the static designs on Google's console and the dynamic data
stored in Salesforce. This template defines the static design and layout of the pass.
Cross-Platform Setup
To successfully issue digital passes, you must complete specific configuration tasks across both the

Loyalty Management Integrate Loyalty Management With Google Wallet
Google Cloud and Salesforce environments.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Developer, Enterprise, Performance, and Unlimited editions that have Loyalty
Management enabled.

|  | Environment | Configuration Actions |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Integration Prerequisites and Setup | Google | • Set up Google service account • Create Google Cloud Project and enable APIs • Configure Roles • Set up Google Pay & Wallet Console • Set up Google Wallet API Issuer Account |
|  | Salesforce | • Set up a Salesforce org • Upload Java Keystore (JKS) certificate to Salesforce • Create a JSON Web Token (JWT) External Credential • Create Principals for JWT • Create Named Credentials • Enable External Credential Principals |
| Design Setup | Google | • Design classes on the Google Pay and Wallet Console • Customize Pass Visuals |
|  | Salesforce | • Create digital pass template and add parameters • Activate digital pass template |
| Runtime Execution | Salesforce | The Loyalty Management system performs these actions: • Triggers invocable action • Executes Flow upon member enrollment or voucher issuance |

Loyalty Management Integrate Loyalty Management With Google Wallet

|  | Environment | Configuration Actions |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  | • Creates digital pass |

Integration Prerequisites and Setup
Integrate Loyalty Management with Google Wallet, allowing a loyalty program to generate digital passes
and provide real-time membership updates to your members.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Developer, Enterprise, Performance, and Unlimited editions that have Loyalty
Management enabled.
Make sure that you set up these prerequisites before you integrate Google Wallet with Loyalty
Management.
•
Salesforce Org: A Salesforce org with appropriate permissions to configure Named Credentials.
•
Google Service Account: Create a Google service account to configure the required certificates and
ensure secure communication. See Service accounts overview.
•
Google Cloud Project and API Activation: Create a Google Cloud project and enable both the Google
Wallet API and the IAM Service Account Credentials API.In your project, under API & Services, go to
Enable API and Services, search for the APIs and enable them. See Create a Google Cloud project.
•
Role Configuration: Associate the Service Account Token Creator role with your service account email.
In your Google Cloud project, under IAM & Admin, go to Grant Access. Add your Service Account
email as the Principal and assign the Service Account Token Creator role. See Grant roles to service
agents.
•
Google Pay & Wallet Console User Access: Add your service account as a user with Admin access. In
Google Pay and Wallet Console, under Users, go to Invite a User. See Add multiple users.
•
Google Wallet API Issuer Account: Set up a Google Wallet API Issuer account to access the Google
Wallet API Dashboard in the Google Pay & Wallet console. See Google Wallet API onboarding guide.
Generate and Upload Java Keystore (JKS) Certificate
Use the Java Development Kit to generate a Java Keystore (JKS) Certificate. You need a service account
key in the form of a JSON file (.jks) to generate a JKS certificate. Import your certificates and private
keys from a keystore to Salesforce.
Create JSON Web Token (JWT) External Credentials
The external credential represents the details of how Salesforce authenticates to an external system via
an authentication protocol. To use named credentials, you must first configure an external credential.
Create Principals for JWT
After you create an external credential that uses JWT authentication, create principals for it. Use the
principal to grant access to your external credential.
Create Named Credentials

Loyalty Management Integrate Loyalty Management With Google Wallet
Named credentials offer a declarative and secure way to store and manage the credentials needed for
HTTP callouts so that Salesforce can authenticate with external APIs.

title: "Integrate Loyalty Management with Marketing Cloud Engagement"

Integrate Loyalty Management with Marketing Cloud Engagement
For more information about the data model, see the Digital Pass Template, Digital Pass, and Digital Pass
Template Parameter objects in the Loyalty Management Standard Objects.
Create a Digital Pass Template
Create a digital pass template to connect the static designs on Google's console and the dynamic data
stored in Salesforce. This template defines the static design and layout of the pass.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions
Available in: Orgs that have the B2C - Loyalty, B2C - Loyalty Plus, B2B - Loyalty, Loyalty Management -
Growth, or Loyalty Management - Advanced license enabled
11.. From the App Launcher, search and select Digital Pass Templates.
22.. Click New.
33.. Enter the name of the template.
44.. Select Google as the service provider.
55.. Select the type of template.
••
Use the Loyalty template to create a digital version of loyalty membership card that stores the
loyalty program member profile information.
••
Use the Gift Card template to create a digital version of the member's vouchers which includes
details such as voucher code, value, and expiry date.
66.. Enter the service provider template identifier which is the ID of the external template used by the
digital pass.
77.. Enter a description for the digital pass template.
88.. Save your changes.
Note You can add custom parameters to the template based on your business requirements. For
example, if you want to display tailored information on the digital pass, you can add custom
paramters.
Activate the digital pass template to make it available. After you create the template, the details include
the template status. If activation fails, the Status Reason field provides specific details regarding the
failure.
Integrate Loyalty Management with Marketing Cloud
Engagement
Keep your loyalty program members informed and engaged with content blocks and journeys.

Loyalty Management Integrate Loyalty Management with Marketing Cloud Engagement
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions that
have Loyalty Management and Marketing Cloud Engagement enabled.
You can use Marketing Cloud Connect to send data from Loyalty Management to Marketing Cloud
Engagement.
Use Loyalty Management Content Blocks in Email Templates and Template-Based Emails
Send personalized emails to your loyalty program members by using the Loyalty Management content
blocks.
See Also
Trailhead: Loyalty Marketing Campaigns
Use Loyalty Management Content Blocks in Email Templates and
Template-Based Emails
Send personalized emails to your loyalty program members by using the Loyalty Management content
blocks.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions that
have Loyalty Management and Marketing Cloud Engagement enabled.
Note Loyalty Management content blocks can't be used in multi-org Marketing Cloud Engagement
instances where the Engagement instance is connected to multiple Salesforce CRM instances.
Loyalty Management Content Blocks

| Content Block | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Loyalty Campaign Promotion | Send emails to inform members about their promotions. |
| Loyalty Tier Upgrade | Send emails to inform members when their tier is upgraded. |
| Loyalty Accrual Transaction | Send emails to inform members about the points they've earned for an activity. |
| Loyalty Redemption Transaction | Send emails to inform members about the points they've redeemed for an activity. |

Loyalty Management Integrate Loyalty Management with Marketing Cloud Engagement
You can add Loyalty Management content blocks to your Engagement org by installing the Loyalty
Management Content Blocks package. But before you install the package, perform these prerequisites.
Prerequisites for Using Loyalty Management Content Blocks
Before you complete the prerequisites for each Loyalty Management content block, ensure that you
import the data for these objects into Engagement by using Marketing Cloud Connect and synchronized
data extensions:
•
User
•
Contact
For each Loyalty Management content block, import data into Engagement for some object fields. We
also recommend that you import a few object fields for content blocks. The recommended fields are
ones that most companies are likely to use in their emails. Depending on your company's requirements,
you can also import data for fields that aren't mentioned here,
Prerequisites for Using Loyalty Campaign Promotion Content Block
•
Associate at least one promotion with a campaign.
•
Import these object field data into Marketing Cloud Engagement by using Marketing Cloud Connect
and synchronized data extensions:
Marketing Cloud Connect API user must be assigned the Loyalty Management permission set in the
Salesforce org. This ensures that the Loyalty Management objects are available to be added as data
sources for data extensions in the Engagement org.

| Object | Fields |
| --- | --- |
| Promotion | - Id - Name - CampaignId |
| Campaign | - Id - Name |
| CampaignMember | - Id - ContactId |

Prerequisites for Using Loyalty Tier Upgrade Content Block
Import these object field data into Marketing Cloud Engagement by using Marketing Cloud Connect and
synchronized data extensions:

Loyalty Management Integrate Loyalty Management with Marketing Cloud Engagement

| Object | Fields |
| --- | --- |
| LoyaltyTierBenefit | • LoyaltyTierId • BenefitId • Name |
| LoyaltyProgram | • Id • Name |
| LoyaltyTier | • Id • Name |
| LoyaltyTierGroup | • Id • Name |
| VoucherDefinition | • Id • Name • Description |
| Contact | Id |
| LoyaltyMemberCurrency | • Id • LoyaltyProgramCurrencyId • LoyaltyMemberId • PointsBalance (recommended) |
| LoyaltyProgramMember | • Id • ContactId • MembershipNumber (recommended) |
| LoyaltyMemberTier | • Id • LoyaltyMemberId • TierExpirationDate (recommended) |
| Voucher | • Id • LoyaltyProgramMemberId |

Loyalty Management Integrate Loyalty Management with Marketing Cloud Engagement

| Object | Fields |
| --- | --- |
|  | • TransactionJournalId • BenefitId • VoucherDefinitionId • VoucherCode • ExpirationDate • Status |

Prerequisites for Using Loyalty Accrual Transactions and Loyalty Redemption
Transactions Content Blocks
Import these object field data into Marketing Cloud Engagement by using Marketing Cloud Connect and
synchronized data extensions:

| Object | Fields |
| --- | --- |
| Contact | Id |
| LoyaltyProgramMember | • Id • ContactId • MembershipNumber |
| LoyaltyMemberCurrency | • Id • LoyaltyProgramCurrencyId • LoyaltyMemberId • PointsBalance (recommended) |
| LoyaltyProgramCurrency | • Id • Name • CurrencyType |
| LoyaltyLedger | • Id • TransactionJournalId • EventType • Points • LoyaltyProgramCurrencyId |

Loyalty Management Integrate Loyalty Management with Marketing Cloud Engagement

| Object | Fields |
| --- | --- |
| TransactionJournal | • Id • VoucherCode • MemberId • JournalTypeId • LoyaltyProgramId • Status • OrderId (recommended) • ProductId (recommended) • ProductCategoryId (recommended) • PartnerId (recommended) • UnitOfMeasure (recommended) • ReferredMemberId (recommended) • ReferredCorporateMemberId (recommended) • JournalSubTypeId (recommended) • PromotionId (recommended) |
| Product2 (optional) | • Id • Name |
| ProductCategory (optional) | • Id • Name |
| Partner (optional) | • Id • Name |
| UnitOfMeasure (optional) | • Id • Name |
| LoyaltyProgramPartner (optional) | • Id • Name |
| JournalType (optional) | • Id • Name |

Loyalty Management Integrate Loyalty Management with Marketing Cloud Engagement

| Object | Fields |
| --- | --- |
| JournalSubType (optional) | • Id • Name |
| VoucherDefinition | • Id • Name • Description |
| Order (optional) | • Id • OrderNumber |
| Voucher | • Id • TransactionJournalId • VoucherDefinitionId • ExpirationDate • Status |

Add the Loyalty Management Content Blocks
To add the Loyalty Management content blocks in your Engagement org, install the Loyalty Management
Content Blocks package.
Install the Loyalty Management Content Blocks Package
To add Loyalty Management content blocks to your Marketing Cloud Engagement org, install the
Loyalty Management Content Blocks package in your Engagement org.
Use Loyalty Management Content Blocks to Create Emails and Email Templates
Create emails and email templates that provide members personalized information about their
activities.
See Also
Salesforce Help: How to Use the File Transfer Activity in Automation Studio
Salesforce Help: Create a Data Extension in Marketing Cloud Engagement
Install the Loyalty Management Content Blocks Package
To add Loyalty Management content blocks to your Marketing Cloud Engagement org, install the Loyalty
Management Content Blocks package in your Engagement org.

Loyalty Management Integrate Loyalty Management with Marketing Cloud Engagement
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions that
have Loyalty Management and Marketing Cloud Engagement enabled.
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To install a package in Engagement: Marketing Cloud Administrator role
Loyalty Management's Marketing Cloud Engagement Content Block package is available here.
Important If the package doesn't install in your Engagement org, use this link: https://{Account
stack location}/cloud/#app/Administration/InstalledPackages/
1bc99e36-dccd-41bb-8c0d-e99128b83f8d/install . At the beginning of the URL replace
{Account stack location} with the location of your Engagement account stack.
11.. Paste the URL for the package into your browser navigation bar, and then press Enter.
22.. Enter your Salesforce credentials.
The Loyalty Management Content Blocks page under Installed Packages in Setup opens.
33.. Click Install | I Agree..
See Also
Knowledge Article: Find the stack location for a Marketing Cloud Engagement account
Use Loyalty Management Content Blocks to Create Emails and Email
Templates
Create emails and email templates that provide members personalized information about their activities.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions that
have Loyalty Management and Marketing Cloud enabled.
Before you use the content blocks to create emails, ensure that you've created the required data
extensions.
To preview and test email templates created using content blocks, create these sendable custom data
extensions:

| Content Block | Required Data Extensions Fields |
| --- | --- |
| Campaign Promotion and Tier Upgrade | • ContactId (Text data type) |

Important When previewing an email template, ensure that merge fields resolve the information
provided by sendable data extensions. When trying to preview Contact object fields, specify the
Contact ID that's mentioned in the sendable data extension and not the Contact ID mentioned in
Salesforce Platform. When previewing a person account record, specify the associated Contact ID
that's mentioned in the sendable data extension.
Complete these steps to create an email template using one of the Loyalty Management content blocks:
11.. From Content Builder, click Create.
22.. Click Email Template | From Existing Template.
33.. Select an email template, and then click Next.
44.. From the Custom section of the Blocks tab, drag a Loyalty Management content block onto the
content area of the editor.
55.. If you dragged the Loyalty Campaign Promotion or the Loyalty Tier Upgrade content block, select
these fields:
••
Loyalty Campaign Promotion: Campaign and Promotion
••
Loyalty Tier Upgrade: Loyalty program, Tier group, and Tier
66.. Click Next.
77.. Select one of these content types:
••
Rich Text: Allows you to add the text content for your email. For example, you can add Loyalty
Management field content as merge fields, added by the data extensions that you created.
••
Button: Allows you to add buttons to your email.
88.. Add text to your email.
99.. After you create your email, click Done Editing.
Note You can save an email as a template. Click Save as on the top right of the pane, and then
click Save as Template.
1100.. Save your changes.
1111.. Define your email template, and then save your changes.
Preview, test, and then save your email by using Journey Builder, Automation Studio, or Email Studio.
Integrate Loyalty Management with Service Cloud
Your service team members can create a case and populate the fields right on a loyalty member profile.

Loyalty Management Integrate Loyalty Management with Service Cloud
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Developer, and Unlimited Editions that
have Loyalty Management enabled.
Create a Case for a Loyalty Program Member
Service representatives can create a case for a member when interacting with them from the
member's Loyalty Program Member record. The case is automatically associated with the member's
Contact record.
Associate a Case with a Loyalty Program Member
You can associate a case with a loyalty program member.
Create a Case for a Loyalty Program Member
Service representatives can create a case for a member when interacting with them from the member's
Loyalty Program Member record. The case is automatically associated with the member's Contact record.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Developer, and Unlimited Editions that
have Loyalty Management enabled.
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To associate a case with a loyalty program Loyalty Management
member:
11.. In the Loyalty Programs tab, select your loyalty program.
22.. Select a loyalty program member.
33.. On the loyalty program member's profile page, click Cases, and then click New.
If the Cases related list isn't available on the Loyalty Program Member page layout, add it to the page
layout from Setup.
44.. The Contact Name and Account Name fields are prepopulated.
55.. Enter the case details.
66.. Save your changes.
After you create the case, the case is associated with the member's Contact record and isn't listed on the
Case related list of the member's Loyalty Program Member record. If you want to list the case on the
member's Loyalty Program Member record, associated the member and the case in a Loyalty Program
Member Case record.
You can add a member to a case from the Loyalty Program Members related list on the Case record or
create a Loyalty Program Member Case record.

---
title: "Deploy and Use Analytics for Loyalty"
source_pdf: "loyalty_management_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "925-965"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Deploy and Use Analytics for Loyalty
Associate a Case with a Loyalty Program Member
You can associate a case with a loyalty program member.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Developer, and Unlimited Editions that
have Loyalty Management enabled.
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To associate a case with a loyalty program
Edit on cases
member:
AND
Loyalty Management
11.. On the Case page, click Related. The Loyalty Program Members related list appears.
Note This list is not available by default. Navigate to Setup to enable it.
22.. Click Add Member to Case. The member case details page appears.
33.. Select the Loyalty Program Member. The case number is prepopulated.
44.. Save your changes.
Deploy and Use Analytics for Loyalty
The Analytics for Loyalty app gives you actionable insights into loyalty programs that help you devise
strategies to increase membership, retain customers, improve processes, and grow your business.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Developer, Enterprise, Performance, and Unlimited editions that have Loyalty
Management enabled.
Deploy Analytics for Loyalty
Before you create an app from the Analytics for Loyalty template and use the dashboards, deploy
Analytics for Loyalty in your Salesforce org.
Analytics for Loyalty Dashboards
Use Analytics for Loyalty dashboards to get actionable insights into loyalty programs that help you
devise strategies to increase membership, retain customers, improve processes, and grow your
business.
Einstein Discovery Apps
The Loyalty Einstein Discovery apps give you predictions that help you improve member engagement

Loyalty Management Deploy and Use Analytics for Loyalty
and increase your promotion revenue.
Loyalty Analytics Base App
With the Loyalty Management license, your Salesforce org is also provisioned with five user licenses for
the Loyalty Analytics Base App.
Deploy Analytics for Loyalty
Before you create an app from the Analytics for Loyalty template and use the dashboards, deploy
Analytics for Loyalty in your Salesforce org.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Developer, Enterprise, Performance, and Unlimited editions that have Loyalty
Management enabled.
11.. Enable CRM Analytics
Before you create an app from the Analytics for Loyalty template, enable CRM Analytics in your
Salesforce org.
22.. Assign Analytics for Loyalty Administrator Permissions
Allow admins to create and manage the Analytics for Loyalty app by assigning admin permissions.
33.. Assign Analytics for Loyalty User Permissions
Allow your users to view the Analytics for Loyalty app by assigning the relevant permissions.
44.. Data Requirements to Create the Analytics for Loyalty App
To create the Analytics for Loyalty app, make sure that your Salesforce data meets these requirements.
55.. Set Field-Level Security to Enable Creation of the Analytics for Loyalty App
Before you create the Analytics for Loyalty app, make sure that the Analytics Cloud Integration User
profile has access to all fields in the app.
66.. Create and Share an App from the Analytics for Loyalty Template
Create an app from the Analytics for Loyalty template and share it with your users.
77.. Create an App from the Predictions for Member Engagement Template
Create an app from the Predictions for Member Engagement template and share it with your users.
88.. Create an App from the Predictions for Promotion Revenue Template (Beta)
Create an app from the Predictions for Promotion Revenue template and share it with your users.
99.. Add Einstein Predictions to a Loyalty Member Page
To display predictions on the likelihood of member inactivity on a member page, add the Einstein
Predictions component on the Loyalty Program record page and configure the component visibility
settings.
1100.. Schedule Dataflow for the Analytics for Loyalty App
The app creation process includes a dataflow that imports the latest data to CRM Analytics. Schedule
the app to refresh every day to ensure that your app uses up-to-date data.
1111.. Delete Records from Trending Datasets
General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) requires you to delete customers' personal data when
customers request it, or when it's no longer necessary to keep. You can remove customers' data from
the Analytics for Loyalty app by deleting the records from a trending dataset.

Loyalty Management Deploy and Use Analytics for Loyalty
1122.. Understand Analytics for Loyalty Limitations
The Analytics for Loyalty app gives you access to most CRM Analytics capabilities and features, but
with limitations.
1133.. Embed Analytics for Loyalty Dashboards in Lightning Pages
The Analytics for Loyalty app includes dashboards that you can embed and access in Lightning
Experience pages.
Enable CRM Analytics
Before you create an app from the Analytics for Loyalty template, enable CRM Analytics in your
Salesforce org.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Developer, Enterprise, Performance, and Unlimited editions that have Loyalty
Management enabled.
Note If you see the Launch CRM Analytics button, Analytics is already enabled. You can skip to
Assign Analytics for Loyalty Administrator Permissions.
11.. From Setup, in the Quick Find box, enter Getting Started, and then select Getting Started under
Analytics.
22.. Click Enable CRM Analytics.
Assign Analytics for Loyalty Administrator Permissions
Allow admins to create and manage the Analytics for Loyalty app by assigning admin permissions.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Developer, Enterprise, Performance, and Unlimited editions that have Loyalty
Management enabled.
11.. From Setup, in the Quick Find box, enter Users, and then select Users.
22.. Click the name of a user with the System Administrator profile.
33.. Click Permission Set Assignments, and then click Edit Assignments.
44.. Select permission sets.
••
If you have the Loyalty Analytics Plus subscription, select both the CRM Analytics Plus Admin and
Loyalty Analytics Admin permission sets.
••
If you have only the Loyalty Analytics Base subscription, select CLAAnalytics Base Admin
permission set.
55.. Click Add, then save your changes.
66.. Repeat Steps 2-5 for all users who create and manage the Analytics for Loyalty app.

Loyalty Management Deploy and Use Analytics for Loyalty
Assign Analytics for Loyalty User Permissions
Allow your users to view the Analytics for Loyalty app by assigning the relevant permissions.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Developer, Enterprise, Performance, and Unlimited editions that have Loyalty
Management enabled.
11.. From Setup, in the Quick Find box, enter Users, and then select Users.
22.. Click the name of a user who requires access to Loyalty Analytics.
33.. Click Permission Set Assignments, and then click Edit Assignments.
44.. Select permission sets.
••
If you have the Loyalty Analytics Plus subscription, select both the CRM Analytics Plus User and
Loyalty Analytics User permission sets.
••
If you have only the Loyalty Analytics Base subscription, select CLAAnalytics Base User permission
set.
55.. Click Add, then save your changes.
66.. Repeat Steps 2-5 for all users who view the Analytics for Loyalty app.
Data Requirements to Create the Analytics for Loyalty App
To create the Analytics for Loyalty app, make sure that your Salesforce data meets these requirements.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Developer, Enterprise, Performance, and Unlimited editions that have Loyalty
Management enabled.
Your org must have at least one record in each of these objects.
•
LoyaltyLedger
•
LoyaltyMemberCurrency
•
LoyaltyMemberTier
•
LoyaltyProgram
•
LoyaltyProgramCurrency
•
LoyaltyProgramMember
•
LoyaltyTier
•
LoyaltyTierGroup
•
TransactionJournal
When you create the app, if your org's data doesn't meet the minimum requirements, you see a message
describing what to fix.

Loyalty Management Deploy and Use Analytics for Loyalty
Set Field-Level Security to Enable Creation of the Analytics for Loyalty App
Before you create the Analytics for Loyalty app, make sure that the Analytics Cloud Integration User
profile has access to all fields in the app.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Developer, Enterprise, Performance, and Unlimited editions that have Loyalty
Management enabled.
To successfully run a dataflow, users need field-level security permissions. To set Salesforce field-level
security to enable your Analytics Integration users to see all fields used in the app, take these steps.
11.. In Setup, go to Object Manager.
22.. In the Quick Find box, enter the name of the object whose field-level security you want to edit.
33.. Select the object, then select Fields & Relationships.
44.. Select the field that you want to edit, then select Set Field-Level Security.
55.. For the Analytics Cloud Integration User profile, select Visible, and click Save.
66.. Repeat Steps 5 and 6 for the fields that you want to use.
77.. Refresh your browser cache.
Create and Share an App from the Analytics for Loyalty Template
Create an app from the Analytics for Loyalty template and share it with your users.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Developer, Enterprise, Performance, and Unlimited editions that have Loyalty
Management enabled.
11.. Navigate to CRM Analytics Studio.
22.. Click Create, then select App.
33.. Select Analytics for Loyalty, then click Continue.
44.. Take a quick look at the preview page, then click Continue to open the configuration wizard.
55.. To create an app or use settings from an existing app, make a selection, and click Continue.
66.. Analytics performs a compatibility check of your Salesforce org's data. If it uncovers any issues, you see
error messages with instructions about how to address them. Fix the issues and try app creation again.
If it completes the check successfully, click Looks good, next.
77.. The next page of the wizard asks you to make two selections.
aa.. The first wizard question asks if you want to see tier history data in your dashboards. To see it,
enable field history tracking for the Loyalty Tier and the Reason for Change fields in the Loyalty
Member Tier object.
bb.. The second question asks if you want your program managers to also see the Member Services

---
title: "Loyalty Management Limits and Considerations"
source_pdf: "loyalty_management_4-7-2026.pdf"

purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"

Loyalty Management Limits and Considerations
•
The app supports only data from the standard Salesforce objects.
•
You can analyze up to 10 million rows.
•
The base app license doesn't provide your Salesforce org the permission to use Einstein Prediction
Builder, Einstein Discovery in Reports, CRM Analytics Platform, and Fast Start Templates.
•
Custom CRM Analytics apps can't be created.
•
Einstein Discovery isn't available for the apps you create.
Note Contact your Salesforce representative to subscribe to the Loyalty Analytics Plus license if:
•
You want to use the complete capabilities of Loyalty Analytics
•
Your org requires more than 5 licenses.
Loyalty Management Limits and Considerations
Loyalty Management provides a set of features and capabilities that enable companies to manage and
run business processes related to their loyalty programs. Each of your business processes can use
features such as Promotion Setup and Salesforce Flows, and different services such as business APIs,
Data Processing Engine, and Business Rules Engine. When planning your implementation of Loyalty
Management, make sure that you account for the Loyalty Management limits and considerations.
Loyalty Events
When you purchase a Loyalty Management license, event entitlements are granted upon contract
activation. You can use these granted events according to the contract length, without any daily or
monthly limits.
Feature Availability with Loyalty Management Licenses
Learn about the features that are available with each Loyalty Management license.
Loyalty Management Standard Object Access
The Loyalty Management data model comes with a set of objects and fields to manage information
related to loyalty programs, rewards, members, and member activities. The objects and fields available
in your org depend on the features included with your Loyalty Management license. Most of the
objects can be customized and extended based on your company's requirements but there are a few
objects that either can't be customized have restricted customization options.
Loyalty Management Process and Configuration Limits
Review the loyalty program-specific recurring process limits and loyalty program process rule
configuration limits.
Salesforce Platform and Common Feature Limits
Learn about the various Salesforce Platform limits and common feature limits that can impact your
Loyalty Management implementation.
Loyalty Management Feature Limits
Learn about the limits that loyalty program managers, marketing managers, and sales reps come
across while using Loyalty Management.

Loyalty Management Loyalty Management Limits and Considerations
Admin and Developer Resources with Loyalty Management Lite

with Partner Ecosystem Management.
Loyalty Events
When you purchase a Loyalty Management license, event entitlements are granted upon contract
activation. You can use these granted events according to the contract length, without any daily or
monthly limits.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Developer, and Unlimited Editions that
have Loyalty Management enabled.

Loyalty Management - Advanced

add-on.

| Event Type | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Accrual and Custom Events | An accrual event is an activity that creates a transaction journal associated with the Accrual journal type. A custom event is an activity that creates a transaction journal associated with any custom journal type you have defined. |

For more information on how usage is billed, refer to your contract or contact your account executive.

chunks.body:
---
title: "Data Protection and Privacy for Loyalty Management"
source_pdf: "loyalty_management_4-7-2026.pdf"

purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"

Data Protection and Privacy for Loyalty Management

| Capability | Invocable Actions | DATA PROCESSING ENGINE templates | Business APIs |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  |  | run in batches. |  |
| Tier Management | • Get Tier • Change Tier • Change All Tiers or None | Not Available | Not Available |
| Voucher Management | Issue Voucher Action | Not Available | Redeem Voucher |

Data Protection and Privacy for Loyalty Management
When companies use Loyalty Management to run their loyalty programs, they must comply with local
and global data protection and privacy regulations. As part of your company's loyalty program strategy,
decide how you want to delete, export, archive, restrict access, manage consent, and restrict the
processing of customer data stored as part of the loyalty programs.
Data Deletion for Loyalty Management
Delete customers' personal data when it's necessary to comply with data protection and privacy
regulations. Determine a plan of action for complying with the regulations that apply to your company.
Data Access and Export for Loyalty Management
When your customers request it, prepare and pack up the data you received from them to comply
with data protection and privacy regulations. Determine the best way to work toward complying with
the regulations that apply to your company.
Restrict Data Processing for Loyalty Management
When situations require you to do so, prevent the processing of your customers' data. We give
guidance to help you restrict forms of data processing. That way, you can work toward complying with
the laws that are important to your company.
Consent Management for Loyalty Management
Honor and respect your customers' wishes when they request only specific forms of contact from your
company or opt-out of certain types of data-sharing. We provide details to help you determine the
best way to comply with the data protection and privacy regulations that apply to your company.
Data Archive for Loyalty Management
Archiving lets you continue to store files in Salesforce without them being in a library or exposed in
search results.
Data Deletion for Loyalty Management
Delete customers' personal data when it's necessary to comply with data protection and privacy

Loyalty Management Data Protection and Privacy for Loyalty Management
regulations. Determine a plan of action for complying with the regulations that apply to your company.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience in Enterprise, Performance, Developer, and Unlimited Editions that
have Loyalty Management enabled.
Many data protection and privacy regulations can require you to delete customers' personal data when
customers request it or when it's no longer necessary to keep it. Examples of regulations that are
important to many companies include:
•
General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), European Union
•
Personal Information Protection Act (PIPA), Japan
•
Privacy Act, Australia
If you have customers or users who want the data that you collected on them deleted, review these
common requests and the procedures related to them.

| Common Customer Request | Actions to Consider | Things to Consider |
| --- | --- | --- |
| My loyalty program member wants to delete all the information related to activities and profile from the loyalty system. | Delete Records | Before you delete a loyalty program member, ensure that you delete the member's transaction journal records. You can't delete transaction journals from the User Interface. You can delete it only using API. Deleting loyalty program member removes all the related records from the loyalty member currency, loyalty member tier objects. It also removes related records from member benefit, loyalty program member promotion objects, and voucher objects. The contact associated with the loyalty program member isn't removed from the system. Optionally, you can delete the associated record from Loyalty Ledger object. |

Loyalty Management Data Protection and Privacy for Loyalty Management

| Common Customer Request | Actions to Consider | Things to Consider |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Loyalty Management logs or error data that includes public profile data and content. | Monitor Debug Logs | Logs don't contain any personally identifiable information (PII) or data. Additionally, log data is deleted automatically after 30 days. |

Note Deleting files moves them to the Recycle Bin, where files remain for 15 days before being
permanently, automatically deleted. Within 15 days of deleting a file, you can restore it from the
Recycle Bin.
Data Access and Export for Loyalty Management
When your customers request it, prepare and pack up the data you received from them to comply with
data protection and privacy regulations. Determine the best way to work toward complying with the
regulations that apply to your company.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Performance, Enterprise, Developer, and Unlimited Editions.
Some laws require your company to let customers take their data with them. Examples of regulations
that are important to many companies collecting and processing their customers' data include:
•
General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), European Union
•
Customer Contracts
If you have customers or users who want to take the data that you received from them, review these
common requests.

| Common Customer Request | Actions to Consider | Things to Consider |
| --- | --- | --- |
| My loyalty program member asked for the personal data we collected from them and the transaction activities in the loyalty program. | Export Data Build a Report | Consider which data format best suits your lead's needs. You can use Data Loader to download and share CSV files of a customer's data. See Data Loader Guide. |
| We received a legal hold on | Export Data | When you export this customer's |

Loyalty Management Data Protection and Privacy for Loyalty Management

| Common Customer Request | Actions to Consider | Things to Consider |
| --- | --- | --- |
| certain customer records. And a court order requires us to provide the data that's relevant to a specific customer. |  | data: • Retain a copy for restriction of processing purposes. • Provide a copy for the court order. |

Restrict Data Processing for Loyalty Management
When situations require you to do so, prevent the processing of your customers' data. We give guidance
to help you restrict forms of data processing. That way, you can work toward complying with the laws that
are important to your company.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Performance, Enterprise, Developer, and Unlimited Editions.
Some laws and regulations can require you and your company to sometimes restrict the processing of
your customers' data. We've listed a few of the laws that are important to many companies collecting and
processing their customers' data.If you encounter situations that require you to restrict data processing
for any of your customers, review these common requests and the procedures related to them.

| Common Customer Request | Actions to Consider | Things to Consider |
| --- | --- | --- |
| My company has received a legal hold for certain loyalty member records. | Export Data | Export the customer's data to retain it. You can also add notes for any other restrictions to processing until the legal hold is lifted. The implementation partner must ensure that they do not process inactive members. |
| My loyalty program member told me that the personal data we have on file for them is inaccurate. Because of those errors, they want us to stop processing their data until we've verified the data's accuracy. | Export Data Delete Records | Export the customer's data to retain it. Then, delete the data from Salesforce. Work with your customer to verify the data's accuracy. Then, get their approval to resume |

| Common Customer Request | Actions to Consider | Things to Consider |
| --- | --- | --- |

| My loyalty program member canceled their participation in the loyalty program. Although we no longer need the data, the customer asked that we keep it because they're involved in a legal proceeding that possibly needs the data. | Export Data Delete Records | Export the customer's data to retain it. Then, delete the data from Salesforce. |

•
General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), European Union
•
Federal Rules of Civil Procedure, United States
Consent Management for Loyalty Management
Honor and respect your customers' wishes when they request only specific forms of contact from your

Many data protection and privacy regulations require you and your company to honor people's requests
about how you use their data. We've listed a few of the regulations that are important to many

•

| Common Customer Request | Actions to Consider | Things to Consider |

The data privacy fields help you track a wide range of opt-out and privacy preferences. To reduce the chances of inadvertently revealing sensitive information, encrypt fields that contain personal information using Shield Platform Encryption. If any third-party integrations are within the scope of the request, pass the request along to those vendors. |
| A loyalty program member who lives in California canceled membership in the program, and asked us to delete their personal data. | Set Up Tracking and Storage of Certain Data Privacy Preferences Data Deletion for Loyalty Management Data Access and Export for Loyalty Management | Honor your customers' wishes when they request restricting or deleting their personal data. Look for customer data in other records, including Contact, Account, and Member. Offer customers an exported copy of their data before your company deletes it. If any third-party integrations contain the customer's personal data, ask those vendors to delete |

---

source_pdf: "referral_marketing_4-7-2026.pdf"

purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"

|  | Referral Marketing |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

Referral Marketing helps your brand run referral programs with evergreen and time-bound promotions
for your customers. Marketing managers can design and launch referral promotions within minutes.
Referral Marketing takes care of the rest with its inbuilt automations. The inbuilt automations track
referrals across their complete lifecycle, send targeted marketing emails, and reward brand advocates
and their referred friends. After the launch, marketing managers and business executives can track the
performance of the referral program by using prebuilt CRM Analytics dashboards.
To try the capabilities of Referral Marketing hands-on, get a Referral Marketing trial org.
Watch this video to understand how Salesforce Referral Marketing works.
Watch the video: https://play.vidyard.com/9HXuA6dzrm2TgTEjiRuJLy
If you aren't able to watch the video in full screen, open the video in a new tab: How Salesforce
Referral Marketing Works.

Learn About Referral Marketing
Learn About Referral Marketing
Learn about the key terms and roles in Referral Marketing. Explore the Referral Marketing data model
and the referral program workflow.
Key Roles in Referral Marketing
Learn about the different user personas in a typical Referral Marketing implementation.
Key Terms in Referral Marketing
Get familiar with the key terms that you come across while using Referral Marketing.
B2C Referrals Data Model
Learn about the objects in the B2C Referrals data model.
Referral Workflow
The workflow of a referral program involves two personas, an advocate and their referred friends.
Before you create a referral program, learn how advocates and their referred friends interact with the
referral program.
Considerations for Referral Marketing Implementation
When you plan your brand's Referral Marketing implementation strategy, you must account for certain
key considerations.
Key Roles in Referral Marketing
Learn about the different user personas in a typical Referral Marketing implementation.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing
Roles and Responsibilities in Referral Marketing

| Role | Responsibilities |
| --- | --- |
| Referral Marketing Admin | Performs the one-time setup tasks in the Referral Marketing org. • Provides marketing managers access to the Referral Marketing app and objects. • Installs the OmniStudio package. • Collaborates with the admin of the |

Referral Marketing Learn About Referral Marketing

| Role | Responsibilities |
| --- | --- |
|  | communication method to connect Referral Marketing with the Engagement business units. • Creates the Referral Marketing Analytics app. • Installs the communication method packages and provides API user access to Referral Marketing objects. |
| Communication Method Admin | Takes care of the one-time setup tasks in Engagement. • Collaborates with the Referral Marketing admin to create packages in Engagement business units to connect Referral Marketing with the correct business units. • Creates Synchronized Data Sources for Referral Marketing objects. |
| Marketing Managers | Launches and manages the referral program and its promotions. Marketing managers have complete visibility of the performance of the referral program and take care of its day-to-day operations. Marketing managers can track advocate engagement, referral conversions, and the program's impact on the company revenue. |
| Business Executives | Provides marketing managers the budget and approval to launch referral promotions for the brand. Business executives usually review the performance of referral programs and their impact on the brand. |

Key Terms in Referral Marketing
Get familiar with the key terms that you come across while using Referral Marketing.

Referral Marketing Learn About Referral Marketing
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing
Advocate
A person who refers or is willing to refer their friends to your brand
Friend
A person referred by an advocate.
Extended Reward
A tailored referral reward that's used to incentivize the advocate and their referred friend to join a
referral promotion. You can offer rewards like payout commission and gift cards.
Referral
A record that tracks the status of a referral that an advocate shares for a friend.
Referral Program
A program to manage and track the advocate referrals.
Voucher
A reward that's provided to advocates and their friends.
Promotion Template
A reuseable template that's used to create similar promotions.
Referral Promotion
A referral program can consist of multiple promotions. You can create referral promotions that
are evergreen or time-bound.
Transaction Journal
A record that tracks an activity of an advocate or of their referred friend.
B2C Referrals Data Model
Learn about the objects in the B2C Referrals data model.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing

Referral Marketing Learn About Referral Marketing
Objects to Design a B2C Referral Program
Use these objects to create a B2C referral program and its promotions.
Loyalty Program
Stores information about referral programs.
Voucher Definition
Stores information about voucher templates that are used to issue vouchers to advocates or to
their referred friends. Each voucher definition is associated with a referral program.
Promotion Template
Stores information about the reusable referral promotion templates that are used to create
promotions.
Promotion Stage Template
Stores information about the stages of a promotion template. A promotion template's associated

Referral Marketing Learn About Referral Marketing
promotion stage templates contain information on when advocates and their referred friends
are rewarded and the activities that rewards are offered for.
Promotion
Stores the configuration and detailed information of referral promotions. Each promotion is
associated with a referral program.
Promotion Stage
Stores information about the stages of referral promotions.
Email Template
Stores the content of the marketing emails that are sent to advocates and to their referred
friends for referral promotions.
Promotion Stage Email Template
Stores information about email templates that are used to send marketing emails to advocates
and to their referred friends at each promotion stage.
Loyalty Program Widget
Stores the details of a referral promotion's widget.
Segment
Stores information about a Data 360 segment. This object is available in orgs where Data 360 is
enabled.
Promotion Market Segment
Stores information about the Data 360 segment that consists of referral promotions' target
audience. This object is available in orgs where Data 360 is enabled.
Campaign
Stores the details of a campaign that consists of a referral promotion's target audience.
Internal Organization Unit
Stores the details of a Marketing Cloud Engagement business unit's connection with Referral
Marketing. Referral promotions are associated with a connected business unit. The marketing
journeys and emails of the promotion are automatically created in the associated business unit
when you activate the promotion.
Loyalty Program Engagement Attribute
Stores the details of a referral promotion's reward limit or daily referral limit for advocates.

Referral Marketing Learn About Referral Marketing
Objects to Run a B2C Referral Program
Use these objects to run a B2C referral program and its promotions.
Contact
Stores information about an advocate or referred friend.
Loyalty Program Member
Stores information about an advocate.

Referral Marketing Learn About Referral Marketing
Loyalty Program Member Promotion
Stores information about the referral promotions that advocates have joined. The count of active
Loyalty Program Member Promotion records associated with active referral promotions
determine the count of active advocates in the org.
Referral
Stores information about each advocate referral. Each referral contains the details of the
advocate who referred their friend and of the referred friend.
Transaction Journal
Stores information about the activities of advocates and of their referred friends.
Journal Type
Stores information about the type of referral activities that you create transaction journals for.
Referral Marketing provides a journal type named Referral out-of-the-box and you can use it to
track all referral activities. You can't extend or customize the Journal Type object or the Referral
journal type.
Journal Subtype
Stores information about the subtype of referral activities that you create transaction journals for.
Voucher
Stores information about the vouchers that are issued as referral rewards to advocates and to
their referred friends.
Product
Stores information about the product that a referred friend purchased.
Product Category
Stores information about the product category that a referred friend purchased a product from.
Loyalty Program Member Attribute Value
Stores information to track an advocate's progress towards reaching their referral promotion's
reward limit or daily referral limit.
See Also
Referral Marketing Developer Guide: Referral Marketing Standard Objects
Referral Workflow
The workflow of a referral program involves two personas, an advocate and their referred friends. Before
you create a referral program, learn how advocates and their referred friends interact with the referral
program.

Referral Marketing Learn About Referral Marketing
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing
Marketing managers in your company can decide when advocates and their referred friends are
rewarded. You reward the advocate or their friends only one time during the referral journey. For
example, you can reward an advocate when they refer their friends and reward advocates for signing up
with the brand. You can't reward advocates and their friends again after friends complete an activity.

| Who Does the Action | What's the Action | When Is a Reward Offered |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Advocate | Joins a referral promotion | A reward isn't offered to the advocate for joining the referral promotion. |

Referral Marketing Learn About Referral Marketing

| Who Does the Action | What's the Action | When Is a Reward Offered |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Advocate | Sends their referral link to friends via email, social channels, or offline. | • The advocate can be rewarded for referring their friends • The friend can be rewarded with a reward that they can redeem when they sign up with the brand. |
| Friend | Signs up by using the advocate's referral link | • The advocate can be rewarded for a successful referral. • The friend can be rewarded with a welcome voucher after signing up. |
| Friend | Completes an activity | • The advocate can be rewarded for helping the brand gain an engaged customer. • The friend can be rewarded with a discount voucher for engaging with the brand. |

Considerations for Referral Marketing Implementation
When you plan your brand's Referral Marketing implementation strategy, you must account for certain
key considerations.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing
Person Account
Referral Marketing uses both contacts and person accounts to store information about advocates and
their referred friends. Before you implement Referral Marketing in your company's org, we recommend
you to set up Person Accounts.
If you have a limitation on setting up person accounts in your org, you can still implement Referral
Marketing. The process of enrolling advocates and tracking referral activities differs from the standard

Referral Marketing Learn About Referral Marketing
procedure. For more details, refer to these sections:
•
Advocate Enrollment Without Person Account
•
Referral Activity Tracking Without Person Account
Active Advocate Count
With the Referral Marketing license, your brand can have 100,000 active advocates. An active advocate
represents a customer's active engagement with an active referral promotion. The active advocate count
denotes the number of customers that the brand is actively eng

and service representatives to track vouchers issued to advocates and their referred friends.

chunks.heading: 04-design-a-referral-program
chunks.body:
---
title: "Design a Referral Program"
source_pdf: "referral_marketing_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "54-123"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Design a Referral Program
The Subtype field identifies whether a record represents a B2B or B2C referral promotion. The Subtype
field comes with these values by default:
•
Business to Business-Added to the org by B2B Referral Management
•
Business to Customer-Added to the org by the Referral Marketing license
Promotion Page Layout
Update the standard and custom Promotion page layouts to include the Promotion Code field. In
Referral Marketing, marketing managers use a unique code for each referral promotion. The code
uniquely identifies each promotion and the promotion that the referral activities belong to.
The promotion code is optional in Loyalty Management. Loyalty program managers can use the code to
uniquely identify loyalty promotions.
See Also
Salesforce Help: Page Layouts
Design a Referral Program
Marketing managers can design a referral program and its promotions by completing a few simple steps
in the Referral Marketing app.
Here are the steps to design a referral program:

| Sequence | Task |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | Create a referral program |
| 2 | Add rewards for your referral promotion |
| 3 | Add advocates for your referral program |
| 4 | Create a promotion • Complete the prerequisites for creating a promotion • Design a promotion • Activate the promotion |

Referral Marketing Design a Referral Program

Referral Marketing Design a Referral Program
How Referral Programs and Promotions Work Together
Different dynamics come into play when a brand decides to run a referral program. The timing and the
messaging for the brand's referral program can vary depending on the target audience and the timing of
the planned customer outreach programs. Referral Marketing is designed to support the unique needs of
each customer.
A referral program can consist of multiple promotions. Each promotion can be tailored to the referral
program's various needs. Brands can run evergreen and time-bound referral promotions that are targeted
towards different sections of the brand's customer base or all customers.
Example Cloud Kicks wants to run one evergreen promotion that runs throughout the year and
offers a US$10 discount for the advocates and their referred friends. They also want to run two time-
bound promotions, one during the company's anniversary week and another on December 31 and
January 1. The marketing manager creates one referral program and adds three promotions to it.
The evergreen promotion has a start date but no end date. The company anniversary promotion
runs for a week and the year-end promotion's start date is December 31 and the end date is January
1.
Example Cloud Kicks wants to run a referral program for their advocates in France and Canada. For
France, they want to reward the advocates and their referred friends with a 10% discount voucher.
For Canada, Cloud Kicks wants to provide a 15% discount to the advocates and a 10% voucher to
the advocates' friends. In this case, Cloud Kicks can run two promotions, one for each country.
Example Cloud Kicks wants to run a referral program that consists of an evergreen promotion for all
its customers and a time-bound promotion for a specific customer group. For the evergreen
promotion, the marketing manager selects a start date but doesn't select an end date or a target
audience. For the time-bound promotion, the marketing manager selects a start date, an end date,
and a Data 360 segment campaign that consists of the promotion's target audience.
Explore and Learn
Watch this video to understand how to create a referral program and design its promotions.
Watch the video: https://play.vidyard.com/vzDMwuh97rQSay25RAojMp
If you can't watch the video in full screen, open the video on a new tab: Design a Referral Program.
11.. Create a Referral Program
The launchpad for any referral strategy is the referral program. After marketing managers create a
referral program, they can manage and implement all aspects for their referral strategy from the
referral program.
22.. Add Rewards
Referral programs incentivize the advocates and referred friends for engaging with the brand.
Marketing managers can design a range of rewards such as commission payouts, cashbacks, or gift
cards that appeal to the target audience. They can add multiple rewards types in a referral program

Referral Marketing Design a Referral Program
and offer different rewards to advocates and their friends. If a referral program includes multiple
promotions, each promotion can offer a unique set of rewards to both advocates and their friends.
33.. Add Advocates
Advocates of a referral program are the customers who are willing to refer your brand to their friends.
Marketing managers can either add all customers as advocates or add a select set of customers to the
referral program. If the number of customers to be added is small, marketing managers can add the
advocates one by one. Or, marketing managers can work with the admin to set up a process that adds
all the advocates simultaneously.
44.. Promotion Template
Promotion templates determine the configuration of promotions. In a promotion template, decide
stage names, the stages at which advocates and their referred friends are rewarded, and the activities
that rewards are provided for. Each promotion is based on a promotion template. The promotion
template decides the promotion's stage names, the activities for which the promotion rewards
advocates and their referred friends, and the promotion's email content. Before you create a
promotion, decide if you want to use an existing promotion template or create a unique template for
your promotion.
55.. Create a Promotion
Promotions are the primary vehicle that runs your referral program. Marketing managers can design
different types of promotions with varying duration, audience, rewards, and stages.
66.. Use Marketing Cloud Next for Outbound Engagement
When you set your communication method as Marketing Cloud, you must add Marketing Cloud Next
as an outbound engagement to send emails at different stages of the promotion journey. Emails at the
Promotion Launched stage are segment-based and uses Data 360 segments to target the right
audience. For all other stages, emails are event-based, that trigger a flow for content distribution.
Create a Referral Program
The launchpad for any referral strategy is the referral program. After marketing managers create a referral
program, they can manage and implement all aspects for their referral strategy from the referral
program.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create a referral program:
Referral Marketing permission set
AND
Edit permission on Loyalty Program

Referral Marketing Design a Referral Program
Tip If your brand uses the same org to run their loyalty program by using Loyalty Management, ask
your admin to add the Type and Subtype field to the page layout of the Loyalty Program object. The
Type field helps you indicate that the program is for a referral program. The Subtype field helps you
indicate whether it is a B2B or B2C referral program.
11.. From the App Launcher, find and select Referral Marketing.
22.. Click New.
33.. Enter the name, description and status of the referral program.
44.. Select the type as Referral.
55.. Select the subtype as Business to Customer.
66.. Save your changes.
Add Rewards
Referral programs incentivize the advocates and referred friends for engaging with the brand. Marketing
managers can design a range of rewards such as commission payouts, cashbacks, or gift cards that
appeal to the target audience. They can add multiple rewards types in a referral program and offer
different rewards to advocates and their friends. If a referral program includes multiple promotions, each
promotion can offer a unique set of rewards to both advocates and their friends.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing
Create Voucher Definitions
Marketing managers create voucher definitions that act as templates to issue vouchers. The values
that marketing managers add in a voucher definition are used as the values of the vouchers.
Create Extended Reward Definitions
Marketing managers can design various reward types for your company's referral program, such as gift
cards and cashback for both advocates and referrals. With extended rewards, marketing managers can
add multiple reward types within a referral program that resonate with the target audience.
Create Voucher Definitions
Marketing managers create voucher definitions that act as templates to issue vouchers. The values that
marketing managers add in a voucher definition are used as the values of the vouchers.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing

Referral Marketing Design a Referral Program
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create a voucher definition:
Referral Marketing permission set
AND
Edit permission on:
•
Loyalty Program
•
Voucher Definition
Marketing managers can choose the type of voucher definition from these options:

| Business Scenario | Voucher Type | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| A voucher offers a fixed value discount and must be redeemed within a month after it's issued. | • Type: Fixed value • Expiration model: Period | • Voucher name: Give $10, Get $10 • Description: Refer a friend and both you and your friend get a $10 voucher that's valid for a month • Type: Fixed Value • Face Value: 10 • Expiration Type: Period • Expiration Period: 1 • Expiration Period Unit: Months |
| A voucher offers a fixed value discount and must be redeemed before a particular date. | • Type: Fixed value • Expiration model: Fixed date | • Voucher name: Give $10, Get $10 • Description: Refer a friend and both you and your friend get a $10 voucher that's valid until November 30 • Type: Fixed Value • Face Value: 10 • Expiration Type: Fixed Date • Expiration Date: November 30 |
| A voucher offers a percentage discount and must be redeemed within 10 days after it's issued. | • Type: Discount percentage • Expiration model: Period | • Voucher name: Shop within 10 days and get 5% off |

Referral Marketing Design a Referral Program

| Business Scenario | Voucher Type | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  | • Description: Refer a friend and both you and your friend get 5% off on a purchase made within 10 days • Voucher name: Shop within 10 days and get 5% off • Type: Discount Percentage • Discount Percentage: 5 • Expiration Type: Period • Expiration Period: 10 • Expiration Period Unit: Days |
| A voucher offers a percentage discount and must be redeemed before a particular date. | • Type: Discount percentage • Expiration model: Fixed date | • Voucher name: Shop before December 31 and get 5% off • Description: Refer a friend and both you and your friend get 5% off on a purchase before year end • Type: Discount Percentage • Discount Percentage: 5 • Expiration Type: Fixed Date • Expiration Date: December 31 |
| A voucher offers a free product and must be redeemed within a week after it's issued. | • Type: Product or service • Expiration model: Period | • Voucher name: Give and Get Customized Laces • Description: Refer a friend and both you and your friend get a custom pair of laces free with a purchase made within 7 days • Type: Product or Service • Expiration Type: Period • Expiration Period: 7 • Expiration Pe
