chunks.heading: 05-configure-referral-processes
chunks.body:
---
title: "Configure Referral Processes"
source_pdf: "referral_marketing_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "124-149"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Configure Referral Processes
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing
Note The outbound engagements that you select for each stage are associated with one campaign.
All subsequent outbound engagements of the promotion are added to this campaign.
11.. On the promotion journey, click the + icon and select an email template.
The outbound engagement templates appear based on the promotion configuration.
22.. Select a template.
The Configure Outreach modal window appears.
33.. Enter the details of the campaign and click Done.
The campaign generates automated events that trigger a flow for content distribution.
Note You can create a campaign only when you add outbound engagement for the first time.
44.. From the editor canvas, preview, test, and then publish your email content.
For more information on email campaigns, see Send an Email Campaign.
55.. Customize the flow as per your requirements and activate it.
To view and edit the campaign details, click the View Campaign icon in the promotion journey stages.
See Also
Salesforce Help: Marketing Campaigns
Salesforce Help: Send an Email Campaign
Configure Referral Processes
After marketing managers create the referral program and add its promotions, the admin or the
developer can configure the everyday processes. The processes include advocate enrollment, activity
tracking, and voucher redemption.
Advocate Enrollment
After marketing managers activate referral promotions, the marketing manager, the admin, and the
developer can decide how they want the brand's advocates to join the referral promotions. For a
promotion, the brand can choose to either embed the promotion's widget on a site or use a business
API to process advocate enrollments.

Referral Marketing Configure Referral Processes
Referral Activity Tracking
The activities of advocates and their referred friends are tracked in a referral program. Rewards are
automatically provided to advocates and their referred friends when reward-eligible activities are
completed. You can use a Business API or a flow action to create records that track the activities of
advocates and their referred friends.
Voucher Redemption
Use the Redeem Voucher API to redeem the vouchers that advocates and their referred friends use.
Integrate the Redeem Voucher API with one or more of your external systems where vouchers are
redeemed. For example, you can integrate the API with the ecommerce site and POS system where
customers use their voucher.
Options to Extend Referral Marketing
Referral Marketing is prebuilt with a set of standard objects and an API to help admins and developers
extend Referral Marketing.
Advocate Enrollment
After marketing managers activate referral promotions, the marketing manager, the admin, and the
developer can decide how they want the brand's advocates to join the referral promotions. For a
promotion, the brand can choose to either embed the promotion's widget on a site or use a business API
to process advocate enrollments.
How Advocate Enrollments are Tracked in Referral Marketing
Each time a referral program advocate or a customer joins a referral promotion, the enrollment is
tracked by using a Transaction Journal record and a set of related records. An email is also sent to the
enrolled advocates.
Advocate Enrollment by Using Refer A Friend Widget
The Refer A Friend widget is available with every referral promotion and the marketing manager can
customize the widget based on the brand's requirements. The widget lets advocates join the
promotion and refer their friends. The widget also lets new customers sign up with the brand, join the
promotion, and then refer their friends. You can embed the widget on Experience Cloud sites and on
external websites.
Advocate Enrollment by Using Business API
Use the Referral Advocate Enrollments Business API to enroll existing and new customers in referral
promotions. Integrate the API with any site or external system where customers can join the referral
promotion. For example, you can integrate the API with your brand's ecommerce site or POS system.
Advocate Enrollment Without Person Account
When you don't have person accounts enabled in your org, the process of enrolling advocates differs
from the standard procedure.
How Advocate Enrollments are Tracked in Referral Marketing
Each time a referral program advocate or a customer joins a referral promotion, the enrollment is tracked
by using a Transaction Journal record and a set of related records. An email is also sent to the enrolled

Referral Marketing Configure Referral Processes
advocates.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing
Enroll Existing Referral Program Advocates
When a customer who is already an advocate of a referral program joins a referral promotion, these
records are created:

| Record Type | Details |
| --- | --- |
| Transaction Journal | Tracks the activity where an advocate joins a referral promotion. The transaction journal is associated with: • Advocate's existing Loyalty Program Member and Contact records • Promotion record • Referral program record The transaction journal is of type Referral and subtype Advocate Enrollment. |
| Loyalty Program Member Promotion | Tracks the advocate's participation in the referral promotion. The loyalty program member promotion is associated with the advocate's existing Loyalty Program Member record and the promotion that the advocate joined. |

Enroll Existing Customers
When a customer who isn't an advocate of a referral program joins a referral promotion, these records
are created:

| Record Type | Details |
| --- | --- |
| Loyalty Program Member | Tracks the customer's participation in the referral program as an advocate. The record is associated with the referral program record that the advocate is added to and the advocate's existing contact record. |

Referral Marketing Configure Referral Processes

| Record Type | Details |
| --- | --- |
| Transaction Journal | Tracks the activity where an advocate joins a referral promotion. The transaction journal is associated with: • Advocate's new Loyalty Program Member record and existing Contact record • Promotion record • Referral program record The transaction journal is of type Referral and subtype Advocate Enrollment. |
| Loyalty Program Member Promotion | Tracks the advocate's participation in the referral promotion. The loyalty program member promotion is associated with the advocate's new Loyalty Program Member record and the promotion that the advocate joined. |

Enroll New Customers
When a new customer who doesn't have a contact record in the org joins a referral promotion, these
records are created:

| Record Type | Details |
| --- | --- |
| Contact | Tracks the new customer's sign-up as a customer of the brand. |
| Loyalty Program Member | Tracks the customer's participation in the referral program as an advocate. The loyalty program member is associated with the referral program that the advocate is added to and the advocate's new contact record. |
| Transaction Journal | Tracks the activity where an advocate joins a referral promotion. The transaction journal is associated with: • Advocate's new Loyalty Program Member and Contact records • Promotion record • Referral program record The transaction journal is of type Referral and |

Referral Marketing Configure Referral Processes

| Record Type | Details |
| --- | --- |
|  | subtype Advocate Enrollment. |
| Loyalty Program Member Promotion | Tracks the advocate's participation in the referral promotion. The loyalty program member promotion is associated with the advocate's new Loyalty Program Member record and the promotion that the advocate joined. |

Send an Email to the Enrolled Advocate
If the marketing manager activated the Thank Advocates for Joining email for the referral promotion that
the advocate joined, the email is automatically sent to the advocate after they join the promotion. The
email contains the advocate's unique referral link that they can share with their friends.
The Remind Advocates to Start Referring reminder email is sent to the advocates who didn't refer their
friends yet if the marketing manager also activated the reminder email. The reminder email is sent after a
few days. The marketing manager decides the gap between the Thank Advocates for Joining email and
the Remind Advocates for Joining email when they design the promotion.
Choose How You Want to Enroll Advocates
Enroll new and existing customers and referral program advocates in a referral promotion in these ways:
•
Refer A Friend widget-The admin can embed the widget on an Experience Cloud site or an external
site. The widget also lets advocates send a referral email to their friends after they join the promotion.
•
Advocate Enrollment business API-The admin or the developer can integrate the API with an external
system where the brand's customers or advocates can join the promotion.
Advocate Enrollment by Using Refer A Friend Widget
The Refer A Friend widget is available with every referral promotion and the marketing manager can
customize the widget based on the brand's requirements. The widget lets advocates join the promotion
and refer their friends. The widget also lets new customers sign up with the brand, join the promotion,
and then refer their friends. You can embed the widget on Experience Cloud sites and on external
websites.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing

Referral Marketing Configure Referral Processes
Embed Widget on Experience Cloud Site
After the marketing manager customizes the Refer A Friend widget for a promotion, the widget must be
activated. Activated widgets are available as FlexCard components on the Experience Cloud site builder.
Admins can embed the widget's FlexCard on the brand's Experience Cloud site. See Add a FlexCard to an
Experience Cloud Page.
Embed Widget on External Site
After the marketing manager customizes the Refer A Friend widget for a promotion, the widget must be
downloaded. The widget is downloaded as a FlexCard Lightning Web Component (LWC) and the
marketing manager can share the downloaded files with the admin.
The admin can use OmniOut to embed the widget on an external site. To learn how to embed widgets by
using OmniOut, see Add a FlexCard LWC to OmniOut.
Track Referral Emails Sent by Advocates from the Widget
After a customer or an advocate joins a referral promotion by using the Refer A Friend widget, they can
send referral emails to their friends. To learn how advocate referrals are tracked, see How Activities are
Tracked in Referral Marketing.
An advocate can send referral emails to 10 friends at a time by using the Refer A Friend widget. The
program manager can set a limit on the number of friends that the advocate can refer each day. For
example, if the limit is five, the advocate can send the referral email to only five friends in a day. If the
limit is 15, the advocate can send the referral email to ten friends first and then send the email to five
more friends.
Watch and Learn
To learn how to embed widgets on Experience Cloud sites and external sites, watch Embed Widgets
See Also
Salesforce Help: Widgets
Advocate Enrollment by Using Business API
Use the Referral Advocate Enrollments Business API to enroll existing and new customers in referral
promotions. Integrate the API with any site

chunks.body:
---
title: "Manage a Referral Program"

purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"

Manage a Referral Program
Tip Before you use the LoyaltyPromotionSetup Metadata API to move the processes,
deactivate the promotion that the process belongs to in the source org.
See Also
Referral Marketing Developer Guide: Introduction to Referral Marketing
Manage a Referral Program
After advocates join the brand's referral promotion and start referring their friends, marketing managers
can track the progress of each referral.
Manage Advocates
After a brand launches their referral promotion, marketing managers can review the details of the
advocates who are part of the referral program and can add advocates to referral promotions.
Manage Activities
While marketing managers review the performance of a referral program and its promotions, they can
review the activities completed by advocates and their referred friends.
Track Advocate Count for Promotions
After an advocate joins a promotion, a Loyalty Program Member Promotion record is automatically
created to track the advocate's participation in the promotion. From the Loyalty Program Member
Promotion related list on a promotion, marketing managers can track the count of advocates who
have joined the promotion and their details. The marketing managers can use a promotion's advocate
count as a key performance indicator of the promotion. The Loyalty Program Member Promotion
related list also helps marketing managers find out the promotion's active advocate count.
Track Referrals
Referral records help marketing managers track the progress of an advocate's referral. When an
advocate's referral progresses across the promotion stages, the Referral record is also updated to the
current stage.
Track Issued Vouchers
Vouchers issued to advocates are associated with the advocate's contact and vouchers issued to
referred friends are associated with the friend's person account. Marketing managers can keep a track
of the vouchers issued to a person by viewing their contact or person account.
View Promotion Configuration
View the detailed configuration of a referral promotion with one click. Users with read-only access to
referral entities can quickly view the promotion configuration. Promotion owners can review the
promotion details and determine the changes they want to make without deactivating the promotion.
Plan Promotions by Using the Promotion Calendar
Marketing managers and business executives can visualize the brand's referral promotions on the
promotion calendar. The calendar lists the brand's referral promotions and events, so the marketing
managers can check whether promotions are planned during company and social events. Like any
other calendar, marketing managers can move across date ranges to see the promotions that are
running or that are scheduled. Along with the marketing managers, business executives get a high-
level view of the brand's promotions, easily find white spaces where new promotions can be added,

Referral Marketing Manage a Referral Program
and identify duplicate promotions.
Manage Advocates
After a brand launches their referral promotion, marketing managers can review the details of the
advocates who are part of the referral program and can add advocates to referral promotions.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To view advocates:
Referral Marketing permission set
AND
View permission on:
•
Loyalty Program
•
Loyalty Program Member
To add an advocate:
Referral Marketing permission set
AND
Edit permission on:
•
Loyalty Program Member
•
Promotion
•
Loyalty Program Member Promotion
11.. On your referral program, click Advocates.
To view the contact details of an advocate, click the advocate's contact.
22.. Click the customer number of the advocate whose details you want to view.
33.. On the Details tab, review the advocate's referral code.
44.. On the Vouchers related list, review the vouchers issued to the advocate.
55.. On the Transaction Journals related list, review the referral activities that the advocate completed.
66.. On the Loyalty Program Member Promotion related list, review the list of promotions that the
advocate joined.
77.. From the Loyalty Program Member Promotion related list, enroll the advocate in a promotion.
aa.. Click New.

Referral Marketing Manage a Referral Program
bb.. Select the promotion that you want to enroll the advocate in.
cc.. Select Enrollment active.
dd.. Save your changes.
The advocate is enrolled in the promotion that you selected.
Manage Activities
While marketing managers review the performance of a referral program and its promotions, they can
review the activities completed by advocates and their referred friends.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS

Transaction Journal

33.. On the Vouchers related list, review the voucher issued for the activity.
44.. On the Details tab, review these details:

| Journal Subtype | Provides information about the type of activity that the transaction was created for. |
| --- | --- |
| Program Member | Provides information about the advocate associated with the activity. The advocate is associated only with transaction journals of the subtypes Advocate Enrollment and Advocate Purchase. |
| Party | Provides information about the person who completes the activity. For transactions of the subtype Advocate |

Referral Marketing Manage a Referral Program

|  | Enrollment, the party is the advocate. For transactions of other subtypes, the party is the referred friend. |
| --- | --- |
| Referral | Provides details of the advocate's referral of a friend. |
| Promotion | Provides information about the promotion as part of which the activity was completed. |

55.. To view the activities of a promotion:
aa.. Go back to the referral program.
bb.. From the Promotions related list, click the promotion that you want to review.
cc.. Click Transaction Journals.
Track Advocate Count for Promotions
After an advocate joins a promotion, a Loyalty Program Member Promotion record is automatically
created to track the advocate's participation in the promotion. From the Loyalty Program Member
Promotion related list on a promotion, marketing managers can track the count of advocates who have
joined the promotion and their details. The marketing managers can use a promotion's advocate count
as a key performance indicator of the promotion. The Loyalty Program Member Promotion related list

Track Referrals
Referral records help marketing managers track the progress of an advocate's referral. When an

RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS

You can review these key details in a Referral record:
•
Referrer-The contact of the advocate.
•

•

•
Current Promotion Stage-The stage that the referral is at currently. The stage changes as the referral
progresses through the various promotion stages.

Vouchers issued to advocates are associated with the advocate's contact and vouchers issued to referred

View Promotion Configuration
View the detailed configuration of a referral promotion with one click. Users with read-only access to

Marketing managers and business executives can visualize the brand's referral promotions on the

calendar, marketing managers can move across date ranges to see the promotions that are running or
that are scheduled. Along with the marketing managers, business executives g

chunks.body:
---
title: "Get Predictions about Advocates’ Likelihood of Referring"
source_pdf: "referral_marketing_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "157-166"

purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"

Referral Marketing Get Predictions about Advocates' Likelihood of Referring
••
Start Date-To change the calendar's starting date to a date of your choice.
••
Period-To decide the period for which you want to view the calendar.
33.. To group promotions by their status, from Group Promotions By, select Activation Status.
44.. To view the details of a promotion, click the promotion on the calendar, and then click View Record.
If you update a promotion when you're viewing the promotion on the calendar, refresh the calendar to
view the promotion's latest updates.
Get Predictions about Advocates' Likelihood of Referring
Use Scoring Framework to get predictions about the likelihood of advocates referring their friends. Use
these scores to pursue the right members with the right promotions. Increase your customers, control
the customer acquisition cost, and reduce your contacts' displeasure with being pursued by focusing on
advocates with higher referral likelihood scores.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing
Create a CRM Analytics Template Configuration to Predict Advocates' Likelihood of Referring
Create and set up a template configuration in Scoring Framework to install a customizable CRM
Analytics app that generates predictions about the likelihood of advocates referring their friends.
Input Features to Get Predictions about Advocates' Likelihood of Referring
Select input features from a set of calculated values.
CRM Analytics App Assets for Advocates' Likelihood of Referring Predictions
The template configuration that you created to predict the likelihood of advocates referring their
friends installs a CRM Analytics app. The Advocates' Likelihood to Refer app template contains
preconfigured recipes that generate datasets, a model, and a dashboard.
Likelihood Score for Referral Marketing Model Card
The Predict Customer Likelihood of Referring model predicts the likelihood of referral program
members referring to their contacts. The model generates a score between 1 and 100, with 100
indicating the highest probability of referral.
Create a CRM Analytics Template Configuration to Predict
Advocates' Likelihood of Referring
Create and set up a template configuration in Scoring Framework to install a customizable CRM Analytics
app that generates predictions about the likelihood of advocates referring their friends.

Referral Marketing Get Predictions about Advocates' Likelihood of Referring
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create a CRM Analytics template configuration: AI Accelerator User AND CRM Analytics Plus
Admin AND Loyalty Analytics Admin
11.. In Scoring Framework, create a CRM Analytics template configuration that uses Advocates' Likelihood
to Refer (Referral Marketing) as the template configuration type.
22.. Select Contact as the object to train your model and get predictions for.
33.. To better analyze your data, include input features that are in a CRM Analytics dataset.
Ensure that the dataset includes both training and scoring data appended together, and includes a
field named "IsTraining" to distinguish between them (true for training, false for scoring).
44.. To calculate the order and order item features, select the datasets.
If you want to use the data from core Salesforce order and order items objects, don't select the
datasets.
55.. To predict the likelihood of referrals, select a referral program.
Select only one referral program.
66.. To create model features, select the training period in months.
Make sure that the selected referral program includes the data for the selected number of months.
77.. To calculate the target variable, select the number of months.
88.. Define the target variable for your model.
••
To define a predefined target variable, select Referral Count in Activity Duration.
••
To define a custom target variable, add conditions.
99.. Select input features from a CRM Analytics dataset, a predefined set of fields, or from the object that
you selected for training and scoring.
1100.. To focus your predictions on a specific subset of data, define filter conditions.
See Also
Salesforce Help: Create and Set Up a CRM Analytics Template Configuration for Predictions
Salesforce Help: Input Features to Get Predictions about Contacts' Likelihood of Referring

| Discount Percentage Voucher Count | The number of vouchers of the Discount Percentage type. |
| Expired Voucher Count | The number of vouchers with the Expired status. |
| Feature Duration First Half Average Day Count between Transactions | The average number of days between transactions in the first half of the feature duration. |
| Feature Duration First Half Case Count | The number of cases in the first half of the feature duration. |
| Feature Duration First Half Discount Percentage Voucher Count | The number of vouchers of the Discount Percentage type in the first half of the feature duration. |
| Feature Duration First Half Expired Voucher Count | The number of vouchers with the Expired status in the first half of the feature duration. |
| Feature Duration First Half Fixed Value Voucher Count | The number of vouchers of the Fixed Value type in the first half of the feature duration. |

| Feature Duration First Half Advocate Purchase Transaction Count | The number of transactions of the Advocate Purchase journal subtype in the first half of the feature duration. |
| Feature Duration First Half Network Referral Count | The number of advocates referred by the network in the first half of the feature duration. |

| Feature Duration First Half Signed Up Referral Count | The number of referrals who signed up in the first half of the feature duration. |

Referral Marketing Get Predictions about Advocates' Likelihood of Referring

| Input Feature Label | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Feature Duration Second Half Transaction Count | The number of transactions made by contacts in the second half of the feature duration. |
| Fixed Value Voucher Count | The number of vouchers of the Fixed Value type. |
| Is Referral | Indicates if the advocate is a referral. |
| Advocate Promotions Enrolled Count | The number of promotions an Advocate has enrolled into. |
| Advocate Purchase Transaction Count | The number of transactions with a transaction amount greater than zero. |
| Member Status | The status of the advocate in the referral program. |
| Member Type | The type of membership of the advocate. |
| Membership Duration | The number of days between the advocate's enrollment date in the program and the current date. |
| Network Referral Count | The number of advocates referred by the network. |
| Order Amount | The amount for the order. |
| Order Unique Product Count | The number of unique products for each order. |
| Product or Service Voucher Count | The number of vouchers of the Product or Service type. |
| Referral Count | The number of referred advocates. |
| Signed Up Referral Count | The number of referrals who signed up. |
| Transaction Amount | The amount associated with transactions made by contacts. |
| Transaction Count | The number of transactions made by contacts. |
| Contact Age | The age of the contact. |

chunks.heading: 08-get-predictions-about-non-advocate-contacts-likelihood-of-referring
chunks.body:
---
title: "Get Predictions about Non-Advocate Contacts’ Likelihood of Referring"
source_pdf: "referral_marketing_4-7-2026.pdf"

---

Referral Marketing Get Predictions about Non-Advocate Contacts' Likelihood of Referring
For example, a marketer opts not to send referral incentives-related information to a loyal customer
based on an inaccurate model prediction that the customer is unlikely to make referrals. This decision
leads to missed opportunities for the customer and impacts the effectiveness of the referral program.
Refresh Cadence
The model doesn't have an established refresh schedule, but you can schedule a score refresh based on
your requirements.
Questions or Comments
Send questions or comments about the model to mce-support@salesforce.com.
Get Predictions about Non-Advocate Contacts' Likelihood of
Referring
Use Scoring Framework to get predictions about the likelihood of non-advocate contacts referring their
friends. Use these scores to pursue the right contacts with the right promotions. Increase the number of
your customers, control the customer acquisition cost, and focus on contacts with higher referral
likelihood scores.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing
Create a CRM Analytics Template Configuration
Create and set up a template configuration in Scoring Framework to install a customizable CRM
Analytics app that generates predictions about the likelihood of non-advocate contacts referring their
friends.
Input Features
Select input features from a set of calculated values to get predictions about the likelihood of your
non-advocate contacts referring their friends.
CRM Analytics App Assets
The template configuration that you created to predict the likelihood of contacts referring their friends
installs a CRM Analytics app. The app contains preconfigured recipes that generate datasets, a model,
and a dashboard.
Create a CRM Analytics Template Configuration
Create and set up a template configuration in Scoring Framework to install a customizable CRM Analytics

Referral Marketing Get Predictions about Non-Advocate Contacts' Likelihood of Referring
app that generates predictions about the likelihood of non-advocate contacts referring their friends.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create a CRM Analytics template configuration: AI Accelerator User AND CRM Analytics Plus
Admin AND Loyalty Analytics Admin
11.. In Scoring Framework, create a CRM Analytics template configuration that uses Contacts' Likelihood to
Refer (Referral Marketing) as the template configuration type.
22.. Select Contact as the object to train your model and get predictions for.
33.. To better analyze your data, include input features that are in a CRM Analytics dataset.
Ensure that the dataset includes both training and scoring data appended together, and includes a
field named IsTraining to distinguish between them, true for training and false for scoring.
44.. To calculate the Order and Order Item features, select the datasets.
If you want to use the data from core Salesforce Order and Order Items objects, don't select the
datasets.
55.. To calculate the target variable, select the number of months.
66.. Define the target variable for your model.
••
To define a predefined target variable, select Referral Count in Activity Duration.
••
To define a custom target variable, add conditions.
77.. Select input features from a CRM Analytics dataset, a predefined set of fields, or from the object that
you selected for training and scoring.
88.. To focus your predictions on a specific subset of data, define filter conditions.
Input Features
Select input features from a set of calculated values to get predictions about the likelihood of your non-
advocate contacts referring their friends.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing

| Input Feature Label | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Days Since Contact Creation | The difference between the current date and the contact's creation date. |
| Case Count in Last 30 Days | The number of cases created in the last 30 days. |
| Case Count in Last 90 Days | The number of cases created in the last 90 days. |
| Transaction Amount In Last 90 Days | The total transaction amount associated with contacts in the last 90 days. |
| Unique Product Count | The count of unique products associated with contacts. |
| Transaction Amount In Last 30 Days | The total transaction amount associated with contacts in the last 30 days. |
| Transactions In Last 30 Days | The number of transactions made by contacts in the last 30 days. |
| Days Since Last Transaction Activity | The number of days since the last transaction activity by the contact. |
| Contact Age | The age of the contact, measured in years since the contact was created. |
| Transactions In Last 90 Days | The number of transactions made by contacts in the last 90 days. |
| Transaction Amount | The total amount associated with transactions made by contacts. |
| Case Count | The count of cases created over a lifetime. |
| Transaction Count | The number of transactions made over the lifetime. |

Prepare Orders and Order Items Dataset
During the template configuration, you can select an external Orders and Order Items dataset in the
Scoring Framework for feature calculations. Prepare the dataset that you want the app to use for
generating predictions.
Prepare Orders and Order Items Dataset
During the template configuration, you can select an external Orders and Order Items dataset in the

generating predictions.

Referral Marketing Get Predictions about Non-Advocate Contacts' Likelihood of Referring
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing
Ensure the dataset that you prepare for generating predictions includes these fields with the exact API
names:
Order Data

| Label | API Name | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Order ID | Id | A unique identifier for each order. |
| Bill To Contact ID | BillToContactId | An identifier for the contact that the bill is addressed to. |
| Order Amount | TotalAmount | The total monetary value of the order. Indicates the sum of all items included in the order. |
| Created Date | CreatedDate | The date and time when the order was created. |

Order Items Data

| Label | API Name | Type |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Order ID | OrderId | A unique identifier that links each item to its respective order. |
| Product ID | Product2Id | A unique identifier for the product that's being ordered. |
| Order Product ID | Id | A unique identifier for each order item. |
| Total Price | TotalPrice | The total price of the individual item in the order. Indicates the cost associated with each product item. |

When creating a dataset with additional features, include a column labeled 'isTraining' to indicate
whether the data point is part of the training set. Refer to the image for an example.
CRM Analytics App Assets
The template configuration that you created to predict the likelihood of contacts referring their friends
installs a CRM Analytics app. The app contains preconfigured recipes that generate datasets, a model,
and a dashboard.

Referral Marketing Get Predictions about Non-Advocate Contacts' Likelihood of Referring
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing
Recipes and Datasets
The CRM Analytics app installed based on the template configuration that you created contains these
preconfigured recipes.

| Recipe | Description | Output |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Get Contacts Example Dataset | The recipe creates a dataset that Einstein learns from by evaluating existing cases, unique contacts, and orders. It generates features based on the specified duration | Contact Example Dataset |
| Get Contacts Prediction Dataset | This recipe creates datasets that store input features by using data from contacts, loyalty program members, case, and orders. This dataset is used in the Get Prediction Dataset recipe to create the Prediction Dataset. | Contact Prediction Dataset |
| Get Referral Likelihood Predictions | This recipe creates a dataset that contains the predicted scores for the records. This recipe uses the Einstein Model to the Prediction Dataset to generate scores from 0-100, indicating the likelihood of referrals. | Referral Likelihood Prediction Dataset |

Modify the recipes for any of these circumstances.
•
Your schema deviates from the Referral Marketing schema.
•
A custom field of an existing entity changes.
•
The storage of feature data changes from an existing entity to a custom entity.
•
The data doesn't load properly.
•
The app stops working because of incorrect data values.

Work with Referral Marketing Analytics
Model
The CRM Analytics app installed based on the template configuration that you created contains the
Predict Contact Likelihood of Referring model. The Generate Referral Likelihood Predictions node of the
Get Referral Likelihood Predictions recipe uses this model to generate predictions.
Dashboard
The Contact Likelihood of Referring dashboard shows predictions generated by the Get Referral
Likelihood Predictions recipe and includes only the non-advocate contacts data.
The dashboard shows the average likelihood of contacts referring, the total contacts, the unique number
of contacts by state, city, and the likelihood of referring score. Use the charts to see the number of
contacts by state, prediction based on the duration since contact creation and the last transaction date,
and how the prediction scores vary based on the values for various transaction metrics.
Work with Referral Marketing Analytics
The Referral Marketing Analytics app provides insights into the performance of your referral programs
based on referral data, advocate participation, referral conversion, and promotion revenue.
Referral Marketing Analytics Dashboards
Use the Referral Analytics app to gain insights into your referral program performance, member
participation, referral conversion, promotion revenue, and channel effectiveness.
Referral Marketing Analytics Dashboards

participation, referral conversion, promotion revenue, and channel effectiveness.

revenue.

Referral Program Analysis

customer engagement patterns, the acquisition of new customers, and the generation of new
referrals. (5)
•

based on revenue, liability, and liability ratio. (6)
Build Solutions Based on Your Requirements
Every company has their own business needs and marketing strategies. Referral Marketing is flexible and
you can extend it to meet your business needs. Review these common solutions that you can build with
Referral Marketing.
Integrate Referral Marketing with B2C Commerce
Use a reference implementation to understand how to integrate Referral Marketing with B2C
Commerce.
Offer Gift Cards to Advocates and Friends
Integrate Referral Marketing with gift card solutions to offer your advocates and their referred friends
gift cards instead of vouchers.
Run Referral Promotions As Part of the Company's Loyalty Program
In orgs that have both Loyalty Management and Referral Marketing, marketing managers can run
referral promotions as part of the company's loyalty program. Your company can run a referral
promotion that's targeted towards members of the company's loyalty program. With loyalty program
members as advocates of your referral promotion, you can generate high-quality referrals from
customers who are already trust your brand. The referral promotion increases the engagement of
loyalty program members with the brand by offering members rewards for the referrals.
Integrate Referral Marketing with B2C Commerce
Use a reference implementation to understand how to integrate Referral Marketing with B2C Commerce.
Important The Referral Marketing for Commerce is a Non-GA Service. Such Non-GA Service is
subject to the terms and conditions of an existing fully executed Unified Partner Research
Agreement ("UPRA"), or in the absence of a signed UPRA, the UPRA available online at Terms and
Agreements.
Prerequisites for Integrating Referral Marketing with Commerce Cloud
Review the prerequisites before using the reference implementation of Referral Marketing's integration
with B2C Commerce.
Configuration of Referral Marketing's Integration with B2C Commerce
Admins are provided a cartridge that must be added to their B2C Commerce site.
Capabilities of Referral Marketing Integration with B2C Commerce
Provide your customers a single location to shop, to join a referral program, to pay with points and
vouchers, and to review their member profile and promotions.

Referral Marketing Build Solutions Based on Your Requirements
Limitations of Referral Marketing's Integration with B2C Commerce
The sample implementation of integrating Referral Marketing with B2C Commerce has some
limitations.
Prerequisites for Integrating Referral Marketing with Commerce Cloud
Review the prerequisites before using the reference implementation of Referral Marketing's integration
with B2C Commerce.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing
Before you integrate Referral Marketing with your B2C Commerce site, you must have:
•
A B2C Commerce site.
•
B2C Commerce Storefront Reference Architecture (SFRA) 6.2.0 installed on your B2C Commerce site.
Configuration of Referral Marketing's Integration with B2C Commerce
Admins are provided a cartridge that must be added to their B2C Commerce site.
Referral Program Prerequisites
Complete the prerequisites to prepare the reference org to run Referral Marketing-specific processes
for orders placed by members on your B2C Commerce site.
Prerequisites for Adding the Cartridge
Complete a set of prerequisites on the org that's provided and on your B2C Commerce site before you
add the cartridge to your site.
Referral Marketing for Commerce Cartridge
The Referral Marketing cartridge contains metadata and HTML pages that allow you to configure your
referral program preferences and run the program on the B2C Commerce site. The cartridge must be
added to your B2C Commerce site. You can add the cartridge only to sites in which SFRA 6.1.0 is
installed and Open Commerce API (OCAPI) settings is complete.
Configure Referral Program Preferences for B2C Commerce Site
After connecting your B2C Commerce site with the provided org and adding the cartridge, configure
the referral program preferences on the site.
Update Referral Program Labels on B2C Commerce Site
The cartridge adds pages and updates pages on your site. You can customize the default labels of the
new and updated pages based on your company's branding requirements.
Data Export from B2C Commerce to a Salesforce Org
Run jobs that export product-related information from your B2C Commerce site to the reference org
provided by Salesforce. Import the data about members who joined or left the referral program into

Referral Marketing Build Solutions Based on Your Requirements
Referral Marketing. View jobs by navigating to Operations in Administration on your B2C Commerce
site. Automate jobs based on your company's requirements in your production site.

orders placed by members on your B2C Commerce site.

chunks.heading: 11-manage-data-protection-and-privacy-for-referral-marketing
chunks.body:
---
title: "Manage Data Protection and Privacy for Referral Marketing"
source_pdf: "referral_marketing_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "197-204"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Manage Data Protection and Privacy for Referral Marketing

| Business Scenario | Things to Keep in Mind |
| --- | --- |
|  | Promotion records. If you want to view the advocates of a promotion and view the promotion's referrals, open the promotion from the Referral Marketing app. |
| View the vouchers that a member has received for their referrals | After a voucher is issued to a member for their referral, you can view the voucher on the member's Loyalty Program Member record and Person Account record. |
| View the voucher that a referred friend has received | After a voucher is issued to a referred friend, you can view the voucher on the friend's Person Account record. |

Manage Data Protection and Privacy for Referral Marketing
When brands run their referral programs, they must comply with local and global data protection and
privacy regulations. As part of your brand's referral program strategy, decide how you want to delete,
export, archive, restrict access, manage consent, and restrict the processing of customer data stored as
part of the referral programs.
Data Deletion for Referral Marketing
Delete the personal data of customers when it's necessary to comply with data protection and privacy
regulations. Determine a plan of action for complying with the regulations that apply to your company.
Consent Management for Referral Marketing
Honor and respect the wishes of your customers when they request only specific forms of contact
from your company or when they opt out of certain types of data-sharing. We provide details to help
you determine the best way to comply with the data protection and privacy regulations that apply to
your company.
Restrict Data Processing for Referral Marketing
When situations require you to do so, prevent the processing of your customers' data. We give
guidance to help you restrict the forms of data processing. That way, you can work toward complying
with the laws that are important to your company.
Data Access and Export for Referral Marketing
When your customers request it, prepare and pack up the data that you received from them to comply
with data protection and privacy regulations. Determine the best way to work toward complying with
the regulations that apply to your company.
Data Deletion for Referral Marketing
Delete the personal data of customers when it's necessary to comply with data protection and privacy

Referral Marketing Manage Data Protection and Privacy for Referral Marketing
regulations. Determine a plan of action for complying with the regulations that apply to your company.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing
Many data protection and privacy regulations can require you to delete the personal data of your
customers when they request for the deletion or when it's no longer necessary to keep it. Here are a few
regulations that are important to many companies:
•
General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), European Union
•
Personal Information Protection Act (PIPA), Japan
•
Privacy Act, Australia
If you have customers or users who want the data that you collected on them deleted, review these
common requests and the procedures related to them.

| Common Customer Request | Actions to Consider | Things to Consider |
| --- | --- | --- |
| An advocate of a referral program wants all information related to their profile and activities to be deleted. | Delete Records | Before you delete an advocate's data, ensure that you delete the Transaction Journal records associated with the advocate's Loyalty Program Member record. You can delete Transaction Journal records programmatically only by using developer resources like the TransactionJournal SOAP API. Deleting an advocate's Loyalty Program Member record also automatically deletes related information such as the advocate's enrolled promotions from the Loyalty Program Member Promotion object and issued vouchers from the Voucher object. The contact associated with the advocate's Loyalty Program Member record isn't deleted. |

Referral Marketing Manage Data Protection and Privacy for Referral Marketing

| Common Customer Request | Actions to Consider | Things to Consider |
| --- | --- | --- |
| A friend referred by an advocate to the brand wants all information related to their profile and activities to be deleted. | Delete Records | Before you delete a referred friend's data, ensure that you delete the Transaction Journal records associated with the friend's Contact record. You can only delete Transaction Journal records programmatically only by using developer resources such as the TransactionJournal SOAP API. Deleting a referred friend's Contact record also automatically deletes related information such as the friend's issued vouchers from the Voucher object. The Referral record associated with the friend's Contact record isn't deleted. |
| Referral Marketing logs or error data that includes public profile data and content. | Monitor Debug Logs | Logs don't contain any personally identifiable information (PII) or referral data. Moreover, log data is automatically deleted after 30 days. |

Deleting files moves them to the Recycle Bin, where the files remain for 15 days before they are
automatically deleted permanently. Within 15 days of deleting a file, you can restore the file from the
Recycle Bin.
Tip To learn how to delete data that's related to advocates and their referred friends in Marketing
Cloud Engagement, see Data Deletion for Marketing Cloud Engagement.
See Also
Salesforce Help: Data Deletion: Delete Personal Data

Honor and respect the wishes of your customers when they request only specific forms of contact from
your company or when they opt out of certain types of data-sharing. We provide details to help you

Referral Marketing Manage Data Protection and Privacy for Referral Marketing

company.

Many data protection and privacy regulations require you and your company to honor people's requests
about how you use their data. Here are a few regulations that are important to many companies who
collect and process customer data. If you have customers or users who request specific methods of
contact from your company, review these common requests and the procedures related to them.
•
General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), European Union
•
California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA), United States
•
Canada's Anti-Spam Law (CASL)

The data privacy fields help you track a wide range of opt-out and privacy preferences. To reduce the chances of inadvertently revealing sensitive |

That way, you prevent the data from repopulating into Referral Marketing. |

Learn About B2B Referral Management
Learn About B2B Referral Management
Learn about the key terms and roles in B2B Referral Management. Explore the B2B Referral Management
data model and the referral program workflow.
Key Roles in B2B Referral Management
Learn about the different user personas in a typical B2B Referral Management implementation.
Key Terms in B2B Referral Management
Get familiar with the key terms that you come across while using B2B Referral Management.
B2B Referral Management Scenarios
Effective referral management can occur through various channels, including customer and partner
networks.
B2B Referral Management Data Model
Learn about the objects in the B2B Referral Management data model.
B2B Referral Workflow
The workflow of a referral program involves two personas, an advocate and their referred party. Before
you create a referral program, learn how advocates and their referred parties interact with the referral
program.
Considerations for B2B Referral Management Implementation
When you plan your B2B Referral implementation strategy, you must account for certain key
considerations.
Key Roles in B2B Referral Management
Learn about the different user personas in a typical B2B Referral Management implementation.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, and Unlimited Editions with Partner Ecosystem Management -

Ecosystem management.

Referral Marketing Learn About B2B Referral Management
Roles and Responsibilities in B2B Referral Management

| Role | Responsibilities |
| --- | --- |
| Referral Partner Admin | Performs one-time setup tasks in the B2B Referral Management org. Provides channel managers access to the B2B Referral Management app and objects. |
| Channel Managers | Launches and manages the referral program and its promotions. Channel managers have complete visibility of the referral program's performance and handle its day- to-day operations. This enables them to track advocate engagement, referral conversions, and how the program impacts company revenue using their own analytics dashboard. |
| Business Executives | Provides channel managers the budget and approval to launch referral promotions for the company. Business executives usually review the performance of referral programs and their impact on the company. |

Key Terms in B2B Referral Management
Get familiar with the key terms that you come across while using B2B Referral Management.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, and Unlimited Editions with Partner Ecosystem Management -
Members or Partner Ecosystem Management - Login. Learn more about Referral Marketing with Partner
Ecosystem management.
Advocate
An advocate is a business that refers, or is willing to refer, another business to your brand. When

Referral Marketing Learn About B2B Referral Management
you use B2B Referral Management with Partner Ecosystem Management, your partners join the
referral program as advocates for the promotions.
Extended Rewards
A tailored referral reward that's used to incentivize the advocate and their referred party to join a
referral promotion. You can offer rewards like payout commission and gift cards.
Referred Party
A business referred by an advocate.
Referral
A record that tracks the status of a referral that an advocate shares for a referred party.
Referral Program
A program to manage and track advocates, reward definitions, promotion templates, and
promotions.
Voucher
A reward that's provided to advocates and their referred party.
Promotion Template
A reusable template that's used to create similar promotions.
Referral Promotion
A referral program can consist of multiple promotions. You can create referral promotions that
are evergreen or time-bound.
Transaction Journal
A record that tracks an activity of an advocate or of their referred party.
B2B Referral Management Scenarios
Effective referral management can occur through various channels, including customer and partner
networks.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, and Unlimited Editions with Partner Ecosystem Management -
Members or Partner Ecosystem Management - Login. Learn more about Referral Marketing with Partner

Referral Marketing Learn About B2B Referral Management
Ecosystem management.
The following scenarios illustrate how referrals can be generated through these channels:
Customer Referrals
Customer referrals occur when your existing customers recommend your business to other potential
customers, helping to drive new business opportunities. For example, Intel, a leading technology
company, has a strong relationship with Dell, a major computer manufacturer. Intel is aware that
Seagate, another technology company, produces high-quality laptop batteries. Intel decides to refer
Seagate to Dell, suggesting that Dell consider Seagate as a supplier for their laptop batteries.
Partner Referrals
Partner referrals occur when your existing business partners recommend your business to other potential
customers. For example, IBM, a leading technology and consulting company, has a strategic partnership
with Tableau, a data visualization platform. IBM also has a strong relationship with a major financial
institution. IBM recognizes that Tableau's data visualization tools could significantly enhance the financial
institution's data analysis and reporting capabilities. IBM decides to refer Tableau to the financial
institution, suggesting that they consider Tableau as an addon to their data analysis feature.
B2B Referral Management Data Model
Learn about the objects in the B2B Referral Management data model.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, and Unlimited Editions with Partner Ecosystem Management -
Members or Partner Ecosystem Management - Login. Learn more about Referral Marketing with Partner
Ecosystem management.
Objects toDesign a Referral Program
Use these objects to create a referral program and its promotions.

Referral Marketing Learn About B2B Referral Management
Loyalty Program
Stores information about referral programs.
Voucher Definition
Stores information about voucher templates that are used to issue vouchers to advocates or to
their referred party. Each voucher definition is associated with a referral program.
Promotion Template
Stores information about the reusable referral promotion templates that are used to create
promotions.
Promotion Stage Template
Stores information about the stages of a promotion template that are used to define when
advocates and their referred parties are rewarded and the activities that rewards are offered for.
Promotion
Stores the configuration and detailed information of referral promotions. Each promotion is
associated with a referral program.
Promotion Stage
Stores information about the stages of referral promotions.
Extended Reward Definition
Stores information about the extended reward templates that are used to issue extended
rewards to advocates or to their referred party. Each extended reward definition is associated
with a referral program.

Referral Marketing Learn About B2B Referral Management
Promotion Template Extended Reward Definition
Stores information about the promotion template associated with an extended reward
definition.
Campaign
Stores the details of a campaign that consists of accounts, which map to campaign members
who are the target audience of a referral promotion.
Loyalty Program Engagement Attribute
Stores the details of a referral promotion's reward limit or daily referral limit for advocates.
Objects to Run a Referral Program
Use these objects to run a referral program and its promotions.

Referral Marketing Learn About B2B Referral Management
Account
Stores information about an advocate or referred party.

Referral Marketing Learn About B2B Referral Management
Lead
Stores information about a potential customer referred by an advocate through a referral
promotion.
Opportunity
Stores information about a qualified potential sale that is actively being pursued. An Opportunity
record is created when a lead, referred by an advocate through a referral promotion, successfully
qualifies and converts into a sales opportunity.
Loyalty Program Member
Stores information about an advocate.
Loyalty Program Member Promotion
Stores information about the referral promotions that advocates have joined.
Referral
Stores information about each advocate referral. Each referral contains the details of the
advocate who referred a party and of the referred party.
Transaction Journal
Stores information about the activities of advocates and of their referred party.
Journal Type
Stores information about the type of referral activities that you create transaction journals for.
B2B Referral Management provides a journal type that you can use to track all referral activities.
You can't extend or customize the Journal Type object or the Referral journal type.
Journal Subtype
Stores information about the subtype of referral activities that you create transaction journals for.
Product
Stores information about the product that a referred party purchased.
Product Category
Stores information about the product category that a referred party purchased a product from.
Loyalty Program Member Attribute Value
Stores information to track an advocate's progress towards reaching their referral promotion's
reward limit or daily referral limit.

Referral Marketing Learn About B2B Referral Management
Objects to Reward Advocates and Referred Parties
Voucher
Stores information about the vouchers that are issued as referral rewards to advocates and to
their referred party.
Extended Reward
Stores information about the extended rewards that are issued to advocates and to their referred
party.
B2B Referral Workflow
The workflow of a referral program involves two personas, an advocate and their referred party. Before
you create a referral program, learn how advocates and their referred parties interact with the referral
program.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience

Referral Marketing Learn About B2B Referral Management
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, and Unlimited Editions with Partner Ecosystem Management -
Members or Partner Ecosystem Management - Login. Learn more about Referral Marketing with Partner
Ecosystem management.
Channel managers in your company can decide when advocates and their referred parties are rewarded.
You reward the advocate or the referred party only one time each during the referral journey. For
example, you can either reward an advocate when they refer a business or reward them when the
referred party signs up using the advocate's referral link. You can't reward advocates and their referred
parties again. You can only reward a referred party when they have an account record.

Set Up B2B Referral Management
B2B Referral Management Invocable Actions
To integrate your referral program with external systems, use the set of invocable actions available with
B2B Referral Management. The number of times that you run the B2B Referral Management invocable
actions counts against your org's invocable action limits.
Set Up B2B Referral Management
Set up capabilities that enable channel managers to design and run referral programs. Set up rules to
prevent duplicate records and protect against malicious activities. Enable channel managers and service
representatives to track referral rewards issued to advocates and their referred parties.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, and Unlimited Editions with Partner Ecosystem Management -
Members or Partner Ecosystem Management - Login. Learn more about Referral Marketing with Partner
Ecosystem management.
Role-Based Permissions for B2B Referral Management
Salesforce admins provide access to features based on the user's role in the company. For B2B Referral
Management, admins provide themselves, channel managers, and business executives the
permissions they require to work with referrals. Depending on the user's role, determine which
permission sets are assigned to users.
Matching Rules and Duplicate Rules for Business Accounts and Leads
When running a referral program, partners and their referred parties might attempt to sign up multiple
times. Create duplicate rules and matching rules to avoid creating duplicate accounts for advocates
and duplicate lead and account records for referred parties.
Add the Extended Rewards Related List to Business Account
In B2B Referral Management orgs, advocates have Account and Loyalty Program Member records,
while referred parties have Lead, Opportunity, and Account records. Channel managers can view
extended rewards that are issued to advocates and their referred parties on their Account and Loyalty
Program Member records. Channel managers can view issued extended rewards on Loyalty Program
Member records by default. To also view issued vouchers on Account records, add the Vouchers
related list to Account page layout.
Page Layout Customizations
If you enable B2B Referral Management, Loyalty Management, and Referral Marketing in the same org,
the admin must update the page layouts of a few objects that B2B Referral Management, Loyalty
Management, and Referral Marketing share.

Management, admins provide themselves, channel managers, and business executives the permissions
they require to work with referrals. Depending on the user's role, determine which permission sets are
assigned to users.

Assign admins the permission set that they require to administer B2B Referral Management in the org
and to complete the setup tasks.

Assign channel managers the permission set that they require to design and run referral programs.

B2B Referral Management comes with a set of objects to view and manage referral programs. Admins
must provide role-specific object access to users in the org. For example, channel managers need
permission to view, create, and edit referral programs and their related records whereas admins need
access to delete records as well.

Use B2B Referral Management to target promotions towards all partners or specific partner groups.
Filter accounts in the org by selecting all advocates or campaign that consists of the accounts you want
to target. Provide channel managers the permission to create campaigns in the org.

Assign advocates the permission set that they require to manage their referrals on Experience Cloud
sites

chunks.body:
---
title: "Design a Referral Program"
source_pdf: "referral_marketing_4-7-2026.pdf"

purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"

Design a Referral Program
Design a Referral Program
Channel managers can use a guided flow to design and launch a referral program and its promotions by
completing a few simple steps.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, and Unlimited Editions with Partner Ecosystem Management -
Members or Partner Ecosystem Management - Login. Learn more about Referral Marketing with Partner
Ecosystem management.
Here are the steps to design a referral program.

| SEQUENCE | TASK |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | Create a referral program |
| 2 | Add rewards for your referral promotion |
| 3 | Add advocates for your referral program |
| 4 | Create promotion templates |
| 5 | Create a promotion • Complete the prerequisites for creating a promotion • Design a promotion • Activate the promotion |

Referral Marketing Design a Referral Program

Referral Marketing Design a Referral Program
How Referral Programs and Promotions Work Together
Brands run referral programs to encourage their partners and existing business customers to refer new
businesses. The timing of these programs can change depending on who they want to reach and when
they plan to contact advocates.
A referral program can include many different promotions, each designed for specific purposes.
Companies can set up promotions that run all the time (evergreen) or for a set period (time-bound).
These promotions can target specific partner groups or all of them.
Example Cloud Kicks wants to run an evergreen promotion, offering a $1000 discount for their
business partners and their referred parties. They also want to run two time-bound promotions: one
during the company's anniversary month and another on December 31 and January 31. The
channel manager creates one referral program and adds three promotions to it. The evergreen
promotion has a start date but no end date. The company anniversary promotion runs for a month,
and the year-end promotion starts on December 31 and ends on January 31.
Example Example Cloud Kicks wants to run a referral program for their business partners in France
and Canada. For France, they want to reward the partners and their referred party with a 10%
discount voucher. For Canada, Cloud Kicks wants to provide a 15% discount to the partners and a
10% voucher to the referred parties. In this case, Cloud Kicks can run two promotions, one for each
country.
Example Example Cloud Kicks wants to run a referral program that consists of an evergreen
promotion for all its business partners and a time-bound promotion for a specific partner group. For
the evergreen promotion, the partner manager selects a start date but doesn't select an end date or
a target audience. For the time-bound promotion, the partner manager selects a start date, an end
date, and a campaign that consists of the promotion's target audience. Cloud Kicks launches an
evergreen referral program for its entire network of channel partners, the evergreen promotion
offers a standard 5% commission on all successful referrals.
Create a Referral Program
The cornerstone of a referral strategy is the referral program. After setting up a referral program,
channel managers can manage and implement all aspects of their referral strategy from the referral
program.
Add Rewards
Incentivize advocates and their referred parties to engage with your brand by adding rewards to your
referral program. Channel managers can include rewards like vouchers and payout commissions that
resonate with their audience. They can add multiple reward types within a referral program, tailored
for both advocates and their referred parties. If a referral program includes several promotions, each
can offer its own unique set of rewards to create a personalized experience.
Add Advocates
Advocates of a referral program are the partners who are willing to refer your brand to another
businesses. Channel managers can either add all partners as advocates or add a select set of partners
to the referral program. If the number of partners to be added is small, partner managers can add the

Referral Marketing Design a Referral Program
advocates one by one. Or, they can work with the admin to set up a process that adds all the
advocates simultaneously.
Promotion Template
Promotion templates determine promotion configuration. Use promotion templates to define stage
names, the stages at which advocates and their referred parties are rewarded, and the activities that
trigger rewards. Every promotion uses a promotion template. Before creating a promotion, decide
whether to use an existing template or create a unique one.
Create a Promotion
Promotions are the primary vehicle that run your referral program. Channel managers can design
different types of promotions with varying duration, audience, rewards, and stages.
Create a Referral Program
The cornerstone of a referral strategy is the referral program. After setting up a referral program, channel
managers can manage and implement all aspects of their referral strategy from the referral program.

Ecosystem management.
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create a referral program:
Manage B2B Referrals permission set
AND
Edit permission on Loyalty Program
Tip If your brand uses the same org to run their loyalty program with Loyalty Management or
Referral Marketing, ask your admin to add the Type and Subtype fields to the Loyalty Program
object's page layout. The Type field helps identify whether a program is for loyalty or referral, and
the Subtype field indicates if a referral program is B2B or B2C.
11.. From the App Launcher, find and select B2B Referral Management.
22.. On the Home page, click Create B2B Referral Program.
33.. Enter the name, description, and status of the referral program.
44.. Save your changes.

Referral Marketing Design a Referral Program
Add Rewards
Incentivize advocates and their referred parties to engage with your brand by adding rewards to your
referral program. Channel managers can include rewards like vouchers and payout commissions that
resonate with their audience. They can add multiple reward types within a referral program, tailored for
both advocates and their referred parties. If a referral program includes several promotions, each can
offer its own unique set of rewards to create a personalized experience.

Available in: Enterprise, Performance, and Unlimited Editions with Partner Ecosystem Management -

Ecosystem management.
Create Voucher Definition
Channel managers can create voucher definitions to incentivize advocates and their referred parties
with diverse rewards. They can add multiple vouchers to a referral program, offering different reward
types to advocates and their referred parties. Each referral promotion within a program can feature its
own unique set of vouchers, enhancing engagement and driving conversions.
Create Extended Reward Definitions
Channel managers can design diverse reward types for the company's referral program, including
payout comissions for both advocates and referred parties. With extended rewards, channel managers
get the flexibility to adjust reward values at runtime and control when rewards are issued. Use services
like Flows or Apex to define processes for integrating with external downstream rewarding systems.
Create Voucher Definition
Channel managers can create voucher definitions to incentivize advocates and their referred parties with
diverse rewards. They can add multiple vouchers to a referral program, offering different reward types to
advocates and their referred parties. Each referral promotion within a program can feature its own
unique set of vouchers, enhancing engagement and driving conversions.

Voucher Definition
Channel managers create voucher definitions that serve as templates for issuing vouchers to advocates
and their referred parties. The values specified in a voucher definition are used to generate the actual
vouchers
Here are a few business scenarios where channel managers can create voucher definitions to reward
advocates and their referred parties.

| Business Scenario | VOUCHER TYPE | EXAMPLE |

They decide to create a voucher that offers a fixed value discount and must be redeemed within a year after it's issued. | • Type: Fixed Value • Expiration model: Period | • Voucher name: Refer and Earn $1000 in Spare Parts Credit • Description: Refer a manufacturing company that purchases our automated assembly line, and both companies receive a $1000 credit towards spare parts. • Type: Fixed Value • Face Value: 1000 • Expiration Type: Period • Expiration Period: 12 • Expiration Period Unit: Months |
| A CRM software company wants to expand its market share by incentivizing existing partners to refer other businesses. They decide to create a voucher that offers a percentage discount and must be redeemed before a particular date. | • Type: Discount Percentage • Expiration model: Fixed date | • Voucher name: Refer a business before December 31 and Get 5% Off • Description: Enjoy a 5% discount on your next purchase by referring other businesses to our CRM |

| A Data Insights company wants to expand its user base and build a community of certified professionals by incentivizing partners to refer other businesses. They decide to create a voucher that offers a free certification course and must be redeemed before a particular date. | • Type: Product or service • Expiration model: Fixed date | • Voucher name: Refer a Business and Master Analytics • Description: Refer a business to our data analytics platform, and both you and the referred company get a free certification course in advanced analytics • Type: Product or service • Expiration Type: Fixed Date • Expiration Date: August 15 • Product: Advanced Analytics Certification |

---
title: "Configure Processes"

purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
