Configure Processes
Can I Reward Leads or Opportunities?
Rewards can only be issued to advocates and referred parties who have an Account record. You can
reward them at any referral stage if them have an account record.
Configure Processes
After channel managers create the referral program and add its promotions, the admin or the developer
can configure the everyday processes. The processes include advocate enrollment and activity tracking.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, and Unlimited Editions with Partner Ecosystem Management -
Members or Partner Ecosystem Management - Login. Learn more about Referral Marketing with Partner
Ecosystem management.
Advocate Enrollment
After channel managers activate referral promotions, you can use a flow action to process advocate
enrollments.
Referral Activity Tracking
The activities of advocates and their referred parties are tracked in a referral program. Rewards are
automatically provided to advocates and their referred party when reward-eligible activities are
completed. You can use a flow action to create records that track the activities of advocates and their
referred parties.
Advocate Enrollment
After channel managers activate referral promotions, you can use a flow action to process advocate
enrollments.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, and Unlimited Editions with Partner Ecosystem Management -
Members or Partner Ecosystem Management - Login. Learn more about Referral Marketing with Partner
Ecosystem management.

Referral Marketing Configure Processes
How Advocate Enrollment are Tracked in B2B Referral Management
Each time a referral program advocate or a partner joins a referral promotion, the enrollment is
tracked by using a Transaction Journal record and a set of related records.
Enroll Advocate in B2B Referral Promotion Action
Use the Enroll Advocate in B2B Referral Promotion Enrollment action to enroll business account as an
advocate if B2B referral promotions. Integrate the action with external system or site where partners
can join the referral promotion.
How Advocate Enrollment are Tracked in B2B Referral Management
Each time a referral program advocate or a partner joins a referral promotion, the enrollment is tracked
by using a Transaction Journal record and a set of related records.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, and Unlimited Editions with Partner Ecosystem Management -
Members or Partner Ecosystem Management - Login. Learn more about Referral Marketing with Partner
Ecosystem management.
Enroll Existing Referral Program Advocates
When a customer who is already an advocate of a referral program joins a referral promotion, these
records are created:

| RECORD TYPE | DETAILS |
| --- | --- |
| Transaction Journal | Tracks the activity where an advocate joins a referral promotion. The transaction journal is associated with: • Advocate's existing Loyalty Program Member and Account records • Promotion record • Referral program record The transaction journal is of type Referral and subtype Advocate Enrollment. |
| Loyalty Program Member Promotion | Tracks the advocate's participation in the referral promotion. The loyalty program member promotion is associated with the advocate's |

Referral Marketing Configure Processes

| RECORD TYPE | DETAILS |
| --- | --- |
|  | existing Loyalty Program Member record and the promotion that the advocate joined. |

Enroll Existing Partners
When a partner who isn't an advocate of a referral program joins a referral promotion, these records are
created:

| RECORD TYPE | DETAILS |
| --- | --- |
| Loyalty Program Member | Tracks the partner's participation in the referral program as an advocate. The record is associated with the referral program record that the advocate is added to and the advocate's existing account record. |
| Transaction Journal | Tracks the activity where an advocate joins a referral promotion. The transaction journal is associated with: • Advocate's new Loyalty Program Member record and existing Account record • Promotion record • Referral program record The transaction journal is of type Referral and subtype Advocate Enrollment. |
| Loyalty Program Member Promotion | Tracks the advocate's participation in the referral promotion. The loyalty program member promotion is associated with the advocate's new Loyalty Program Member record and the promotion that the advocate joined. |

Enroll New Partners
When a new customer who doesn't have a contact record in the org joins a referral promotion, these
records are created:

| Record Type | Details |
| --- | --- |
| Account | Tracks the new partner's enrollment as a partner of the brand. |

Referral Marketing Configure Processes

| Record Type | Details |
| --- | --- |
| Loyalty Program Member | Tracks the partner's participation in the referral program as an advocate. The loyalty program member is associated with the referral program that the advocate is added to and the advocate's new contact record. |
| Transaction Journal | Tracks the activity where an advocate joins a referral promotion. The transaction journal is associated with: • Advocate's new Loyalty Program Member and Account records • Promotion record • Referral program record The transaction journal is of type Referral and subtype Advocate Enrollment. |
| Loyalty Program Member Promotion | Tracks the advocate's participation in the referral promotion. The loyalty program member promotion is associated with the advocate's new Loyalty Program Member record and the promotion that the advocate joined. |

Enroll Advocates
You can use the Advocate Enrollment action to enroll partners as advocates in a referral promotion. The
admin or the developer can add the action to an existing business process. The action is available for
admins in Flow Builder, and developers can access it as an invocable action or from Apex.
Enroll Advocate in B2B Referral Promotion Action
Use the Enroll Advocate in B2B Referral Promotion Enrollment action to enroll business account as an
advocate if B2B referral promotions. Integrate the action with external system or site where partners can
join the referral promotion.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, and Unlimited Editions with Partner Ecosystem Management -

Referral Marketing Configure Processes
Members or Partner Ecosystem Management - Login. Learn more about Referral Marketing with Partner
Ecosystem management.
Learn About the Enroll Advocate in B2B Referral Promotion Action
To learn about the Enroll Advocate in B2B Referral Promotion Action's resource, request body, and
response body, see Referral Advocate Enrollments in the Referral Marketing Developer Guide.
Enroll Existing Referral Program Advocates
To enroll an advocate of your referral program in a promotion, run the action with the membership
number of the advocate. The advocate's membership number is stored in the Membership Number field
on the advocate's Loyalty Program Member record.
Enroll Existing Partners
To enroll an partner who isn't an advocate of your referral program in a promotion, run the action with
the ID of the partner's account record.
Referral Activity Tracking
The activities of advocates and their referred parties are tracked in a referral program. Rewards are
automatically provided to advocates and their referred party when reward-eligible activities are
completed. You can use a flow action to create records that track the activities of advocates and their
referred parties.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, and Unlimited Editions with Partner Ecosystem Management -
Members or Partner Ecosystem Management - Login. Learn more about Referral Marketing with Partner
Ecosystem management.
How Activities are Tracked in B2B Referral Management
A referral promotion must track activities that advocates and their referred parties complete through
the promotion's stages. For activities in each stage, a set of records is created or updated.
Process B2B Referral Event Action
Use the Process B2B Referral Event action in Flow Builder or through Connect API to create tracking
records for referral activities that advocates and their referred party complete during the last three
stages of promotions. The action also automates the issuing of rewards after advocates and their

Referral Marketing Configure Processes
referred party complete eligible activities. For each activity, a transaction journal is created and other
records are created or updated. Use the additional elements in Flow Builder to decide the eligibility
criteria for referral activities. You can also add follow-up actions after the Process B2B Referral action.
Example Flows to Track B2B Referrals Across Sales Lifecycle
Design flows that progressively track B2B referrals provided by customers and partners as referred
businesses advance through sales lifecycle stages. For example, the flow can track the progression of
referrals from lead generation to opportunity closure or from opportunity generation to first order
fulfillment. B2B Referral Management provides businesses the flexibility to plug their referral programs
in any type of B2B sales lifecyle. In this example, two reference flows are designed to visualize how
partner referrals are tracked across the referral lifecycle during the lead to opportunity sales lifecycle
and how partners are paid out for successful referrals.
How Activities are Tracked in B2B Referral Management
A referral promotion must track activities that advocates and their referred parties complete through the
promotion's stages. For activities in each stage, a set of records is created or updated.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer Editions with Referral Marketing
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, and Unlimited Editions with Partner Ecosystem Management -
Members or Partner Ecosystem Management - Login. Learn more about Referral Marketing with Partner
Ecosystem management.
Understand the records created or updated when you create a referral promotion with the Refer a Lead
promotion template.
Stage 3
In the third stage of a promotion, records are created to track the activities of advocates and their
referred party based on whether advocates and their referred party are rewarded. In most promotions,
advocates refer businesses at this stage.

| Scenario | Records Created |
| --- | --- |
| Advocates and their referred parties aren't rewarded. | • Lead-A record is created for the referred party as a potential business customer. • Referral-A record is created to track the advocate's referral of the referred party. The record is associated with: - Advocate's account record |

Referral Marketing Configure Processes

| Scenario | Records Created |
| --- | --- |
|  | - Referred Party

Manage a B2B Referral Program
After advocates join the brand's referral promotion and start referring another businesses, channel
managers can track the progress of each referral.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS

Ecosystem management.
Manage Advocates
After a brand launches its referral promotion, channel managers can review the details of the
advocates who are part of the referral program and can add advocates to referral promotions.
Manage Activities
While channel managers review the performance of a referral program and its promotions, they can
review the activities completed by advocates and their referred parties.
Track Advocate Count for Promotions
When an advocate joins a promotion, a Loyalty Program Member Promotion record is automatically
created to track their participation. From the Loyalty Program Member Promotion related list on a
promotion, channel managers can track the count of advocates who have joined the promotion, the
active advocate count, and their details. channel managers can use a promotion's advocate count as a
key performance indicator of the promotion.
Track Referrals
Referral records help channel managers track the progress of an advocate's referral. When an
advocate's referral progresses across the promotion stages, the Referral record is also updated to the
current stage.
Track Issued Vouchers
Vouchers issued to advocates are associated with the advocate's loyalty program member record.
Channel managers can keep track of the vouchers issued to an advocate by viewing their loyalty
program member record.
Track Issued Extended Rewards
Extended rewards issued to advocates are associated with the advocate's account and extended
rewards issued to referred parties are associated with the referred party's account. channel managers
can keep a track of the extended rewards issued to a person by viewing their account.
View Promotion Configuration

Referral Marketing Manage a B2B Referral Program
View the detailed configuration of a referral promotion with one click. Users with read-only access to
referral entities can quickly view the promotion configuration. Promotion owners can review the
promotion details and determine the changes they want to make without deactivating the promotion.
Manage Advocates
After a brand launches its referral promotion, channel managers can review the details of the advocates
who are part of the referral program and can add advocates to referral promotions.

chunks.heading: 02-marketing-cloud-next-for-account-engagement
chunks.body:
---
title: "Marketing Cloud Next for Account Engagement"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_account_engagement_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "7-33"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Marketing Cloud Next for Account Engagement
Get Started with Marketing Cloud Next for Account
Engagement
With Marketing Cloud Next for Account Engagement, you gain access to next-generation features while
keeping the campaigns, assets, and engagement programs that you've created over the years. These
resources outline some of the latest offerings and other recommendations from across Salesforce.

| Get Started • Web Page: Small Business Marketing with Marketing Cloud Growth and Advanced Editions • Video: Welcome to Marketing Cloud Next • Trailhead: Email Marketing with Marketing Cloud Growth or Salesforce Starter • Trailhead: Lead Generation with Marketing Cloud Signup Forms • Webinar: Grow Your Small Business Marketing Footprint with AI | Realize Value • Blog: How Account Engagement Customers Can Access New Functionality • Video: Set Up Marketing Cloud • PDF: Marketing Cloud Growth Deskside Reference • PDF: Marketing Cloud Setup Checklist • PDF: Marketing Cloud Implementation Guide • Trailhead: Data 360 Quick Look • Video: Introduction to Data 360 • Salesforce Help: Marketing Cloud Quick Start Guides | Optimize • Prepare for a New Campaign Experience with Marketing Cloud Features for Account Engagement • Prerequisites for Using Data 360 • Blog: What Is Marketing Cloud Advanced Edition? • Video: Dreamforce 2024: Three Ways To Personalize Journeys with Data |
| --- | --- | --- |

Key Concepts
Before you start using Marketing Cloud campaign features, get familiar with key concepts and how
functionality differs between Account Engagement and Marketing Cloud.
Connecting Account Engagement and Data 360
Data 360 organizes and unifies data across Salesforce and other external data sources. To drive
calculated insights and build segments, ingest Account Engagement data into Data 360.
Manually Match Consent and Subscriptions Between Account Engagement and Marketing Cloud
Next
Marketing Cloud Next consent management is powered by Data 360 so that you can manage multiple
marketing channels with subscriptions. By default, consent isn't shared between Account Engagement
and Marketing Cloud Next. To use your prospect consent preferences in Marketing Cloud Next, export

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Get Started with Marketing Cloud Next for Account Engagement
them from Account Engagement or API, and then import them into Marketing Cloud Next, one
subscription at a time. The resulting output file includes prospects that need a corresponding
subscription in Marketing Cloud Next. We recommend using a small, simple subscription to start.
Build Segments and Analytics with Data 360
To drive calculated insights, build segments, and get the most out of other Data 360 features, use
Account Engagement data with Data 360.
Prepare Account Engagement Content for Campaigns in Marketing Cloud
Reuse your Account Engagement content in Marketing Cloud campaigns by copying assets to
Salesforce CMS. Marketing Cloud allows users to share content across CMS workspaces from a source
workspace in the Shared with Workspaces folder.
Create a Campaign and View Performance in Marketing Cloud Next
Marketing Cloud Next campaigns provide a centralized hub where you can design, automate, and
track your marketing efforts. When creating a campaign in Marketing Cloud Next, you select a
preconfigured flow to start with, such as a single email or signup form.
Quick Start: Set Up WhatsApp for Account Engagement
This Quick Start guide covers the steps that are required to set up WhatsApp for Marketing Cloud
Account Engagement. It outlines the most direct implementation, to get your team get up and
running.
Key Concepts
Before you start using Marketing Cloud campaign features, get familiar with key concepts and how
functionality differs between Account Engagement and Marketing Cloud.

| Concept | How It Works | Related Tasks | For More Information |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Data 360 | Data 360 organizes and unifies data across Salesforce and external data sources. It ensures data continuity between Account Engagement and Marketing Cloud. | Prerequisites for Using Data 360 | Trailhead: Get to Know Data 360 About Salesforce Data 360 |
| Prospects and Unified Individuals | You can connect and sync prospect and email engagement data from Account Engagement to Data 360. However, the concept of a prospect in Account Engagement doesn't directly translate to Marketing | Prerequisites for Using Data 360 | Trailhead: Data and Identity in Data 360 Unify Source Profiles |

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Get Started with Marketing Cloud Next for Account Engagement

|  | Cloud. In Marketing Cloud, you market to a unified individual profile, which is a consolidated record of multiple lead and contact records associated with the same individual. The unified profile ensures correct contact details for marketing activities and dictates proper behavior for consent and personalization, and so on. |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Segmentation | Marketing Cloud uses segments to define the criteria for an audience and to group those individuals together. A segment is similar to a dynamic list in Account Engagement and uses the same process as Account Engagement to create segments via the Data 360 connector. If a segment in Marketing Cloud is set to run on a schedule, it behaves similarly to a dynamic list by adding and removing individuals based on the schedule. | Prerequisites for Using Data 360 | Understanding Segmentation in Marketing Cloud |
| Consent | In Account Engagement, consent is managed at both the email level and the subscription or | Set Up Consent and Subscriptions | Understanding Consent Concepts in Marketing Cloud Import Consent Data to Marketing Cloud |

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Get Started with Marketing Cloud Next for Account Engagement

|  | preference level. If a user opts out via email, they're opted out of all email communication. In Marketing Cloud, consent is managed only at the subscription or preference level. If a user unsubscribes, they unsubscribe from the subscription but not from all email marketing. Because consent isn't shared across Account Engagement and Marketing Cloud, you must export consent preferences from Account Engagement and then import them into Marketing Cloud. |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Promotional Emails | The content editing experience in Marketing Cloud uses a single design framework so that building content is familiar no matter what you're creating. The same experience for creating an email is available in both Account Engagement and Marketing Cloud. You can also use emails built for Account Engagement in Marketing Cloud, but the inverse isn't true. You can't use emails | Prepare Account Engagement Content for Campaigns in Marketing Cloud Create a Campaign and View Performance in Marketing Cloud Next | Create an Email in Marketing Cloud Add Required Consent Details to Promotional Emails |

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Get Started with Marketing Cloud Next for Account Engagement

|  | built in Marketing Cloud with Account Engagement. Before an email is sent, the system checks for the latest consent for the selected subscription, similar to the behavior in Account Engagement. |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Campaigns | In Account Engagement, a campaign tracks a prospect's first touch. The campaign is set when a prospect first clicks a tracked link or lands on a tracked page. A prospect remains associated with that campaign and with only one campaign. You can change which campaign the prospect is associated with. In Marketing Cloud, a campaign is the central organizational structure for your marketing efforts. It organizes the audience, assets, and metrics for a specific marketing effort. Your journey begins with a campaign and ends with reviewing your campaign's performance. We recommend using Marketing Cloud | Create a Campaign and View Performance | Enhance your Marketing with Marketing Cloud Campaigns |

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Get Started with Marketing Cloud Next for Account Engagement

|  | campaigns for multichannel messaging, including SMS, alongside your MCAE campaigns. |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Automations and Salesforce Flow | Marketing Cloud uses Salesforce Flow to automate tasks such as content distribution and lead creation. An email send is a common starting point for marketing journeys. A wait step causes a flow to resume for a specified event or when a condition is met. A wait step roughly equates to an activity trigger in Engagement Studio. An event trigger (such as an email send or form submission) can kick off a complete journey. This functionality is like combining the Account Engagement features of automation rules and Engagement Studio in a single automation. Think of Flow as giving you the ability to use trigger-based Engagement Studio programs. Account Engagement automation features are similar to these Flow features in Marketing Cloud. | Create a Campaign and View Performance | How Campaigns and Flows Work Together Working with Campaigns and Flows |

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Get Started with Marketing Cloud Next for Account Engagement

|  | • Completion actions > Event-based triggers and Flow actions • Automation rules > Event-based triggers, decisions, and actions • Engagement Studio criteria > Decisions • Engagement Studio actions > Actions • Engagement Studio activity triggers > Wait steps |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Scoring | Marketing Cloud provides a default scorecard to quantify the interest of a lead based on their tracked engagements. Scoring is implemented via Data 360 calculated insights. The score is displayed on the lead, contact, and account records. Leads and contacts are scored based on how engaged they are with your marketing efforts and how closely they resemble your ideal customer. You can customize default scoring rules and decide how engagement and fit scores factor into the overall marketing score for a lead or contact. You can use the engagement, fit, and | View Campaigns and Campaign Performance | Score People Based on Engagement and Fit in Marketing Cloud |

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Get Started with Marketing Cloud Next for Account Engagement

|  | overall marketing scores to build segments and create targeted content. When testing, keep in mind that scoring can take some time. We recommend validating multiple scenarios at a time. |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Reporting | When you've connected Account Engagement data to Data 360 and your marketing activities are synced to Marketing Cloud, Marketing Cloud reporting begins to include those events in its reporting. For example, as email engagement is ingested into Data 360, features like scoring and reporting, which depend on that activity type, begin to access those events. | View Campaigns and Campaign Performance | Measure Success in Marketing Cloud |

Connecting Account Engagement and Data 360
Data 360 organizes and unifies data across Salesforce and other external data sources. To drive
calculated insights and build segments, ingest Account Engagement data into Data 360.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, and Unlimited Editions with Account Engagement
You can bring these data types from Account Engagement into Data 360.
•
Prospect data
•
Custom URL engagement data
•
Email engagement data

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Get Started with Marketing Cloud Next for Account Engagement
•
Form engagement data
•
Form handler engagement data
•
Landing page engagement data
•
Tracked web page engagement data
To get 

…

Account Engagement Basic Resources
Other options and features are available to use with WhatsApp messaging, and can be configured on the
same Setup page.
Manage WhatsApp Consent
11.. From the Your Numbers page in WhatsApp Setup, click on a phone number.
22.. Open the Consent tab, click the Consent Type dropdown, and then select Explicit Opt-In.
33.. Review or update your consent validation settings in Marketing Cloud.
aa.. From Setup, in the Quick Find box, enter Channels, and then select WhatsApp.
bb.. Check consent on a communication subscription before sending promotional and transactional
messages.
Next Steps: Optional Features, Channels, and Customization
Here are some features and common enhancements that you can implement alongside the streamlined
recommendations in this Quick Start guide.

| Enhancement | Admin needed | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Set up Data 360 | • Salesforce Admin • Account Engagement Admin Salesforce Admin | Use Account Engagement data for WhatsApp for Data 360 segmentation. See Connecting Account Engagement and Data 360. |
| Add channels | Salesforce Admin | Marketing Cloud also supports sending publishing landing pages, and embedding forms on landing pages or a connected external site. See Salesforce Help: Manage Content in Marketing Cloud. |

Account Engagement Basic Resources
Explore downloadable implementation guides, keyboard shortcuts, tracking basics, glossary terms, and
more.
Downloadable User Guides for Account Engagement
Use these guides for easier navigation.
Account Engagement Glossary
Here are the most common terms you encounter while using Account Engagement.
Cookies and Activity Tracking

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Account Engagement Basic Resources
Account Engagement tracks visitor and prospect activities on your website and landing pages by
setting cookies on their browsers. Cookies are set to remember preferences (like form field values)
when a visitor returns to your site. We also set a cookie for logged-in users to maintain the session and
remember table filters.
Keyboard Shortcuts
Try keyboard shortcuts to get around more quickly. To get a list of available shortcuts, type Shift+?
(Windows) or Ctrl+? (Mac) at the same time.
Account Engagement Interface Versions
Account Engagement offers two interfaces: a standalone Account Engagement app and an Account
Engagement Lightning app. The interfaces differ in appearance and features, but offer the same
essential functionality. Both experiences require Salesforce SSO to log in and access the app. The
newest features and enhancements are available in Account Engagement Lightning app.
Automation and Asset Builder Versions
As Account Engagement interfaces are developed, content and automation tools are enhanced. You
can access classic builders in the standalone app. In Account Engagement Lightning App, you can
choose from classic builders and the enhanced experiences.
Downloadable User Guides for Account Engagement
Use these guides for easier navigation.

| B2B Marketing Analytics Implementation Guide |
| --- |
| B2B Marketing Analytics Plus Implementation Guide |
| Campaign Influence Implementation Guide |
| Connected Campaigns Implementation Guide |
| Account Engagement Email Experience Implementation Guide |
| Email Deliverability in Account Engagement |
| Account Engagement Enhanced Landing Page Experience Implementation Guide |
| Engagement History Implementation Guide |
| Engagement Studio Implementation Guide |
| Account Engagement and Advertising Studio Implementation Guide |
| Account Engagement Multiple Business Units Implementation Guide |
| Account Engagement Sandbox Implementation Guide |
| Enable Salesforce User Sync (Legacy) |
| Account Engagement User Migration Admin Guide |

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Account Engagement Basic Resources

| Account Engagement Business Units Upgrade Implementation Guide |
| --- |
| Account Engagement Einstein Features Implementation Guide |
| Account Engagement Lightning App Implementation Guide |
| Account Engagement Setup Implementation Guide |
| Account Engagement for Slack Implementation Guide |
| Sales Emails and Alerts Implementation Guide |
| Salesforce Connector for Account Engagement v1 Implementation Guide |
| Salesforce Connector for Account Engagement v2 Implementation Guide |
| Salesforce User Sync Implementation Guide |
| Account Engagement Data 360 Implementation Guide |
| Set Up a Confirmed Opt-In Process in Account Engagement |

Tip Want to dig into more information on the powerful marketing tools in Account Engagement?
Check out these Trailhead Modules.
•
Plan Marketing Content and Strategy with Marketing Cloud Account Engagement
•
Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Basics
•
Email Marketing for Marketing Cloud Account Engagement
•
Account Engagement for Targeted Audiences
•
Lead Scoring and Grading in Account Engagement
•
Lead Generation for Marketers
•
Extensibility Features for Account Engagement
•
Engagement Studio for Marketing Cloud Account Engagement
•
B2B Marketing Analytics for Lightning App
Account Engagement Glossary
Here are the most common terms you encounter while using Account Engagement.
Active Prospect
An active prospect is a prospect that has had at least one activity-any activity besides an email
send, email open, email bounce, or opportunity.
Automation Rule
An automation rule is a repeatable, criteria-based rule that finds matching prospects and applies
actions to them.

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Account Engagement Basic Resources
Campaign
An Account Engagement campaign is a thematic touchpoint (similar to a source in other
systems) that tracks a prospect's first touch. A prospect's Account Engagement campaign is set
when the prospect first clicks a tracked link or lands on a page that contains tracking code.
After a prospect is associated with a campaign, the prospect remains associated with that
campaign. Each prospect is associated with only one Account Engagement campaign, but you
can edit which campaign they're associated with.
Connector
These tools sync with third-party applications, like webinar services and Google Ads, and pass
data between the two applications. Using connectors, you can manage disconnected marketing
channels.
CRM
CRM stands for Customer Relationship Management and typically refers to Salesforce. At its
simplest, a CRM system allows businesses to manage business relationships and the data and
information associated with them.
Custom Object
Custom objects allow Account Engagement to run automations around custom or default
objects in your CRM that aren't default objects.
Custom Redirect
Use custom redirects to track banner ad clicks, links to third-party sites and links on social media
and to access files hosted outside of Account Engagement.
Dashboard
The Dashboard is the home page that you land on after you log in. It provides a high-level
overview of your notifications, active prospects, and more.
Dynamic Content
Use dynamic content to display custom HTML on your website or marketing forms, landing
pages, layout templates, and emails based on prospect criteria. When a prospect matches the
criteria for your dynamic content, a variation of the content displays.
Dynamic List
A dynamic list is rule-based. It automatically adds prospects when they match the criteria and
removes them when they don't.
Email Draft
An email draft is an email that hasn't been sent yet. Drafts are created for a single list email send.

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Account Engagement Basic Resources
Email Preference Center Page
The Email preference center page is where your prospects can subscribe and unsubscribe from
your public lists.
Email Template
An email template is a reusable email design that you can base new emails on. After you design
and build a template, you can personalize an email for each recipient. You can also modify a
template and limit what kind of changes your users can make. Email templates are also used
with Engagement Studio.
Form Handler
A form handler is an alternative to a marketing form. You can use a form handler to integrate
your third-party or custom forms to track submission data in Account Engagement.
Grade
Grading reflects a prospect's persona, fit, or demographic. The grade is based on information
you have about the prospect, like industry, job title, and company size.
Landing Page
A landing page is the page that a visitor typically is sent to after clicking a link or advertisement.
The page generally displays tailored content specific to the advertisement, search keyword, or
link clicked.
Layout Template
Use a layout template to format landing pages, forms, and site search results.
Mailable Prospect
A mailable prospect is a prospect that can receive marketing emails. Mailable prospects haven't
unsubscribed, had a hard bounce or five soft bounces, or manually opted out.
Page Action
A page action is a completion action that is triggered by a prospect's page view. You can apply
page actions to any page that contains your tracking code.
Profile
A profile is used to grade prospects based on your ideal customer. Set criteria, such as location,
company size, job title, and other factors, and use them to tier your prospects.
Prospect
A prospect is an anonymous visitor that has converted and is now identified.
Prospect Account
The company that a prospect is associated with. Group prospects who work for the same

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Account Engagement Basic Resources
company to keep all the information about the company in one place.
Score
A score is different from a grade. A score is based on activities (pages viewed, forms completed,
and so on) that a prospect performs. Scoring indicates how interested or engaged a prospect is
with your company.
Scoring Rules
Use scoring rules to adjust a prospect's score. You start with baseline scoring rules. You can also
create your own custom rules.
Segmentation
Segmentation refers to how you create a target list of prospects for marketing needs, such as a
newsletter or engagement program.
Segmentation Rule
Use a segmentation rule to pull a one-time list of prospects based on specific criteria. Unlike an
automation rule, a segmentation rule doesn't run continuously.
Table Actions
A table action is an action that you can apply to an entire table or selected items in a table. You
can use table actions with most tables in Account Engagement, including prospect tables, user
tables, forms, and lists. Available actions vary by table.
Tag
Use tags to filter data tables and asset lists, label prospects, and label assets for easier updating.
You can create tags and apply them to assets and prospects from almost any table, record, or
action.
Unmailable Prospect
An unmailable prospect has either opted out of emails, was opted out on import, marked as Do
Not Mail, or had a hard bounce or five soft bounces.
Unsubscribe Page
All business units include an unsubscribe page where prospects can opt out of emails. The
unsubscribe page is linked in every marketing email that you send.
Visitor
A website visitor who has visited your tracked assets. A visitor can be anonymous or a prospect.
Anonymous visitors become prospects when they're associated with an email address. Visitor
also refers to the first step in the prospect lifecycle funnel.

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Account Engagement Basic Resources
Visitor Filter
Use filters to exclude some types of visits, clicks, or other actions from your campaign results and
email notifications. The most c

…

chunks.heading: 04-managing-your-business-unit
chunks.body:
---
title: "Managing Your Business Unit"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_account_engagement_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "46-115"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Managing Your Business Unit
Engagement metrics are available in the Account Engagement Email Template report.
Classic List Emails
Classic list emails are available in the Account Engagement Email tab in Account Engagement
Lightning App or through the Marketing menu in the standalone app.
Classic emails are sent to segmentation lists and support completion actions and automation
rules. A/B testing is available on the Building tab of a classic email draft. Engagement metrics
appear in the Account Engagement List Email report.
Classic Landing Pages
Classic landing pages are available in the Content tab in Account Engagement Lightning App or
through the Marketing menu in the standalone app.
Classic landing pages support scripting, form styling, and multivariate testing. Engagement
metrics appear in the Account Engagement Landing Page report.
Managing Your Business Unit
You can edit your business unit settings in different places in Account Engagement, based on whether
you use Lightning Experience or the standalone app.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: All Account Engagement Editions
In the standalone app, click the cog icon on any page to access Settings.
In the Account Engagement Lightning App, most settings are available on the Account Engagement
Settings tab (1). Others, such as the setup tasks for B2B Marketing Analytics and Marketing App
Extensions, appear in the Marketing Setup app. Access Marketing Setup by clicking the cog icon on any
page in the app (2).

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Managing Your Business Unit
Managing Users and Preferences
Admins can create and manage users, who can manage some of their own settings and preferences.
Logging into Account Engagement requires Salesforce single sign-on (SSO). To simplify user
management and transfer it to Salesforce, we recommend setting up Salesforce User Sync.
Browser Support for Account Engagement
You can use the standalone Account Engagement app in the most popular browsers.
Business Unit Settings for Account Engagement
Admins can control settings for the entire business unit. Depending on your edition or app, some
feature-level settings also appear on the Account Engagement Settings page. Review feature-specific
help for information about those settings.
Copy Marketing Assets Between Account Engagement Business Units
Share marketing assets, such as email templates, files, custom fields, and custom redirects, across
business units without manually recreating them.
Monitor Business Unit Health with Account Engagement Optimizer
Use Optimizer to help you diagnose and correct critical configuration issues and review
recommendations to improve processing efficiency in your business unit. You can also pause and
resume actions that are currently processing and see which features are causing the most changes to
prospect records.
Visitor Tracking for Account Engagement
To collect activity data and give visitors a seamless experience, set up tracker domains and add
Account Engagement tracking code to your web pages. When you create marketing assets, you can
choose which tracker domain you want to use for vanity URLs and link rewriting.
Prospect Security and Privacy
Account Engagement offers tools to help you protect the privacy of your prospects and the security of
their data. Many of these tools are enabled by default when you create your business unit.
Manage Encryption Keys with Bring Your Own Encryption Key (BYOK)
Organizations find BYOK useful for key lifecycle management and control, data protection and
security, regulatory compliance, data portability, and more. With BYOK, you can secure your marketing

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Managing Your Business Unit
database with an encryption key that you manage and then, in an emergency, revoke access to your
Account Engagement data. No one has access to this key-not even Salesforce-so after access is
removed, the data becomes unreadable.
Manage Marketing Needs
Account Engagement admins have access to tools that can help your marketing team get the most out
of their efforts.
Data Usage and Limits
There are limits on objects in Account Engagement, such as mailable prospects, email templates, and
landing pages. The limits for each object depend on your edition of Account Engagement. You can
view your current usage and move objects to the recycle bin as needed.
Product and Service Notifications
Account Engagement sends different types of product and service notifications. You can designate a
different point of contact for each notification in the Product and Service Notifications table in your
business unit settings.
Use Marketing Cloud Account Engagement APIs
Use the Account Engagement APIs to create integrations that meet your business needs. Access
prospect and visitor data in a third-party system, automate processes, and more.
System Emails
Admins can see a log of all system email notifications for the past 14 days.
Role-Based Email Addresses
Because role-based email addresses often represent multiple recipients, they're more likely to generate
spam complaints. To help protect your sending reputation, Account Engagement restricts importing
and sending to these email addresses by default. If you have a legitimate business reason to import
role-based email addresses, contact Salesforce Customer Support.
Grant Salesforce Customer Support Access to Account Engagement
Grant users Salesforce Customer Support access to Account Engagement so that they can view and
edit records and users in your business unit. Access is timestamped.
Managing Users and Preferences
Admins can create and manage users, who can manage some of their own settings and preferences.
Logging into Account Engagement requires Salesforce single sign-on (SSO). To simplify user
management and transfer it to Salesforce, we recommend setting up Salesforce User Sync.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: All Account Engagement Editions
Create Account Engagement Users
If you don't use Salesforce User Sync to manage users, you can create users in Account Engagement.
To access the app, users must be linked to a Salesforce user and have Salesforce single sign-on (SSO)
enabled. Before you add Account Engagement users, verify that they have Salesforce credentials.

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Managing Your Business Unit
Account Engagement User Roles
A user role defines which features and data a user has access. Choose a default or custom role when
you create a user.
Import Users
You can create or update up to 50 users at once by importing a CSV file.
Create a User Group for Round-Robin Assignments
Use groups for round-robin lead assignment. When you assign a prospect to a group, the prospect is
assigned to a group member, equally distributing leads.
Reassign Prospects in Account Engagement
You can change lead assignments in Salesforce. If needed, you can also reassign prospects from a
user's record in Account Engagement.
Delete a User in Account Engagement
If you don't use Salesforce User Sync to manage users, you can disable Salesforce single sign-on and
delete a user in Account Engagement. Deleting a user archives them in the recycle bin.
Limit the Number of Prospects That a User Can Work With
An Account Engagement admin can set security limits on a per-user basis to limit the number of
prospects that a user can import, export, or send a list email to.
Change Your Language and Locale
To use Account Engagement in a different language, update your personal language and locale
settings. Admins can change language and locale at the account level.
Update Your User Profile
Most users can update their own personal information and email preferences on the My Profile page.
Only an admin can update a Sales user's profile or change a user's role.
Email Signatures
Users can create custom email signatures for their newsletters and automated emails. Admins can
customize signatures for any user.
User Email Preferences
Account Engagement sends notifications for some prospect activities, inactive automations, and
search marketing reports. A user's email preferences control which notification emails are sent.
Create Account Engagement Users
If you don't use Salesforce User Sync to manage users, you can create users in Account Engagement. To
access the app, users must be linked to a Salesforce user and have Salesforce single sign-on (SSO)
enabled. Before you add Account Engagement users, verify that they have Salesforce credentials.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: All Account Engagement Editions
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create a user: Account Engagement Administrator role

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Managing Your Business Unit
To simplify user management, we recommend using Salesforce User Sync. When Salesforce User Sync is
set up for your business unit, you create and manage users in Salesforce.
11.. Open the Users page. Select Admin (Account Engagement Settings in the Lightning app), and then
User Management | Users.
22.. Click + Add User.
33.. Enter the user's first and last name.
44.. Enter the user's unique email address. The email address must be unique across all Account
Engagement business units.
55.. Select a Salesforce user.
66.. To ensure that the user can access Account Engagement, select Use Salesforce single sign-on.
77.. Choose a user role.
88.. When you're done, click Create User.
See Also
Account Engagement User Roles
Account Engagement User Roles
A user role defines which features and data a user has access. Choose a default or custom role when you
create a user.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: All Account Engagement Editions
Default User Roles
Account Engagement includes four default user roles: Administrator, Marketing, Sales Manager, and
Sales. You can't edit the default user roles.
Custom User Roles
You can create a custom role to provide specific permissions to users. You can create a role from
scratch or clone a default role and modify the permissions.
Default User Roles
Account Engagement includes four default user roles: Administrator, Marketing, Sales Manager, and
Sales. You can't edit the default user roles.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: All Account Engagement Editions

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Managing Your Business Unit
Administrator Role
Administrators have access to all settings, prospect data, and modules.
Marketing Role
Marketing users have control over prospect data and marketing modules, including creating lists, forms,
and landing pages and sending list emails.
Sales Manager Role
Sales Managers have access to all prospect data and visitors but no marketing functionality. Sales
Managers can send one-to-one emails to prospects, but can't send list emails.
Sales
Sales users only have access to prospect management functionality. Sales users can send one-to-one
emails to prospects but can't send list emails. They can view, edit, and export the prospects assigned to
them.
Role Permissions
Note A user with No Access for an asset type can't view details for those assets, but can see the
asset names in the app. For example, a user with the View permission for Forms can see a list of
landing pages that a form is used on. However, if they don't have View access to Landing Pages, they
can't view details for landing pages.
Dashboard

| Module | Administrator | Marketing | Sales Manager | Sales |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Prospects Analytics graphic | View | View | No access | No access |
| Prospects table | View all | View all | View all | View only prospects assigned to self |

chunks.body:
---
title: "Using Account Engagement with Salesforce (part 1/2)"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_account_engagement_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "116-243"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Using Account Engagement with Salesforce
•

Grant Salesforce Customer Support Access to Account
Engagement
Grant users Salesforce Customer Support access to Account Engagement so that they can view and edit
records and users in your business unit. Access is timestamped.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: All Account Engagement Editions
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To grant access to Account Engagement: Account Engagement Administrator or Marketing
role
Note Pardot is now known as Marketing Cloud Account Engagement. We wish we could snap our
fingers to update the name everywhere, but you can expect to see the previous name in a few
places until we replace it, including in the app itself.
11.. Open the settings for your business unit.
••
In Account Engagement, click and then select Settings.
••
In the Lightning app, select Account Engagement Settings.
22.. Click Grant account access to Salesforce Employees.
33.. Select the duration and then click Grant access.
Using Account Engagement with Salesforce
To streamline your sales and marketing efforts into one platform, connect Account Engagement with

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Using Account Engagement with Salesforce
Salesforce. Bring data, assets, and actions together with tools like Engagement History, Sales Emails and
Alerts (Salesforce Engage), and the Salesforce connector.
Connecting Account Engagement and Salesforce
To share data across systems, set up the Salesforce connector.
Track Prospect Engagement in Salesforce
Your campaigns track valuable engagement data that can tell you how well your marketing assets
resonate with your customer base. When you bring all of the activity types and metrics in one place,
you have a collection of features called Engagement History. Decide what Account Engagement data is
most valuable to you and your users, and surface it throughout Salesforce.
Account Engagement in Lightning Experience
With the Account Engagement Lightning app, sales and marketing can operate side by side on a single
platform rather than work in separate applications.
Sales Emails and Alerts
The Sales Emails and Alerts feature helps marketing share content with sales reps. Sales reps can use
marketing-approved email templates to contact prospects at the right moment and track the
effectiveness of the messages in Salesforce.
Connecting Account Engagement and Salesforce
To share data across systems, set up the Salesforce connector.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: All Account Engagement Editions
Designate an Admin for Marketing Setup Tasks
Setup and feature configuration often requires both a Salesforce admin and an Account Engagement
admin. To bypass the need for multiple admins, you can create a custom permission set in Salesforce
to delegate Account Engagement tasks to a marketing admin. This custom permission set grants
access to all the nodes in Marketing Setup without granting full access to Salesforce Setup.
Syncing Prospects
When syncing prospects between Account Engagement and Salesforce, changes to prospect records
are pushed to Salesforce, and information from Salesforce records is pulled into the corresponding
fields on prospect records. Syncing occurs every 2-4 minutes.
What Salesforce Data Is Available in Account Engagement?
Account Engagement and Salesforce have different authorization and sharing models. Data is shared
through the Salesforce connector via the connector user, so Account Engagement can show any data
that the connector user has access to. Because of how the systems connect, you can assume that
Account Engagement users have at least as much access to Salesforce data as the connector user.
Account Engagement and Salesforce API Usage
The Salesforce connector makes it possible to share data between the platforms using an API.
Find Your Salesforce Connector Version

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Using Account Engagement with Salesforce
Before you begin connector setup, determine which connector version you have.
Considerations for Upgrading to v2 of the Salesforce Connector
Version 2 of the Salesforce connector offers improvements, like on-demand metadata sync, Business
Unit Switcher, and the integration user. Before you upgrade, familiarize yourself with the changes from
v1 to v2.
Setting Up a Salesforce Connector for Business Units Purchased After February 11, 2019
For business units purchased after February 11, 2019, only a Salesforce admin can install and enable
Account Engagement. To complete setup, the Salesforce admin appoints an Account Engagement
admin who can configure Account Engagement. These admins can be different users. Review
prerequisites before you begin.
Setting Up the Salesforce Connector for Business Units Purchased Before February 11, 2019
The Salesforce connector syncs prospect and opportunity data between Account Engagement and
Salesforce. Account Engagement can sync with Salesforce Professional Edition and higher. If you
purchased Account Engagement before February 11, 2019, follow these steps to install and configure
the Salesforce connector.
Best Practices for the Salesforce Connector
Although not critical to a successful connector setup, these references and tasks can help you make
the most out of your Salesforce connector implementation.
Managing Multiple Business Units
Whether your enterprise has marketing goals based on geography or product lines, help each division
focus on their own unique marketing objectives using multiple Account Engagement business units.
Prospects and marketing assets are partitioned by business unit, so your divisions can market
independently and still get a global view with B2B Marketing Analytics.
Create a Sandbox for Account Engagement
Use Sandboxes for Account Engagement to test configuration changes before implementing them in
production.
Opportunity Syncing with Salesforce
A Salesforce opportunity tracks pending or actual revenue from a sale. After you set up the Salesforce
connector, your Salesforce opportunities become available in Account Engagement for ROI reporting.
This feature is available as part of the connector, and doesn't have to be enabled.
Person Account Syncing with Salesforce
Account Engagement can sync with Salesforce person accounts.
Custom Object Syncing with Salesforce
You can run automations around custom Salesforce objects that are related to a prospect record
through a prospect ID. To tie a custom Salesforce object to a prospect ID, link the object to a contact,
lead, or account record.
Designate an Admin for Marketing Setup Tasks
Setup and feature configuration often requires both a Salesforce admin and an Account Engagement
admin. To bypass the need for multiple admins, you can create a custom permission set in Salesforce to
delegate Account Engagement tasks to a marketing admin. This custom permission set grants access to

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Using Account Engagement with Salesforce
all the nodes in Marketing Setup without granting full access to Salesforce Setup.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: All Account Engagement Editions in Lightning Experience
Designating a user as an Account Engagement admin in Salesforce grants them access to Marketing
Setup only. Access to Marketing Setup requires two specific user permissions:
•
View Setup and Configuration
•
Manage Marketing Setup Tasks
If you want your Account Engagement admin to set up the enhanced email experience, assign them two
more permissions:
•
Manage Profiles and Permission Sets
•
Assign Permission Sets
You can create a custom permission set with these permissions and apply it to the user of your choice.
Alternatively, you can add these permissions to an existing custom permission set.
Make sure your marketing admin also has the admin role in Account Engagement. This setup gives one
person all the access they need to set up Account Engagement.
See Also
Create Permission Sets
Syncing Prospects
When syncing prospects between Account Engagement and Salesforce, changes to prospect records are
pushed to Salesforce, and information from Salesforce records is pulled into the corresponding fields on
prospect records. Syncing occurs every 2-4 minutes.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: All Account Engagement Editions
Run a Full Sync
Sync all your prospect records with Salesforce.
What Causes a Prospect to Sync from Account Engagement to Salesforce?
For a prospect to sync to Salesforce, the prospect record must meet the prerequisites and then an
activity must trigger the sync.
How Email Address Changes Sync
When you change a contact or lead email address in Salesforce, it can sync the new address to the
corresponding prospect record in Account Engagement. This functionality is useful if your sales team

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Using Account Engagement with Salesforce
updates email addresses manually or via a data append service, or if you merge lead records.
How Does Account Engagement Handle Deleted Leads and Contacts?
When a synced lead or contact is deleted in Salesforce, it's flagged as [[crm_deleted]] and sent to the
Account Engagement recycle bin. Account Engagement doesn't recreate the lead or contact in
Salesforce, but some actions can remove the flag.
Review and Clear Prospect Sync Errors
After you resolve prospect sync errors, you can resync those prospects from the sync error page in
Account Engagement. Depending on the errors, it's possible your Salesforce admin has to resolve
them.
Prevent a Prospect from Syncing with Salesforce
You have a few options for keeping a prospect from syncing with Salesforce.
Run a Full Sync
Sync all your prospect records with Salesforce.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: All Account Engagement Editions
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To use the Sync All Prospects option: Account Engagement Administrator role
Note Business units with fewer than 500,000 prospects can trigger a full sync once every 7 days
using the Sync All Prospects option. For larger volumes of data, contact Salesforce Customer
Support to run a full sync.
11.. Open the Connectors page.
••
In Account Engagement, select Admin and then select Connectors.
••
In the Lightning app, select Account Engagement Settings and then Connectors.
22.. Next to the Salesforce connector, click the icon, and select Sync All Prospects.
33.. Review the information in the pop-out box, and select Sync Prospects.
44.. In the pop-out box, confirm that all prospects are queued to sync, and then click OK.
Review the sync queue and sync errors. A synced prospect isn't removed from the queue until their
record has been synced with Salesforce without issues. Synced prospects with issues are sent to the sync
error queue.
See Also
Review and Clear Prospect Sync Errors

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Using Account Engagement with Salesforce
What Causes a Prospect to Sync from Account Engagement to Salesforce?
For a prospect to sync to Salesforce, the prospect record must meet the prerequisites and then an
activity must trigger the sync.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: All Account Engagement Editions
Syncing Prerequisites for Accounts That Don't Allow Multiple Prospects with the Same Email
Address
Syncing a prospect to Salesforce requires a corresponding Salesforce record or the prospect must be
assigned in Account Engagement. If your business unit doesn't allow multipl

chunks.heading: 06-managing-visitors-and-prospects
chunks.body:
---
title: "Managing Visitors and Prospects"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_account_engagement_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "244-285"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Managing Visitors and Prospects
Use the dropdowns to filter your reports to the exact level of detail you want (1).
Use the date picker to narrow your reporting to a specific time period (2). Click the hyperlinked text to
drill further into the data (3).
Managing Visitors and Prospects
Account Engagement helps you acquire, qualify, and convert prospects into customers. Everything starts
with an unidentified visitor. After visitor provides an email address, you have a prospect and you're ready
to go. Assign prospects to users and create opportunities to track the customer journey.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: All Account Engagement Editions
Anonymous Visitors
An anonymous visitor is someone who has visited a page with your tracking code on it, but hasn't
converted to a prospect yet. Prospects are visitors who have an associated email address in Account
Engagement.
Active Prospects
An active prospect has performed an activity, such as submitting a form, visiting a page, or clicking a
link. Active prospects have a green person icon next to their name. A prospect who has never been
active has a red person icon.
How a Prospect's Source Field Is Populated
The value in a prospect's source field is determined by the referring URL. The referring URL is the page
the visitor was on before they visited a page with tracking code for the first time. Account Engagement

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Managing Visitors and Prospects
pulls in source data when the referring URL is present and matches up with the expected website for a
given vendor.
Prospect Audits
Each prospect record includes an audit table that displays details about changes to the record.
Prospect List Table Views
Marketing, administrator, and sales manager users can change the prospect table view to sort and
filter prospects by category.
Prospect Table Actions
Apply mass actions to the prospect table without having to use an automation rule. You can select the
entire table or individual prospects.
Add Prospects Manually
If you often meet prospects in person or want to test a prospect record, you can create a prospect
record manually.
Assigning Prospects
Assign a prospect to an Account Engagement user to give the user ownership of the record and sync it
to Salesforce. You can manually assign a prospect or use automation rules and completion actions to
assign a prospect to the appropriate rep based on activity and information.
Delete or Undelete Prospects
When you delete a prospect, the record is moved to the recycle bin. From the recycle bin, you can
restore the prospect or permanently delete the record. Deleting a prospect doesn't delete the record
that it's syncing with in Salesforce.
Merge Prospects
Duplicate prospects sometimes end up in your database. You can merge up to three prospects at once
and select one to be the primary. When merged, the activities of the merged prospects are
aggregated, and Account Engagement recalculates the primary prospect's score. Non-primary
prospects are permanently deleted and don't go to the recycle bin.
Export Prospects
Export prospects in CSV format to perform a bulk update, fix sync errors, or use prospect data for
external reporting.
Importing Prospects
You can import a CSV file of new prospects or perform a bulk update of existing prospects.
Tracking Prospect Field Updates
The Updated At field displays the date and time that the prospect record was last updated. Account
Engagement users and prospects can trigger changes to this field.
Tracking Most Recent Prospect Activity
The Last Activity field shows the date and time a prospect last interacted with your marketing assets.
The field updates when a prospect takes an action, such as clicking a link or submitting a form. Most
prospect activity updates the Last Activity field, but there are some exceptions. The field doesn't
update if the activity is the initiated by an Account Engagement user, such as sending an email.
Managing Prospects with Excessive Tracked Activity
When a prospect's activity levels are high enough to impact performance for your business unit,
Account Engagement pauses activity tracking for the prospect. Often, this level of activity is the result

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Managing Visitors and Prospects
of a bot.
Prospect Actions
In Account Engagement, actions are applied to prospects in various ways. Not all actions are available
in every type of asset. Automation rules, completion actions, engagement programs, and
segmentation rules have different available actions.
Manage Prospect Mailability
You can view and edit a prospect's mailability status on their prospect record in Account Engagement.
If a prospect asks to be opted in or out, you must complete their request manually. You can also use
the Do Not Email field to suppress prospects from marketing emails.
Prospect Accounts
A prospect account groups prospects that work for the same company under the same umbrella,
keeping all the information about the company in one place. Use prospect accounts to reduce
duplication and keep information consistent across prospect records. If you use Salesforce, this
account information syncs from Salesforce to Account Engagement.
Anonymous Visitors
An anonymous visitor is someone who has visited a page with your tracking code on it, but hasn't
converted to a prospect yet. Prospects are visitors who have an associated email address in Account
Engagement.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: All Account Engagement Editions
Converting Visitors to Prospects
A prospect is a visitor with a known email address. Account Engagement tracks visitors and converts
them to prospects when an email address can be assigned, which typically happens via a form submit.
Visitors Report
The Visitors report displays the captured website sessions of anonymous visitors and identified
prospects. You can filter sessions based on page views, campaign, and date range. To access the
visitors report, navigate to Prospects | Visitors. The visitor report contains at least a year of data for
anonymous visitors.
Associate a Visitor with a Prospect
Most prospects are related to their visitor activity when they convert, but you can manually associate a
visitor with a prospect. This feature comes in handy when you're in contact with a potential prospect
whose company is in your visitor report.
Automatically Remove Unconverted Visitor Records
Free up system bandwidth to improve overall business unit performance and adhere to privacy
regulations by automatically deleting stale unconverted visitor records. Deleting stale unconverted
visitors is permanent. You can't retrieve the records after they're deleted.

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Managing Visitors and Prospects
Converting Visitors to Prospects
A prospect is a visitor with a known email address. Account Engagement tracks visitors and converts
them to prospects when an email address can be assigned, which typically happens via a form submit.
Here are common ways that prospect records are created.
•
A visitor submits an Account Engagement form that is in an iframe on your web page
•
A visitor submits a form on your site that's connected to an Account Engagement form handler
•
A visitor submits a form on a landing page
•
A user manually associates a visitor with a prospect
See Also
Delete Visitor Records to Restore Paused Prospects
Visitors Report
The Visitors report displays the captured website sessions of anonymous visitors and identified
prospects. You can filter sessions based on page views, campaign, and date range. To access the visitors
report, navigate to Prospects | Visitors. The visitor report contains at least a year of data for anonymous
visitors.
These graphs display basic information about your visitors.
•
Page Views Graph - Displays the number of page views over the past 24 hours and the percentage
change since the previous day.
•
New Visitors Graph - Displays the number of new visitors over the past 24 hours and the percentage
change since the previous day.
•
Top Companies -Displays the top 20 companies based on page views over the past 24 hours.
Filter the visitor table using one of these views.
•
Identified Visitors - Displays all visitors with identified companies.
•
Active Visitors - Displays all visitors who visited two or more pages, regardless of whether their
company could be identified.
•
All Visitors - Displays all visitor sessions.
Associate a Visitor with a Prospect
Most prospects are related to their visitor activity when they convert, but you can manually associate a
visitor with a prospect. This feature comes in handy when you're in contact with a potential prospect
whose company is in your visitor report.

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Managing Visitors and Prospects
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create or associate a visitor to a prospect: Account Engagement Administrator or Marketing
role
Note When you manually associate a visitor, the email address you enter for the prospect is prefilled
in Account Engagement forms.
11.. Navigate to Prospects | Visitors and find the visitor you want to associate.
22.. Click Create / Associate next to the visitor.
33.. Enter an email address for the prospect record. If you have multiple prospects with the same email
address, Account Engagement associates the visitor to the prospect with the most recent value in the
Last Activity field.
See Also
Converting Visitors to Prospects
Automatically Remove Unconverted Visitor Records
Free up system bandwidth to improve overall business unit performance and adhere to privacy
regulations by automatically deleting stale unconverted visitor records. Deleting stale unconverted
visitors is permanent. You can't retrieve the records after they're deleted.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: All Account Engagement Editions
Before permanently deleting unconverted visitor records, keep these considerations in mind regarding
reporting.
•
Depending on your settings, automatically deleting stale unconverted visitors impacts B2BMA
reporting. Reports that use pdVisitor can be impacted. In the Pipeline dashboard, the conversion rates
can appear higher or the number of visitors can decrease.
•
If you're using B2BMA reporting, we recommend setting your deletion date to 3 years to match the
B2BMA data retention rate.
•
Deleted stale unconverted visitors can make conversion rates appear higher and unique clicks lower.
11.. Open the settings for your business unit.
••
In Account Engagement, click and then select Settings.
••
In the Lightning app, select Account Engagement Settings.
22.. Click Edit, select Visitor Importance Time Frame, and choose the date range after which visitor
records are automatically deleted.
33.. Enable Automatically delete all visitor records after the Visitor Importance Time Frame lapses
which didn't convert to prospects.

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Managing Visitors and Prospects
••
For business units created on or after October 16, 2023, this setting is auto-enabled with the
deletion date range set to 365 days. You can adjust this duration between 6 months up to 3 years.
••
This setting is auto-enabled with a default date set to 365 days in sandbox and demo business units.
Active Prospects
An active prospect has performed an activity, such as submitting a form, visiting a page, or clicking a link.
Active prospects have a green person icon next to their name. A prospect who has never been active has
a red person icon.
Some activities are passive and aren't go
