Marketing Analytics app that reference Account Engagement datasets.
Each time Salesforce releases new features or enhancements to B2B Marketing Analytics, users must
upgrade their apps. When you load the new version, it overwrites dataset IDs and details that break the
customizations you have made. To make sure that you don't lose work, save a backup copy of your app
and log the Account Engagement dataset IDs that are being used.
During the upgrade, we recommend creating an app instead of overwriting the current one. After you
verify that everything works as expected, you can delete the old version.
First, identify the dataset IDs that are in use. Create a tracking spreadsheet where you can list IDs, names,
and usage for reference throughout this process. To prevent custom lenses and reports from being
overwritten, save everything to a private or shared app.
Tip Some datasets in Analytics Studio aren't used in B2B Marketing Analytics apps. You can find out
which app uses a dataset in the Usage table.
11.. Open Analytics Studio, and select Data Manager and then select Data.
22.. For the datasets used in a B2B Marketing Analytics app, click the action menu and select Edit Dataset.
33.. In the page URL, find the dataset ID and add it to your tracking document.
44.. In the Usage section, find out where the dataset is being used and copy that information to your
tracking document.
Some datasets in Analytics Studio aren't used in B2B Marketing Analytics apps. Make sure that the App
column shows a B2B Marketing Analytics app. Other apps, such as Engagement History, Private App,
or Shared App, don't need any changes.
Repeat this task for all your Account Engagement datasets. Save the IDs, dataset names, and where each
one is used in your tracking document. You can reference this information while you edit your custom
app.
Everything left in the B2B Marketing Analytics app is overwritten when you upgrade. If you use custom
reports or lenses, copy them into the My Private Apps or My Shared Apps folders to protect them from
deletion.

Optional Uses for B2B Marketing Analytics
Your business goals dictate how you set up each B2B Marketing Analytics app. For example, track
opportunities at each touchpoint or dig into engagement on a specific account. Optional dashboards are
available with B2B Marketing Analytics.

Multi-Touch Attribution Dashboard

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Report on and Analyze Account Engagement Data
Multi-Touch Attribution dashboards show you which marketing efforts are most influential during each
stage of the purchase lifecycle. After you enable Connected Campaigns, users can find asset
engagement from Account Engagement and data from Sales Cloud together in one dashboard.
Account-Based Marketing Dashboard
Use Account-Based Marketing dashboards to understand how opportunities and contacts from one
account engage with your marketing and sales assets. To use Account-Based Marketing dashboards,
enable the integration user's access to B2B Marketing Analytics.
Prospect and Activity Dataset
To identify which content influences prospects in your sales funnel, add the optional Prospect and
Activity dataset. Create a lens using this dataset to identify the individual prospect record that acted on
a certain asset.
Einstein Behavior Scoring Dashboard for B2B Marketing Analytics
The Einstein Behavior Scoring dashboard provides a look into how Einstein uses engagement data to
create its model. You can learn which engagement activities impact opportunities the most. Select an
activity to learn more about which individual assets perform best.
Multi-Touch Attribution Dashboard
Multi-Touch Attribution dashboards show you which marketing efforts are most influential during each
stage of the purchase lifecycle. After you enable Connected Campaigns, users can find asset engagement
from Account Engagement and data from Sales Cloud together in one dashboard.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Account Engagement Plus, Advanced, and Premium Editions with Salesforce Enterprise
Edition or higher
Available for an extra cost in legacy Account Engagement Pro and Ultimate Editions with Salesforce

Create a dedicated Account Engagement Lead Management Agent with Agentforce actions. View and

Engagement
Einstein is built on data. When using Marketing Cloud Account Engagement, review this table to
understand the type of data used by Einstein features.
The table lists both the Customer Data that is used, which is data submitted by the Customer to our
services as defined in our Main Services Agreement (MSA), and the usage data that is used, which is data
relating to users' interactions with Salesforce. Data submitted to an Einstein feature may be used to train
AI models, to improve your services and features, or to develop new features that you can access without
additional cost.
The table also indicates which features use global models, which are models that look for aggregated
trends across multiple Salesforce Customers. The Customer has control over whether their data
contributes to global models. For more information on how to control how your data is used,
see Manage Salesforce Access to Customer Data.

Considerations for Using Agentforce with Account Engagement
Keep these considerations in mind when using Agentforce actions for Account Engagement.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: all Account Engagement editions with Salesforce Enterprise, Performance, and Unlimited
editions
Account Engagement Agentforce actions deploy from Account Engagement. Before you can deploy the
actions, you must create an External Client App and a Named Credential.
Agent User Requirements
•
Provide the agent with the Named Credential user and business unit ID for each Account Engagement
Agentforce actions.
•
Actions don't run if the Named Credential isn't authenticated. To confirm access, we recommend
testing a related invocable action after you authenticate the named credential.
•
The agent user must have access to the APEX classes that are used to power the Account Engagement
actions. To provide access, manually update their permission set.
Using Account Engagement Fields and Records with Agentforce
•
To have the agent pull a value from a specific Account Engagement field, include the field in the agent
instructions. This is especially helpful for record IDs, such as a list ID.
•
The IDs used in Account Engagement actions almost always refer to Account Engagement ID fields.
Prospect record actions include Account Engagement IDs. To translate IDs from a Salesforce record,
additional actions are needed.
•
Account Engagement actions return only 1,000 records, sorted by the most recently updated records.

chunks.heading: 14-einstein-assistant-in-account-engagement
chunks.body:
---
title: "Einstein Assistant in Account Engagement"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_account_engagement_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "738-739"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Einstein Assistant in Account Engagement
Use Account Engagement Actions in Agentforce
Create a dedicated Account Engagement Lead Management Agent with Agentforce actions. View and
update prospect records and add them to nurture campaigns from anywhere in Salesforce.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: all Account Engagement editions with Salesforce Enterprise, Performance, and Unlimited
editions
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To deploy Agentforce actions for Account
APEX Class Access to:
Engagement:
•
getB2BEngagementStudioProgram
•
getB2BMarketingListMemberships
•
getB2BMarketingLists
•
signUpProspectB2BMarketingLists
•
upsertB2BProspect
•
viewB2BProspect
Before you begin:
•
Install the latest version of the Account Engagement (Pardot) AppExchange package.
•
Enable Agentforce.
•
Create an External Client App and a Named Credential.
Allow for up to an hour for an action to deploy. An Agentforce action can't be deleted after you deploy it.
Each time an agent uses an action, standard credits are consumed.
11.. Deploy actions to Agentforce from Account Engagement.
aa.. Open Account Engagement Settings.
bb.. Under Agentforce Actions for Account Engagement, click Deploy Actions.
cc.. Confirm your choice, and then click Close.
22.. In Salesforce, add the Account Engagement actions to an Agentforce Topic.
Einstein Assistant in Account Engagement
Use Content Creation for Account Engagement, powered by Einstein to draft forms and landing pages
and to create email subject lines, headers, and body copy. The Einstein Assistant prompts you to describe
what you want to draft. The more specific you are, the more useful and accurate your results. The
Einstein Assistant window appears when you create a landing page, form, or email, or edit an existing
one. After acknowledging you're about to use generative AI, Einstein is ready to help. If you don't see the

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Einstein Assistant in Account Engagement
assistant window, ask your admin to enable it in Einstein Generative AI Setup.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience Available in: Account Engagement Advanced or
Premium
Einstein chat history is retained per account, per record, and per tab with the asset. For example, if you
create an email using Einstein, you see the history for that email when you edit it. However, you don't see
that history when you create another email. Currently, Einstein Assistant supports English, French,
German, Japanese, Portuguese (Brazil), and Spanish.
Drafting Landing Pages
Based on your prompt entry, Einstein suggests a name, tags, a title, and a description. You can accept or
redraft the elements individually. For the description, you can edit the suggested text to fully customize
it. A diamond next to a field indicates that the information was drafted using Einstein Assistant.
If Einstein can't help you with a task, you're notified in the chat window. For example, when you continue
to select a form or content layout, Einstein lets you know it's unable to help with that part of the process,
and to continue on your own until Einstein can help.
Creating Forms
Based on your prompt entry, Einstein suggests a name and tags. Einstein can't help you with the Fields
tab, but can help you with the Look and Feel tab description, headlines, and paragraphs. Einstein can
help you draft details in the Completion Actions tab.
Writing Emails
On the Account Engagement Email tab, you can use Einstein in the Drafts section. For list emails and list
email drafts, use Einstein to draft a subject line, header, and body copy paragraphs in the editor. When
Einstein provides the information that you want to use, copy it from the prompt to where you want to
use it in your email. When you save your draft and continue to the Sending page, you can modify the
description and subject line.
Configure Einstein Generative AI
Enable Einstein generative AI to help draft forms, landing pages, and email subject lines and body
copy. You can remove a business unit or disable the Einstein Generative AI option completely at any
time. The feature isn't enabled by default.
Configure Einstein Generative AI
Enable Einstein generative AI to help draft forms, landing pages, and email subject lines and body copy.

chunks.heading: 15-extending-account-engagement-with-connected-apps
chunks.body:
---
title: "Extending Account Engagement with Connected Apps"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_account_engagement_4-7-2026.pdf"

extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Extending Account Engagement with Connected Apps
You can remove a business unit or disable the Einstein Generative AI option completely at any time. The
feature isn't enabled by default.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Available in: Enterprise, Performance, and Unlimited Editions with Account Engagement
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To enable Einstein generative AI:
Customize Application
OR
Modify All Data
OR
Manage marketing setup tasks
11.. Enable Einstein in your org.
aa.. In Salesforce Setup, search for Einstein.
bb.. Under Einstein Generative AI, select Einstein Setup and turn on Einstein.
22.. Select the business units that can use the generative AI features.
aa.. In Salesforce Setup, under Einstein Account Engagement, select Einstein Assistant.
bb.. Select the business units that can use Einstein Assistant, and then click Connect.
Extending Account Engagement with Connected Apps
A connector enables Account Engagement to sync with a third-party application, like a webinar service or
Google Ads, so that you don't have to manage content and tasks in multiple apps. The connection passes
data back and forth between the two applications.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: All Account Engagement Editions
Google Analytics Connector
The Google Analytics connector pulls UTM parameters into Account Engagement, where they can then
sync with Salesforce. Five Google Analytics tags are collected: campaign name, medium, source,
content, and term. This connector simplifies the flow of Google Analytics data between Account
Engagement and Salesforce.
Streamline Tasks with Marketing Cloud Account Engagement for Slack

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Extending Account Engagement with Connected Apps
Automatically notify teams about important prospect activity with the Marketing Cloud Account
Engagement app for Slack. Install the Account Engagement app in your Slack workspace and set up
the connector in your business unit. Then, the completion action to notify a Slack channel is available
for marketing assets in Account Engagement.
Google Ads Connector
Account Engagement connects with your Google Ads account to track prospects that reach you
through paid search. You can tie cost data from Google Ads to opportunity data from Salesforce to
determine your true cost per qualified lead and search engine marketing ROI.
Social Media and Social Posting Connectors
Social media connectors enrich prospect data and post content to Facebook and LinkedIn.
Add the Bitly Connector
You can generate a default Bitly short link for landing pages, multivariate tests, and files. To track link
performance, set up the Bitly connector. If you use a custom domain Bitly account, that domain is
used in your shortened URLs.
Add the UserVoice Connector
To add suggestions, support tickets, and other UserVoice data to prospect records, add the UserVoice
connector.
Add the Twilio Connector
If you use Twilio, create a connector to instantly follow up with prospects via phone. The selected user
gets a call when a prospect submits a connected form so that the user can connect with the prospect
by phone. Twilio activities appear on prospect records, where you can view details about calls.
Integrating with Olark
Use the Olark connector to create prospects from your chats and view chat activities on prospect
records.
Integrating with Wistia
Wistia is a video hosting service that measures engagement with your content. Use the Wistia
connector to record prospects' video activity and create prospects from video conversions.
Integrating with WordPress.org Sites
Use the Pardot WordPress plug-in to add marketing forms and dynamic content to your WordPress
pages and posts without using code. The plug-in is available only for sites that use WordPress.org.
GoToWebinar Connector
To bring GoToWebinar data into Account Engagement, set up the connector.
Integrating with Eventbrite
Account Engagement syncs with Eventbrite to provide reporting and scoring on event registrations and
attendance and track prospect events activity.
Integrate with Third-Party Site Search
Integrate your third-party site search to report on prospect queries. Searches on your website show a
prospect's intent. A prospect searching your site for "pricing" is a more clear indicator of interest than
just a pricing page view. Site search details vary by provider, so these instructions are general
guidelines.
Tracking Search Keywords and Competitor SEO
Search marketing tools help you monitor your site and search results. Add keywords that you want to

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Extending Account Engagement with Connected Apps
monitor, or use a default list of common keywords.
Google Analytics Connector
The Google Analytics connector pulls UTM parameters into Account Engagement, where they can then
sync with Salesforce. Five Google Analytics tags are collected: campaign name, medium, source, content,
and term. This connector simplifies the flow of Google Analytics data between Account Engagement and
Salesforce.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: All Account Engagement Editions
Considerations for Using the Google Analytics Connector
Before using the Google Analytics connector with Salesforce, keep these considerations in mind.
Add the Google Analytics Connector
Adding the Google Analytics connector can simplify the flow of information between Google Analytics,
Account Engagement, and Salesforce.
Considerations for Using the Google Analytics Connector
Before using the Google Analytics connector with Salesforce, keep these considerations in mind.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: All Account Engagement Editions
Note Pardot is now known as Marketing Cloud Account Engagement. We wish we could snap our
fingers to update the name everywhere, but you can expect to see the previous name in a few
places until we replace it, including in the app itself.
This connector doesn't sync directly with Google Analytics. It captures Google Analytics keyword tags on
your marketing assets.
This connector is compatible with Google Analytics 4.
To capture keyword tags, add Google Analytics tracking code to your marketing assets, such as forms and
landing pages.
Google Analytics data appears on new prospect records that originate from Google Analytics-tagged
links. When an existing prospect clicks a tagged link, no changes are made to their record.
The "utm_source" value captured by Google Analytics parameters populates the Source field in the
Google Analytics section of a prospect record. It can also be set for the default Source field.

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Extending Account Engagement with Connected Apps
The "utm_campaign" value captured by the Google Analytics parameters populates the Campaign field
in the Google Analytics section of a prospect record. To create Account Engagement campaigns instead,
enable Automatically create Pardot campaigns and associate prospects to Pardot campaigns from
Google Analytics campaigns.
UTM is updated only for the first touchpoint.
UTM parameters longer than 50 characters are truncated.
Fields in the Google Analytics connector use Account Engagement values and can't be changed.
Add the Google Analytics Connector
Adding the Google Analytics connector can simplify the flow of information between Google Analytics,
Account Engagement, and Salesforce.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: All Account Engagement Editions
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To add the Google Analytics connector: Account Engagement Administrator role
11.. Open the Connectors page.
••
In Account Engagement, select Admin and then select Connectors.
••
In the Lightning app, select Account Engagement Settings and then Connectors.
22.. Click + Add Connector.
33.. Click Google Analytics.
44.. Click Create Connector.
Streamline Tasks with Marketing Cloud Account Engagement for
Slack
Automatically notify teams about important prospect activity with the Marketing Cloud Account
Engagement app for Slack. Install the Account Engagement app in your Slack workspace and set up the
connector in your business unit. Then, the completion action to notify a Slack channel is available for
marketing assets in Account Engagement.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: All Account Engagement Editions
Available in: Slack (where approved by a workspace admin)
See Also

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Extending Account Engagement with Connected Apps
Salesforce Help: Marketing Cloud Account Engagement for Slack
Google Ads Connector
Account Engagement connects with your Google Ads account to track prospects that reach you through
paid search. You can tie cost data from Google Ads to opportunity data from Salesforce to determine
your true cost per qualified lead and search engine marketing ROI.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Account Engagement Plus, Advanced, and Premium Editions
Considerations for Using Google Ads
With the Google Ads connector, you can track prospects via paid search. Before using the Google Ads
connector with Account Engagement, keep these considerations in mind.
Add the Google Ads Connector
Add the Google Ads connector to track prospects that reach your marketing assets via paid search. To
add the connector, you need an Account Engagement admin and a Google Ads admin.
Set Up Tracking for Google Ads
To track Google Ads in Account Engagement, add a Final URL Suffix to each ad. It's up to you on
whether to place the suffix on your Google Ads account, ad groups, campaigns, individual ads, or
keywords.
Change the Default Google Ads Campaign
Prospects that come to your site for the first time through a Google ad are tagged with the Google Ads
campaign in Account Engagement. After the initial Google Ads campaign sync, an admin can change
this default campaign in your business unit settings.
Change Individual Ads Campaigns
You can change the Account Engagement campaign that prospects are tagged with for individual
Google Ads campaigns.
Tracking Google Ads Conversions
To track prospect conversions, you can add your Google Ads conversion code to hosted marketing
forms. To track Google Ads conversions, add your conversion code to the Thank You Code area of the
hosted form. If your form redirects to a separate Thank You Page, place your Google Ads conversion
code on the Thank You Page that you redirect to.
Considerations for Using Google Ads
With the Google Ads connector, you can track prospects via paid search. Before using the Google Ads
connector with Account Engagement, keep these considerations in mind.

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Extending Account Engagement with Connected Apps
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Account Engagement Plus, Advanced, and Premium Editions
•
Tracked parameters are {creative}, {matchtype}, {keyword}, {network}, and {device}.
•
To use the connector to capture other field values in Account Engagement, such as campaign, source,
or medium, disable auto-tagging. When auto-tagging is enabled, you must manually add parameters
with Google's URL builder.
•
The connector shares insights about your Google Ads, but you continue to manage your ads account
from Google.
•
After you verify the Google Ads connector, you can't change the account number.
•
If you have multiple Google Ads accounts, add a Google Ads connector for each account.
Add the Google Ads Connector
Add the Google Ads connector to track prospects that reach your marketing assets via paid search. To
add the connector, you need an Account 

…

chunks.heading: 16-account-engagement-for-enterprise-customers
chunks.body:
---
title: "Account Engagement for Enterprise Customers"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_account_engagement_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "766-771"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Account Engagement for Enterprise Customers

| Metric | Definition |
| --- | --- |
|  | backlinks, referring domains, organic search traffic, and other URL data. |
| Domain Authority | A score on a 100-point scale that measures the overall quality and SEO performance of a domain. The score is based on the number of backlinks, referring domains, organic search traffic, and other data for the URL's root domain. |
| Links to Root Domain | The number of external links, including no follow links, that point to the root domain of the URL. |
| Root Domain External Links | The number of follow links that point to the URL's root domain. |

Account Engagement for Enterprise Customers
If your database contains millions of prospects, use your features efficiently to ensure the best possible
performance.
Salesforce Connector for Enterprise Customers
Connect Account Engagement with Salesforce to sync information between prospects, contacts, leads,
and other objects. If you have millions of prospects in your database, use these tips to ensure the best
performance.
Email Marketing for Enterprise Customers
The powerful email sending tools in Account Engagement help you connect you with your prospects.
If you have millions of prospects in your database, use these tips to ensure the best performance.
Dynamic Lists for Enterprise Customers
Dynamic lists in Account Engagement automatically add or remove prospects based on the criteria
that you set up. If you have millions of prospects in your database, use these tips to ensure the best
performance.
Automation Rules for Enterprise Customers
With automation rules in Account Engagement, you can create repeatable, criteria-based rules that
find matching prospects and apply actions to them. If you have millions of prospects in your database,
use these tips to ensure the best performance.
Salesforce Connector for Enterprise Customers
Connect Account Engagement with Salesforce to sync information between prospects, contacts, leads,
and other objects. If you have millions of prospects in your database, use these tips to ensure the best
performance.

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Account Engagement for Enterprise Customers
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: All Account Engagement Editions
System Resources
The amount of system resources that are used by the Salesforce connector is related to how often
information changes in either system. The number of prospects in your database can also affect system
resources during a sync. Another factor to consider is data skew, which affects performance, because
sometimes when a mapped object changes, all of its related records are synced.
Best Practices for Connecting to Salesforce
By using the Salesforce connector wisely, business units with millions of prospects can have the best
possible performance.
Reduce Data Skew
Where possible, reduce data skew in your Salesforce object model. To reduce data skew and
improve performance, create multiple business units instead of a single generic one.
Limit Object Relationships
When information is synced from a Salesforce object, it can sync all the related objects. In
general, try to have fewer than 30 objects related to each Salesforce object that you sync.
Map Required Information Only
When setting up the Salesforce connector, map only the information that you need.
See Also
Data Skew and Account Engagement
Email Marketing for Enterprise Customers
The powerful email sending tools in Account Engagement help you connect you with your prospects. If
you have millions of prospects in your database, use these tips to ensure the best performance.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: All Account Engagement Editions
System Resources
As with all email sending providers, Account Engagement can send a fixed number of emails per hour. In
general, the amount of time it takes to send an email is directly related to the number of recipients on

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Account Engagement for Enterprise Customers
the distribution list. Emails with more than 1 MB of text content can take more time to process and send.
Images and variable tags don't affect performance.
Best Practices for Email Marketing
Using email marketing wisely helps deliver great performance, even when your distribution lists have
many prospects.
Choose the Best Order for Email Sends
Account Engagement sends emails in the order they're scheduled. Collaborate with your team
and decide the best order for scheduling the emails, including A/B testing emails and campaign
emails. Consider that automation rules and engagement programs can also send emails.
Consider Smaller Distribution Lists
If you can, create and use smaller distribution lists. These emails are sent more quickly.
Schedule Large Email Sends Early
It takes time to prepare and send an email, and the amount of time it takes is related to the
number of recipients. We recommend that you schedule large email sends a little earlier than
you want them to be delivered. For example, let's say that you have a distribution list of with
more than ten thousand recipients. We recommend that you set up the email send a couple of
hours before the scheduled send time.
Dynamic Lists for Enterprise Customers
Dynamic lists in Account Engagement automatically add or remove prospects based on the criteria that
you set up. If you have millions of prospects in your database, use these tips to ensure the best
performance.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: All Account Engagement Editions
When you create a dynamic list, Account Engagement checks each of your prospects to see if they
belong on the list. This initial process takes several hours if you have millions of prospects in your
database.
Dynamic lists continually check for updated prospects. When a prospect's information changes, the
dynamic lists checks if they must be added to or removed from the list. When prospect information
changes frequently, it can take several hours for a changed prospect to be added to an existing dynamic
list.
Example Suppose that you have a dynamic list that contains prospects who have downloaded a

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Account Engagement for Enterprise Customers
white paper. If the prospect Kim downloads a whitepaper, Kim is added to the dynamic list within a
few hours.
System Resources
A dynamic list refreshes constantly, adding prospects who match the criteria and removing prospects
who don't. The amount of resources used to keep dynamic lists up to date depends on how often your
prospect information changes. It also depends on how many dynamic lists you have. For example, these
three activities can cause dynamic lists to be updated.
•
Syncing information from Salesforce
•
A prospect submitting a form
•
Using the Account Engagement API to update prospect information
Best Practices for Dynamic Lists
A dynamic list continuously updates. To maximize efficiency in your business unit, keep these points in
mind.
•
Reduce Data Skew-Where possible, reduce the complexity of relationships between objects. To
prevent objects with data skew syncing from Salesforce, consider using multiple business units instead
of a single generic one.
•
Limit the Frequency of API Updates to Prospect Information-If you use the API to update prospect
data, consider running the updates one time per day rather than continuously.
•
Verify Dynamic List Logic-To avoid needlessly consuming system resources, always check your list rules
before you run a preview.
•
Consider Building a Dynamic List Overnight-It takes time and resources to look at each prospect and
decide whether to add them to your dynamic list. Consider waiting until the end of the day before
clicking Run Rules and populating your list.
•
Only Use Completed Lists-After you click Run Rules, we start building your list. If you have millions of
prospects in your database, building a list can take several hours. You receive an email when the
dynamic list is ready to use. Wait until you receive this email before using the list for the first time.
•
Remove or Convert Unnecessary Dynamic Lists-Maintaining a dynamic list uses system resources, so
delete dynamic lists that you no longer need or convert them to static. For best performance, we
recommend maintaining no more than 1,000 dynamic lists. If you move a deleted dynamic list out of
the recycle bin, wait until you receive a confirmation email that the list is restored before sending.
See Also
Data Skew and Account Engagement
Automation Rules for Enterprise Customers
With automation rules in Account Engagement, you can create repeatable, criteria-based rules that find
matching prospects and apply actions to them. If you have millions of prospects in your database, use

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Account Engagement for Enterprise Customers
these tips to ensure the best performance.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: All Account Engagement Editions
When you create an automation rule and click Resume Automation Rule, Account Engagement checks
each of your prospects to see if the rule applies to them. When a prospect matches the rule criteria, the
rule's action is applied. This initial process can take several hours if you have millions of prospects in your
database.
Automation rules continually check for updated prospects. When a prospect's information changes, an
automation rule checks if the rule applies to them. When prospect information changes frequently, it can
take several hours for a rule to run on an updated prospect.
Example You have an automation rule that assigns all prospects with the title CFO to a queue. If a
prospect's title changes to CFO, then the updated prospect is assigned to the queue within a few
hours.
System Resources
An automation rule consumes system resources the first time it runs. The amount of system resources
initially consumed is related to the number of prospects in your system. Automation rules also consume
system resources when your prospect information changes, because rules check to see if the changes
require action. The amount of system resources consumed is related to how frequently your prospect's
information changes, and how many automation rules that you have. For example, these activities can
cause prospect information to change:
•
An email is sent to a prospect
•
A prospect submits a form
•
Information is synced from Salesforce
Best Practices for Automation Rules
Using automation rules wisely can help deliver great performance, even when your database has millions
of prospects.
Give Previews Time to Run
Previewing an automation rule takes the same amount of time and resources as running an
automation rule.
Verify Automation Rule Logic
It's a good idea to check your automation rule's logic before you preview it, because previews
consume system resources. Accidentally using Match Any when you mean to use Match All can

Marketing Cloud Account Engagement Account Engagement for Enterprise Customers
create an automation rule that unintentionally matches millions of prospects.
Use Default Fields on Default Salesforce Objects
Where possible, use default fields on default Salesforce objects in your automation rules.
Account Engagement can evaluate default fields and default objects more quickly than custom
fields and custom objects. For example, a prospect's title or name are evaluated quickly, because
these fields are default fields. Checking a field on a custom object, such as a custom purchase
order object, takes longer.
Avoid Changing an Existing Rule
Creating an au

…

chunks.heading: part-01
chunks.body:
---
title: "Audience Builder (part 1/2)"
source_pdf: "engagement_audience_builder_and_contact_builder_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "6-121"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Audience Builder and Contact Builder Audience Builder
Audience Builder
Segment your customers in a variety of ways with Audience Builder in any Marketing Cloud Engagement
org. Enterprise 2.0 customers can contact an account executive to enable additional features, such as
business unit filters.
Audiences
An audience represents a collection of contacts that are grouped based on attributes that are used when
they receive a message. An audience draws information from a sendable data extension. Audience
Builder sets a flag in the header of the data extension noting it as an audience. Outbound Mail Manager
(OMM) uses this information to determine which channel, audience, and segment engaged the contact
with a message.
You can publish an audience for use in a send or other activities.
Audience Builder can use any lists, groups, or data extensions linked through a common subscriber key
or ID. You can add attributes to Audience Builder without adding more dimensions to them. Audience
Builder only supports queries performed via the expression builder.
Note We recommend using a subscriber key with Audience Builder. You can implement Audience
Builder to use the email address as the customer key.
To send an email via Marketing Cloud Engagement, use the audience data extension template. When
sending to the audience object, subscriber behavior is tracked in the template. Audience Builder also
supports other channels, such as mobile and social.
You can use audiences for suppression with user-initiated and wizard-based sending tools.
Audiences can include ID and code values:
•
IDs and Codes represent distinct entities.
•
Audience Builder requires IDs. Codes represent optional values.
•
IDs uniquely identify the audience object.
•
IDs help reporting and tracking activities. Use these values to manage audiences and segments.
•
The Summary screen lists a field labeled Audience ID that actually displays a code value.
•
Codes don't require unique values. You can use any applicable value as a code.
•
Codes help manage or associate an audience with a campaign.
Audience Builder lists activities in Automation Studio under Data Factory and the Audience Builder
Folder. To pick an audience to refresh, use the Build Audience activity.

Engagement: Audience Builder and Contact Builder Audience Builder
Segments
A segment represents a group of people who meet a set of user-defined criteria. Use segments for
analysis and for targeting in an audience. If you choose not to add a filter, Audience Builder builds
segments using the entire available population of contacts from your account. Audiences don't require
segments or audience filters.
You can append attributes to segments in the Publish tab. Appending attributes to segments associates
contact content with a segment. Also, you can use AMPscript and Server-Side JavaScript to power a
content decision in email. The action is based on a contact's segment assignment in a specific audience.
Reporting
You can use audiences and segments for reporting. Audience Builder provides a report in the standard
email reports section that breaks down the campaign metrics by segment name.
Performance
Every Audience Builder implementation has unique performance characteristics. Work with your
implementation team to understand performance expectations.
All systems that process more instructions take longer to complete, but this operation can occur many
times faster than a standard query.
We recommend one refresh of Audience Builder data per day. The refresh updates lists and data
extensions.
Enterprise Accounts
Audience Builder limits the number of values per dimension to under 1,000. You can use attributes with
more than 100 values. Also, you can manage the total at the enterprise, such as 100 dimensions in
business unit A and 100 dimensions in business unit B. With this method, despite the same number of
dimensions in both business units, the dimensions aren't from the same dataset. By using the custom
attribute selectors, such as starts with, ends with, contains, greater than, and less than,
users can access all the values stored in the attribute and use that for criteria in a selection.
Get Started with Audience Builder
Audience Builder dynamically creates targeted audiences from contacts stored in your account based
on stored attribute and behavioral values. Use these audiences to either target or exclude contacts
from activities in Marketing Cloud Engagement.
Filter Tab
The Filter tab in Audience Builder allows you to take your total population and query it to match the
subscriber data of interest. Filter an audience either for send purposes, such as sending emails to
specific subscribers that match your query target. You can also filter for analytic purposes, such as

Engagement: Audience Builder and Contact Builder Audience Builder
examining the number of subscribers that match a given criteria.
Exclusions Tab
Use the Exclusions tab in Audience Builder to create suppression criteria from your attribute library.
You can then remove a subset of contacts from a filtered audience or segment.
Segment Tab
Segment an audience in Audience Builder to suppress certain subscribers from either your total
population or a filtered population.
Audience Publishing
After you filter and segment your audience in Audience Builder, you can publish your audience as a
publish definition. Publish definitions represent data extensions containing the filter and segment data
you defined in the audience. Use publish definitions across the Marketing Cloud Engagement in other
applications. Access your published definition in the Audience data extension folder, located in Email
Studio under the Subscribers tab.
Dimension Packages
Dimension packages describe the way you use Audience Builder to interact with the subscriber data in
Marketing Cloud Engagement. Dimension packages translate raw subscriber attributes into readable
information in your Attribute Library. Use this information to build and segment audiences.
Audience Builder for Administrators
A Marketing Cloud Engagement admin can manage user access to Audience Builder attributes,
existing dimensions, and segment templates.
Audience Builder Attribute Library
Use the Attribute Library in Audience Builder to drag subscriber criteria onto the canvas as you filter or
segment your audience. The Attribute Library displays raw subscriber data as readable attribute labels.
The data that appears in the Attribute Library depends on the Data Packages in your Marketing Cloud
Engagement org.
Audience Builder Scenarios
Review these Audience Builder scenarios for examples on how to use this Marketing Cloud
Engagement feature. These scenarios use sample data that doesn't always match your own data.
Audiences and Segments
These diagrams show the relationship between audience filters and segments in Audience Builder for
Marketing Cloud Engagement.
Operator Use Cases
When you define and segment your audience in Audience Builder, work with operators to change the
logic of your expressions.
Publish an Audience to Salesforce Data Extension Folder
Publish an audience of your subscribers to the Salesforce data extension folder so that you can use a
user-initiated Salesforce send. Marketing Cloud Engagement receives only subscriber tracking data
that's returned from Salesforce. Subscribers without a Salesforce ID can't send data back to Salesforce.
Automation Studio Activities for Audience Builder
You can increase productivity in Marketing Cloud Engagement when you use a combination of tools.
Audience Builder actions are available as activities in Automation Studio and are related to an Email
Studio program. Add one or more activities to an automation to accomplish your tasks.

Engagement: Audience Builder and Contact Builder Audience Builder
Supported Characters for Audience Builder
Audience Builder supports these characters for the specified locations.
Get Started with Audience Builder
Audience Builder dynamically creates targeted audiences from contacts stored in your account based on
stored attribute and behavioral values. Use these audiences to either target or exclude contacts from
activities in Marketing Cloud Engagement.
Audiences
The audience represents a basic group of contacts determined by the rules and attributes associated
with the audience. The audience dynamically changes the contacts included based on how these
attribute and behavioral values change.
Audience Builder Rules
Audience Builder uses rules you assign to create audiences. For example, you can create an audience
that includes all contacts who live in Colorado and purchased ski equipment within the past six months.
Use that audience to either send a message to those contacts regarding a new offer or to keep them
from receiving a "We Miss You" message.
Audience Builder Attributes
Contact Builder stores attribute values for each contact, and Audience Builder uses the values for these
attributes to determine whether the contact appears within an audience. For example, if a contact
includes a value of Phoenix for Home City, that contact appears in an audience that includes Phoenix
residents. If that contact moves to another city and updates that attribute value, a refreshed version of
that audience doesn't include that contact.
Filter Tab
The Filter tab in Audience Builder allows you to take your total population and query it to match the
subscriber data of interest. Filter an audience either for send purposes, such as sending emails to specific
subscribers that match your query target. You can also filter for analytic purposes, such as examining the
number of subscribers that match a given criteria.
Filter an Audience
Follow these steps to filter an audience in Audience Builder.
Audience Builder Channels
Audience Builder can add filters to your overall population, such as by channel. When Marketing Cloud
Engagement includes more than one channel-activated application, the channel selector filter appears
in the Filter tab.

Engagement: Audience Builder and Contact Builder Audience Builder
Modify the Label and Inclusion Logic for Criteria
Follow these steps to modify the label and inclusion logic for criteria in Audience Builder.
Group Audience Criteria
Follow these steps to group audience criteria in Audience Builder.
Change Group Operators
In Audience Builder for Marketing Cloud Engagement, you can change the operator used on an
attribute group.
Filter an Audience
Follow these steps to filter an audience in Audience Builder.
11.. Click Create Audience or select a saved audience from the list. The audience opens on the Filter tab
when you create a new audience or the Summary tab when you select a saved audience.
22.. Replace the Untitled Audience label with your audience name and click Done.
33.. On the Filter tab, drag applicable criteria from the Attribute Library to the Define Audience Criteria
canvas by completing these steps:
aa.. To populate the filter with values and standard operators, such as Equals, Greater than, Less than,
and Range, select an item in the Attribute Library. If you use the drill tree Attribute Library, drill
down on attributes until you see applicable values.
bb.. Drag your criteria or custom criteria selectors to the Filter canvas. After adding new criteria, the blue
Refresh button appears. To refresh your counts, click Refresh. You can select multiple attributes.
You cannot select multiple standard operators.
cc.. To refresh your counts, click Refresh. Click Cancel to stop the refresh.
44.. Click Save Audience.
Audience Builder Channels
Audience Bu

…

Web Analytics Dimension Package Technical Specifications
The Web Analytics Dimension Package Set includes standard dimension packages derived from a
standardized data extension-based data model. It brings web analytics data into Audience Builder
without needing custom implementation.

Engagement: Audience Builder and Contact Builder Audience Builder
Web Analytics Dimension Package Technical Specifications
The Web Analytics Dimension Package Set includes standard dimension packages derived from a
standardized data extension-based data model. It brings web analytics data into Audience Builder
without needing custom implementation.
The Audience Builder Web Analytics Dimension Package Set contains these components.
•
Data extension templates in Email Studio
•
Dimension package set in Audience Builder
These data extensions are required. Review the fields and details in the tables.
•
Standard Page Browse data extension
•
Standard Cart Abandon data extension
•
Standard Product Purchase data extension
•
Standard Product Catalog 3-Level data extension
Standard Page Browse Data Extension
This data extension acts as a universal page or product browse event repository. While some analytics
providers separate this information into two or more output files, we chose to merge them for the sake
of simplicity. For ProductID values, we perform a lookup on the Product Catalog Data Extension
(STD_PRODUCT_CATALOG_3LEVEL) and use the related product data to populate various dimension
packages with the related Product Data.

| Related Dimension Packages |  |
| --- | --- |
| • Products Browsed By SKU • Product Browse Count By Days • Products Browsed By Date • Products Browsed By Days • Products Browsed By Hierarchy • Products Browsed By Name | • Page Browse Count By Days • Page Category Count By Days • Categories Browsed By Days • Categories Browsed By Name • Pages Browsed By Date • Pages Browsed By Days • Pages Browsed By Name |

Because this value often contains an email address or other PII-type data, some web analytics providers choose to use the Subscriber ID bigint value from Marketing Cloud Engagement. - **Split:** 2 files (~2500 lines each) for easier search and AI context windows.

chunks.heading: part-01
chunks.body:
---
title: "Contact Builder in Marketing Cloud Engagement (part 1/2)"
source_pdf: "engagement_audience_builder_and_contact_builder_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "122-185"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Contact Builder in Marketing Cloud Engagement
Contact Builder in Marketing Cloud Engagement
The Contact Builder app provides access to contact data contained within your account and the
relationships designed to help you use that data. Use Contact Builder to manage, consolidate, organize,
and link data from all Marketing Cloud Engagement apps and external sources.
•
Enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems
•
Customer relationship management (CRM) systems
•
Point-Of-Sale (POS) systems
Contact Builder provides a single view of a customer that displays their interactions with your brand. You
can use the collected information to personalize customer conversations at any size in any channel. That
single view of the customer can contain the mobile number necessary to send SMS messages, the email
address to send email messages, and the mobile device identification used for sending push messages.
All associated address, subscription, and tracking information associated with activities and journeys
relate back to that single contact record.
Contact Builder provides several tools used to manage contact data for use in building 1:1 relationships.
•
Contacts Configuration-Determine how Contact Builder processes imported contact information.
•
Data Designer-Define information about your contacts and relate that data directly to the contact
record by linking data extensions.
•
Data Extensions-Create and manage the data extensions that hold contact information.
•
Imports-Create the processes that move contact information into your data extensions.
•
Data Sources-Visualize where your contact data originates and assign attributes to those sources.
Get Started with Contact Builder
New accounts include Contacts and Contact Builder. Account administrators with existing accounts
can opt to replace their current data structure with the new Contacts structure. After switching to the
Contacts structure, your account can't return to the previous data structure.
All Contacts in Contact Builder
Reference information for the All Contacts screen in Contact Builder
Contact Builder Data Sources
Data sources in Contact Builder indicate where your account stores and locates contact attributes. To
determine the origin of the attribute values that you use to filter and segment contacts, use data
sources in Marketing Cloud Engagement. When you know the origin, you can use the most recent and
effective data to connect your attribute groups for your communication activities. For example, you
can tag customer purchasing data as arriving from an outside source. Other users acting on this data
can then identify the custom data source and use the most up-to-date data in your activities.
Contact Builder Events
Events in Contact Builder allow you to take contact behavior into account as you segment and filter
your audience. Use this information to begin marketing processes and activities within your account,
including Journey Builder. The events collect data based on what actions your contact performs or
occurrences involving your contact. You can target that behavior and modify your communications to
better target that contact. For example, you can find out which contacts abandoned shopping carts

Engagement: Audience Builder and Contact Builder Contact Builder in Marketing Cloud Engagement
and contact them to complete their order.
Data Extensions in Contact Builder
Data extensions in Contact Builder form the basis of contact information in Marketing Cloud
Engagement. By creating and linking data extensions, you can access information about contacts and
use that information to segment, filter, and target contacts with relevant sends. You can also use this
data to target contacts across multiple channels, such as email, social, and mobile messaging. Finally,
you can use data extensions to hold information imported from external sources and use that
information as part of your segmenting, filtering, and targeting activities.
Contact Builder Imports
The Imports tab in Contact Builder displays a complete list of the import definitions within your
account. Use import definitions to bring data linked to your contacts into your account.
Data Designer in Contact Builder
The Data Designer tool in Contact Builder defines, organizes, and relates information to contacts
within your account. All contact information resides on subscriber lists and data extensions contained
in Marketing Cloud Engagement. Data Designer enables you to manage how data extensions store
that information and how those data extensions relate to each other.
Contacts Analytics in Contact Builder
Contacts Analytics shows the status of your contact delete requests via either the Marketing Cloud
Engagement Contact Builder app or your REST API calls. Use this dashboard to view four separate
types of contact delete statistics. Use this dashboard in your top-level account.
Enable Contact Events in Contact Builder
Enabling Contact Events in Marketing Cloud Engagement requires admin permissions. It's scheduled
for feature retirement, and we recommend that you filter or query activities in Automation Studio.
Contact Definition and Count Determination in Contact Builder
A contact in Contact Builder is an individual added to any contact database in Marketing Cloud
Engagement. A contact can be someone who received a message from any Engagement tool, such as
GroupConnect, Journey Builder, MobileConnect, or MobilePush.
Contact Builder Best Practices
Contact Builder helps you maintain the contact information you use to identify individual contacts
across multiple channels and personalize messaging and marketing activities. To ensure that your
marketing activities work the way you want them to, make sure that all contact information relates to a
single contact record.
Get Started with Contact Builder
New accounts include Contacts and Contact Builder. Account administrators with existing accounts can
opt to replace their current data structure with the new Contacts structure. After switching to the
Contacts structure, your account can't return to the previous data structure.
When you use the Contacts data structure, you can expect these changes.
•
The Contact Builder app appears in the app switcher. Manage your contact data and data extensions
in this app.

Engagement: Audience Builder and Contact Builder Contact Builder in Marketing Cloud Engagement
•
Subscriber information is stored in the Contacts framework and appears in the All Contacts view. A
Contact Detail page is a cross-channel view of a single person and the associated information.
•
If your account doesn't use data extensions, your subscriber information resides in an attribute group
set up for your account. Use Data Designer to manage this attribute group.
•
All attributes from Email Studio and any Mobile apps are available in Data Designer. Manage, filter, and
edit the attributes within Data Designer.
•
You must edit Email Profile Attributes in Email Studio at this time. However, Data Designer recognizes
edits or additions and can use these values for filtering in Data Designer.
•
You can use Contacts and Data Designer without using the Subscriber Key feature.
•
Contact Builder uses customer-supplied data and app-defined data, such as data collected by
Engagement or third-party apps.
•
Perform complex filters using the entire Contacts data model to create or edit lists for use in your
cross-channel marketing efforts.
11.. Navigate Through Contact Builder
Find out how to get around the Contact Builder app in Marketing Cloud Engagement.
22.. Attribute Group Creation in Contact Builder
Attribute groups enable you to organize your data in Contact Builder. Each attribute group includes a
data model consisting of data extensions linked to either the contact record or other data extensions.
You can create attribute groups including all relevant data extensions at the same time to ensure that
all necessary data resides in the correct group.
33.. Data Extension Linking in Contact Builder
Link data extensions in Contact Builder to relate data in existing data extensions to either the contact
record or another data extension.
44.. Data Source Creation in Contact Builder
Data Sources establish where the data contained within Contact Builder originates. You can assign
attributes to these data sources and view precisely how data enters your account and how you can
best use that data.
55.. Contacts Configuration
Use the Contact Configuration screen to enable Contact Builder functionality.
66.. Data Import
You can import contact data into Marketing Cloud Engagement. You can also save and reuse import
definitions that bring information into Contact Builder.
77.. Contact Builder Access
A Marketing Cloud Engagement admin can restrict which Marketing Cloud Engagement users can
access Contact Builder.
See Also
Data Extensions in Contact Builder
Data Designer in Contact Builder
Contact Builder Imports
Navigate Through Contact Builder
Find out how to get around the Contact Builder app in Marketing Cloud Engagement.

Engagement: Audience Builder and Contact Builder Contact Builder in Marketing Cloud Engagement
11.. From Engagement, select Audience Builder | Contact Builder.
22.. Use the workspace tabs to move through Contact Builder tools.
33.. To organize your data, click Create Attribute Group.
44.. To modify your data model by linking data extensions via similar attributes, click the attribute group.
Attribute Group Creation in Contact Builder
Attribute groups enable you to organize your data in Contact Builder. Each attribute group includes a
data model consisting of data extensions linked to either the contact record or other data extensions.
You can create attribute groups including all relevant data extensions at the same time to ensure that all
necessary data resides in the correct group.
After you create your attribute group, you can perform either one of these procedures and begin creating
your data model:
•
Create a data extension linked to the contact record.
•
Link an existing data extension to the contact record.
You can also use templates containing predefined attribute groups within Contact Builder.
These templates include existing data relationships, and you can customize these attributes and
relationships as needed.
See Also
Create an Attribute Group in Contact Builder
Create Attribute Groups with Templates
Manage Data Extensions
Link Data Extensions in Contact Builder
Data Extension Linking in Contact Builder
Link data extensions in Contact Builder to relate data in existing data extensions to either the contact
record or another data extension.
Start your process by linking at least one data extension to a contact record.
•
Contact Builder prompts you to select a data extension to link to the contact record.
•
Your available data extensions appear in the modal. Select the data extension you wish to link.
•
With this step, you create your data model in an attribute group. To most effectively organize your
data, select and link all data extensions that contain information about the specified topic.
Tip The word 'Contact' is a reserved word. As a result, you can't link a data extension called 'Contact'
to an attribute group in Data Designer.
Tip Data Designer doesn't support linking data extensions with non-GSM characters.

Engagement: Audience Builder and Contact Builder Contact Builder in Marketing Cloud Engagement
Select Cardinality
When linking data extensions, you must select the cardinality of the relationship. You can specify one-to-
one, many-to-one,

…

Limit Populations to Three Entities
Use populations to create large and distinct subgroups of your contacts, then segment contact records
from there. For example, a doctor's office creates populations for staff, for patients, and for vendors. If
you create populations in your account, limit those populations to three or fewer to improve
performance.

Create a Campaign in Marketing Cloud Engagement
Create a Campaign in Marketing Cloud Engagement
Create a campaign in the Campaigns app.
11.. Open the Campaigns app, and click Create Campaign.
22.. Perform these actions on the Create New Campaign page.
aa.. Give your campaign a name that makes it easy to identify. You can enter up to 50 characters.
bb.. Add an optional description to identify the purpose of the campaign.
cc.. Assign a calendar color to your campaign.
dd.. Choose when to begin the campaign.
ee.. Choose a campaign owner.
ff.. Assign the campaign an optional unique code so that you can identify it in your reports.
gg.. If you use tags to organize and track campaigns, select the tags.
33.. Save the campaign.
See Also
Edit or Delete a Campaign in Marketing Cloud Engagement
Add or Remove Campaign Associations in Marketing Cloud Engagement

Campaign Tags in Marketing Cloud Engagement
Use tags in Marketing Cloud Engagement to categorize campaigns by any criteria, such as geography,
marketing tactic, and product line.
For example, each campaign for a monthly newsletter can have the tag "newsletter." You can aggregate
campaign analytics with the "newsletter" tag in reports. See Campaign Email Tracking and Multi-
Campaign Email Tracking Summary.
Create campaigns and add tags to new or existing campaigns on the Campaign Overview page. You can
associate multiple tags to a campaign and add or remove them at any time.
Filter Campaign Tags in Marketing Cloud Engagement
Learn about filtering tags for campaigns in Marketing Cloud Engagement.
See Also
Create a Campaign in Marketing Cloud Engagement
Filter Campaign Tags in Marketing Cloud Engagement
Learn about filtering tags for campaigns in Marketing Cloud Engagement.

Campaign Associations in Marketing Cloud Engagement
Overview Filtering with Tags
You can filter your view of the campaign overview list based on tags.
Selecting a tag filter displays the campaigns associated with that tag.
Calendar Filtering with Tags
You can filter your view of the campaign planning calendar based on tags. Selecting a tag filter displays
the campaigns that are related to that tag.
Campaign Associations in Marketing Cloud Engagement
A campaign association is a channel-specific message, content, or activity you want to include in the
campaign.
Campaign associations are cross-channel objects used to build campaigns and help you do these other
tasks.
•
Organize the associations needed to execute a campaign
•
Plan messaging in the short and long-term future
•
Act as parameters for analytics around a marketing objective
Add or Remove Campaign Associations in Marketing Cloud Engagement
You can add or remove an association to a campaign from the Campaigns app or from the app where
the association lives.
Edit or Delete a Campaign in Marketing Cloud Engagement
You can edit or delete a campaign.
See Also
Add or Remove Campaign Associations in Marketing Cloud Engagement
Create a Campaign in Marketing Cloud Engagement
Add or Remove Campaign Associations in Marketing Cloud
Engagement
You can add or remove an association to a campaign from the Campaigns app or from the app where
the association lives.

chunks.heading: 05-engagement-campaign-faqs
chunks.body:
---
title: "Engagement Campaign FAQs"
source_pdf: "engagement_campaigns_4-6-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "8-9"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Engagement Campaign FAQs
Add an Association to a Campaign
11.. On the Campaigns Overview page, select a campaign.
22.. Click Add to Campaign, and select a campaign association from the list.
33.. Go to the item that you want to add as an association and select it. A check mark appears next to the
item. Click again to remove it.
44.. Click Finished.
When you add an association to a campaign, only the future activity for that association is associated
with the campaign.
Remove an Association from a Campaign
11.. On the Campaigns Overview page, select the campaign that you want to remove the association from.
22.. In the campaign's storyboard, hover over the association name and click Remove from Campaign.
33.. Click Remove.
Removing an association from a campaign doesn't delete the association. It's still available in
Engagement.
Edit or Delete a Campaign in Marketing Cloud Engagement
You can edit or delete a campaign.
11.. To edit a campaign, select it on the Campaigns Overview page, click the edit icon, and then edit the
campaign. Changes are saved automatically.
22.. To delete a campaign, select it on the Campaigns Overview page, click the delete icon, and then click
Confirm.
To delete a campaign, users must have delete permissions.
Warning Deleting a campaign removes all associations and then deletes the campaign from the
system. Tagged objects aren't deleted.
Engagement Campaign FAQs
Learn about where to find things and how to use the Campaigns app in Marketing Cloud Engagement.
Where are my old Campaigns? I don't see them on the campaign Overview
tab.
Campaigns that contain an email that has been sent automatically have their status set to Deployed. By

Engagement: Campaigns Engagement Campaign FAQs
default, the Overview tab only shows Campaigns with a status of In Process. To view older campaigns
that have a status of Deployed, simply adjust the filter in the upper left.
Setting the campaign status to Deployed now happens automatically when associated emails are sent.
This feature was enabled in January 2014.
What's the difference between the campaign Overview tab and the
Campaign Summary tab?
The campaign Summary tab is the campaigns list view originally implemented when Campaigns
launched. It has columns for Campaign Code, Last Activity, and Next Activity. The campaign Overview
tab has a column for Campaign Status, Deployment Date, Campaign Owner, and, if enabled, Approval
status.
What's the difference between the Campaign status and Approvals status
columns on the Campaign Overview?
Campaign status has two options: In Process or Deployed. Each campaign starts by default in the In
Process state. After the first job is sent, the status changes to Deployed. The approval status displays
these states on the Approvals tab: Draft, Submitted, Reviewed, Approved, Returned, and Withdrawn.
What's the difference between the Campaign Planning Calendar and the
calendar in Marketing Cloud Engagement?
You use the Campaign Planning Calendar to schedule future campaigns. The calendar in Marketing
Cloud Engagement shows past, current, and near future activity, past sends, and scheduled sends.
Why don't I see my storyboard data when I open a campaign?
To load the storyboard data for a campaign, open the campaign and click STORYBOARD.
Can I associate a campaign to a Salesforce data extension?
No, data extensions that live in the Salesforce data extension folder aren't available to relate to a
campaign.

Content Builder and CloudPages Content Builder
88.. Add other styles or HTML as needed.
Use a Smart Capture Form in Journey Builder
You can initiate activities after someone submits a Smart Capture form on a landing page. For
example, send a confirmation email or add the person as a lead or contact in Sales Cloud. You can add
a Smart Capture form only to a multistep journey.
Use a Smart Capture Form in Journey Builder
You can initiate activities after someone submits a Smart Capture form on a landing page. For example,
send a confirmation email or add the person as a lead or contact in Sales Cloud. You can add a Smart
Capture form only to a multistep journey.
If your journey includes an email, before you activate the journey, complete the email's Content
Detective and Validation steps. Use each Smart Capture form one time per journey.
11.. Create a landing page that contains a Smart Capture form block.
22.. In Journey Builder, create a multistep journey.
33.. Select the CloudPages entry source, and drag it to the entry source target.
44.. Define the entry source.
aa.. Click the entry source icon.
bb.. Select the form from the list, and then click Next.
cc.. Review the summary, and then click Done.
55.. Drag in other activities and controls to configure the journey.
If you're using a Smart Capture form as a trigger event, configure the re-entry step, and the default
channel address in the journey's settings.
Review CloudPages Analytics
CloudPages offers built-in analytics that track views of a page over the past 90 days. To see more
information, create a report in Analytics Builder.
You can choose from Microsite Tracking, CloudPages Impressions, or CloudPages Smart Capture
Submissions reports.
11.. Open a CloudPages folder, and locate the content that you want to review.
22.. From the page's thumbnail or the editing view, click , and select Activity.
33.. To filter what's shown, adjust the start and end dates, and then click Submit.
Content Builder
Content Builder is our cross-channel content management tool that allows you to consolidate images,
documents, and content in a single location for use in Marketing Cloud Engagement. With an easy-to-

Engagement: Content Builder and CloudPages Content Builder
use, drag and drop interface, Content Builder is more efficient than our previous create and edit tools.
Content Builder also includes the content repository (CMS) and API, the content management
application (formerly referred to as Content Canvas), and the new HTML content editor (formerly
referred to as Content Builder).
Get Started with Content Builder
Content Builder consolidates images, documents, and content in a single location for use in Marketing
Cloud Engagement. Create subject lines and body copy for messages by harnessing the power of
Einstein generative AI. Use the third-party Typeface integration option to generate images to
completely tailor your message.
Content Builder FAQs
Review frequently asked questions about Marketing Cloud Engagement Content Builder.
Content Builder Editor Best Practices and Tips
Learn best practices and tips for the Content Builder editor in Marketing Cloud.
Upload Files
Upload content to Content Builder by dragging and dropping existing content, by browsing your
computer for the content, or by uploading content from an external URL. The maximum file size per
file is 5 MB.
Create Emails in Content Builder
Use this task to create an email in Content Builder for Marketing Cloud Engagement. Email names
must be unique within a folder.
Create Templates
Use Content Builder to create templates by pasting HTML or using a layout tool.
Create Mobile Messages
Create SMS, push notification, and LINE messages in Marketing Cloud Engagement Content Builder.
Create Content Blocks
Use content blocks to create reusable modular content for messages. Manage content blocks in
Content Builder.
Create a Code Snippet
Use code snippets in Marketing Cloud Engagement Content Builder to save reusable bits of HTML,
AMPscript, and Guide Template Language. Insert snippets when editing HTML via the Code Snippets
toolbar selector, or by typing the Customer Key value for the snippet followed by the Tab key. You can
also create a code snippet content block by clicking Create > Content Blocks > Code Snippet. You can
reference Code Snippets with the AMPscript ContentBlockByKey and ContentBlockByID functions.
Share Content
The share content feature allows you to share content, templates, and emails with other business units
in your account. Enterprise 2.0 accounts, you can share from any business unit to any other business
unit. For Enterprise 1.0 and Agency accounts you can share down to child business units. Contact your
relationship manager to enable this feature.
Duplicate Content
Use duplicate content in Content Builder to copy the style or format of a previously created content
asset. We recommend copying the structure of a piece of content before adding actual content. When
duplicating a content area in an email or template, the duplicate option is disabled if the content area

Engagement: Content Builder and CloudPages Content Builder
block limit is reached.
Content Builder Approvals in Marketing Cloud Engagement
To review and approve content in Content Builder, use Approvals.
Content Builder Tags
Create and manage nested and Einstein tags in Marketing Cloud Engagement Content Builder.
Sitecore Content
Sitecore content is available via Sitecore Connect for Marketing Cloud Engagement, which enables a
real-time sync of images created and updated in Sitecore to Content Builder.
Content Builder Recycle Bin
Content Builder Recycle Bin is a repository for recently deleted items that can be restored to Marketing
Cloud Engagement. Items moved to the Recycle Bin are unavailable in emails and journeys. You can
restore items from the Recycle Bin and use them again.
Get Started with Content Builder
Content Builder consolidates images, documents, and content in a single location for use in Marketing
Cloud Engagement. Create subject lines and body copy for messages by harnessing the power of Einstein
generative AI. Use the third-party Typeface integration option to generate images to completely tailor
your message.
With the editor, you can:
•
Create professional emails without knowing HTML
•
Preview changes to email content in real time
•
Use intelligent HTML paste tools, such as color coding and line numbers
•
Insert code snippets for adding common links, such as Forward to a Friend and Open Tracking
With the content repository, you can:
•
Use global search and filtering tools
•
Add tags and campaign associations to connect related content
•
Edit all your content inside the application
Content Builder Experience
Explore Content Builder features in Marketing Cloud Engagement.
Content Builder Administration
Access Content Builder setup from the Marketing Cloud Engagement toolbar by hovering over your
username and selecting Setup, then searching for Content Builder Settings.
Customer Key
Each Content Builder asset has a Customer Key, a unique 36 character identifier that can be
referenced via AMPscript.
Search for Content
Content Builder provides several ways to locate specific content.
Folder Management

Engagement: Content Builder and CloudPages Content Builder
Store images, documents, templates, emails, and more in the same or different folders in Marketing
Cloud Engagement Content Builder. Create, rename, move, or delete folders by right-clicking in the
appropriate folder space.
See Also
Content Builder Editor Best Practices and Tips
Content Builder Message Creation Tips
Content Builder Experience
Explore Content Builder features in Marketing Cloud Engagement.
Icon Descriptions
•
- Feature Not Available
•
- Feature Replaced
•
- Feature Changed

Engagement: Content Builder and CloudPages Content Builder
Content Builder Administration
Access Content Builder setup from the Marketing Cloud Engagement toolbar by hovering over your
username and selecting Setup, then searching for Content Builder Settings.
Email Autosave
Users who want to be more intentional about when edits are saved can disable Email Autosave. Disabling
Email Autosave prevents Content Builder from saving accidental edits. We also disabled the timestamp
and modified by fields from being updated when you made no changes, whether you have Email
Autosave enabled or disabled.
When enabled, Content Builder automatically saves all changes in the editor. When disabled, Content
Builder does not autosave when you edit and exit an email before clicking Save, or when you open and
close an email without editing. Content Builder still autosaves when you navigate to a different step of
the wizard.
Email Autosave is enabled by default to minimize disruption. Autosave is only available for editing emails.
Click Edit, check or uncheck EnableEmail Autosave, then click Save.
Across Enterprise Sharing
This setting allows all users to share content with existing and future business units. Click Edit to enable
or disable, then click Save.
Predictable File URLs
Enable this setting to generate a friendlier and more informative name as part of the URL path for
published content by using the file name. By default, Content Builder generates a random number at the
end of the URL. After uploading, you can edit the file name on the properties page, but you can't edit the
URL. You can disable this setting after enabling. The URL uses the structure of the setting at the time of
upload.
If Predictive URL is enabled and you want to update an image but use the same name, use Replace
rather than Delete. Deleting a file removes it from the Content Builder library but the file remains
published by our third party.
Editor Configuration
Administrators can select the editor options they want available for their busines
