Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement
Get Started with Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement
With Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement, you gain access to the next-generation features of
Marketing Cloud Next. You also keep all of the campaigns, data extensions, and journeys that you've
created over the years in Marketing Cloud Engagement. It's the best of both worlds!

| Learn More Access the Features of Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement Where to Start with Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement Message Sending Recommendations | Configure Your Account Connect Marketing Cloud Engagement Set Up Marketing Performance Configure Data 360 Components Assign User Access Synchronize Data Extensions Set Up Business Units Configure Test Environments | Get to Know Campaigns and Flows Add a Journey to a Campaign Send Flow Members to a Journey Create and Use the Journey Decisioning Agent Marketing Cloud Next: How Campaigns and Flows Work Together Marketing Cloud Next: Working with Campaigns and Flows |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Analyze Send and Engagement Data Unified Reporting Concepts and Terminology View Engagement and Delivery Data Create Custom Analytics Reports Key Data Model Object Fields for Reporting Troubleshoot Reporting Issues | Understand Your Marketing Spend Track Message Usage in Digital Wallet Salesforce Message Credits | Discover Additional Resources Video Library for Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement Salesforce Help: Marketing Cloud Next Trailhead: Discover Salesforce Marketing Solutions |

Marketing Cloud Engagement Get Started with Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement
Access the Features of Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement
Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement unifies your existing Marketing Cloud Engagement data with
the Salesforce Platform. This integration enhances your marketing efforts by giving you access to more
features, and by giving you a single view of your overall marketing program. You can access these
features and capabilities from your existing Marketing Cloud Engagement account, or directly through
the Salesforce Platform.
Where to Start with Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement
To find out which next-generation marketing features are the best first steps for you, consider whether
you already use Data 360 or Journey Builder. Think through which features are likely to bring the most
value based on the entities and assets that you already have.
Recommendations for Message Sending
With your Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement subscription, you can continue to send messages by
using the Marketing Cloud Engagement apps you know and love, such as Email Studio, Mobile Studio,
and Journey Builder. You can also augment and extend your existing marketing programs by using
Salesforce Platform tools such as Flow Builder to send your messages. The right product to use
depends on your objectives and use case.
Configure Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement
Before your marketing team can access the next-generation features of Marketing Cloud, get your
account ready by completing these setup steps. For most accounts, the setup process takes about 1-2
hours to complete.
Business Units in Marketing Cloud Next for Marketing Cloud Engagement
Use business units in Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement to scale and control your brand and your
marketing activities. Business units make it possible for divisions within an organization to focus on
their own marketing objectives, while also enabling reporting and access control across the entire
enterprise.
Working with Data Extensions
Bring the data from your existing Marketing Cloud Engagement data extensions into Data 360. Your
data can power the next-generation marketing capabilities of Marketing Cloud Next, including drag-
and-drop segmentation, custom reporting, and flows with the power of Agentforce.
Working with Campaigns and Flows in Marketing Cloud Next for Marketing Cloud Engagement
Supercharge your Marketing Cloud Engagement content by adding it to campaigns and orchestrating
it with flows.
Unified Reporting Tools in Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement
Marketing Cloud Next for Marketing Cloud Engagement includes two reporting tools that give you
deep insights into your sending and engagement data. These reports include data from Marketing
Cloud Engagement and Marketing Cloud Next.
Track Message Credit Usage in Digital Wallet
Use the consumption cards in Digital Wallet to see how many messages your organization has sent
from Marketing Cloud Next for Marketing Cloud Engagement.
Video Library for Marketing Cloud Next for Marketing Cloud Engagement
Accelerate your marketing journey with video walkthroughs that spotlight important features and
setup steps for Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement.

Marketing Cloud Engagement Get Started with Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement
Access the Features of Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement
Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement unifies your existing Marketing Cloud Engagement data with the
Salesforce Platform. This integration enhances your marketing efforts by giving you access to more
features, and by giving you a single view of your overall marketing program. You can access these
features and capabilities from your existing Marketing Cloud Engagement account, or directly through
the Salesforce Platform.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Marketing Cloud Engagement Pro+, Corporate+, and Enterprise+ Editions
Here's an overview of the features that are new to Engagement customers.
•
Digital Wallet-View spending information across Salesforce products. All marketing message usage
appears in these reports, tagged by channel, date range, and business unit. SMS and WhatsApp
channels also break out message sends by destination country.
•
Next-Gen Campaigns-Organize your marketing initiatives with next-gen campaigns that combine
assets, automations, and even Engagement journeys. Comprehensive engagement rolls up to the
campaign.
•
Flow Builder-Automate customer experiences and platform activities by adding triggers, branches,
decision points, and message send activities. These flows integrate natively with Service Cloud, Sales
Cloud, and Data 360.
•
Segments-Build highly targeted audiences, breaking down your universe of customers by
demographics, behaviors, and history. Available attributes include Engagement data, so you can
connect next-gen automation with geofencing, journey, or mobile activity data.
•
Conversational Agents-Deploy AI agents that automate certain tasks, such as creating campaign
content and managing approvals.
•
Agent-Powered Decisioning-Specify a group of journeys for customers to participate in and identify
your campaign goals. Agentforce determines the best journey based on the behavioral and
demographic trends for each customer.
•
Marketing Performance-View and share sending and engagement trends or analyze data with filters
for channel, date range, or account ID. These reports can combine data from Engagement and Next.
•
Analytics-Create custom reports, pivot tables, and dashboards by using clicks, not code. Your reports
can include data from across the Salesforce Platform.
Access Marketing Cloud Next Features from Marketing Cloud Engagement
When you subscribe to Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement, a Next button appears in the navigation
bar in Marketing Cloud Engagement. Use this menu to open the corresponding features on the
Salesforce Platform.
11.. Log in to Marketing Cloud Engagement.
22.. In the navigation bar, click Next.
33.. Select a destination.

Marketing Cloud Engagement Get Started with Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement
••
Agentforce Builder-Build powerful agents to automate marketing and business processes.
••
Analytics-Create custom reports in a drag and drop interface.
••
Campaigns-Organize all of your campaign content and integrate it with objects from across the
Salesforce Platform.
••
Flow-Create workflows to automate your marketing campaigns.
••
Marketing Performance-View engagement data for your marketing messages.
••
Segments-Create highly targeted customer segments.
Access Marketing Cloud Next Features on the Salesforce Platform
You can also access the features of Marketing Cloud Next without logging in to Marketing Cloud
Engagement.
11.. Log in to Salesforce.
Note If you aren't sure which URL to use, contact your Salesforce administrator.
22.. Click the App Launcher ( ).
33.. In the search field, enter marketing, and then select Marketing.
44.. Use the tabs at the top of the page to go to the section you want to access.
For example, to manage campaign content such as images and messages, click Content.
Where to Start with Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement
To find out which next-generation marketing features are the best first steps for you, consider whether
you already use Data 360 or Journey Builder. Think through which features are likely to bring the most
value based on the entities and assets that you already have.
Which combination of products do you use with Marketing Cloud Engagement?
•
I use Salesforce CRM and Data 360.
•
I use Salesforce CRM, but not Data 360.
•
I use Data 360, but I use a different CRM.
•
I use Engagement with different CRM and CDP apps.
Digital Wallet: Monitor Data Usage and Cost
Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement uses consumption billing for certain features and functionality.
Digital Wallet tracks this consumption so that you can monitor spending and allocation. Standard and
custom reports and dashboards provide trend analysis and forecasting for budgeting purposes across
business units and lines of business. You can even set alerts to help you avoid overages.
Unified Reporting: Aggregate Engagement Data Across Apps
Create insightful reports across Marketing Cloud Engagement activities and next-gen content that's
built in Marketing Cloud Next. These reports can include marketing data and data from other
Salesforce products such Sales Cloud, Service Cloud, and Data 360. Best of all, marketers can create
in-depth reports and dashboards using the drag-and-drop Tableau Next interface, without writing any
code.

Marketing Cloud Engagement Get Started with Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement
Salesforce and Data 360 Users
You're already using Salesforce as your CRM and Data 360 for your customer data platform. Several
Marketing Cloud Next features are ready to work with implementations like yours! Some features are
new to Engagement and available out of the box, while others require some preparation and
configuration in Data 360.
Based on the apps that you already use, these features can bring you value quickly with minimal setup.
Items with an asterisk require Journey Builder.
Start Here

| Digital Wallet | View your digital wallet and create custom reports |
| --- | --- |
| Campaigns and Flows | • Trigger Engagement emails via marketing flows • Relate Salesforce objects to next-gen campaigns • Connect next-gen campaigns to journeys* • Trigger Engagement journeys via marketing flows* |

After you've successfully used these tools, consider tackling these more advanced use cases. These
features require more setup and configuration, particularly around your data model in Data 360 and the
connections across apps. Items with an asterisk require that you also use Journey Builder.
Take the Next Steps

| Agentforce | • Create agents and agent actions • Add agents to marketing flows • Trigger the next best Engagement journey* |
| --- | --- |
| Channels and Messaging | • Set up conversational messaging for SMS • Set up conversational messaging for WhatsApp |

Salesforce CRM Users
Since you're already using

This table lists the fields in the Digital Content DMO that you're likely to use when you use the unified
reporting system to create reports related to WhatsApp messaging.

| Field Label | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Data Source | A reference ID for the source of records identified |

Marketing Cloud Engagement Get Started with Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement

| Field Label | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | by an external record ID. |
| Data Source Object | The ID of the data source that the object came from. |
| Id | The message ID in Marketing Cloud Engagement. This ID is different from the asset ID. |
| Name | The name of the asset in Content Builder. |

Message Engagement
The Message Engagement Data Model Object (DMO) contains information about the sending, delivery,
and engagement of WhatsApp messages sent from your account.
This table lists the fields in the Message Engagement DMO that you're likely to use when you use the
unified reporting system to create reports related to WhatsApp messaging. For a complete list of fields,
API names, and data types for this DMO, see Data 360 DMO and Mapping Guide: Message Engagement
DMO.

| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Bulk Message ID | A job ID for the WhatsApp send. Use this field to join to BulkMessageDMO. |
| Contact Point ID | A reference ID that's associated with the recipient's phone number. |
| Country ID | An ID associated with the recipient's country. |
| Data Source ID | The ID of the data source that's associated with the WhatsApp send. |
| Data Source Object ID | The ID of the data source object that produced the WhatsApp send. |
| Device Type | Indicates whether the recipient viewed the message on a mobile device or on a computer. |
| Engagement Action Reason Text | A description of the reason that a WhatsApp error occurred. Available for NOT_SENT , FAILED , and WARNING events only. |

Marketing Cloud Engagement Get Started with Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement

| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Engagement Channel Action | The type of engagement event that occurred. Possible values are: • DR -The status of an inbound message is available. Retrieve it in your WhatsApp Business account. • INBOUND -A customer sent a message to you. • SEND_STATUS -The message was sent and the resulting status is available. If your account used Data 360 with the Marketing Cloud Engagement channel bundles before the Winter '26 release, the possible values for this field are: • Inbound -A customer sent a message to you. • Sent -The message has been sent but hasn't been delivered to the recipient's device. • Undelivered -The message couldn't be delivered to the recipient's device. |
| Engagement Channel Type ID | A reference ID to the type of engagement channel. For the WhatsApp channel, the value of this field is WHATSAPP . |
| Engagement Date Time | The date and time when the engagement event occurred. |
| Engagement Event Direction ID | Indicates where the event originated. Possible values: • Inbound -The message was sent from a customer to your brand. • Outbound -The message was sent from your brand to a customer. |
| Engagement Notes | Indicates the Salesforce product that the message was sent from. Possible value: MARKETING_CLOUD_ENGAGEMENT . |

Marketing Cloud Engagement Get Started with Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement

| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| ErrorMessageText | A description of the reason that a WhatsApp error occurred. Available for NOT_SENT , FAILED , and WARNING events only. This field contains the same information as the Engagement Action Reason Text field. |
| ID | The unique ID of the engagement event. |
| Individual ID | The ID of the unified individual that the message was sent to. |
| Internal Organization ID | The ID of the business unit or other internal organization that owns the business account. This ID can also refer to a Marketing Cloud Engagement member ID (MID). |
| Marketing Journey Activity ID | The ID of the journey activity that sent the message. Use this value to join to Journey Builder DMOs. |
| Marketing Batch Number | The batch ID for the WhatsApp send. |
| Message Recipient Send Status | The status of an attempt to deliver a message. Possible values: • NOT_SENT -The message wasn't sent to the WhatsApp platform. • SENT -The WhatsApp platform accepted the message. • DELIVERED -The message was successfully delivered to the recipient. • READ -The recipient viewed the message. • FAILED -The WhatsApp platform was unable to deliver the message to the handset. • WARNING -The WhatsApp platform flagged the message content. |
| Message Text | The text in the message body. |
| Messaging Error Code | A status code that indicates the reason a message |

Marketing Cloud Engagement Get Started with Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement

| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | wasn't delivered. This field is only provided for messages where the value of the Engagement Channel Action ID field is NOT_SENT , FAILED , or UNDELIVERED . The error code begins with MC if the error code originated from Marketing Cloud, or WA if the error code originated from the WhatsApp platform. |
| Recipient Message ID | A unique ID for each instance of an outbound or inbound message. |
| Send Classification ID | The send classification for the message send, which impacts the price you pay for each message. Possible values: • Promotional -Messages sent for a marketing- related purpose. • Transactional -Messages sent in response to a customer action, such as a confirmation or authentication message. |
| Sender Code | The WhatsApp channel ID that was used to send the message. |
| Sender Display Name | The sender name that was used to send the message. |
| Send Time Phone Number | The recipient's phone number. |

Key Push Data Model Objects for Unified Reporting
In the unified reporting system for Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement, there are three data model
objects (DMOs) that contain helpful data for creating reports related to push messaging. These DMOs
contain information about your send definitions, engagement data, and message content.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Marketing Cloud Engagement Pro+, Corporate+, and Enterprise+ Editions
These DMOs contain information that's useful for creating reports that focus on Push messages:

Marketing Cloud Engagement Get Started with Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement
•
Bulk Message: Contains metadata about your messages, such as the campaign ID and send method.
The data applies to push messages that you send from Automation Studio and Journey Builder in
Marketing Cloud Engagement. It also contains data for API-triggered messages.
•
Digital Content: Contains metadata about the send.
•
Device Application Engagement: Contains send and engagement data related to push messages.
Bulk Message
The Bulk Message DMO contains metadata related to a push send, based on the send definition in
Marketing Cloud Engagement.
This table lists the fields in the Bulk Message DMO that you're likely to use when you create push-related
reports in the unified reporting system.

| Field Label | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Campaign ID | The ID of the campaign in Marketing Cloud Next that sent the bulk send. This ID isn't the same as a campaign ID from Marketing Cloud Engagement. |
| Data Source ID | The ID of the Marketing Cloud Engagement enterprise ID (EID) that the data came from. |
| Data Source Object ID | The ID of the Marketing Cloud Engagement data stream that the data came from. |
| Engagement Channel ID | The messaging channel of the message. For push messages, the value of this field is PUSH NOTIFICATION . |
| ID | The job ID of the send. For journey sends, this value contains the ID of the journey activity that sent the message. |
| Name | The name of the send definition used to send the push message. For journey sends, this value contains the name of the journey activity that sent the message. |
| Send Method Type | The mechanism used to send the push message. Possible values are: • API Triggered |

Marketing Cloud Engagement Get Started with Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement

| Field Label | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | • Automation Studio • Journey • User Initiated |

Digital Content
The Digital Content DMO contains information about the content that's associated with your message
sends.
This table lists the fields in the Digital Content DMO that you're likely to use when you use the unified
reporting system to create reports related to push messaging.

| Field Label | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Data Source | A reference ID for the source of records identified by an external record ID. |
| Data Source Object | The ID of the data source that the object came from. |
| Id | The message ID in Marketing Cloud Engagement. This ID is different from the asset ID. |
| Name | For Mobile Studio, API Triggered, and Automation Studio sends, this field contains the name of the send definition. For journey and transactional sends, this field contains the name of the asset in Content Builder. |

Device Application Engagement
The Device Application Engagement data model object (DMO) contains information about the sending,
delivery, and engagement of mobile app messages sent from your account.
This table lists the fields in the Message Engagement DMO that you're likely to use when you use the
unified reporting system to create reports related to mobile app messaging.

| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Bulk Message ID | A job ID for the send. Use this field to join to the BulkMessage DMO. |

Marketing Cloud Engagement Get Started with Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement

| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Device ID | The unique device ID associated with the app user's device. |
| Device Locale ID | The locale that applies to the app user's device. |
| Device Model Name | The model name of the app user's device, such as Google Pixel 7 . |
| Data Source Object ID | The ID of the data source object that produced the send. |
| Engagement Action Reason Text | A description of the reason that an error occurred. Available for NOT_SENT and BOUNCE events only. |
| Engagement Channel Action | The type of engagement event that occurred. Possible values are: • APP OPEN -An app session occured. The event is logged when the session is complete and the app is closed or moved to the background. • BOUNCE -The push notification service, such as Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM) or Apple Push Notification service (APNs), rejected the message. • DELIVERED -The message was successfully delivered to the push notification service. • ENTERED -The device entered a geofence. • EXITED -The device exited a geofence. • OPEN -The user opened the notification. • SEND_STATUS -The message was sent and the resulting status is available. If your account used Data 360 with the Marketing Cloud Engagement channel bundles before the Winter '26 release, the possible values for this field are: • Geofence Entry -The device entered a geofence. • Geofence Exit -The device exited a geofence. |

Marketing Cloud Engagement Get Started with Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement

| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | • Open -The user opened the notification. • Send -The message was sent and the resulting status is available. • Undelivered -The push notification service rejected the message. |
| Engagement Channel ID | The type of engagement channel. For mobile app messaging, the value of this field is MOBILE APP MESSAGING . If your account used Data 360 with the Marketing Cloud Engagement channel bundles befor

title: "Marketing Cloud Engagement Tenant Types"

Marketing Cloud Engagement Tenant Types
Data Extensions

| Synchronize Data Extensions with Marketing Cloud Next (11:37) | Watch the video: https://play.vidyard.com/ tXk3TeMAV2suGsojLDyVgR |
| --- | --- |

Reporting and Analytics

| Analytics Dashboards in Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement (2:57) | Watch the video: https://play.vidyard.com/ J6JH3LhF1CdEwzdeYDP9Xf |
| --- | --- |
| Custom Reports in Marketing Cloud Next for Engagement (3:47) | Watch the video: https://play.vidyard.com/ TWad1Hs8V3DoiBiQsKGs9q |

Marketing Cloud Engagement Tenant Types
Learn about the different types of tenants in Marketing Cloud Engagement.
The definition of a tenant depends on the type of Marketing Cloud Engagement account you use:
•
Enterprise 2.0 accounts-The top-level account and all associated business units under it.
•
Enterprise accounts-The top-level account and all associated On-Your-Behalf or Lock & Publish
business units.
•
Core accounts-The single account.
•
Agency accounts-Each top-level account and each associated client account is a separate tenant.
Each Core account, top-level Enterprise or Enterprise 2.0 accounts, Agency account, and any associated
child accounts have a member ID (MID) value that identifies the entity. Tenants can include a single or
multiple MIDs, depending on the account type.
Comparison of Salesforce-Hosted and Hyperforce
Environments
Marketing Cloud Engagement is hosted in two environments. The first environment uses servers that
Salesforce hosts, operates, and maintains. The second environment, called Hyperforce, is built on public
cloud services. Hyperforce offers several benefits over Salesforce-hosted instances, and there are
differences between the features available in Salesforce-hosted environments and the features available
on Hyperforce.
chunks.productLabel: Marketing Cloud Engagement (MCE; Email Studio, Journey Builder, Automation Studio, Content Builder, Mobile Studio)

chunks.heading: 04-comparison-of-salesforce-hosted-and-hyperforce-environments
chunks.body:
---
title: "Comparison of Salesforce-Hosted and Hyperforce Environments"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_engagement_4-6-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "104-108"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Comparison of Salesforce-Hosted and Hyperforce Environments
Hyperforce Benefits
Salesforce products that run on Hyperforce benefit from its integration of enhanced standards for
compliance, security, agility, and scalability, and from Salesforce's commitment to privacy. Benefits of
Hyperforce include:
•
Data Residency and Compliance: Hyperforce instances offer global reach while giving you choice and
control over where your customer data resides. This feature of the Hyperforce environment makes it
possible for you to obtain additional compliance certifications.
•
Scalability: Hyperforce instances can flexibly and sustainably scale to meet your business needs.
•
Security: By default, customer data in Hyperforce is encrypted at rest and in transit. Hyperforce
instances are secured using least-privilege access controls and zero-trust principles.
•
Privacy: Hyperforce provides comprehensive privacy standards that help give you more control over
your customer data.
•
Agility: Because it's built on the public cloud, Hyperforce provides interoperability with other public
cloud services.
•
Reliability: Hyperforce is hosted across multiple availability zones, giving your organization support for
tight Recovery Point Objective (RPO) and Recovery Time Objective (RTO) SLAs.
Regional Availability
Salesforce-hosted environments are based in the European Union and the United States.
Hyperforce environments are built on public cloud services based in Australia, Canada, India, and Japan.
Feature Support
This table lists differences in Marketing Cloud Engagement features for accounts deployed in Salesforce-
hosted environments compared to Hyperforce environments. The contents of this table are subject to
change.

| Service | Feature | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Email Studio | Bring your own certificate (BYOC) for Sender Authentication Package | Not available on Hyperforce. |
|  | Configure Sender Authentication Package and Private Domain via user interface | Not available on Hyperforce. To configure these features in Salesforce-hosted accounts, contact Salesforce Customer Support. |
|  | Email Archiving | Not available on Hyperforce. For Salesforce-hosted accounts, an |

| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Dedicated Message Transfer Agents | Not available on Hyperforce. For Salesforce-hosted accounts, an add-on purchase is required to use this feature. |
|  | Domain Allowlist | On Hyperforce, similar functionality is available by performing redirect validation on CloudPages. |
|  | Web Collect | On Hyperforce, similar functionality is available by using AMPscript or SSJS on a CloudPages page to add customer information to a data extension. |
|  | DE Manager | On Hyperforce, similar functionality is available by using AMPscript or SSJS on a CloudPages page to add customer information to a data extension. |
|  | S/MIME Support | Not Available on Hyperforce. |
|  | Email File Attachments | An add-on purchase is required to use this feature. Attachments from the SFTP folder aren't supported on Hyperforce. Attachments from Content Builder and via URL are supported. |
| Journey Builder | High Throughput Sending | Not available on Hyperforce. |
| Automation Studio | Enhanced SFTP | Not available on Hyperforce. |
|  | Custom Extracts | Not available on Hyperforce. |

|  | Intelligence Reports for Engagement Advanced | Not available on Hyperforce. For Salesforce-hosted accounts, an add-on purchase is required to use this feature. |
| Security | Tokenized Sending | This functionality isn't available or necessary in Hyperforce because of its data residency capabilities. |
|  | Unsecured HTTP postbacks for asynchronous APIs | On Hyperforce, only HTTPS postbacks are supported. |
|  | Unsecured HTTP Sender Authentication Package custom domains | On Hyperforce, custom domains use HTTPS by default. |

chunks.body:

title: "Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup (part 1/5)"

purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"

Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup

|  |  | account is first configured. It can't be added later. |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Partner Connect | ― | Not available on Hyperforce. |

For more information, contact your Salesforce account executive.
Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup
Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup is a centralized location for viewing and managing your account. It
also contains links to other helpful sources of information.
Setup Assistant
Use the Setup Assistant to configure a new Marketing Cloud Engagement account so that you can take
advantage of all of its features.
Limits and Guidelines
There are limits that apply to your usage of Marketing Cloud Engagement. Salesforce implements
some of these limits, and some are due to external factors.
Account Settings in Marketing Cloud Engagement
Marketing admins can control configuration information in Account Settings. Key information
regarding your account is stored here, and an admin can modify that information for users.
Business Units in Marketing Cloud Engagement
Business units let you control access to and the sharing of information. Use the hierarchical structure
of business units to control your brand while enabling access to messaging technologies to your users.
Manage user roles for the business unit, view users who have access to the business unit, and define
filter criteria for subscribers. Business units are available in Enterprise and Enterprise 2.0 accounts.
Security Settings and Tools for Marketing Cloud Engagement
Security Settings include parameters for session timeout, username and password conventions, and
lockout logic. These features help improve the security of your account.
Feature Settings
Review and configure settings for Marketing Cloud Engagement features.
Roles and Permissions in Marketing Cloud Engagement
Use roles and permissions to grant your users access to the areas and features that pertain to their job
functions.
Marketing Cloud Engagement Users
An admin can manage users in Setup for Marketing Cloud Engagement.
Marketing Cloud Engagement User Preferences
User preferences manage the information and behavior a user encounters in Marketing Cloud
Engagement.
Account Login Access
Enable Account Login Access to give Salesforce Support permission to log in to your account to
configure features or troubleshoot problems.

Marketing Cloud Engagement Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup
Authorizing File Locations
A file transfer location is an external public cloud file storage location or FTP site used to transfer files
to or from Marketing Cloud Engagement. For example, to import encrypted files from an Amazon
Simple Storage Service (S3) bucket, you set up an Amazon S3 file location. The location settings give
authorization details to access the files in your bucket.
SFTP Accounts in Marketing Cloud Engagement
SSH File Transfer Protocol (SFTP) is a method of securely transferring data from one computer to
another over the internet. Use the Marketing Cloud Engagement SFTP server to import lists, export
analytics data, and extract customer data. You can also use the SFTP server with Data Extract and File
Transfer activities in Automation Studio.
Key Management
Security keys and other settings are used to encrypt and decrypt data, digitally sign email messages,
and implement SAML single sign-on (SSO). The encryption applies only to information stored in
Marketing Cloud Engagement and not in other Salesforce apps.
Enable Data Governance Permissions for Marketing Cloud Engagement Objects
To use Data 360 Data Governance features for Marketing Cloud Engagement, you need to grant access
to the impacted objects according to an access policy.
Setup Assistant
Use the Setup Assistant to configure a new Marketing Cloud Engagement account so that you can take
advantage of all of its features.
Setup Assistant guides you through the process of configuring Marketing Cloud Engagement for your use
case. It also helps you implement a data structure that meets the needs of your organization. At the end
of the process, you create, send, and track an email message.
Limits and Guidelines
There are limits that apply to your usage of Marketing Cloud Engagement. Salesforce implements some
of these limits, and some are due to external factors.
Overview
We group limits into two categories: usage limits and scale limits. When usage limits are exceeded,
features can become unavailable, performance can be throttled, or billing charges can be applied. Usage
limits are typically related to resource consumption. For example, the maximum size of a data extension
or the maximum number of objects in an API request are both usage limits.
Scale limits are typically related to throughput rates that can't be exceeded. Scale limits are often outside
of the control of Salesforce. For example, mobile phone carriers in many countries impose limits on the
volume of messages that can be sent from a single phone number in a given period. This external factor
creates a limitation on the scale of a campaign that sends SMS messages.

Marketing Cloud Engagement Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup
API Limits and Guidelines
Use the Marketing Cloud Engagement APIs to integrate your applications and other technology
systems with Marketing Cloud Engagement. To protect our Service and ensure the availability of
Marketing Cloud Engagement for all customers, Salesforce places limits on API usage.
Data Extension Limits and Guidelines
The size and structure of your data extension and contact attribute data can impact their
performance. To optimize the performance of your data extensions and contact attributes, stay within
recommended ranges.
Data Retention Limits and Guidelines
Marketing Cloud Engagement limits how long it stores some of the data related to your account.
Database Query Limits and Guidelines
Query limits protect the health and performance of the Marketing Cloud Engagement database. There
are two types of limits: hard and soft. If you issue a query that exceeds a hard limit, the request is
rejected. You can exceed a soft limit, but doing so can degrade performance. Salesforce reserves the
right to limit the execution of queries that exceed soft limits if they cause system performance or
security issues.
Email Limits and Guidelines
There are several ways to use Marketing Cloud Engagement to send email, including Email Studio,
Journey Builder, SOAP API, and REST API. When you send email, there are limits that apply to
throughput, file size, and data retention policies.
SMS Limits and Guidelines
Get to know the limits that apply to sending SMS messages in MobileStudio.
Push Notification Limits and Guidelines
Learn about the limits that apply to creating and sending push notifications to users of your app.
See Also
Limits in Marketing Cloud Personalization (formerly Interaction Studio)
API Limits and Guidelines
Use the Marketing Cloud Engagement APIs to integrate your applications and other technology systems
with Marketing Cloud Engagement. To protect our Service and ensure the availability of Marketing Cloud
Engagement for all customers, Salesforce places limits on API usage.
Limit Types
There are two types of API limits. A hard limit is one that you can't exceed. In most cases, if you issue an
API request that exceeds a hard limit, the API rejects the request and returns an error.
A soft limit is one that can degrade performance if you exceed it. Salesforce reserves the right to rate
limit API requests if they cause system performance or security issues. In some cases, you must purchase
additional licenses or allocations if you exceed a soft limit.

Marketing Cloud Engagement Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup
General API Limits
The limits in this section apply to both the Marketing Cloud Engagement SOAP and REST APIs.

| Resource | Limit | Limit Type | Details |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Maximum API request volume | The limit depends on your edition • Pro: 2 million requests per year • Corporate: 6 million requests per year • Enterprise: 200 million requests per year | Soft limit | You can purchase an additional allocation of contacts or API requests. Contact your account executive for more information. |
| Maximum number of asynchronous requests per day | 50 million requests per day | Soft limit | This limit applies to asynchronous REST API and SOAP API requests. |
| Maximum number of access token requests | 1 request every 20 minutes per client machine | Soft limit | Don't request a new token with every API request. Instead, use the expires_in parameter in the response to a token request to determine how long an existing access token remains valid. Request a new token only when the existing token expires. |
| Maximum batch size for synchronous API requests | 50 objects | Soft limit | - |
| Maximum batch size for asynchronous API requests | 32,000 objects | Hard limit | - |
| Minimum request timeout period for synchronous API requests | The timeout period depends on the type | Soft limit | Match the timeout values of your API clients with these values. |

Marketing Cloud Engagement Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup

| Resource | Limit | Limit Type | Details |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | of API operation • 300 seconds for tracking and data retrieval operations • 120 seconds for non-tracking operations |  |  |
| Minimum request timeout period for asynchronous API requests | 30 seconds | Soft limit | Match the timeout values of your API clients with these values. |

Marketing Cloud Engagement REST API Limits
The Marketing Cloud Engagement REST API gives you broad access to the features and capabilities of
several apps, including Content Builder and Journey Builder.

| Resource | Limit | Limit Type | Details |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Maximum request rate for Transactional Messaging API requests | 1,000 requests per second for static and simple messages | Soft limit | A static message contains no content blocks or personalization elements. A simple message includes one HTML content block and 1-2 personalization elements, such as the recipient's name and order number. Transactional messages that include more than one content block and personalization elements other than name and order |

Marketing Cloud Engagement Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup

| Resource | Limit | Limit Type | Details |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  | number have lower throughput rates. |
| Maximum request rate for batched Transactional Messaging API requests | 250 requests per second | Soft limit | This limit assumes a batch size of 50 recipients per request and a static or simple message body. |
| Maximum request rate for synchronous Device API requests | 4,000 requests per minute | Soft limit | - |
| Maximum request rate for synchronous API requests | 2,500 requests per minute | Soft limit | This limit applies to all API requests except Device API requests and access token requests. |
| Maximum number of concurrent connections | 100 connections | Soft limit | - |
| Maximum request payload size for Auth API | 8 KB | Hard limit | - |
| Maximum request payload size for Data API | 5.9 MB | Hard limit | - |
| Maximum request payload size for Asset API | 25 MB | Hard limit | - |
| Maximum request payload size for Messaging API | 16 MB | Hard limit | - |
| Maximum request payload size for Automation API | 27.9 MB | Hard limit | - |
| Maximum request payload size for all other Marketing Cloud Engagement APIs | 4 MB | Hard limit | - |
| Maximum batch size for | 5,000 objects | Hard limit | - |

Marketing Cloud Engagement Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup

| Resource | Limit | L

chunks.heading: part-03
chunks.body:
---
title: "Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup (part 3/5)"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_engagement_4-6-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "109-404"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

add more business units. Any days with events from an additional business unit display a small blue
indicator for that day on the Calendar. If you select more business units, your Calendar displays
applicable campaigns, broken out by business unit at the top of the screen. Hover over these events and
click the business unit or that event in the detailed Calendar view. This action takes you to that business
unit to view more information. Any changes you make in the filter persist through future logins,
movements to other apps, or other activity. Clear these filters to change the Calendar view.
Calendar in Account with More than 500 Business Units

Marketing Cloud Engagement Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup
Calendar in Account with Less than 500 Business Units
Add a Campaign to Calendar
Use the calendar to create a visual representation of your campaign schedule.
Add an Event to the Calendar
Create events on your calendar to mark milestones and other important dates. You can create single-
day and multi-day events.
Filter Events and Messages
Use filters to view only the automated or scheduled messages to review in Marketing Cloud
Engagement.
Information on a Calendar Event or Message
View information on a Calendar.
Add a Campaign to Calendar
Use the calendar to create a visual representation of your campaign schedule.
11.. On the Marketing Cloud Engagement dashboard, click Calendar.
22.. Hover over the day on which you want to create a campaign, and then click Add Campaign.
33.. On the Add Campaign window, enter the details of your campaign.
44.. Save the campaign.
See Also
Campaigns in Marketing Cloud
Campaign Tags in Marketing Cloud

Marketing Cloud Engagement Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup
Add an Event to the Calendar
Create events on your calendar to mark milestones and other important dates. You can create single-day
and multi-day events.
11.. On the Marketing Cloud Engagement dashboard, click Calendar.
22.. Hover over the day on which you want to create an event, and then click Add Event.
33.. On the Add Event window, enter the details of the event.
44.. Save the event.
Filter Events and Messages
Use filters to view only the automated or scheduled messages to review in Marketing Cloud Engagement.
Each view lists the number of messages associated with a specific campaign or message type. While in
the monthly view, messages of the same type roll up to show an aggregated count. While in the weekly
view, messages aggregate to the campaign level so you can see more hours.
11.. In the Campaigns window, click the campaign you wish to use as part of the filter. The view displays
only those events or messages associated with the specific campaign.
22.. To see all messages associated with that campaign, hover over the campaign and click the button to
the right.
33.. In the Message Types window, click the check boxes for the types to view.
44.. Follow the steps below to filter a message type based on scheduled or automated status:
aa.. Hover over the message type.
bb.. Click Scheduled or Automated to the message type to filter.
Information on a Calendar Event or Message
View information on a Calendar.
Calendar Event
When you click Fullscreen, the Calendar displays a full month of scheduled marketing activities. If you
hover over a scheduled event, the Calendar displays properties for the scheduled message and any
relevant automation information. If you hover over a sent event, the Calendar displays information for
that event or message and any relevant automation information. The Calendar shows automation
information based on user permissions, including the name of the automation used to send the email.
The Calendar only displays information for automations that originate from Automation Studio. Other
automations display past information but not information for future or scheduled events or messages. To
navigate to a page with additional information on that event, click the link available by hovering on the
Calendar event or message.

Marketing Cloud Engagement Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup
Automated Messages
The calendar displays messages scheduled via Automation Studio using the icon. Click that icon to
link to either the automation itself or the source application and review or edit information regarding
that message. This icon identifies only messages created or scheduled via Automation Studio.
Email Settings in Marketing Cloud Engagement
Review and configure settings for marketing email features.
Optional Email Features
Learn more about the optional email-related features that you can enable in Marketing Cloud
Engagement.
Working with the Email Sender Authentication Package
To brand your links and image URLs with your authenticated sending domain, use the Sender
Authentication Package (SAP) for Marketing Cloud Engagement. The consistent branding that SAP
offers can help any marketer improve sender reputation and subscriber trust, while ensuring domain-
level compliance with bulk-sender guidelines. Customers who send more than 250,000 email
messages per month must use SAP.
Domain Verification for Marketing Cloud Engagement Email
Domain Verification and From Address Management are Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup features
used to verify From addresses used when sending email through Engagement. To prevent sends from
going out with email addresses that aren't verified and to avoid sending restriction, all accounts must
verify all possible From addresses. You can verify From addresses in several places within Engagement
depending on your email sending use cases.
Reply Mail Management for Email Studio
Reply Mail Management (RMM) manages replies to the email jobs you send. RMM processes the reply
automatically, if appropriate, and forwards the message if the message requires personal review.=
Enterprise Reply Mail Management
To automate management of incoming messages received by your reply-to email address, turn on
Reply Mail Management in Marketing Cloud Engagement. These message types include out-of-office
replies, auto-replies, auto-forwards, unsubscribes, and individual customer responses.
Send Classifications for Email Studio
Send classifications define parameters for an email job in a central location. The account can reuse
those parameters for multiple email send definitions. For example, you can create a send classification
that's used every time a message is sent to make sure that it always uses the appropriate parameters.
Select a send classification, together with content information and audience information, when you
define a message send by creating a message interaction.
URL Expiration
The URL Expiration feature allows administrators to configure when to expire tracked links and where
to redirect them. An admin sets a threshold for expiration, and as long as the click occurs before that
time, the link resolves to its original location. After that point, the link opens the redirect URL.

Marketing Cloud Engagement Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup
Optional Email Features
Learn more about the optional email-related features that you can enable in Marketing Cloud
Engagement.
The options in this table are available in Setup | Email Optional Features.

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Double Opt-In | Requires subscribers to confirm their email address by clicking a link in an email they receive after subscribing. |
| HTML Paste | When enabled, this option lets you create email messages and templates using the HTML paste option. |
| Text Only Email | When enabled, this option lets you specify a plain- text email part in addition to the HTML part. |
| Send Throttling | Limits the number of emails that you send during specific hours. Sends continue until all messages are sent. |
| BCC Emails | Enable messages to include blind carbon copy (BCC) recipients. You're billed for every email you send, so if one BCC address is added, you pay for both the message sent to the To recipient and the BCC recipient. |
| From Name Drop-Down of Users | Enables the sending of email using any account user's address as the From address. |
| Impression Tracking | Enables performance tracking for emails that contain content built by AMPscript or dynamic content in Email Studio. When you create an email using AMPscript or dynamic content, you can give each region a unique identifier for tracking and segmenting. |
| Profile Link Check | Marketing Cloud Engagement checks your emails to make sure that they include a profile center link. You can disable this check. Before you turn off this option, ensure that your emails comply with local regulations. |
| Physical Address Check | Marketing Cloud Engagement checks your emails to make sure that they include your mailing address. You can disable this check. Before you |

Marketing Cloud Engagement Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup

| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | turn off this option, ensure that your emails comply with local regulations. |
| Unsubscribe Link Check | Marketing Cloud Engagement checks your emails to make sure that they include an unsubscribe link. You can disable this check. Before you turn off this option, ensure that your emails comply with local regulations. |

Working with the Email Sender Authentication Package
To brand your links and image URLs with your authenticated sending domain, use the Sender
Authentication Package (SAP) for Marketing Cloud Engagement. The consistent branding that SAP offers
can help any marketer improve sender reputation and subscriber trust, while ensuring domain-level
compliance with bulk-sender guidelines. Customers who send more than 250,000 email messages per
month must use SAP.
SAP includes several features.
•
Account Branding-View-as-a-webpage, link tracking, and image tracking addresses in your emails use
your SAP domain, rather than a shared Marketing Cloud Engagement domain. All references to
Marketing Cloud Engagement are replaced with your branding.
•
Private Domain for email sending-This domain acts as the From address for your email sends.
Marketing Cloud Engagement authenticates your email sends using the Sender Policy Framework
(SPF), Sender ID, and DomainKeys Identified Mail (DKIM) authentication.
•
Custom Domain for CloudPages-CloudPages associated with your account are hosted on a private
domain.
•
Dedicated IP Address-All email messages sent from your account are sent using a dedicated IP
address.
•
Reply Mail Management-Control the replies that you receive from your subscribers. You can assign
filters for out-of-office messages and manual unsubscribe requests.
While you can purchase these features individually, the view-as-webpage link and image wrapping that
brands your account only comes with SAP, which is included in all Marketing Cloud Engagement editions.
To purchase an additional SAP, contact your account executive.
Marketing Cloud Engagement only makes DNS changes that are required for SAP setup. You're
responsible for purchasing domains to use with SAP.
You can configure SAP on a tenant in Salesforce-hosted environments by using the Marketing Cloud
Engagement user interface. For more information, see Configure Sender Authentication Package. To
configure SAP on an account in the Marketing Cloud Engagement Hyperforce environment, to change
the domain used for your existing SAP, or to set up additional CloudPages, contact your account
executive.

Marketing Cloud Engagement Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup
Important If an SAP image domain is unsecured, Content Builder displays images with a Marketing
Cloud Engagement-branded domain to avoid browser mixed-c

chunks.heading: part-04
chunks.body:
---
title: "Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup (part 4/5)"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_engagement_4-6-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "109-404"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Marketing Cloud Engagement APIs and Programmatic Languages: Create Callback
Marketing Cloud Engagement APIs and Programmatic Languages: Verify Callback
Create a Subscription for Event Notifications
Follow these steps to create a subscription for event notifications in Marketing Cloud Engagement.
11.. In the Setup page, click Event Notification and select Subscriptions.
22.. Click Subscribe New.
33.. Enter a name for the subscription.
44.. Select the callback and event for your subscription. You can select more than one event for your
subscription.
55.. Enter any attributes and values used to filter results for the subscription. The subscription returns only
events where an attribute matches the specified value. For example, enter definitionKey and a value
to return events only with that key.
66.. Click Subscribe.
See Also
Get Started with Event Notification Service
Supported Notification Events
Delete a Callback for Event Notifications
Follow these steps to delete a subscription to event notifications in Marketing Cloud Engagement.
11.. In the Setup page, click Event Notification and select URL Callbacks.
22.. Click the arrow next to the callback to delete and click Delete.

Marketing Cloud Engagement Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup
33.. Click Delete.
Delete a Subscription for Event Notifications
Follow these steps to delete a subscription to event notifications in Marketing Cloud Engagement.
11.. In the Setup page, click Event Notification and select Subscriptions.
22.. Click the arrow next to the subscription to delete and click Delete.
33.. Click Delete.
Mobile Studio Settings
Review and configure settings for Mobile Studio in Marketing Cloud Engagement.
MobileConnect Account Administration
Admins can access the send blockout, from name, headers and footers, subscription information,
keywords, short and long codes, and other account-level settings in the Setup app by searching for
MobileConnect.
MobilePush App Administration
An admin can manage MobilePush in Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup. Here, access the send
period, stored headers and footers, subscription information, custom sound optional settings,
OpenDirect, custom keys, interactive notifications, and other account-level settings.
MobileConnect Account Administration
Admins can access the send blockout, from name, headers and footers, subscription information,
keywords, short and long codes, and other account-level settings in the Setup app by searching for
MobileConnect.
Note As a Marketing Cloud Engagement customer, you agree to comply with applicable laws in
using the Engagement services, and to allow recipients of your commercial messages to
unsubscribe from future communications. If you change the Sender ID to something other than a
phone number (for example, by replacing a phone number in the From Name field with the name
of your company), the recipient of your message can't reply to the message with an opt-out term
like STOP or HELP, so you may must provide opt-out capability through a different method (such as
by providing instructions for unsubscribing in the text of the message).
If you need account-specific help or to have a feature enabled, contact your account executive.
From Names in MobileConnect
Set a personalized word instead of a code to send from Marketing Cloud Engagement.
Stored Headers and Footers
Use headers and footers to ensure consistency across SMS messages sent from MobileConnect in
Marketing Cloud Engagement.

Marketing Cloud Engagement Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup
Send Blockouts
Set a period during which your MobileConnect account doesn't send outgoing messages, such as
when subscribers might be asleep. This feature prevents you from scheduling sends or beginning an
immediate send during a specified send blockout period.
Keywords and Codes
Use codes and keywords to conduct SMS and MMS sends in MobileConnect.
HELP Keywords
HELP keywords allow people sending messages to or receiving messages from the account to request
assistance in MobileConnect. For an example response to a HELP keyword, text HELP to 88769.
STOP Keywords in MobileConnect
Every Marketing Cloud Engagement account must have a STOP keyword so that people sending SMS
messages to or receiving messages from the account can unsubscribe.
Bounced Messages and Held Numbers
MobileConnect logs a message send as a bounce when it receives a report from the aggregator that
the message send failed.
Enable the Marketing Cloud Engagement Shortener
You can shorten links and track the total and unique clicks using Marketing Cloud Engagement
shortener.
MobileConnect Conversation Windows
Use conversation windows to respond to inbound messages that don't have keywords assigned to
them. Use the double opt-in template to opt users in to an SMS campaign. Conversation windows are
enforced at the keyword level.
DLT Template Settings for SMS Messages in India
In MobileConnect, use Digital Ledger Technology (DLT) templates to ensure that you comply with the
Telecoms Regulatory Authority of India (TRAI) regulations. DLT templates reduce spam messages and
streamline the campaign process. You can use DLT template only for sending messages in India.
From Names in MobileConnect
Set a personalized word instead of a code to send from Marketing Cloud Engagement.
You can use a personalized word, called a From Name, in place of a long code. For example, if you use
BRAND1 in place of your long code, recipients see BRAND1 instead of the long code that the message
was sent from. From Names are limited to 11 or fewer characters, and you can only use one From Name
value.
Note Non-GSM characters aren't supported in From Names.
Only use From Names for one-way outbound communication, such as alerts, updates, and notifications.
Recipients can't respond directly to messages sent via From Name. To receive a response, set up a
separate short code and include that number in the message. To respond, the recipient manually sends a
response to the short code that was included in the message.

Marketing Cloud Engagement Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup
Note Outbound messages triggered through MobileConnect instead of Journey Builder append the
From Name.
After you configure the From Name, all outbound messages sent from your account use this value.
Outbound messages include both triggered sends and keyword responses. You can also set From Names
at the level of a long code or a message. Code- or message-level From Names override account-level
From Names. You can also set different From Names for the business units within an Enterprise 2.0
account.
To enable From Names for your account, contact your account executive. Character restrictions for From
Names vary by location.
Note On iOS devices, From Names display without spaces. iOS doesn't support two-character From
Names.
From Names aren't available in these countries and locations:
•
Argentina
•
Armenia
•
Azerbaijan

Set the MobileConnect Account-Level From Name
Set the account-level from name in Marketing Cloud Engagement.
Set the MobileConnect Account-Level From Name
Set the account-level from name in Marketing Cloud Engagement.
Note If you change the from name for your account during a campaign, this change affects your
interactions and tracking information calculations. Ensure that you have no active, ongoing
campaigns running in your account before changing the from name.
11.. In the app switcher, hover over your name and click Setup.
22.. Search for MobileConnect.
33.. Click Edit next to From Name.
44.. Enter the name.
Note The maximum is 11 characters, including information from personalization strings.
MobileConnect truncates any from name longer than 11 characters.
55.. Save your changes.
Stored Headers and Footers
Use headers and footers to ensure consistency across SMS messages sent from MobileConnect in
Marketing Cloud Engagement.

Marketing Cloud Engagement Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup
Insert standardized and reusable content, such as branding or legal text, into your messages. You can
create up to three headers and three footers. Choose which header and footer to include when you
create the message.
Add Headers and Footers in MobileConnect
This action creates SMS headers and footers in Marketing Cloud Engagement.
Delete Stored Headers and Footers in MobileConnect
Delete stored headers and footers in Marketing Cloud Engagement.
Add Headers and Footers in MobileConnect
This action creates SMS headers and footers in Marketing Cloud Engagement.
11.. In the app switcher, hover over your name and click Setup.
22.. Search for MobileConnect.
33.. Click Edit next to Headers and Footers.
44.. Click Add New.
55.. Enter the header or footer text.
66.. Save your text.
Delete Stored Headers and Footers in MobileConnect
Delete stored headers and footers in Marketing Cloud Engagement.
11.. In the app switcher, hover over your name and click Setup.
22.. Search for MobileConnect.
33.. Click next to the header or footer to delete.
44.. Save your changes.
Send Blockouts
Set a period during which your MobileConnect account doesn't send outgoing messages, such as when
subscribers might be asleep. This feature prevents you from scheduling sends or beginning an
immediate send during a specified send blockout period.
MobileConnect prevents you from sending immediately during the send blockout, based on the time
zone set for your account within Marketing Cloud Engagement. MobileConnect also prevents you from
scheduling a send that starts during the send blockout in any time zone. Sends don't pause at the send
blockout. For example, if your send starts at 9 PM and takes two hours, and your send blockout starts at
10 PM, the send continues into the send blockout time period until 11 PM.
Any send blockout time specified in the MobileConnect app doesn't affect sends conducted using
MobileConnect API calls. Specify blockout times separately on the API call for your message. Any
messages triggered during an API send blockout window occur after the send blockout period elapses. If
you attempt to send during the blockout window, the message is scheduled to send one second after
the blockout window ends.

Marketing Cloud Engagement Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup
Note In Journey Builder, a send blockout window completely prevents messages from sending.
Messages don't queue for sending at a later time. Send blockouts set in Journey Builder are
independent of send blockouts set in MobileConnect. Journey Builder doesn't honor blockouts set
in MobileConnect.In Journey Builder, when you set a send blockout window for an SMS activity, the
displayed current account time doesn't automatically adjust for Daylight Savings Time (DST).
Set a Send Blockout in MobileConnect
Set the send blockout for Marketing Cloud Engagement.
Edit a Send Blockout in MobileConnect
Enable, disable, or change the start and end times of your send blockout for Marketing Cloud
Engagement.
Set a Send Blockout in MobileConnect
Set t

chunks.heading: part-05
chunks.body:
---
title: "Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup (part 5/5)"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_engagement_4-6-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "109-404"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Set Up an Enhanced FTP or Safehouse File Location
Transfer files to and from Marketing Cloud Engagement using a file location with Enhanced FTP. Available
locations are the import directory, export directory, a subfolder, or the Safehouse. Files stored in
Enhanced FTP and the Safehouse are available for 21 days and then deleted.
Choose the Safehouse when you need additional security. Files are encrypted and decrypted without
exposing them to the FTP directories. Only authenticated users can access the Safehouse, which resides
on highly redundant, highly available central storage servers.
To complete this task, you must have administrative permissions to view and manage file locations.
11.. Hover over your name and click Setup.
22.. In the Data Management section, click File Locations.
33.. Click Create.
44.. Complete the information in the Properties section.
Name-Enter a unique, recognizable name.
External Key-Enter a unique key that identifies the file location when using the API.

Marketing Cloud Engagement Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup
Description-Enter descriptive, recognizable details that help further identify the file location.
55.. Select the location type.
Marketing Cloud Enhanced FTP Site Import Directory: Standard directory for file imports.
Marketing Cloud Enhanced FTP Site Export Directory: Standard directory for file exports.
Safehouse: A secure location for encrypting and decrypting files.
Relative location under Marketing Cloud FTP Site: A subfolder of the Enhanced FTP Site.
66.. For relative location types only, specify the subfolder that you want to target. For example,
folderPath\folderPath. Don't use a leading backslash ("\").
77.. Save the file location.
See Also
Perform a File Transfer to a Safehouse Location
How to Use the File Transfer Activity in Automation Studio
Perform a File Transfer to a Safehouse Location
When you import an encrypted file with your Marketing Cloud Engagement safehouse location set as the
default location, follow these steps to perform an encrypted file transfer
11.. Import the encrypted file into the Enhanced FTP Import Directory.
Note If you use an external file location instead of Enhanced FTP, use a file transfer activity to
move the file to the safehouse.
22.. Use the File Transfer Activity to decrypt the file to the safehouse by selecting a private key.
33.. Use the Import Activity to import the file from the safehouse.
Note The safehouse location can be the default destination for imports. Making the safehouse
your default destination for imports also places import results files in that same location. To make
the safehouse location your default import location, contact your account executive.
See Also
How to Use the File Transfer Activity in Automation Studio
Import File Activity
Set Up an External FTP File Location with Marketing Cloud Engagement
Transfer files with an external FTP, SFTP, or FTPS site using a file location. The file location contains the
site URL, port, and login details that Marketing Cloud Engagement uses to access your site.
To complete this task, you must have administrative permissions to view and manage file locations.

Marketing Cloud Engagement Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup
Note Marketing Cloud Engagement only supports passive mode connections for FTP interactions.
11.. In Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup, in the Quick Find box, enter file, and then select File
Locations.
22.. Click Create.
33.. Complete the information in the Properties section.
aa.. For Name, enter a unique name that's recognizable when you create an activity in Marketing Cloud
Engagement.
bb.. For External Key, enter a unique key that identifies the file location when using the API.
cc.. For Description, enter details that help further identify the file location when you create an activity
in Marketing Cloud Engagement.
44.. Select the location type.
••
External FTP Site
••
External SFTP Site
••
External FTPS Site
55.. Complete the location information.
aa.. For URL, enter the location of the FTP site. This URL must directly open the default directory with no
further navigation. For example, sftp.example.com/destinationDirectory.
bb.. For Port, enter the FTP server port. For FTP and FTPS sites, use Port 21. For SFTP sites, use Port 22.
cc.. For Auth Type, select the authentication method used to access the site. For FTP and FTPS sites, the
only supported option is password authentication. For SFTP sites, you can choose Password or Auth
Key authentication.
dd.. For Username, Password, and Auth Key, enter the credentials to access the site.
66.. Save the file location.
SSH Keys for External SFTP
You can use an SSH key to authenticate external SFTP connections. Upload each key that you want to use
in the Data Management section of Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup.
Supported Ciphers, Algorithms, and Key Exchanges
Host Key Algorithms:
•
rsa-sha2-256
•
rsa-sha2-512
•
ssh-dss
•
ssh-rsa
Ciphers and Encryption Algorithms:
•
3des-cbc
•
aes128-cbc
•
aes128-ctr
•
aes128-gcm@openssh.com

Marketing Cloud Engagement Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup
•
aes192-cbc
•
aes192-ctr
•
aes192-gcm@openssh.com
•
aes256-cbc
•
aes256-ctr
•
blowfish-cbc
Key Exchanges:
•
diffie-hellman-group-exchange-sha1
•
diffie-hellman-group-exchange-sha256
•
diffie-hellman-group1-sha1
•
diffie-hellman-group14-sha1
•
diffie-hellman-group14-sha256
•
ecdh-sha2-nistp256
•
ecdh-sha2-nistp384
MAC Algorithms:
•
hmac-md5
•
hmac-sha1
•
hmac-sha1-96
•
hmac-sha1-etm@openssh.com
•
hmac-sha2-256
•
hmac-sha2-256-etm@openssh.com
•
hmac-sha2-512
•
hmac-sha2-512-etm@openssh.com
See Also
How to Use the File Transfer Activity in Automation Studio
SFTP Accounts in Marketing Cloud Engagement
SSH File Transfer Protocol (SFTP) is a method of securely transferring data from one computer to another
over the internet. Use the Marketing Cloud Engagement SFTP server to import lists, export analytics data,
and extract customer data. You can also use the SFTP server with Data Extract and File Transfer activities
in Automation Studio.
SFTP Considerations
•
The Marketing Cloud Engagement SFTP server only supports connections on port 22.
•
The username and password that you use to connect to the SFTP server are different from the
credentials that you use to log in to Marketing Cloud Engagement.
•
The names of files that you transfer to the SFTP server can't contain these characters: \ / : * < > | ? "
•
Up to 10 simultaneous connections can be made from your account to the SFTP server.

Marketing Cloud Engagement Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup
•
If a user tries to connect to the SFTP server too many times using an incorrect password, they're
blocked from attempting to login again for 30 minutes.
•
The SFTP server automatically closes connections that have been inactive for more than 10 minutes.
•
Your SFTP folder includes Import, Export, and Reports folders. Don't delete these folders. Imported
files must reside in the Import folder so that the system can find them. Marketing Cloud Engagement
uploads your data extracts to the Export folder and your reports to the Reports folder.
•
Marketing Cloud Engagement retains files on the SFTP server for at least 21 days. After this time
period elapses, these files can be automatically removed.
SSH Keys for SFTP
You can use an SSH key to use to authenticate SFTP connections. Upload each key that you want to
use in the Data Management section of Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup.
Create SFTP User Accounts
Create user accounts for SFTP users in Marketing Cloud Engagement. You can have up to 10 active
SFTP user accounts per member ID (MID).
Connect to the SFTP Server
Learn how to use a third-party SFTP client to connect to the Marketing Cloud Engagement SFTP server.
After you connect, you can download files from the SFTP server and upload files to it.
See Also
Create an SSH Key to Use with Marketing Cloud Engagement SFTP
SSH Keys for SFTP
You can use an SSH key to use to authenticate SFTP connections. Upload each key that you want to use
in the Data Management section of Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup.
Key Requirements
Use a tool such as ssh-keygen or PuTTYgen to generate an SSH key. The SSH key you generate must
follow these standards:
•
OpenSSH2 key in PEM standard
•
At least 2048-bit strength, with a recommended strength of 4096 bits
•
RSA format
•
A passphrase is allowed but not required
Marketing Cloud Engagement supports OpenSSH 9.1.
See Also
Import Activity in Email Studio
Connect to the SFTP Server

Marketing Cloud Engagement Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup
Create SFTP User Accounts
Create user accounts for SFTP users in Marketing Cloud Engagement. You can have up to 10 active SFTP
user accounts per member ID (MID).
To use an SSH key to authenticate a user, first create a key in Key Management.
11.. In Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup, in the Quick Find box, enter ftp, and then select FTP
Accounts.
22.. Click Create User.
33.. Enter the email address and password for the new user.
44.. On the Authentication Options window, choose the authentication method to use for the user.
55.. On the Permissions window, choose the permissions to apply to each directory. Each directory inherits
the permissions of its subdirectories.
66.. To allow SFTP connections only from a specific IP address, enter the address in the Allowlist IPs field,
and then click Add. Repeat this step for each IP address that you want to allow. If you don't enter any
addresses, SFTP connections can be opened from any IP address.
77.. On the Folders window, select a home directory for the user. The user is allowed to access only the
selected directory and its subdirectories.
88.. Save your changes.
Connect to the SFTP Server
Learn how to use a third-party SFTP client to connect to the Marketing Cloud Engagement SFTP server.
After you connect, you can download files from the SFTP server and upload files to it.
Download and install an SFTP client. There are numerous SFTP clients available for every operating
system. If you aren't sure which client to use, contact your IT organization. This information assumes that
you use a graphical file transfer client, as opposed to a command-line-based client.
11.. In Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup, in the Quick Find box, enter ftp, and then select FTP
Accounts.
22.. Click the copy button ( ) to copy the server URL.
33.. In your client, open a new SFTP connection. For the URL, enter the server URL that you copied from
Marketing Cloud Engagement. For the port, enter 22.
44.. Enter the username and password for your SFTP account. If your account is configured to use an SSH
key for authentication, select your key.
Note Your SFTP login credentials aren't the same as the credentials that you use to log in to
Marketing Cloud Engagement.
55.. Connect to the server.
After you connect, begin transferring files to or from the server. The SFTP server has three directories:
•
Import: Upload files to this directory to make them available for import jobs.
•
Export: Download subscriber exports from this directory.

Marketing Cloud Engagement Marketing Cloud Engagement Setup
•
Reports: Download report data from this directory.
Supported SSH Algorithms
Note Only the ssh-rsa host key algorithm is supported.
The SFTP service supports these SSH algorithms.
Ciphers:
•
aes128-cbc (legacy)
•
aes128-ctr
•
aes256-cbc (legacy)
•
aes256-ctr
MAC Algorithms:
•
hmac-sha1
•
hmac-sha2-256
•
hmac-sha2-512
Key Exchanges:
•
diffie-hellman-group-exchange-sha256
•
diffie-hellman
