chunks.heading: 06-package-manager-in-marketing-cloud-engagement
chunks.body:
---
title: "Package Manager in Marketing Cloud Engagement"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_engagement_4-6-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "405-428"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Package Manager in Marketing Cloud Engagement

| Object Type | Associated Bundle | Name |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  | Consent |
|  |  | Contact Point Phone |
|  |  | Message Engagement |
|  |  | SMS Template |
|  | MCE Mobile Push | Contact Point App |
|  |  | Device |
|  |  | Device Application Engagement |
|  |  | Device Application Template |
|  |  | Einstein Push Engagement Scores |
|  | MCE WhatsApp | Contact Point OTT Service |
|  |  | Message Template |

If you have more than one data space, grant access to objects in each data space. Object names in non
default data spaces follow the convention of <DataSpace Prefix>_<Object Name>.
Package Manager in Marketing Cloud Engagement
Scale your best practices and reduce time to value by bundling company or industry-specific solutions
and deploying them across business units with Package Manager.
Have a set of journeys or automations that you want to reuse? Bundle them and all their dependencies,
and then deploy them to other business units. You can create packaged solutions containing fully
configured journeys, automations, content assets, data extensions, attribute groups, and more. Keep
those items current and version-controlled by updating your packaged solutions across all instances.
Manage Marketing Items Across Business Units
Use packaged solutions to deploy best practices and company or industry-specific solutions across
your Marketing Cloud Engagement business units. You can create packages containing fully configured
journeys, automations, content assets, data extensions, attribute groups, and more. Then keep those
items current and version-controlled by updating your packaged solutions across all instances.
Package Manager Reference
Learn how to get the most out of Package Manager by understanding supported objects, supported
actions, and notifications for Marketing Cloud Engagement.

Marketing Cloud Engagement Package Manager in Marketing Cloud Engagement
Manage Marketing Items Across Business Units
Use packaged solutions to deploy best practices and company or industry-specific solutions across your
Marketing Cloud Engagement business units. You can create packages containing fully configured
journeys, automations, content assets, data extensions, attribute groups, and more. Then keep those
items current and version-controlled by updating your packaged solutions across all instances.
The packaging and deployment process involves these high-level steps.
11.. Define your package.
aa.. Choose the items that you want to add, such as specific journeys or automations. Dependencies are
automatically included.
bb.. Define any custom fields in the selected items.
22.. Download the packaged solution.
••
If you plan to deploy using the Engagement package manager, download as a ZIP.
••
To host the file in a secure location and link to it from another package, download the JSON.
33.. Deploy the packaged solution.
aa.. If you have a ZIP file, upload it to Package Manager. Or, if you're deploying from an installed
package, select Choose Solution Package and find your package in the list.
bb.. You can create a subfolder for the package or use prefixes or suffixes to customize the file names of
the items in the package.
cc.. If the package contains custom fields, enter the required data.
dd.. Choose an action for each item being deployed.
44.. After your package is deployed, keep it updated.
aa.. Open the original package definition.
bb.. Recreate the package.
cc.. Redeploy the package using the same name.
dd.. When you choose an action for the items being deployed, you can update existing items with the
new version.
For more information, review these topics.
Package Items for Use in Marketing Cloud Engagement
Create a packaged solution to contain fully configured journeys, automations, content assets, data
extensions, attribute groups, and more.
Customize Branding in Packaged Items
Include custom fields within the items in your packaged solution to enable the user who deploys the
package to customize branding. For example, if an asset contains a logo that varies across
environments, you can create a custom field where the correct logo is inserted during deployment.
Deploy Packaged Items
Marketing Cloud Engagement business units and enterprise accounts support package installations
from AppExchange and ZIP.
Update Packaged Items
To keep your Marketing Cloud Engagement packages working as expected, update them regularly.

Marketing Cloud Engagement Package Manager in Marketing Cloud Engagement
Package Items for Use in Marketing Cloud Engagement
Create a packaged solution to contain fully configured journeys, automations, content assets, data
extensions, attribute groups, and more.
Before you begin, identify which items you want to package. Dependencies are packaged automatically.
Note There's no limit to the number of items that you can include in a packaged solution. But each
item must be smaller than 5 MB, and the packaged solution can't exceed 50 MB.
11.. Hover over the Platform menu, and select Package Manager.
22.. On the Packaging tab, click Create and select New Definition or From Existing.
33.. Select the items to include in the package. You can select any combination of supported objects.
The selection view defaults to journeys, but you can click the tabs to view other objects.
To view all selected items, click Show Details. To return to the grid view, click Hide Details.
When you select an item, all its associated items are also included, such as email messages and data
extensions.
44.. Click Save & Continue.
The number of items and dependencies packaged appears in the screen header.
55.. To create items in the root folders of each app when you deploy the package rather than in the same
folder structure of the source, deselect Preserve Folder Structure.
66.. Click Package Items.
77.. Review the items included and resolve any issues.
88.. Click Next.
99.. If the package includes customizable assets, such as personalization strings, logos, or names,
configure and save the defaults and parameters.
1100.. Click Download Package.
If you plan to deploy using the Engagement package manager, download as a ZIP.
To host the file in a secure location and link to it from another package, download the JSON.
To update the package, use the same package definition to create a package containing updated
versions of all items and deploy it.
See Also
Permissions for Setting Up Marketing Cloud Engagement
Customize Branding in Packaged Items
Deploy Packaged Items
Update Packaged Items

Marketing Cloud Engagement Package Manager in Marketing Cloud Engagement
Customize Branding in Packaged Items
Include custom fields within the items in your packaged solution to enable the user who deploys the
package to customize branding. For example, if an asset contains a logo that varies across environments,
you can create a custom field where the correct logo is inserted during deployment.
Custom fields are supported in objects such as Journey Builder activities, Content Builder entities,
CloudPages, and Automation Studio Send Email activities. Some fields, such as email names and journey
names, aren't supported because they don't allow special characters. Also, some objects, such as email
and SMS activities, have custom fields that are preset and can't be edited.
Before you begin, identify the items that you want to add custom fields to.
11.. In the item, insert a placeholder for each a custom string or URL.
Format your placeholders like this: {{mcpm:fieldName}}. fieldName represents a prompt to your
users about what information to enter. For example, {{mcpm:logo}} prompts the user to input a URL
to their logo image. Or enter {{mcpm:companyName}} and {{mcpm:companyWebsite}} to prompt
the user to input the business unit's name and website.
You can reuse a placeholder repeatedly and use as many placeholders as you want.
22.. Create a packaged solution that contains the item with the placeholders.
33.. Use Custom Fields Configuration to add more information for the user who deploys the package.
Each custom field must have a label. For example, if you use the {{mcpm:logo}} placeholder, you can
enter a label of Logo URL. When a user deploys the package, they see a field labeled Logo URL and
can then input the link to the correct logo. You can also add a more detailed description to help the
user deploying the package know how to format the placeholder.
Note If you don't see custom fields, Package Manager didn't find the correct syntax. Make sure
that your syntax is correctly formatted and try again.
44.. Download your package.
55.. When a user attempts to deploy the package, they must enter values for all custom fields.
66.. When the package is deployed, Package Manager replaces all instances of each placeholder with the
corresponding value.
Deploy Packaged Items
Marketing Cloud Engagement business units and enterprise accounts support package installations from
AppExchange and ZIP.
To avoid permissions errors as you work, review the contents of the package and assign any necessary
create or edit permissions for each item before you start.
11.. Hover over the Platform menu and select Package Manager.
22.. On the Deployment tab, click Deploy and choose a deployment method.

Marketing Cloud Engagement Package Manager in Marketing Cloud Engagement
If you're uploading a ZIP package, drag it into the box or click Upload to find the file on your
computer.
If you're deploying an installed package, click Choose Solution Package and find your package in the
list.
33.. You can enter folder structure names and text to prepend or append to the items. You can also add a
unique timestamp to each file.
Folders are placed directly under the root folder of each app. For example, NewFolder/item.
If you preserved the folder structure when you created the package, the new folder still is inserted
directly under the root folder. For example, NewFolder/YourStructure/YourStructure/item.
44.. Enter and save values for any customizable fields.
55.. Click Next.
66.. Review the items and resolve any issues.
If you rename an item, click Refresh to apply your changes.
When updating a deployed package, use the Action menu to select how to handle updated items. You
can keep the existing version, update to the new version, or keep both items by renaming the new
version.
You can update all supported items, except filter data extensions, synchronized data extensions, and
data extensions that your business unit doesn't have permission to update.
If your package contains new items, you can create or skip them.
You can skip items only if they have no required dependencies or if they're a dependency of an item
that has been skipped or reused.
77.. Click Deploy.
88.. Review the Deploy Summary, and check that everything deployed correctly.
See Also
Permissions for Setting Up Marketing Cloud Engagement
Package Items for Use in Marketing Cloud Engagement
Update Packaged Items
Package Manager Supported Actions
Package Manager Notifications
Update Packaged Items
To keep your Marketing Cloud Engagement packages working as expected, update them regularly.
Before you begin, identify the deployed package you want to update and the package definition
associated with it.

Marketing Cloud Engagement Package Manager in Marketing Cloud Engagement
Note There's no limit to the number of items that you can include in a packaged solution. But each
item must be smaller than 5 MB, and the packaged solution can't exceed 50 MB.
11.. Hover over the Platform menu and select Package Manager.

chunks.heading: 07-reconcile-transactional-sends-in-marketing-cloud-engagement
chunks.body:
---
title: "Reconcile Transactional Sends in Marketing Cloud Engagement"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_engagement_4-6-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "429-429"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Reconcile Transactional Sends in Marketing Cloud Engagement
deployed.
•
When deploying a package for the first time, you can create or skip each item.
•
When updating a package, you can update existing items to the new version or use the previous
version.
•
When updating a package, you can create or skip new items.
•
You can skip an item only if it has no required dependencies or if it's a dependency of an item that has
been skipped or reused.
See Also
Package Manager Supported Objects
Package Manager Notifications
If a packaged solution has unsupported objects, a notification appears before you deploy the package.
To help you troubleshoot issues, review this list of in-app notifications.

| Notification | Definition |
| --- | --- |
|  | Success: The object deployed successfully. |
|  | Warning: There's an issue, such as an unsupported or missing object, which can result in a blank or unconfigured item after deployment. |
|  | Information: After deployment, some properties of this object can differ from the original account. |
|  | Task: Action is needed after deployment to configure this object. When you complete the action, click the checkbox to remove the task notification and mark the action as resolved. |
|  | Error: An error in this object is preventing package creation or deployment. |

Reconcile Transactional Sends in Marketing Cloud
Engagement
To help you meet regulatory reporting requirements regarding the delivery and cadence of SMS
messages and transactional emails, Marketing Cloud Engagement provides a data extension that
contains this information and their final delivery status of Sent or Not Sent. Use this information to
determine whether to send the message again, log how often you're communicating with customers, or
complete other time-sensitive business processes.

chunks.heading: 08-determine-your-marketing-cloud-engagement-instance
chunks.body:
---
title: "Determine Your Marketing Cloud Engagement Instance"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_engagement_4-6-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "430-430"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Determine Your Marketing Cloud Engagement Instance
Enable reconcilation on the send's setup screen when you first create it. By default, the final send status
is saved to the data extension and the Email Notification Service within 12 hours of delivery. You can set
this value from 1 to 72 hours to fit your specific business process needs.
Marketing Cloud Engagement also supports including a custom MessageKey value to identify
transactional sends. This value links messages back to your own account. You can align your message
status with customer communications generated from an external system or link relevant information to
support cases. MessageKey values remain unique for 72 hours. If an issue causes messages to be
submitted multiple times to Salesforce, Salesforce only sends them once.
To retrieve information about your sends for regulatory compliance reports, use the data extract activity
in Automation Studio or the Event Notification Service API.
See Also
MobileConnect Reports
Tracking in Email Studio
Determine Your Marketing Cloud Engagement Instance
To check the status of your Marketing Cloud Engagement account, use the member ID (MID) for your
instance. It's also helpful to know your MID when you configure a Web Collect URL, or when you want to
obtain the URL endpoints for the Marketing Cloud Engagement APIs.
11.. In Marketing Cloud Engagement, click the name of your account.
22.. Copy the ID next to MID.
33.. (Optional) Go to the Marketing Cloud Engagement status page and enter your MID in the search field.
See Also
Salesforce Trust - Marketing Cloud

Expired Marketing Cloud Engagement Subscriptions
When your Marketing Cloud Engagement subscription expires, you have limited access to your account
for 90 days. If you don't renew your account within 90 days, all access is revoked.
An expired account still captures unsubscribes from messages that you sent while your account was
active. If you don't want to renew your account, we recommend that you take several actions before your
account is marked inactive.
•
Export and download subscriber and campaign data.
•
Stop all automated or manual processes, except for processes that capture unsubscribes or export or
download data.
•
Cancel all scheduled sends.
•
Unpublish all sites or content, except for sites or content that capture unsubscribes or export or

Suspended Marketing Cloud Engagement Accounts
download data.
•
Uninstall, delete, or disable all API integrations, except for integrations that capture unsubscribes or
export or download data.
Suspended Marketing Cloud Engagement Accounts
Access to a suspended Marketing Cloud Engagement account is restricted, and the account is deleted if
you don't resolve the issue and reactivate it. To reactivate your service, contact your Salesforce account
executive.
If your account moves from suspended to expired status, your ability to send messages remains
restricted. If you reactivate your account, sending restrictions are removed, but you must reactivate your
journeys, automations, and triggered sends.
When an account is suspended, it still captures unsubscribes from messages that you sent before the
account was suspended. If you don't want to reactivate your account, complete these steps before your
account is deleted:
•
Export and download subscriber and campaign data.
•
Stop all automated or manual processes, except for processes that capture unsubscribes or export or
download data.
•
Cancel all scheduled sends.
•
Unpublish all sites, except for sites that capture unsubscribes or export or download data.
•
Uninstall, delete, or disable all API integrations except for integrations that capture unsubscribes or
export or download data.

chunks.heading: 12-marketing-cloud-engagement-glossary
chunks.body:
---
title: "Marketing Cloud Engagement Glossary"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_engagement_4-6-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "432-443"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Marketing Cloud Engagement Glossary
•
Microsoft Internet Explorer™ 11 or earlier
•
Any version of Internet Explorer in compatibility mode
•
Any version of Microsoft Edge released before 2019
• 1
Opera 12™ or earlier
Screen Resolution
We recommend a minimum screen resolution of 1024×768 pixels for interacting with Marketing Cloud
Engagement.
Recommendations and Requirements for All Browsers
To use Marketing Cloud Engagement, set your browser to accept first-party and third-party cookies. If
cookies are disabled and you attempt to log in to the application, you're returned to the login page with
no error message.
Configure your browser to check for newer versions of cached pages automatically. This step prevents
errors related to outdated cached information.
Some components of Email Studio open in a new window. To ensure full functionality, configure your
browser to allow popup windows that originate from your Marketing Cloud Engagement subdomain.
Mixed Content Errors
Secure private image domains using HTTPS and SSL certificates. Marketing Cloud Engagement can't load
images from unsecured domains. For more information, see Marketing Cloud - Chrome Blocking Mixed
Content.
1
Google Chrome is a trademark of Google LLC. Firefox is a trademark of the Mozilla Foundation. Microsoft
Edge and Internet Explorer are trademarks of the Microsoft group of companies. Safari is a trademark of
Apple Inc. Opera is a trademark of Opera Software AS.
Marketing Cloud Engagement Glossary
Learn about important terms and concepts related to Marketing Cloud Engagement.
Glossary of Terms
A/B testing
A method of market testing in which you send two versions of your communication to two test

Marketing Cloud Engagement Marketing Cloud Engagement Glossary
audiences from your subscriber list and track which version receives the highest unique open or
click-through rate.
Account Send Summary Report
A report that contains metrics related to messages sent from your account.
Allowlisting
Configuring a mail server so that it doesn't reject or filter the email campaigns that you send
using Marketing Cloud Engagement.
AMPscript
A scripting language that you can embed within HTML emails, text emails, landing pages, and
SMS messages.
Attribute by Tracking Event Report
A report that contains information about a subscriber who performed a tracking event in
response to a particular send. You choose a subscriber attribute to include in the report, and
specify the time period for the report.
Audience Builder
An application that helps you understand customer data in real time and build targeted
segments.
Application
A product that allows marketers to execute a marketing process, such as Automation Studio.
Marketing Cloud Engagement includes several applications, and certified partners can build
additional applications.
Application Switcher
The list of applications the user has permission to use. Users can toggle between applications
within the switcher.
Automation Studio
An application that helps marketers build multistep marketing programs where events trigger
actions. For example, a message sends when an import refreshes group data.
BrandBuilder
A private-labeling tool for Marketing Cloud Engagement. You upload a logo and BrandBuilder
provides color scheme options. The color scheme is then applied to Marketing Cloud
Engagement, giving your users an experience that aligns with your brand.
Business Unit (BU)
A hierarchical administration structure that controls access to information and sharing of

Marketing Cloud Engagement Marketing Cloud Engagement Glossary
information. A business unit allows you to manage user roles for the business unit, view users
who have access to the business unit, and define filter criteria for subscribers.
Campaign
A series of related messages.
CAN-SPAM
A United States law related to commercial emails. Among other guidelines, the law imposes
stricter requirements on commercial email than on transactional email. Complying with these
requirements protects you from litigation related to the CAN-SPAM act and demonstrates your
commitment to providing relevant messaging to your customers.
Commercial message
A commercial message is any electronic mail message with the primary purpose of
communicating a commercial advertisement or promoting of a commercial product or service.
Commercial messages must include these elements:
•
A mechanism to unsubscribe
•
The physical mailing address of the sender
•
A notice that the message is an advertisement
When you send a commercial message, Marketing Cloud Engagement checks for the presence of
an unsubscribe link. If an unsubscribe link is present and the subscriber has an unsubscribed
status, the message isn't sent.
Content Box
An area of an email where you can insert text, links, images, and tables. Build and edit content by
managing content boxes in an email. Content boxes are based on a standard template or an
HTML template. You design the layout and input the content of each content box.
Content Detective
A tool that helps you identify spam triggers in your email content. This feature mirrors the logic
used by spam-filtering software to identify words, phrases, and patterns that are likely to trigger
filters and then recommends a resolution to each identified problem.
Content-Building Wizard
A tool that leads you through the process of creating content for template-based emails. The
wizard is designed to handle all content types, such as SMS and Smart Capture, regardless of the
size or impact on the email.
Conversion Tracking Statistics Report
A report that contains a list of conversions and their value for links across multiple sends. To use
this report, enable the conversion tracking feature.

Marketing Cloud Engagement Marketing Cloud Engagement Glossary
Coupon
An offer that renders on an email landing page when the recipient opens it. Associate a coupon
code with each offer.
Data extension
A table that contains data that you specify. Usually, the data you keep in a data extension is
related to subscribers but doesn't fit in the subscriber profile or preference attributes.
Data extract
A feature that exports tracking information from the Marketing Cloud Engagement database.
Data is exported to a CSV file, which is then compressed in a ZIP file and uploaded to your
enhanced FTP server.
Data Extract Activity
An activity that exports data to a file that you can use outside of Marketing Cloud Engagement.
You can also use a data extract activity to transform an XML file to a comma-delimited, tab-
delimited, or pipe-delimited file.
Data filter
A group of criteria that segments a subscriber list or data extension. The data filter segmentation
is based on subscriber attribute values or measures that you create from behavioral data.
Data relationship
A relationship that connects data from a column in a data extension to a column in another data
extension or in a subscriber attribute.
Dedicated IP
An IP address that is only used for sending email from your Marketing Cloud Engagement
account.
Delivery profile
A method of specifying the delivery information for a message. You can reuse delivery profiles
across multiple sends without specifying the information for each one.
Dynamic content
Tailored content that's displayed to the subscriber based on rules that you define.
Email archiving
The process of saving a copy of every email that you send. After an email is initiated, either by a
user or by another trigger, Marketing Cloud Engagement processes the request. At the same
time that the email is being sent to the subscriber, a copy of the email is also sent to the email
archive.

Marketing Cloud Engagement Marketing Cloud Engagement Glossary
Email history
A record of past and scheduled email deliveries. You can also use it to cancel or modify an
upcoming delivery.
Email Performance by Domain Report
A report that provides statistics for the top-performing domains that you sent emails to.
Email Performance by List Report
A report that provides statistics for each subscriber list that received an email.
Email Performance Over Time Report
A report that provides statistics for all sends of a single email during a specified time period.
Email Send Report
A report that shows email send totals over a time period you specify. You can run this report for
your child accounts, if applicable.
Email Send Wizard
A program that guides you through the steps to send an email. This feature is available when you
use the Guided Send option.
Email Sends by User Report
A report that shows which user initiated each send.
Email Studio
An application for marketers to create, target, deliver, track, and manage email marketing
programs.
Enhanced FTP
A secure and flexible system for transferring files, such as data that you export from Marketing
Cloud Engagement.
File Transfer Activity
An activity that uploads or downloads a file from a location that you specify. This activity can also
unencrypt or uncompress a file.
File Transfer Location
A file storage location that you can use to transfer files into your Marketing Cloud Engagement
account, or to transfer files out of your account.
Filter Activity
An activity that applies the logic of a data filter to a subscriber list to create a group of
subscribers who satisfy the filter criteria.

Marketing Cloud Engagement Marketing Cloud Engagement Glossary
Fixed content email
An email that doesn't contain any dynamic content.
Forward to a Friend
A feature that allows subscribers to share the information in an email with friends. When a
subscriber clicks the icon, they complete a form and submit it. Marketing Cloud Engagement
forwards the message and collects tracking information on the forwarded message.
Grid
The list of specific items that you can interact with in a workspace. A grid contains columns of
detailed information about the items in the list.
Group
A subset of subscribers from a list.
Guided send
A send process that uses the Email Send Wizard.
HTML Paste
A feature that helps you to create emails with HTML.
Import activity
An activity that uses information from an external file to update a subscriber list or data
extension. If your account is integrated with a Salesforce account, you can use an import activity
to create and populate a data extension with data from a Salesforce object or report.
Impression tracking
A feature that lets you track the performance of emails that contain content built using
AMPscript or selected by the dynamic content feature.
Impression Tracking by Job Report
A report that shows the performance of an Impression Region for a single send. The report
shows the number of times the content area was sent as part of a job and the link performance
for links in the impression region. This report also contains the overview tracking summary for
the send. Only emails that use dynamic content or AMPscript can be tracked using these reports.
Impression Tracking for Triggered Sends Report
A report that shows the performance of impression regions in emails that were sent over a
specified time period. The report shows the number of impressions and the performance for
links found in the impression region. This report also contains the overview tracking summary for
the entire end. Only emails that use dynamic content or AMPscript can be tracked using this
report.

Marketing Cloud Engagement Marketing Cloud Engagement Glossary
Inbox Detective
An information center that tracks the deliverability of your email campaigns. By sending to a
seed list, Inbox Detective provides visibility into ho

…

title: "What’s Unified Messaging?"
source_pdf: "unified_messaging_4-7-2026.pdf"

purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"

Unified Messaging What's Unified Messaging?
What's Unified Messaging?
Learn how Unified Messaging makes it easier to message with current and potential customers from
Salesforce.

| Available in: Lightning Experience |
| --- |
| Unfied Messaging for Email and SMS is available in: Salesforce Enterprise and Unlimited Editions for Service Cloud AND Enterprise and Unlimited Editions for with Marketing Cloud Next Growth and Advanced Editions |
| Unified Messaging for WhatsApp is available in: Enterprise and Unlimited Editions for Marketing Cloud Engagement Growth and Advanced Editions |
| Not supported in Government Cloud Plus |

WhatsApp Unified Messaging: Service and Marketing in One Channel
Provide your company's service and marketing interactions in one channel built on the Unified
Conversation Platform. This unified experience makes it easy to shift between one-way marketing
promotions and two-way customer support interactions, with Data 360 powering every message. Unified
Messaging also lets you deliver personalized interactions throughout a customer's journey using one
WhatsApp number.
Note To use an existing WhatsApp number or WhatsApp Business account in Unified Messaging,
see Number Reuse and Migration.
Email and SMS Unified Messaging: Marketing with Data 360
Unified Messaging provides the framework you need to connect your marketing activities to information
contained in Data 360. This functionality lets you store subscriber demographic and consent information
in Data 360. When the time comes to send messages or perform other marketing activities, you can use
this framework to segment and target your subscribers for meaningful interactions. This framework also
helps you maintain resources associated with your marketing activities, such as authenticated domains
for email channels and brand, campaign, and code requests for SMS channels.
SMS Unified Messaging: Service and Marketing in One Channel
Manage your company's service and marketing interactions in one SMS channel built on the Unified
Conversation Platform. This unified experience makes it easy to shift between one-way marketing
promotions and two-way customer support interactions. Unified Messaging enables you to provide
personalized interactions throughout a customer's journey by using one phone number.

chunks.heading: part-01
chunks.body:
---
title: "Unified Messaging Setup (part 1/4)"
source_pdf: "unified_messaging_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "8-220"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Unified Messaging Setup
In SMS Unified Messaging channels, Omni-Channel determines the routing of service conversations, and
Flow handles the marketing messages. If a customer interaction is service-related, Omni-Channel routes
the message to a bot or service agent. If the interaction is marketing-related, Flow determines how to
handle the interaction. You can customize the keywords and responses associated with each use case.
SMS Unified Messaging supports text-only messages.
Important You can't upgrade a Service Cloud Messaging channel or Marketing Cloud Engagement
channel to a Unified Messaging channel. When you set up a Unified Messaging SMS channel, use a
new phone number.
See Also
How Conversation Transitions Work in WhatsApp Unified Messaging
Set Up an SMS Unified Messaging Channel
Unified Messaging Setup
Configure each channel in the Unified Messaging section of Salesforce Setup. All channels include
required setup steps as well as optional configuration steps specific to that channel. For example, you
can enable WhatsApp for Digital Engagement and Marketing Cloud Engagement. To fully enable a
WhatsApp channel for these apps, you must both create the connection and deploy data kits and
streams for each channel in Data 360. These streams establish the relationships required to target
subscriber segments and capture data on how subscribers engage with your messages.

| Available in: Lightning Experience |
| --- |
| Unfied Messaging for Email and SMS is available in: Salesforce Enterprise and Unlimited Editions for Service Cloud AND Enterprise and Unlimited Editions for with Marketing Cloud Next Growth and Advanced Editions |
| Unified Messaging for WhatsApp is available in: Enterprise and Unlimited Editions for Marketing Cloud Engagement Growth and Advanced Editions |

Connect to Data 360 for Unified Messaging
Before you can send messages, connect to Data 360 and deploy the required data kits. A data kit is
similar to a managed package, and it contains everything that you need to get started. Use a data kit to
create data streams, which establish the relationships that are required to track engagement and
honor consent. Create a Data 360 connection for each channel that you plan to use.
Create a Branded Domain for Unified Messaging
Use a branded domain to shorten URLs to personalized links. You can then use shortened text in your
messages and use the links to track interactions. A branded domain requires a valid CA-signed HTTPS
certificate.
Set Up a WhatsApp Unified Messaging Channel
Create a WhatsApp channel that supports both marketing and service interactions. To set up a
WhatsApp channel in Unified Messaging, deploy and configure the appropriate data streams in Data

Unified Messaging Unified Messaging Setup
360. Also, make sure that an existing WhatsApp Business account is associated with your Salesforce
account. Contact your account executive to make sure that your account is eligible to use this channel.
Set Up an Email Unified Messaging Channel
Configure these settings to send email messages with Unified Messaging.
Set Up an SMS Unified Messaging Channel
Create an SMS channel that supports both marketing and service interactions. To set up an SMS
channel requires requesting the code to send your SMS messages, including any brands or campaigns
used as part of your sends.
Connect to Data 360 for Unified Messaging
Before you can send messages, connect to Data 360 and deploy the required data kits. A data kit is
similar to a managed package, and it contains everything that you need to get started. Use a data kit to
create data streams, which establish the relationships that are required to track engagement and honor
consent. Create a Data 360 connection for each channel that you plan to use.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
To process data correctly, preserve field visibility for the Data Cloud Salesforce Connector permission set.
Visibility can change when you delete a field, change the permissions on a field, or change the API name
for a custom field.
For best performance in Unified WhatsApp, keep your data kits up to date.
Before connecting, choose how to track and store data privacy preferences.

| Available in: Lightning Experience |
| --- |
| Unified Messaging for Email and SMS is available in: Salesforce Enterprise and Unlimited Editions for Service Cloud AND Enterprise and Unlimited Editions for with Marketing Cloud Next Growth and Advanced Editions |
| Unified Messaging for WhatsApp is available in: Enterprise and Unlimited Editions for Marketing Cloud Engagement Growth and Advanced Editions |
| Not supported in Government Cloud Plus |

USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To install the Consent Data Cloud package: Read and View All Records on Communication
Subscriptions, Communication Subscription
Channel Types, and Engagement Channel Types
Each unified channel type requires two data kits. One package relates to consent settings, and the other
relates to channel engagement data. You can install both at the same time, or you can install each one
separately.

Unified Messaging Unified Messaging Setup
11.. From Setup, in the Quick Find box, enter Unified Messaging, and then select the channel that you
want to configure.
22.. Locate the Channel Setup section for your channel, and then click Install.
••
For the Email channel, install the data kits as part of the Marketing Cloud Next setup process.
••
For the SMS channel, find the data kits at SMS Codes in the SMS section of Unified Messaging.
••
For the WhatsApp channel, find the data kits at Your Numbers in the WhatsApp section of Unified
Messaging.
33.. After the installation completes, click Done.
The required data streams are now connected in Data 360.
Create a Branded Domain for Unified Messaging
Use a branded domain to shorten URLs to personalized links. You can then use shortened text in your
messages and use the links to track interactions. A branded domain requires a valid CA-signed HTTPS
certificate.

| Available in: Lightning Experience |
| --- |
| Salesforce Enterprise and Unlimited Editions for Service Cloud AND Enterprise and Unlimited Editions for with Marketing Cloud Next Growth and Advanced Editions |
| Not supported in Government Cloud Plus |

11.. In Setup, in the Quick Find box, search for the appropriate domain page.
aa.. For SMS domains, enter URL Shortening Domain, and then select URL Shortening Domain.
bb.. For email domains, enter Links, and then select Links in the Email section of Unified Messaging.
22.. Click New Domain.
33.. Enter the name of your domain, and select the CA-signed HTTPS certificate to use. This process
automatically adds the prefix https:// to your URL. We recommend using a subdomain for this
branded domain.
44.. Save your domain.
55.. Click the dropdown next to the domain record that you created and select Details.
66.. Configure the required DNS CNAME record with your domain registrar. Work with your IT team to
update your DNS settings and authenticate the domain.
77.. After the DNS information propagates, click Status to activate the domain.
After you activate the domain, you can select it for use when sending your messages. Shortened links
expire in 90 days and after the links expire, subscribers receive a page not found error.
See Also
Generate a Certificate Signed by a Certificate Authority

Unified Messaging Unified Messaging Setup
Set Up a WhatsApp Unified Messaging Channel
Create a WhatsApp channel that supports both marketing and service interactions. To set up a WhatsApp
channel in Unified Messaging, deploy and configure the appropriate data streams in Data 360. Also,
make sure that an existing WhatsApp Business account is associated with your Salesforce account.
Contact your account executive to make sure that your account is eligible to use this channel.
Note
•
To use a bot or service agent, first set up a Messaging channel in Service Cloud.
•
For a few UCP WhatsApp channels, you may see the error: Unable to display Data
Storage Region . This is because of a known technical issue that temporarily prevents our
system from retrieving and showing this specific piece of information. This is a display issue only
and has no impact on core functionality, such as channel activation, messaging, or your channel's
compliance with data storage policies. We are aware of the issue and are working on a solution.

| Unfied Messaging for SMS is available in: Salesforce Enterprise and Unlimited Editions for Service Cloud AND Enterprise and Unlimited Editions for with Marketing Cloud Next Growth and Advanced Editions |
| --- |
| Unified Messaging for WhatsApp is available in: Enterprise and Unlimited Editions for Marketing Cloud Engagement Growth and Advanced Editions |
| Not supported in Government Cloud Plus |

Prepare for WhatsApp Unified Messaging
Before setting up a WhatsApp channel in Unified Messaging, get to know how it works. Compare
Unified Messaging channels side by side with Marketing Cloud Engagement and Service Cloud Digital
Engagement, and learn about number reuse and conversation transitions that can occur within a
WhatsApp Unified Messaging channel.
Connect Your WhatsApp Business Account
To use WhatsApp in Unified Messaging, connect your WhatsApp Business Account (WABA) and
number to your Salesforce account. If you don't have a WABA, we walk you through creating one.
Manage WhatsApp Consent in Unified Messaging
Set up the data streams that are used to track consent settings for WhatsApp. After you deploy the
data streams, you can manage WhatsApp consent and keywords for each connected number.
Choose Your Unified WhatsApp Data Residency
Ensure industry and regulatory compliance by selecting the country where your Unified WhatsApp
data is stored at rest. While you make your selection in Salesforce Setup, your data storage is managed
by Meta.
Add a Cloud to a WhatsApp Unified Messaging Channel
You can connect an existing WhatsApp Unified Messaging channel to another cloud in your Salesforce
account.

Unified Messaging Unified Messaging Setup
Guidelines for Transferring a WhatsApp Number
To comply with Meta's requirements and ensure a smooth migration of your WhatsApp number, follow
these guidelines.
Migrate to Unified WhatsApp
In Unified Messaging, service and marketing interactions occur in a single channel. If you're already
interacting with customers in a WhatsApp channel created in Digital Engagement (Service Cloud), a
WhatsApp-First channel in Marketing Cloud Engagement, or via another business service provider,
migrate your channel to Unified Messaging to access Unified Messaging features. After migration, your
existing message templates are available in Unified Messaging.
Configure How to Handle Incoming WhatsApp Messages
If an incoming WhatsApp message isn't associated with a keyword or conversation, you can send it to
a flow for additional processing. Depending on your Salesforce setup, you can also send these types of
messages to a support agent or bot or to Marketing Cloud Engagement. Each of your WhatsApp
numbers has its own configuration for processing incoming messages. If you don't configure how to
handle incoming messages, the messages are dropped.
Configure Messaging During a Support Session for Unified WhatsApp
Specify whether a message is sent to customers while they work with Service Cloud support. You can
prevent customers from receiving promotional or other low-priority content during conversations with
an agent or bot. This feature is available for only Unified WhatsApp numbers.

Use a custom alphanumeric code with an international long code set up for one-way sending. This code

preference center. Alphanumeric codes must be associated with the brand, enterprise, product, or
service. These codes can include only alphanumeric characters only. Local carriers must approve and
allowlist all alphanumeric code requests. Alphanumeric codes must include 3 to 11 GSM characters.
Include the brand name in all OTP messages.
Local enterprises with headquarters in Lebanon must include this documentations:
•
Local trade license that's valid for at least a month
•
Copy of the company's Chamber of Commerce Circular that's valid for the previous 12 months
•
Ministry of Finance registration document

You're responsible for complying with local rules and regulations. To ensure compliance, familiarize
yourself with the legislation of the local market. Failure to comply can result in service suspension,
cancellation, and potential fines. Lebanon generally forbids content related, but not limited to, these
products and services:
•
Adult

Gambling

Politics
•
Religion

Ministry of Telecommunications (MPT).

chunks.body:
---
title: "Unified Messaging Setup (part 4/4)"

Unified Messaging Unified Messaging Setup

| EventType | Field Name | Description | Values |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Click | EventType | Type of event that occurred | CLICK |
|  | LinkName | Name or alias for clicked link |  |
|  | LinkURL | Full target URL of clicked link |  |
|  | ResolvedURL | Personalized URL for clicked link |  |
|  | RecipientPhone | Phone number associated with the clicked link |  |
|  | UserAgent | Browser-provided information about the device, OS, browser, and client used when the message was clicked. Salesforce doesn't guarantee the accuracy or completeness of this data. Use as reference only. |  |
|  | IndividualID | Unique identifier of recipient |  |
| Undelivered | EventType | Type of event that occurred | UNDELIVERED |
|  | MessageID | Unique identifier for the originating send |  |
|  | Message | Message text received |  |
|  | DeliveryErrorText | Available standard error text |  |
|  | DeliveryErrorCode | Standard error code mapped to supplier error code |  |

Unified Messaging Unified Messaging Setup
Sample Use Case
After the events take place, you can use the data captured to create segments for use in sending targeted
messages. For example, you can create a segment of unified individuals based on who clicked links from
your messages at least three times in the past month. Segment on the EventDateTime value associated
with the Open event types, then collect the IndividualID values to determine who receives further
messages.
Pause Traffic in a Unified SMS Channel
To troubleshoot your channel configurations, pause your messaging traffic. You can pause all traffic to
the channel, or just pause sent or received messages via Flow and access to bots and human agents.

| Available in: Lightning Experience |
| --- |
| Salesforce Enterprise and Unlimited Editions for Service Cloud AND Enterprise and Unlimited Editions for with Marketing Cloud Next Growth and Advanced Editions |
| Not supported in Government Cloud Plus |

11.. In Setup, in the Quick Find box, enter SMS Codes, and then select SMS Codes.
22.. Click the SMS code that you want to deactivate.
33.. Click Deactivate next to one or both applications.
44.. Save your changes.
When traffic is paused, SMS can't be sent from or to the number in question. Agents and bots receive
errors if they try to send messages, and automated outbound or campaign messages aren't delivered.
Agents can mark open messaging sessions as inactive if you intend to resume traffic within 48 hours. The
number is still associated with your channel in Salesforce, and it isn't available for use in another channel
or with a different business service provider.
When you're ready to resume traffic, activate the capabilities on the same page in Setup. After 48 hours
of paused traffic, all open sessions are ended. If traffic is resumed before 48 hours pass, agents and bots
can continue to handle their open sessions.
Set Up an SMS Conversation in Unified Messaging
When you set up Conversational SMS, recipients can reply to your marketing and service messages.
Then, you can route those replies to people or bots that can address them. To set up a conversation,
enable settings and create a flow.

| Available in: Lightning Experience |
| --- |
| Salesforce Enterprise and Unlimited Editions for Service Cloud AND Enterprise and Unlimited Editions for with Marketing Cloud Next Growth and Advanced Editions |

Unified Messaging Unified Messaging Setup
Not supported in Government Cloud Plus
This feature is available only in countries that are supported by Digital Engagement for Service Cloud.
11.. From Service Cloud, set up the bot or agent that you want to handle the conversation.
See Create a Bot in Your Commerce Store or Set Up Agents.
22.. Create a flow, and configure how you want the bot or agent to direct messages.
See Route to an Enhanced Bot or Prepare to Route to an Agent.
33.. Activate message routing in SMS settings.
aa.. From Setup, in the Quick Find box, enter SMS, and then select SMS Codes.
bb.. Select the code that you want to use for your conversations, and make sure that its consent
requirements match your segment.
See Manage SMS Consent in Unified Messaging.
cc.. From the sender code's Details tab, find the Capabilities section, and activate Send and Receive
Messages via Flow and Access to Agent and Bot Conversational Support.
44.. In Data 360, create a segment to target with conversational messaging.
See Create a Segment.
55.. In your flow, add two elements that monitor and route responses.
aa.. Add a Wait Until Event element.
bb.. In the Flow Action to Monitor field, select the original marketing or service SMS message.
cc.. Set the Content to Interaction field to Responded to Message.
dd.. Add a Forward Message to Bot or Agent element.
ee.. Configure the element to route messages to the correct place.
66.. Update the flow with other elements or settings that you want, and then activate the flow.
Configure a Blockout Window for SMS and WhatsApp in Unified Messaging
To increase the chances that your messages are well-received by your customers, set a blockout window
for when not to send SMS and WhatsApp messages.

| Available in: Lightning Experience |
| --- |
| Salesforce Enterprise and Unlimited Editions for Service Cloud AND Enterprise and Unlimited Editions for with Marketing Cloud Next Growth and Advanced Editions |
| Not supported in Government Cloud Plus |

WhatsApp blockout windows can only be created if there is at least one WhatsApp Number added with
an active Customer Conversation Engagement capability.
11.. In Setup, in the Quick Find box, enter Unified Messaging.
22.. Under Settings, search for Blockout Window.
33.. Click Add New on the list view page.
44.. Select the Channel.
55.. Select the Subscription Type.

chunks.heading: 04-unified-messaging-consent
chunks.body:
---
title: "Unified Messaging Consent"
source_pdf: "unified_messaging_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "221-221"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Unified Messaging Consent
66.. Configure the blockout window start and end times and time zone.
77.. Save your work.
To edit an existing blockout window, select Edit in the dropdown list next to the blockout window you
want to modify.
Unified Messaging Consent
To send messages to your subscribers, you must have their explicit consent. You can't transfer consent
from one Unified Messaging number or channel to another. You can set different required consent levels
for each application within a Unified Messaging number or channel. For example, in a unified SMS
channel used for both service and marketing, change the consent requirements of your marketing
interactions to require an explicit opt-in or double opt-in. You can manage how subscribers provide or
withdraw that consent within Unified Messaging.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
Available in: Lightning Experience
Unfied Messaging for Email and SMS is available in: Salesforce Enterprise and Unlimited Editions for
Service Cloud AND Enterprise and Unlimited Editions for with Marketing Cloud Next Growth and
Advanced Editions
Unified Messaging for WhatsApp is available in: Enterprise and Unlimited Editions for Marketing Cloud
Engagement Growth and Advanced Editions
Not supported in Government Cloud Plus
Unified Messaging uses communication subscriptions to manage consent. Each SMS code or WhatsApp
channel number is mapped to one or more communication subscriptions. If a recipient opts in to a
Unified Messaging channel, they opt in to all of the communication subscriptions mapped to that
Unified Messaging channel. If a recipient opts out of a channel, they opt out of all communication
subscriptions mapped to that channel. See Understanding Consent Concepts in Marketing Cloud Next
and Considerations for Managing Consent Data
See Also
Manage WhatsApp Consent
Manage SMS Consent

Your Unified Messaging channel becomes inactive if your Salesforce Message Credits contract for that
channel expires, you cancel your Salesforce contract, your contract no longer includes Data 360, or you
inactivate your Data 360 instance. When your channel is inactive, you can still review the configuration

Billing Considerations for Unified Messaging
and tracking information, but all send activities cease immediately. To reactivate your channel, contact
your Salesforce account executive.

| Available in: Lightning Experience |
| --- |
| Unified Messaging for Email and SMS is available in: Salesforce Enterprise and Unlimited Editions for Service Cloud AND Enterprise and Unlimited Editions for with Marketing Cloud Next Growth and Advanced Editions |
| Unified Messaging for WhatsApp is available in: Enterprise and Unlimited Editions for Marketing Cloud Engagement Growth and Advanced Editions |
| Not supported in Government Cloud Plus |

After an account is inactive for 30 days due to an expired Salesforce Message Credits contract or 120
days because of an expired Salesforce or Data 360 contract, the channel is deprovisioned. The channel
data is queued for deletion, but your engagement data and billing and usage information remain as long
as you maintain an active Data 360 contract. All channel services (such as DKIM key retirement for email)
are fully deprovisioned at this time.
If not renewed, SMS codes also become inactive but remain under Salesforce management for 30 days.
After 30 days, all customer data (including operational and setup data and any applicable keywords) is
queued for deletion, and Salesforce stops maintaining the SMS code.
If your Data 360 instance is deleted, Unified Messaging no longer works. Creating a new Data 360 tenant
will not automatically restore Unified Messaging. To create the Data 360 tenant and restore Unified
Messaging, contact Salesforce Customer Support.
Billing Considerations for Unified Messaging
When you send a message, Unified Messaging uses both messaging services and Data 360 services. In
addition to Salesforce Message Credits, these activities also consume Data 360 credits.

| Available in: Lightning Experience |
| --- |
| Unified Messaging for email and SMS is available in: Salesforce Enterprise and Unlimited Editions for Service Cloud AND Enterprise and Unlimited Editions for with Marketing Cloud Next Growth and Advanced Editions |
| Unified Messaging for WhatsApp is available in: Enterprise and Unlimited Editions for Marketing Cloud Engagement Growth and Advanced Editions |
| Unified Messaging for email is supported in Government Cloud Plus |
| Unified Messaging for Mobile Apps is available in: Enterprise and Unlimited Editions for Marketing Cloud Advanced Edition. Mobile App Messaging is available only in Marketing Cloud Next accounts hosted in Germany and the United States. |

| Salesforce Message Credits - SMS | SMS | Salesforce Message Credit usage is calculated based on the number of messages sent. | You're charged for all mobile- originated (MO) and mobile- terminated (MT) messages, whether they're delivered or not. Billing rates differ based on country destination and the originating phone number. To determine subtype rates, review the rate card at Multipliers for Salesforce Message Credits. |
| Salesforce Message Credits - WhatsApp | WhatsApp | Salesforce Message Credit usage is calculated based on the number of messages sent. | Billing rates differ based on the subtype that each message falls into. To determine subtype rates, review the rate card at Multipliers for Salesforce Message Credits. You're charged for all messages delivered from your account. |
| Salesforce Message Credits - Mobile App | Mobile App | Salesforce Message Credit usage is based on the number of monthly active devices. | A device becomes a monthly active device after the first instance in the monthly billing window that it emits a registration or custom event to Salesforce. You're charged for all messages sent from your account, whether they're delivered or not. |

---

|  | Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

Integrate, harmonize, and unify your disparate marketing data, making it accessible to your entire
organization-wide data strategy with Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines (MDP). Automate your
marketing data integration and easily export it into your data warehouse and data consumption tools of
choice. Actively monitor and update your raw data and receive notifications when something goes awry.
Note Creating a new help portal takes time. We're in the process of creating all the necessary topics
for MDP. New content will become available over the next several months.
If you'd like more information on how MDP can help your campaigns, contact your account
representative.
