chunks.heading: 02-get-started-with-mdp
chunks.body:
---
title: "Get Started with MDP"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_intelligence_data_pipelines_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "7-34"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Get Started with MDP
Get Started with MDP
With Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines, you can integrate all your data from marketing and
advertising platforms, web analytics, CRM, e-commerce, and more. You can then use this unified view of
your data to optimize campaign performance and discover and act on insights in real time.
Note Creating a new help portal takes time. We're in the process of creating all the necessary topics
for MDP. New content will become available over the next several months.
To get started with MDP, you set up an account and create workspaces to integrate your data.
Get Ready for MDP
Before we start, explore the Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines platform. Then check that you
are using one of Salesforce's support browsers. Finally, familiarize yourself with the user roles that MDP
includes.
MDP Accounts and Workspaces
A Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipeline account is divided into workspaces. An account can
contain multiple workspaces. Each workspace is a separate work environment with its own set of
permissions. Changes that you make in a workspace apply only to that workspace.
Workspace Settings in MDP
Each Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines account can have multiple workspaces. Each
workspace has its own data streams, dashboards, and reports. You typically use workspaces to
separate clients, business units, channel views, internal views, or role-based views-whatever best fits
your needs.
Get Ready for MDP
Before we start, explore the Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines platform. Then check that you
are using one of Salesforce's support browsers. Finally, familiarize yourself with the user roles that MDP
includes.
MDP Navigate the Platform
The Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines platform is divided into five tabs. Here you can
explore the different tabs.
MDP Supported Browsers
You can access Marketing Intelligence Data Platforms from a browser on a Windows, macOS, or Linux
computer.
MDP Security
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines uses a variety of industry-standard and state-of-the-art
security technologies and procedures to help protect your information from unauthorized access, use,
or disclosure. You can decide on which methods best suit your needs. Keeping up with the evolving

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines Get Started with MDP
security landscape is an ongoing, joint partnership between MDP and our customers. As security
threats grow more common, it's important to implement stronger measures to protect your business
and customers.
MDP Navigate the Platform
The Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines platform is divided into five tabs. Here you can explore
the different tabs.
•
Import - Upload all of your marketing data for classification and manipulation via our various
methods: API connectors, TotalConnect, and LiteConnect.
•
Harmonize - Connect your disparate data into one solid foundation, while ensuring data governance,
with a robust set of functionalities.
•
Manage - Monitor and update your raw data.
•
Explore - Ensure your data is accurate before exporting it for further analysis and consumption.
•
Export - Export your data into your database, data warehouse, or Tableau for consumption and
analysis.
When you click a new tab, the left menu bar changes to display the available actions for the tab you
selected. For example, when you click the Import tab, the left menu bar shows your data stream lists,
data stream templates, data load rules and data source authentication.
MDP Supported Browsers
You can access Marketing Intelligence Data Platforms from a browser on a Windows, macOS, or Linux
computer.
To access the platform on a mobile device, your mobile browser must meet these minimum
requirements:
•
Screen resolution of at least 1024 x 768 (when browsers are maximized)
•
Cookies enabled
•
JavaScript enabled
If you use a browser other than the ones listed below, you cannot log in to MDP.
•
Google Chrome
•
Firefox
•
Internet Explorer
•
Safari
Browser-Specific Compatibility
In general, MDP supports the current and prior major releases.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines Get Started with MDP
•
Chrome: Latest stable version supported.
•
Microsoft Internet Explorer:
- Version 11
- Compatibility View isn't supported.
Note Microsoft Internet Explorer is no longer supported. If bugs occur with IE11, they can't be
fixed.
•
Mozilla Firefox: Latest stable version supported.
•
Safari: Latest stable version supported on macOS only.
•
Microsoft Edge: Latest stable version supported.
•
Mobile Browsers:
- Mobile Safari (iOS) - Latest stable version supported.
- Android - The default browser on Android 4.x.x supported.
MDP Security
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines uses a variety of industry-standard and state-of-the-art
security technologies and procedures to help protect your information from unauthorized access, use, or
disclosure. You can decide on which methods best suit your needs. Keeping up with the evolving security
landscape is an ongoing, joint partnership between MDP and our customers. As security threats grow
more common, it's important to implement stronger measures to protect your business and customers.
To learn about our trust and compliance information, see:
•
Marketing Cloud Trust and Compliance Documentation
•
Certifications and documents
•
Privacy Statement
Multi-Factor Authentication in MDP
Multi-factor authentication (MFA) requires users to enter two or more pieces of evidence, or factors, to
prove identification. One factor is something the user knows, such as a username and password
combination. Other factors are verification methods that the user has in possession, such as an
authenticator app or security key.
Multi-Factor Authentication in MDP
Multi-factor authentication (MFA) requires users to enter two or more pieces of evidence, or factors, to
prove identification. One factor is something the user knows, such as a username and password
combination. Other factors are verification methods that the user has in possession, such as an
authenticator app or security key.
Users can enable or disable this functionality from the MDP login page. As of February 1, 2022, MFA is
mandatory for all users. For more information, see the Salesforce Multi-Factor Authentication FAQ.
Note If you're using single sign-on (SSO) to access the MDP platform, you're required to use MFA for

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines Get Started with MDP
your SSO identity provider as of February 1, 2022. For more information, see the MFA requirements
for SSO.
Enable MFA in MDP
Users can enable MFA in their account.
MDP Supported Verification Methods
When a user logs in to Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines for the first time using MFA, the
user must select at least one verification method.
Manage MFA Settings in MDP at the User Level
Users can enable MFA and select verification methods from their user profile.
Single Sign-On (SSO) in MDP
SSO makes access easier and more secure for everyone. You can connect to Marketing Cloud
Intelligence Data Pipelines without another password to manage.
What Are Cookies?
Cookies temporarily store a small amount of information in a user's browser. For example, logging in to
a website, preferences for particular content, capping how often a user sees ads, or tracking what a
user does online. Cookies are set at the user level, allowing every user to customize their own cookie
settings.
Enable MFA in MDP
Users can enable MFA in their account.
MFA isn't applicable for accounts that have SSO enabled. All new accounts have MFA enabled by default.
Users complete MFA each time they log in to Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines and after each
session timeout. Session timeout is defined per MDP account and determines the amount of inactive
time after which a user is logged out of the platform. By default, MDP times out after 60 minutes for
existing accounts and 300 minutes for new accounts.
11.. To the right of the navigation bar, click the dropdown arrow, and then click the Account Settings icon.
22.. Select Account Settings.
33.. Under Settings, select Require Multi-Factor Authentication.
44.. To adjust the session timeout for all the users in the account, in Session Timeout change the timeout.
55.. Save your work.
MDP Supported Verification Methods
When a user logs in to Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines for the first time using MFA, the user
must select at least one verification method.
Email and SMS text messages aren't allowed as a second factor for MFA, because email credentials can
be compromised and text messages can be intercepted.
Supported verification methods:

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines Get Started with MDP
•
Salesforce Authenticator-A Salesforce mobile app that provides secure and fast MFA via push
notifications. It's highly recommended that users set up a PIN or biometric requirement on their
mobile device to ensure that unauthorized parties can't access Salesforce Authenticator. For more
information, see the Salesforce Authenticator web page.
•
One-time password generator-A third-party authenticator app, such as Google Authenticator or
Authy, that generates specific time-based, one-time passcodes that users enter when they log in.
•
Security key-A small physical device, such as a Yubikey or Google Titan Key, that users connect to their
computers when logging in.
•
Built-in authenticator-A service, such as Windows Hello or Touch ID, that verifies a user's identity with
a fingerprint, iris, or facial recognition scan. This method is bound to a specific device, and users must
register a built-in authenticator on each device that they log in from.
•
Recovery code-Generates a list of 10 codes that you can copy or download. Each code can be used
only once. This method doesn't require additional devices. Recovery codes are only recommended as
a back up option and not your primary verification method. If you run out of recovery codes, contact
your Datorama Admin.
Manage MFA Settings in MDP at the User Level
Users can enable MFA and select verification methods from their user profile.
11.. To the right of the navigation bar, click the dropdown arrow, and then click Profile Settings next to
your name and email.
22.. Select the user, and click Edit.
33.. Under Multi factor Authentication, click Enable.
44.. Select a verification method. See Supported Verification Methods.
55.. Save your work.
Single Sign-On (SSO) in MDP
SSO makes access easier and more secure for everyone. You can connect to Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Data Pipelines without another password to manage.
When you set up SSO as the default setting, your identity provider authenticates end users. When a user
wants to access MDP, all processes, policies, and security features that were set up with your identity
provider are applied. Security features that MDP provides, such as two-step verification or the ability to
reset passwords, are no longer in effect because your identity provider handles all aspects of
authentication.
Note RelayState redirection within SSO settings isn't supported. The interaction between MDP and
SSO providers is only via authentication. After users are authenticated, they're directed to the
relevant workspace and to the default login page in the MDP platform.
After SSO is turned on, users can log in to the MDP platform in two ways.
•
Idp initiated login-Using your identity provider portal (SSO vendor) by clicking the Datorama app.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data P

…
chunks.productLabel: Marketing Cloud Intelligence (formerly Datorama; cross-channel analytics and data pipelines)

chunks.heading: 03-the-mdp-data-model
chunks.body:
---
title: "The MDP Data Model"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_intelligence_data_pipelines_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "35-64"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

The MDP Data Model
Slack.
Create a Slack Integration for Workspaces in MDP
Configure Marketing Intelligence Data Pipelines Insights for Slack to send alerts from MDP to Slack. After
the integration is set up, users with permissions and access to the workspace can share alerts to Slack.
11.. Click your workspace name, and then the workspace settings gear icon.
22.. On the workspace menu, click Workspace Integration.
33.. Make sure that Slack is turned on.
44.. Select Workspace level.
55.. Name your integration.
66.. Select the webhook type

| Single webhook | One webhook for all channels. |
| --- | --- |
| Webhook per channel | Select the channels that your users can send to. |

77.. Insert your webhook.
88.. Set your sharing permissions. By default, sharing permissions are set to All users.
99.. Select the Enable predefined sender name and icon checkbox to allow users to send messages from
a predefined display name. Add a display name and a logo. For example, add your name and logo so
that users can share it.
1100.. Click Save.
The MDP Data Model
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines (MDP) pulls data from a variety of sources with different
data types. Each one of these different data types has its own unique combination of dimensions and
measurements. The dimensions are made up of entities that have a certain relationship, or hierarchy,
between them. It's important to maintain this relationship in the Salesforce MDP database, otherwise
the data can get aggregated incorrectly.
Note Creating a new help portal takes time. We're in the process of creating all the necessary topics
for MDP. New content will become available over the next several months.
For this reason, for each of the different data types, Salesforce MDP has emulated the unique
relationship, which exists between these entities. This means that the corresponding MDP entities,
within MDP's own database, follow the same relationship. Modeling the data by these relationships for
the various data types is what's collectively known as the Salesforce MDP Data Model.
The legend used by the diagrams that follow:

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines The MDP Data Model
There are two possible relationship types between entities - one-to-many and many-to-many:
One to Many - For example, the connection between Site and Media Buy. One Site can be linked to
many Media Buys, however, one Media Buy can be linked to one Site only.
Many to Many - For example, the connection between Media Buy and Creative. One Media Buy can be
linked to many Creatives and vice versa, and one Creative can be linked to many Media Buys.
The Main Entity
The Data Model considers one of the entities from each data type to be the main entity. The main entity
is the entity around which everything else revolves or is associated with within a given data type. In the
data stream type diagrams below, all main entities appear in a circle. For example in the Ads data stream
type, Media Buy is the main entity.
The different main entities are:
Overarching Entities
Overarching entities exist in all data stream types and allow you to link different data streams by these
entities. This enables slicing different data types by one (or more) overarching entity. For example,
delivery and social data can both be sliced by the overarching entity 'Product' (as seen in the following
image):
In the overarching entities in the following diagram, the entities on the-right hand side are 'Attributes' of
the main entity. They are in a one-to-many relationship with the main entity, so they can each only hold
one value for each main entity value. For example, in the 'Ads Data Stream Type', 'Product' is an attribute
of 'Media Buy' meaning that there can only be one 'Product' value for each 'Media Buy' value. The
entities on the left-hand side are in a many-to-many relationship with the main entity and as such, they
can each hold multiple values for each main entity value. So for example, in the 'Ads Data Stream Type',
there can be multiple 'Device Category' values for 'Media Buy' value.
Note that Channel isn't a mappable entity.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines The MDP Data Model
Data Stream Custom Attributes
Data Stream Custom Attributes behave like Attributes to the Data Stream Entity. Data Stream Custom
Attributes are updated with a slight delay, so they might not appear in your workspace data immediately
after being added.
Cross-Connectivity Between Data Stream Types
A relationship between entities doesn't have to be via a direct link but can also be through a 'third party'
Entity. For example, Campaign and Site aren't linked directly one to another. However, since each one of
them is linked to Media Buy in a one-to-many relationship, the relationship between Campaign and Site
is many-to-many indirectly.
Data Stream Types - Overview
The following diagrams show which entities are included in each data stream type. Not all entities must
be mapped in each data stream, however it's advised that you always map the main entity. The main
entity appears in a circle so you can distinguish it from the other entities. Entities that aren't mapped by
the user are given a default value by the platform. For example, if the 'Media Buy Entity' is mapped in a
data stream, but the 'Campaign Entity' isn't, the 'Campaign Entity' gets a default value.
Note If the existing Data Stream types don't match your data use case, you can use the Generic
Data Stream Type.
In the Data Models below, the largest circle highlights the Main Entity.
The main entity is determined per data stream type. For example, 'Media Buy' is the main entity in the
'Ads Data Stream Type', but it isn't the main entity in the 'Conversions Data Stream Type'. In the data
models that follow, the largest circle highlights the main entity. You can see that some data stream types
include entities from other data stream types. For example, in the data model for the 'Conversions Data
Stream Type', you can see entities from the 'Ads Data Stream Type'.
The types of data streams are:
•
Ads
•
Ads Verification Blocking
•
Buy Data
•
Buy Data Conversions
•
CRM Leads
•
Competitive
•
Conversion Tag
•
Conversion Tag with Keywords
•
Ecommerce

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines The MDP Data Model
•
Messaging
•
Products
•
Search Keywords
•
Social Element Traffic Source
•
Social Listening
•
Social Objects
•
Web Analytics
•
Web Analytics Events
•
Web Analytics Pages
•
Web Analytics Tags
•
Generic Data Stream Type
Ads
In the world of digital marketing, the ads data stream type is where you can find the data for your display
ads campaigns. This data stream type contains the delivery data that comes from these campaigns. For
example, how many times an ad has been seen, clicked or how much money it cost. The ads data stream
type can be used with providers, such as Facebook Ads, Google Ads, Twitter Ads, Bing Ads and more.
Ads Verification Blocking
The ads verification blocking data stream type tracks data where ads were blocked by ad verification
platforms, and therefore weren't served. This usually pertains to ads that were blocked due to a violation
of the platform's terms and conditions and either disrupted user experience or violated brand safety. You
can use this data stream type with providers, such as IAS, and Double Verify.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines The MDP Data Model
Buy Data
The 'buy data' data stream type is intended for ingesting data relating to planned advertising activity,
sometimes referred to as 'planned data' or a 'media plan'. Used in Media Transparency Center
implementations, it facilitates the bulk creation of Insertion Orders (or IOs for short), which themselves
represent the scope and details of the said activity, with information such as the IO start and end dates,
IO cost type, IO rate and IO budget. It's also used to create an association between IOs and their related
delivery data, thereby unifying planned with actuals. This data stream type can be used to ingest media
plans from systems, such as Salesforce, Mediaocean, Nexelus, and Netsuite.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines The MDP Data Model
Buy Data Conversions
Buy data (ingested using the 'buy data' data stream type) can contain IOs that are associated with
conversion tags. However, not all conversion tags associated with a certain delivery item under an IO, are
necessarily relevant for that IO's cost calculations. The buy data conversions data stream type allows you
to associate between existing IOs and the conversion tags of relevance. In a sense, this data stream type
can be thought of as a 'conversion tag filter' for IOs, and is only used as an add-on to an existing use of
the 'buy data' data stream type.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines The MDP Data Model
CRM Leads
The CRM leads data stream type is intended for ingesting data relating to the potential prospects of a
business, commonly known as 'Leads'. Designed to keep a historical record of each lead's evolution over
time, this data stream type is equipped with slowly changing type 2 dimensions, such as lead stage, lead
status and lead modified date. The CRM leads data stream type can be used to ingest data from lead
generation platforms, such as Marketing Cloud Account Engagement, Hubspot Marketing and Intercom,
as well as from CRM platforms, such as Salesforce, Sugar CRM, and CRM Creatio.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines The MDP Data Model
Competitive
The competitive data stream type, which is used side by side with the social objects data stream type,
allows you to compare yourself to your competitor by displaying various social metrics side by side from
the two data stream types. The purpose of this data stream type is to allow you to view your competitors'
data separately from your own data. This data stream type is suitable for any social media platforms,
such as Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, and YouTube.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines The MDP Data Model
Conversion Tag
The conversion tag data stream type is intended for ingesting data related to the various 'calls to action'
tied to your campaigns, also known as conversions. You can see what particular conversion tag prompted
your audience to complete a call to action, and whether this happened after they viewed your ad or after
they clicked it. This data stream type also includes data related to the monetary aspects of your
conversions for example, how much a conversion cost or how much revenue it brought in. This data
stream type can be used with providers, such as Facebook Ads, Google Ads, Google Display&Video 360,
Twitter Ads, and more.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines The MDP Data Model
Conversion Tag with Keywords
Whenever an ad is displayed in a search engine result and drives a conversion, the search keywords that
led to this conversion are attributed to it. In these cases, the conversion tag with keywords data stream
type is used to ingest the conversion data along with the respective search keywords related to it. The
conversion tag with keywords data stream type is suitable for providers, such as Google Ads, Search Ads
360, and Bing Ads.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines The MDP Data Model
ecommerce
In the world of ecommerce, the ecommerce data stream type is where you can find different levels of
data for product catalogs, placed orders, number of purchased items within each order, and more. This
data stream type is intended for ingesting different data types, in their various forms, from ecommerce
plat

chunks.heading: part-01
chunks.body:
---
title: "MDP Uploading Data (part 1/5)"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_intelligence_data_pipelines_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "65-328"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

MDP Uploading Data
the impressions that the same campaign generates in each source.
Overarching Entities
Overarching entities are classification-purpose dimensions that exist in all data stream types and enable
classifying data from multiple sources for matching values. For example, it's possible to see all the data
from messaging and analytics data stream types for the matching product value.
The hierarchical relationship between entities can be a one-to-many or a many-to-many relationship. This
relationship dictates the number of possible values of one entity against the other. For example, the
relationship between product and message send is a one-to-many relationship. One product can have
many message sends, but each message send can only have one product.
Messaging Measurements
This table lists all the measurements in the messaging data stream type alongside their aggregation
method.
This data stream type has the exact same measurements in both Sum Aggregation and Lifetime
Aggregation. For example, message soft bounces from the source data can be mapped to message soft
bounces or message soft bounces (Lifetime) in MDP. You can decide which aggregation method to use
based on the relationship between the date breakdown in the source data.
The messaging data stream type also contains Rate Measurements in percentage terms.
MDP Uploading Data
One of the most crucial steps when working with Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines, is
uploading data. Since MDP supports numerous types of data, you can choose from many different ways
to upload it. During the upload stage, you can map your data to determine which fields appear in MDP
and you can even create custom calculated fields to get the most out of your data. All these actions are
done in the Import tab.
Note Creating a new help portal takes time. We're in the process of creating all the necessary topics
for MDP. New content will become available over the next several months.
MDP Create Data Streams
Whenever you upload data, data streams are created. These data streams can be referenced
throughout the MDP platform, for example in pivot tables and reports. There are several methods
available for creating data streams in Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines. You can use an API

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Uploading Data
connector to connect via API, TotalConnect to upload external files, or LiteConnect if you have data
from a single source. You can choose which method is best for you.
Mapping Your Source Data in MDP
Mapping connects the source fields in your uploaded file to the corresponding Marketing Cloud
Intelligence Data Pipelines fields. MDP uses machine learning algorithms to map your data. The
mapping process is similar regardless of which method you use to upload your data, with some minor
differences. If you're using an API connector to upload your data, MDP automatically maps the fields
for you, but you can make changes to the mapping. If you're using TotalConnect, MDP identifies the
most suitable data stream type according to your data and offers an initial mapping suggestion.
MDP Advanced Settings in Data Streams
Define advanced settings to your data stream to select load modes, load rules, or configure additional
settings. The available settings vary based on the type of data stream that you're creating.
Data Stream List
View, manage, and create data streams from the Data Stream List, in the Import tab.
MDP Create Data Streams
Whenever you upload data, data streams are created. These data streams can be referenced throughout
the MDP platform, for example in pivot tables and reports. There are several methods available for
creating data streams in Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines. You can use an API connector to
connect via API, TotalConnect to upload external files, or LiteConnect if you have data from a single
source. You can choose which method is best for you.
Creating Data Streams with TotalConnect
If you need to upload data from a source and no API connector exists, you can create a data stream
with TotalConnect. For example, if you have a proprietary dataset in Excel, CSV, or another file format,
or if a custom report is sent to you by a data partner that is beyond the scope of what their API can
provide.
MDP Creating Data Streams with API Connectors
API connectors enable you to easily connect data sources such as Google Ads or Facebook to
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines. When you connect an API connector, an API data stream
is created that automatically maps the data fields the vendor provides to the MDP data model,
allowing you to access your information.
MDP Creating Data Streams with LiteConnect
With LiteConnect, you can upload a file into Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines as is and
utilize the data automatically. You don't need to consider the predefined structure and constraints of
the data model or map fields.
Creating Data Streams with TotalConnect
If you need to upload data from a source and no API connector exists, you can create a data stream with
TotalConnect. For example, if you have a proprietary dataset in Excel, CSV, or another file format, or if a
custom report is sent to you by a data partner that is beyond the scope of what their API can provide.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Uploading Data
With TotalConnect, you can either upload a flat file or use a technical vendor. You can define the retrieval
method, for example, if you want to pull your data via an HTTPS server. You can also decide what to do
when new data comes in, for example, replace the existing data with the new data or filter out data
before it's loaded.
MDP Create a Data Stream with TotalConnect
You can upload data manually, automatically using FTP, or an email address, or by scheduling an
update with a technical vendor.
MDP Preview Source Data
After you upload your data with TotalConnect, a preview of your source data lets you see how MDP
reads the raw data, including fields and their types. The table reflects all the fields (columns) from your
source file. Each field is defined with a type-text, number, or date-and you change the type if needed.
You can also change the date format.
Transformers
Transformers are a set of configurations applied to files that are uploaded into Marketing Cloud
Intelligence Data Pipelines. Transformers are used to alter the structure of the data files in order to pull
the exact data sets you need. They instruct the platform as to what data table must be extracted from
your source file allowing you to manipulate the file structure.
MDP Mapping Your Source Data in TotalConnect
Mapping connects the source fields in your uploaded file to the corresponding MDP fields. Marketing
Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines uses machine learning algorithms to map your data. The mapping
process is similar regardless of which method you use to upload your data, with some minor
differences. If you're using TotalConnect, MDP identifies the most suitable data stream type according
to your data and offers an initial mapping suggestion.
MDP Troubleshoot TotalConnect Data Streams
Errors can occur that prevent data streams from being created or processed. You can view errors using
the Log button. The log contains important technical information and can help you diagnose issues.
MDP Create a Data Stream with TotalConnect
You can upload data manually, automatically using FTP, or an email address, or by scheduling an update
with a technical vendor.
When uploading data, be aware of the data limits:
•
Direct upload-40 MB / 15 million rows (subsequent)
• Email-30 МB / 15 million rows (subsequent)
•
Import-500 GB / 15 million rows (subsequent)
•
FTP-500 GB / 15 million rows (subsequent)
11.. Navigate to the Import tab.
22.. Expand Data Streams List.
33.. Click + icon. to add a new data stream
44.. Click TotalConnect.
55.. To upload a file, drag it to the center of the page.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Uploading Data
66.. To use a technical vendor, select the technical vendor from the list.
77.. To use a template, click Use Template.
After the data uploads, a preview screen appears. For information about viewing the source data and
how to change it, see MDP Preview Source Data
MDP Preview Source Data
After you upload your data with TotalConnect, a preview of your source data lets you see how MDP reads
the raw data, including fields and their types. The table reflects all the fields (columns) from your source
file. Each field is defined with a type-text, number, or date-and you change the type if needed. You can
also change the date format.
Field Types
Each field is defined with a different type (text, number, or date), and you can change the type if
necessary. Click the column name and select the type.
Date Type Fields
When it comes to date type fields, recognizing date formats is of utmost importance regarding data
correctness and aggregation. In order to ingest a particular row of data, MDP must be able to read the
date format correctly.
Since there are a variety of different date formats, MDP automatically recognizes the format that was
used in the data source. In some cases, multiple formats are used, therefore more than one format is
found.
To change the date format or add a second date format, click the Day column and then click the Settings
icon. You can select a format from the list or enter a date format. To apply more than two date formats,
use the PARSEDATE() function.
Transformers
Transformers are applied to your source file to create the final data table that is used later when
mapping. Four transformers are automatically applied: File, Table, Headers, and Totals. You can add
transformers for more advanced file structure manipulations.
When you apply transformers, you can see how your source data is affected in Preview.
See MDP Apply File Transformers for more information about what you can do with Transformers.
Highlight Tool

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Uploading Data
Use the highlight tool to focus on a specific field type. For example, you can highlight all the date
columns.
You can highlight date, text, and number fields, as well as columns added by transformers, which create
additional columns, like transpose, or unnest.
Transformers
Transformers are a set of configurations applied to files that are uploaded into Marketing Cloud
Intelligence Data Pipelines. Transformers are used to alter the structure of the data files in order to pull
the exact data sets you need. They instruct the platform as to what data table must be extracted from
your source file allowing you to manipulate the file structure.
For example, if your source file contains extra or missing information- missing headers, multiple sheets,
and extra rows or columns. The transformers allow you to manipulate the file structure, for example
inserting a default header where it's missing so that data is pulled correctly. Transformers are applied
during the preview source step when creating or editing a TotalConnect data stream. Four transformers
are applied to your data automatically: File, Table, Headers, and Totals. Add additional transformers for
more advanced file structure manipulations.
MDP Apply File Transformers
Use file transformers when you want to transform a file that has special characteristics like uncommon
delimiters, multiple sheets, or specific file encoding. For example, if a file uses ~~ as the delimiter
instead of a comma or a tab, use the file transformer to specify the custom delimiter so that you can

MDP Amazon Vendor Central API Connector
Amazon Vendor Central is a web interface used by first-party sellers, such as direct manufacturers and
distributors, for selling products directly to Amazon.
By connecting to the Amazon Vendor Central API, you get information about sales and traffic, consumer
behavior, operations, and more. You can view both order and item-level data using the Amazon Standard
Identification Number (ASIN) of the products. The data stream type is ecommerce, and data lagging is 1
day.
Note Because of an Amazon limitation, you can fetch only the past 7 days of your data. After
connecting your data to MDP, the API brings in data according to the set schedule, (the default is
daily), and it accumulates in MDP.
More details about the Amazon Vendor Central API connector:
•
The Amazon Vendor Central API connector brings in the ASIN product number as shown in Amazon
Seller Central and maps it as 'Product Key' automatically.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Uploading Data
•
You can connect more than one marketplace to the connector, however you must create a separate
data stream per each marketplace.
•
Amazon sometimes requires your approval to share data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data
Pipelines.
•
Amazon Vendor Central allows you to query 7 days back of data. After you connect your data to MDP
the API continues bringing in data according to the set cadence, which is automatically set to daily.
•
Currently you can't retrieve direct-fulfillment data with this connector; however a new connector is
being developed to handle this use case. For more information, reach out to support.
MDP Create an Amazon Vendor Central Data Stream
Connect your Amazon Vendor Central data to Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines.
MDP Create an Amazon Vendor Central Data Stream
Connect your Amazon Vendor Central data to Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines.
11.. On the Import tab expand Data Streams Lists.
22.. Click Add New.
33.. Select the Amazon Vendor Central connector.
44.. In the General section, add an authentication.
55.. In the Authentication Properties window, from the Market Place dropdown, select the location where
you sell your products.
You can have one marketplace per data stream. If you're using more than one marketplace in your
account, create another data stream for this marketplace. You can create as many data streams as
needed.
66.. Click Next.
77.. Enter your Amazon Vendor Central credentials. Amazon sometimes requests your approval to share
some of your data with MDP.
88.. To change the mapping, at the bottom of the Create New Stream page, select Map to Mode.
99.. To apply advanced settings, at the bottom of the Create New Stream page, select Advanced Settings.
1100.. Click Create.
Quality check your data.

22.. Create a report, or pivot table in MDP containing the same dimensions and measurements for the
same report and date range.
33.. Compare the measurement totals and make sure they align.
MDP Account Analytics API Connector
With the Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines Account Analytics connector, you can monitor
engagement, page views, audit changes, and entity creation logs. You can use this data to create
customized views, add dynamic dashboards, and pull actionable reports. The connector includes logs for
both unpublished and published pages.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Uploading Data
To create an API data stream for MDP Account Analytics, you need access to the workspace that you're
requesting data for.
The MDP Account Analytics data stream type is Lite Connect, and data lagging is 1 day.
Users are account level entities. When selecting the user entity from the extended properties, you see
data for all users in the account and not the specific workspace.
MDP Create a Datorama Account Analytics Data Stream
To pull Datorama user activity and measure user engagement into MDP, create a Datorama Account
Analytics data stream.
MDP Create a Datorama Account Analytics Data Stream
To pull Datorama user activity and measure user engagement into MDP, create a Datorama Account
Analytics data stream.
To create an API data stream for Datorama Account Analytics, you need access to the workspace that
you're requesting data for.
11.. On the Import tab, expand Data Streams List.
22.. Click Add New.
33.. Select Datorama Account Analytics.
44.. In the General section, select the account and workspaces from the dropdown.
55.. Set the time period for the initial data fetch. You can process data up to the past 180 days.
66.. In Extended Properties, select the entities that you want to audit from the dropdown.
77.. To change the mapping, click Mapping. You can also access mapping by selecting Map to Model at
the bottom of the Create New Stream page.
88.. To define advanced settings for the data stream, at the bottom of the Create New Stream page, click
Advanced Settings.
99.. Click Create.
1100.. Quality check your data. Compare the data pulled from the connector to the data in Datorama
Account Analytics.
AOL One Video API Connector
The ONE by AOL API connector retrieves data related to Video Ads that are displayed on mobile devices.
In using our API you'll able to retrieve standard Ads and Conversions data, such as Campaigns, Media
Buys, Impressions, Clicks, and Video Measurements.
Additional Measurements and Dimensions, including Viewability Measurements, are also retrieved and
are available unmapped. Their relevance depends on your data configuration.
•
Channel: Display
•
Data Lag: 1 Day

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Uploading Data
Prerequisites
•
Valid Client ID & Secret - Contact your ONE by AOL representative to receive these credentials. Ask for
a Production Client ID and Secret. The Authentication Process is performed by the Marketing Cloud
Intelligence Data Pipelines Platform.
•
Organization ID - The ID is available on the top right menu of the ONE by AOL UI. By clicking your
organization name, you're able to see its ID.
Extended Properties
Continent and Time-Zone - To generate reports within ONE by AOL, specify the Continent and Time-
Zone for the account. This applies to using the API as well and setting the wrong values could lead to
discrepancies in the data.
Default values are set (North America, ETC/UTC), so the Data Stream is created without changing these
values.
How to QA Your Data
Generate a report from the Reports section under the 'Analytics' menu, including all of the relevant
dimensions and measurements. Make sure to set the correct date range, Continent, and Time-Zone.
AppNexus Advertiser API Connector
AppNexus is a global internet technology company that operates an independent marketplace for digital
advertising, and enterprise technology for buyers and sellers of digital ads. AppNexus Advertiser is used
to retrieve performance, revenue, and profit data across a selected advertiser's creatives and campaigns.
•
Channel: Display
•
Data Stream Type:
- Ads
- Conversion Tag
•
Data Lag: 1 Day
Prerequisites
To connect to AppNexus Advertiser, you need a valid AppNexus user with API access enabled on the user
level. If your username and password don't grant you access to the API, contact your AppNexus
representative.
General Setup
After you've entered your credentials, select the Profile you want to pull data for.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Uploading Data
Extended Properties
•
Location Breakdown - Select this to add a further breakdown to the data retrieved by the API, resulting
in a higher level of granularity on which the Measurements can be sliced. The following Breakdown is
available when pulling data from the AppNexus Advertiser API, in addition to the standard data being
pulled:
- Country - Select this to pull country data and add the Dimension 'Country' to the dataset.
- TimeZone Breakdown - Select the time zone for which you want to pull data. (This is only available
for the Geo Analytics Report. Billing data is always be based in the UTC Time Zone.)
•
Get Conversions Data - Select this checkbox to retrieve Conversion and Revenue data in addition to
the Delivery data retrieved by the API by default. The following fields are added and mapped to the
Conversion Tag Data Stream Type:
- Total Conversions
- Revenue
- Post Impression Revenue
- Post Impression Conversions
- Post Click Revenue
- Post Click Conversions
- Conversion Tag Key
•
Get Creative Level Data - Select this checkbox to retrieve creative level data in addition to the Delivery
data retrieved by the API by default. The following fields are added and mapped to the Conversion Tag
Data Stream Type:
- Creative Name
- Creative Key
- Creative Format
•
Get Video Metrics - Select this checkbox to retrieve video metric data in addition to the Delivery data
retrieved by the API by default. (Note that Video Measurements can't be retrieved along with Billing
Period Values). The following fields are added and mapped to the Ads Data Stream Type:
- Video Plays
- Video Fully Played
- Video Competitions 75%
- Video Competitions 50%
- Video Competitions 25%
Known Issues
AppNexus stopped supporting Campaigns in Augmented Line Items (ALI) in August 2019. Instead, they
use Splits. Since Campaign ID and Name fields are mapped by default, these fields return empty for ALIs.
Split ID and Name are available unmapped.
QA Your Data
•
Create the desired report in the AppNexus platform for the required date range.
•
Process the relevant Data Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines for the same date

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Uploading Data
range.

Create a Report/Pivot Table within Intelligence Data Pipelines containing the exact same Dimensions
and Measurements for the same report and date range.

AppNexus Network API Connector
AppNexus is a global internet technology company that operates an independent marketplace for digital

Details
If you don't want to retrieve data for all the available Advertisers, use the Data Load Rules to filter out

---
title: "MDP Uploading Data (part 3/5)"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_intelligence_data_pipelines_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "65-328"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

•
To validate your data go to 'Email' section under the 'Content' tab, then navigate to the 'Analyze'
section, scroll down to the second segment under 'Email Performance' and switch to the 'Campaigns'
view. You can validate the data at the overview level or look into a specific campaign.
•
Be aware when validating the data that having more than one Campaign with the same Email Subject
affects the data view.
Innovid API Connector
Innovid API Connector
•
Channel: Display
•
Data Lagging: 2 days
Prerequisites
A valid Innovid username and password with API permissions. To enable API for your user, contact your
Innovid representative.
Note There's a 90-day limit for customers on processing the Innovid API

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Uploading Data
Extended Properties
•
Get Viewability Data - retrieves ad verification data.
Instagram by Facebook Connector in Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines
The Instagram by Facebook API connector retrieves lifetime post data from Instagram using Facebook's
graph API. This connector pulls a larger variety of measurements, such as organic impressions. Instagram
by Facebook is a social channel connector, the data stream type is social objects, and there's no data lag.
Additional Information regarding Instagram by Facebook:
•
The connector shows LIFETIME type measurements. LIFETIME measurements display the
accumulated value of the measurement as of the current date.
•
When an Instagram data stream is created, all posts from the creation date of your profile are
retrieved.
•
Entries in the data stream log appear under the extraction date's processing date, which is listed as
Today.
•
When processing the data stream, there's no date selection because data for all posts are retrieved
until today and assigned a date value that matches the processing day.
•
Measurement data retrieved for all posts is accurate as of the processing date.
•
To prevent potential data inaccuracies, data streams in LIFETIME aggregation don't allow for any failed
retrievals to be reprocessed post-failure.
•
After data is deleted from an Instagram data stream, the deleted data isn't accessible for retrieval
through reprocessing.
•
To reprocess data retrieved in the past without actually deleting any data, for example, to
accommodate changes in mapping, use the Re-Run Files option.
Create an Instagram by Facebook Data Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines
To create and manage Instagram reports, create an Instagram by Facebook data stream.
Create an Instagram by Facebook Data Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines
To create and manage Instagram reports, create an Instagram by Facebook data stream.

To create an API data stream: ADMIN
To create an Instagram by Facebook data stream you need a valid Facebook username and password and
Instagram and Facebook accounts linked to each other.
11.. On the Import tab, expand Data Streams.
22.. Click Add New.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Uploading Data

44.. In the General section, select the account that you want to pull data for.
55.. To add a new data source authentication, click Add New, and enter your Instagram credentials.
66.. In the Extended Properties set these properties.
aa.. To pull data for a specific page, enter the page ID.
bb.. To retrieve social interaction metrics for Instagram Stories select Get Stories Insights. This checkbox
adds story data to your Instagram by Facebook data stream. You may need to re-authenticate your
Instagram credentials to see the stories.
••
The API only reports organic interaction metrics-interactions on ads containing a media object
are not counted.
••
Data becomes available only after you check the checkbox. Historical story data is not available.
••
Insight stories are collected by webhook notifications. Story data is sent to your data stream after
the story expires. It may take up to 48 hours from the first set up until you see story insights in
your stream.

same date range, dimensions, and measurements.
dd.. Compare and make sure that the measurement totals are aligned.
Instagram Public API Connector
The Instagram Public (competitive) API allows you to get basic metadata and metrics for other
Instagram Business Accounts.
•
Channel: Social, Competitive
•
Data Stream Type: Competitive
Prerequisites
A valid Facebook Username and Password connected to a Facebook Page linked to an Instagram
Business Account. Read more about this API's prerequisites here.
Details
•
Not all fields retrieved via the API are mapped automatically. If you wish to include a certain field that
isn't mapped by default, use Manual Mapping
•
All the Measurements in the Instagram Public API are aggregated as LIFETIME (LIFETIME aggregation
means that Measurements hold the total accumulated value up until the current date.)

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Uploading Data
•
Since this connector only retrieves the data for the date of retrieval - you won't be able to reprocess
past dates in Instagram Public Data Streams
Known Issues
The Instagram API Connector can only retrieve data for Instagram Business Accounts and not private
Instagram Accounts. There are two ways to tell if the account is a business account:
•
The Business Type is listed directly under the account's name.
•
The account's profile includes the 'Email', 'Directions', or 'Call' buttons.
Extended Properties
Instagram Account Name: Enter the Instagram Account Name you wish to track. You can enter up to 5
Instagram Business Account Names per Data Stream, separated by a comma. The Instagram Account
Name appears at the top of the Instagram page.
How to QA
•
In Instagram, click the relevant element - for example, the post you're comparing - and check its totals
for the Measurements retrieved by the Instagram Public API (IE: Followers, Comments, Likes, and so
on).
•
Compare those Measurement totals to the totals you see in Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data
Pipelines for the same element.
Integral Ad Science API Connector
IAS is an Ad Verification Vendor displaying Viewability, Ad Safety, and Ad Fraud data.
•
Channel: Ad Verification
•
Data Stream Type: Ads Verification
•
Data Lagging: 1 day
Prerequisites
To connect to IAS, you need a valid IAS username and password.
Details
•
Data is retrieved on the Campaign, Media Partner, and Placement level for all media types.
•
The Basic IAS measurements are automatically mapped, however many measurements are unmapped
as well.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Uploading Data
General Setup
After entering your credentials, select the relevant Team you want to retrieve data for. This correlates to
the teams in the IAS platform
Extended Properties
•
Report Type - Select the IAS report you want to retrieve data for. You must have permission to generate
the report in the Excel Reports section in IAS for it to be available to you in Marketing Cloud
Intelligence Data Pipelines.
- The following reports are supported for Campaign Monitoring:
- 3MS Viewability - MRC mandated data set that uses measured impressions as the denominator,
providing clients with a tangible in-view number.
- Campaign Viewability - Report that shows the data behind the ability for a digital ad to be seen by
an actual consumer. Extrapolated report that uses data science to provide clients and with an in-
view percentage across all unblocked impressions. This report includes custom metrics to help
provide additional detail.
- Adsafe Monitoring - Brand Safety level reporting that reports back on Risk Level analysis of a
campaign and its ads.
- Country Compliance - Ads Tracked by Country Location in which they were served.
- DMA Compliance - Ads Tracked by Sub Region in the Country's in which they were served. (Sub
Regions vary by Country. (that is, USA's Sub Regions are States)).
- Device Type - Ads Tracked by device on which they were served (that is, Laptop/Desktop, Mobile,
Tablet).
- Traq Score - TRAQ (True Advertising Quality) score. TRAQ combines critical variables - Ad Fraud,
Viewability, Brand Safety, Ad Clutter, and Professionalism - to offer the most comprehensive
appraisal of media quality. You can target web pages based on scores ranging between 250 and
1,000, with higher scores meaning higher quality.
- Suspicious Activity - Suspicious Activity Report is an aggregated report that provides data on
suspicious imps, non-suspicious, their percentages, and the different types of bot impressions
seen per campaign for a given time period.
- Facebook Display Viewability - Detailed analysis for advertisers who want to see Facebook
campaign performance in terms of Display Viewability.
- Facebook Video Viewability - Detailed analysis for advertisers who want to see Facebook
campaign performance in terms of Video Viewability.
- Overview - A summary view of metrics from various detailed reports. Overview Report doesn't
report on any new metrics, all metrics are from Fraud Report, Brand Safety Report, or Viewability
Report.
- Viewability - IAB and MRC reporting that shows a detailed analysis of data with the removal of
fraudulent data from ads that weren't shown to a human due to bot activity, pixel stuffing, could
never be in view, etc.
- Brand Safety - Reporting that provides a detailed analysis of ads categorized by the 7 Brand Safety
Content categories.
- Ad Fraud - Reporting that provides a detailed analysis of any deliberate activity that prevents the

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Uploading Data
proper delivery of ads to the intended people, at the right time, and in the right place.
- The following reports are supported for Firewall:
- Facebook Display Viewability
- Facebook Video Viewability
- Overview
- Viewability
- Brand Safety
- Ad Fraud
•
IAS Product - Select the IAS product you want to retrieve data for. The following products are
supported:
- Campaign Management
- Firewall Note that if the selected product doesn't correspond to the team's settings, the Data Stream
processing will either fail or retrieve zero rows, depending on your user permissions.
Known Issues
•
The IAS vendor operates in US hours. US hours may not be compatible with your time zone processing
hour, which can lead to incomplete data for the previous day. The following run the next day should
retrieve the full data for the incomplete day.
•
Some 'Firewall' reports have been deprecated from both the IAS platform and API, therefore when
trying to process these deprecated Data Streams the following error message appears:
*PROVIDER_INTERNAL_ERROR,Message=The report isn't supported in Firewall Product*
How to QA
•
To QA your data, generate a compatible report in the 'Excel Reports' section located under the
'Overview' menu.
•
Create the desired report in the platform for the required date range. The 'Cut Off' field should be set
to zero. It

…

chunks.heading: part-04
chunks.body:
---
title: "MDP Uploading Data (part 4/5)"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_intelligence_data_pipelines_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "65-328"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

- Total Conversions
- Revenue
- Post Impression Conversions
- Post Click Conversions
- Conversion Tag Name
- Conversion Tag Category
•
Swipe Attribution Window - Select the swipe attribution window that you defined in your Snapchat
account. The following options are available:
- 1 day
- 7 days
- 28 days

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Uploading Data
•
View Attribution Window - Select the view attribution window that you defined in your Snapchat
account. The following options are available:
- None
- 1 Hour
- 3 Hours
- 6 hours
- 1 day
- 7 days
- 28 days
The following table shows all the combinations of Attribution Windows that are allowed:

| View Window | swipe window 1 Day | Swipe window 7 day | swipe window 28 day |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| None | Allowed | Allowed | Allowed |
| 1 Hour | Allowed | Allowed | Allowed |
| 3 Hour | Allowed | Allowed | Allowed |
| 6 Hour | Allowed | Allowed | Allowed |
| 1 Day | Allowed | Allowed | Allowed |
| 7 Day | Not Allowed | Allowed | Allowed |
| 28 Day | Not Allowed | Not Allowed | Allowed |

The default selection for the Attribution Window is: 28 Days swipe, 1-Day view. A wrong combination
of Attribution Windows results in an error and a Data Stream failure.
How to QA Your Data
•
Select the relevant Account from within Snapchat Business. To access the relevant section from which
reports are created, open the Navigation at the top of the page and select Manage Ads
•
You can QA the data from within the Snapchat platform or export the daily data
•
Create a Report/Pivot Table in Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines containing the exact same
Dimensions, Measurements, Filtering, and Date Range, and then compare the numbers.
•
Calculated metrics such as Cost Per Swipe Up aren't retrieved by the API, but you can create them in
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines via the Calculated Measurements feature. Opposed to
how it's displayed in the Snapchat Ads platform, the Conversion Measurements in Intelligence Data
Pipelines are transposed.
For example, in the Snapchat Ads platform, there's a Measurement for each conversion type, Lead,
Purchase, View Content, and so on.
In Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines, the type of the conversion is returned as a value of a
Conversion Tag Category Dimension, while the metric values of all conversions are available in the
Total Conversions metric in order to align with the Data Model and other platforms.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Uploading Data
Socialbakers API Connector
The Socialbakers platform is an AI-powered social media marketing company offering social media
analysis on Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, YouTube, LinkedIn, Google+, VK, and Pinterest. With this new
integration, Socialbakers users can retrieve their owned accounts data across different social media
platforms.
•
Channel: Social
•
Data Stream Type: Social Object
•
Data Lagging: 1 day
Prerequisites
To connect to the Socialbakers API, you need a valid Token and Secret Key. The token and secret key are
linked to a specific user of a Socialbakers Suite account. Contact support@socialbakers.com to get yours.
General Setup
After entering your credentials, select the relevant accounts and social profiles you want to retrieve data
for. Leave the social profiles selection empty to retrieve all profiles.
Extended Properties
•
Get Insights Metrics - Select this checkbox to retrieve insights metrics in addition to the data retrieved
by the API by default. Note that these metrics are available only for your Instagram and Facebook
pages.
How to QA Your Data
•
Create the desired report in the Socialbakers platform for the required date range.
•
Next, process the relevant Data Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines for the same
date range.
•
Create a Report/Pivot Table within Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines containing the exact
same Dimensions and Measurements for the same report and date range.
•
Compare the Measurement Totals and make sure they're aligned.
Social Studio API Connector
Salesforce Social Studio is a Management Center and Social Listening Platform for owned social accounts
on popular social networks, such as Facebook, LinkedIn, Twitter, Instagram, Pinterest, and Google+. It's
also a platform for Social Listening.
•
Channel: Social, Social Listening
•
Data Stream Type: Social Objects, Social Listening

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Uploading Data
•
Data Lagging: 0 Days
Prerequisites
To connect to Salesforce Social Studio, you need a valid username and password.
Details
•
The connector shows LIFETIME type Measurements. LIFETIME Measurements display the
accumulated value of the Measurement as of the current date.
•
When a Salesforce Social Studio Data Stream is created, all posts from the Creation Date of your Social
Accounts are retrieved. Topic Profiles allows reports to be exported up to 90 days back, while Managed
Accounts pulls all available posts - including those that were created further back than 90 days.
•
Entries in the Data Stream Log appear under the processing date of the extraction date ('Today').
•
When processing the Data Stream, there's no date selection, as all data for all posts is retrieved until
today and assigned a date value that is identical to the day of processing.
•
Measurement data retrieved for all posts is accurate as of the date when processing takes place.
•
In order to prevent potential data inaccuracies, Data Streams in LIFETIME Aggregation don't allow for
any failed retrievals to be reprocessed post-failure. For example, if a Data Stream retrieval failed on
January 1, 2019 at 8 AM, a minute later the data will be updated and refreshed, and the data available
for retrieval on the platform will no longer reflect what was attempted to be retrieved a minute prior.
Reprocessing the failed retrieval on January 2 would mean that the data didn't exist in the platform on
January 1 and therefore would be attributed to January 1. This would be a misrepresentation and
could lead to serious inaccuracies.
•
Because of the nature of LIFETIME Measurements, after data is deleted from a Salesforce Social Studio
Data Stream, the deleted data is not accessible for retrieval through reprocessing.
•
In order to reprocess data retrieved in the past without actually deleting any data - for example, to
accommodate changes in mapping - the Re-Run Files option can be used, which is located under the
'Log' button in the Data Streams Management menu.
General Setup
After a Workspace is selected, you can choose to retrieve a single or several Accounts and Topics. Leave
the field empty to retrieve all Accounts and Topics. Social listening data contains a high amount of data
by nature, therefore retrieving many Topics could cause the Data Stream to timeout. If this happens, try
reducing the number of Topics per Data Stream.
Advanced Settings
This section allows you to define advanced settings for your API connector. The settings are the same for
all API connectors.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Uploading Data
Known Issues
Due to Twitter's legal limitations that block the transfer of Twitter posts to third party applications, certain
Twitter Dimensions aren't retrieved by Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines. Nevertheless,
Measurements associated with these posts will be retrieved.
How to QA Your Data
•
Create the desired report in Social Studio.
•
Under Format, select CSV.
•
In the Schedule section click to expand.
•
Select Once and then click Save Changes.
•
Click the down arrow to the right of View Results and then click Run Now.
•
Go back to your reports list and click the report you just created.
•
Click Download.
•
In Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines, compare the Measurement Totals and make sure
they're aligned.
Salesforce Social Studio Topic Profiles allows reports to be exported up to 90 days back, while Marketing
Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines pulls all available posts - including those that were created further
back than 90 days. So, when you compare Measurement Totals between Salesforce Topic Profiles and
Intelligence Data Pipelines, either compare specific posts, or make sure you're looking at the same date
range.
Use Scheduled Reports to Integrate Social Studio with Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data
Pipelines
Salesforce Social Studio is a Management Center and Social Listening Platform for owned social accounts
on popular social networks, such as Facebook, LinkedIn, Twitter, Instagram, Pinterest.
This article goes over the recommended approach when using scheduled reports to push Social Studio
data into Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines using TotalConnect.
Social Studio API Versus Scheduled Reports
Social Studio API
•
Data ingested via the dedicated Social Studio API returns mentions over the past 30 days as a lifetime
measurement.
•
You would need dedicated Topic Profiles for Social Studio/Intelligence analysis, which would result in
double consumption of Social Studio EMV.
•
Keyed off Social Post ID, which means a 1:1 ratio of mentions vs. row consumption Intelligence Data
Pipelines (expensive).
Scheduled Reports from Social Studio

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Uploading Data
•
Greater control on data entering Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines - Can be scheduled from
a Keyword Group level, rather than the entire Topic profile.
•
Historical data can be consumed on the day the mentioned happened allowing for trending analysis.
•
Custom key aggregating post count at the day and custom dimension level - significant reduction in
row consumption.
•
Scheduled reports allow you to pull in the data at a Keyword Group level (KWG), which allows for
Share of Voice or Category trending that the API approach doesn't allow. The data on this dashboard
for scheduled reports is represented by the green segment, whereas the data returned from an API
connection returns all KWGs.
Social Studio Report Data Points
Let's go over the setup on Social Studio's end first.
To provide the maximum amount of control for data pushed into Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data
Pipelines, we'd recommend using Social Studio reports. Using scheduled reports gives you greater
control over the data ingested into Intelligence Data Pipelines, which then impacts the volume of rows
consumed.
Data Selection from Social Studio
Selecting 'CSV' as the export format of your report allows you to customize the fields that you want set as
part of your report.
Here's a breakdown of the suggested Social Studio Report Data Points and what they represent:

| Social Studio Report Data Points | What this data point represents |
| --- | --- |
| Language | The language the post has been classified |
| Media Provider of Post | Media origin |
| Post Type | Type of engagement, limited to tweets (retweets, quotes, replies - and so on) |
| Published Date | The date the post/content was published, different to harvest date that represents when Social Studio ingested the post into our database. |
| Region | Country of origin |
| Sentiment | Sentiment as classified in Social Studio |
| Workflow - Author Tag | Tags applied via social automate or manually inside Social Studio engage or analyze. |
| Workflow - Post Tag | Tags applied via social automate or manually inside Social Studio engage or analyze. |

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Uploading Data
Within the 'filters' section we would then set up an individual report for each of
