chunks.heading: part-05
chunks.body:
---
title: "MDP Uploading Data (part 5/5)"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_intelligence_data_pipelines_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "65-328"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

sent automatically via email or FTPS/SFTP. Automatic data refresh allows you to avoid uploading your
data manually each time new data must be ingested into the data stream.
Using automatic data refresh you configure your schedule frequency and other settings related to your
data refresh automation in TotalConnect and API data streams. You can define automatic data refresh in
two places.
Note Certain settings can differ between TotalConnect and API data streams.
You can click the Automate Data Refresh button at the top-right corner of the Data Stream window,
which appears throughout the entire data ingestion process (1). Or when defining or editing advanced
settings, click the Edit icon (2).
MDP Import Data via Technical Vendor
Importing data via technical vendors schedules the data stream to update automatically via a remote
server. This method tells the platform to import files from remote servers according to the delivery
method of your choice. The settings are dynamic and based on the technical vendor you select.
Typically, you use this method when you want to retrieve data from an external source and have it
update automatically. Available for TotalConnect data streams.
MDP Send Data via Email to the Data Stream
This method allows you to send files via email to the data stream. Every data stream has its own
unique email address and can accept attachments or files downloaded through links.
MDP Upload Files Using FTPS or SFTP

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Uploading Data
This method allows you to upload files into a TotalConnect data stream by using an FTPS or SFTP
connection. Available for TotalConnect Data Streams
MDP Import Data via Technical Vendor
Importing data via technical vendors schedules the data stream to update automatically via a remote
server. This method tells the platform to import files from remote servers according to the delivery
method of your choice. The settings are dynamic and based on the technical vendor you select. Typically,
you use this method when you want to retrieve data from an external source and have it update
automatically. Available for TotalConnect data streams.
For example, if you have monthly reports in a Dropbox folder, you can use this type of connection to pull
the reports from the source monthly.
11.. Navigate to the Import tab.
22.. Expand Data Streams List.
33.. Select the data stream that you want to modify.
44.. At the top right-hand corner of the page, click Automate Data Refresh.
55.. To configure the automatic pull from a technical vendor, click Import Data (1) and then click Select
Technical Vendor (2). The settings can vary per technical vendor, but some of them overlap. This
example shows the settings when configuring a connection to Dropbox.
••
Schedule Frequency - Select the frequency with which the data stream pulls files from the external
source. You can choose once, hourly, daily, weekly, bi-weekly, monthly, quarterly, and yearly.
••
Credentials - Select your credentials to authenticate with the platform where your files are stored.
If a set of credentials hasn't been added to MDP previously, to create a new authentication, click
Add New.
••
Directory - Enter the directory where your files are located. To evaluate any MDP functions that
were used as part of the directory or file name, use the Evaluate button.
••
File Name Wildcard - Set a wildcard to instruct the data stream which file to pick from the directory
or folder. For example, use the asterisk for a part of a file name that could vary. For example,
*.report.xls covers both May.report.xls and June.report.xls. To evaluate any MDP functions that were
used as part of the file name, click Evaluate next to the text box.
•• - If you want to pull a specific file, for example, an Excel file, you can use the File name wildcard
option and enter *.xlsx. If you want to include certain parts of the names of the files you want to
retrieve when uploading the data, you can also use the File name wildcard match option. For
example, let's say you have 100 files in your directory and you want to retrieve only those files that
contain the word "Campaign". Because only 20 files contain the word, we expect only 20 files to
be retrieved.
•• - In this case, you can use the wildcard option using this syntax: *Campaign*. Because this is a file
name, not a file extension, you must use the correct syntax.
•• - Add two asterisks around the word that you want used in the filtering. You can also retrieve files
from cloud storage daily in a directory named with today's date by adding to the directory path
FORMATDATE(TODAY(), 'yyyy-MM-dd') .

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Uploading Data
•• - Functions like TODAY() and NOW() always return the date in UTC timezone (not the
timezone of the workspace) when they're used in this feature.
••
Delete after download - Enable this checkbox to delete the files from your vendor or file storage
after they're downloaded by the platform.
••
Retrieve only new files - Enable this checkbox to only retrieve files that were modified after the
most recent retrieval.
••
Merge files - Enable this checkbox to merge multiple files into a single file before processing. All
files must have identical headers.
MDP Send Data via Email to the Data Stream
This method allows you to send files via email to the data stream. Every data stream has its own unique
email address and can accept attachments or files downloaded through links.
You typically use this method when a data stream is updated regularly. You can set up the automatic data
stream update when creating a new data stream or within an existing data stream.
Available for TotalConnect data streams.
11.. Navigate to the Import tab.
22.. Expand Data Streams List.
33.. Select the data stream that you want to modify.
44.. At the top right-hand corner of the page, click Automate Data Refresh.
55.. Click Copy Email Details.
66.. To forward a file to a TotalConnect data stream via email, add it as an attachment to the email, or
include a direct download link in the email's body. If you're using a link, make sure that it isn't login-
protected and that it allows for non-restricted downloads of the file you're uploading.
MDP Upload Files Using FTPS or SFTP
This method allows you to upload files into a TotalConnect data stream by using an FTPS or SFTP
connection. Available for TotalConnect Data Streams
11.. Navigate to the Import tab.
22.. Expand Data Streams List.
33.. Select the data stream that you want to modify.
44.. At the top right-hand corner of the page, click Automate Data Refresh.
aa.. Click Copy FTPS/SFTP Details.
bb.. Decide if you're using FTPS or SFTP, and then add the unique details to the designated location in
your external FTPS/FTPS service to send files to this data stream. Your data is refreshed every time a
file is sent.
If you're using the same FTPS/SFTP client to send multiple files to multiple different data streams in
the same workspace, it's advisable to add a "$" and the data stream's unique ID after the FTPS/SFTP

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Uploading Data
user. For example, tgornenska@salesforce.com$123456. These additions ensure that your files are
sent to the correct data stream in your workspace, because sometimes FTPS/SFTP clients cache
your FTPS/SFTP username and password.
MDP Configure Data Stream Refresh and Frequency
If you're creating or editing an API connector data stream, you can define the frequency and schedule for
refreshing the data stream.
11.. Navigate to the Import tab.
22.. Expand Data Streams List.
33.. Select the data stream that you want to modify.
44.. At the top right-hand corner of the page, click Automate Data Refresh.
aa.. To determine when the data stream refreshes, select the Frequency from the dropdown.
bb.. To determine the time the data stream refreshes, select the Hour from the dropdown.
cc.. To define the number of days that you want to allow for span days, select the Statistic Span Days
from the dropdown. For example, you select 10 days and your sliding window is defined for 30 days.
When you run the report, you see three different files, each showing 10 days.
dd.. To define the number of days back that you want to retrieve data whenever the data stream is
processed, starting from yesterday, select the Sliding Window from the dropdown.
MDP Define Data Stream Attributes
You can define or modify the data stream attributes, including custom attributes and channels.
11.. Navigate to the Import tab.
22.. Expand Data Streams List.
33.. Create a data stream or edit one.
44.. At the bottom of the Data Stream page, click Advanced.
55.. Next to Data Stream Attributes, click Edit.
66.. To classify the data by attributes, enter custom attributes.
77.. Select the channel all the data coming through this data stream is derived from. You can also add a
channel, or edit an existing one.
MDP Define How Updates Affect Existing Data
Define how new data updates the existing data in the data stream by changing the load mode.
11.. Navigate to the Import tab.
22.. Expand Data Streams List.
33.. Create a data stream or edit one.
44.. At the bottom of the Data Stream page, click Advanced.
55.. Select the load mode.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Uploading Data
••
Replace - Replaces and overwrites all existing data with new data based on overlapping dates.
Replace is the default and the most commonly used option.
••
Upsert - Inserts the new data by updating main entity keys that exist in previous files. New main
entity keys are then inserted into the data as new rows of data.
••
Incremental - Adds the new data alongside the old data.
••
Total Replace - Deletes all existing data every time data is ingested.
MDP Data Load Rules
You can use Data Load rules to filter out unwanted data before you upload it to Marketing Cloud
Intelligence Data Pipelines. Create a load rule to set the criteria for the data that you want to upload.
Then apply that rule to a data stream, and then apply a rule to filter out data before it's loaded. For
example, you want to upload data from your latest campaign, but due to an input error, the campaign
dates are incorrect in your spreadsheet. Rather than create another spreadsheet, you can create a rule
that tells MDP to ignore this campaign's data.
MDP Create a Load Rule
Data load rules filter out unwanted data before you upload data to Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data
Pipelines. After you create load rules, they can be applied to data streams.
MDP Add Load Rules to a Data Stream
Add load rules to a data steam to filter out unwanted data.
MDP Create a Load Rule
Data load rules filter out unwanted data before you upload data to Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data
Pipelines. After you create load rules, they can be applied to data streams.
11.. Navigate to the Import tab.
22.. Expand Data Streams List.
33.. Expand Data Load Rules.
44.. Click Add New.
55.. In the General section, add this information:
••
Name - Name your load rule.
••
Match Action - Select the matching action to use. These match types are available:
•• - Include Rows - Only data that matches the dimension values defined in the filter is uploaded.
•• - Delete Measurements - Measurements are removed from the data according to the filter.
•• - Fail the Entire Load - Any new raw file that meets the criteria doesn't upload.
••
Description - Enter a description to describe what the load rule does.
••
Date Range - Enter a data range so that only data that meets the date range is added to the data
stream.
••


…

chunks.heading: 05-mdp-harmonize-your-data
chunks.body:
---
title: "MDP Harmonize Your Data"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_intelligence_data_pipelines_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "329-344"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

MDP Harmonize Your Data
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To prioritize a task: Admin
11.. On the Import tab, from the Data Stream List window, click the Processing Queue icon.
22.. Click the reorder icon, drag the task to the top of the queue for the highest priority, or lower down on
the queue based on the task's priority, and then click Save.
33.. To view all the data streams being processed and their progress in your task, click the expand icon on a
task tile.
44.. To remove a specific data stream from a task, expand the task tile, hover over the stream, and click the
remove icon.
55.. To cancel a task, click the settings icon on the task tile and then click Cancel Task.
66.. Completed tasks remain in the queue for a while and then disappear so that you can see the current
tasks in progress.
F A Q - Data Stream Prioritization Queue in MDP
Here are some questions that may come up when setting priority for your processing tasks.
Q: Why is my prioritized task still processing while another task has already been completed?
A: A prioritized task at the top of the queue can take longer to process than a task lower down in the
queue. If a higher priority task doesn't require the same resource as another task, both tasks are
processed, and the lower priority task can run faster than a high priority task. The Priority Queue doesn't
determine the order in which tasks are completed. The task's priority only determines what happens
when two tasks require the same resource.
Q: Is it possible to precede manually triggered data streams over scheduled tasks?
A: No. Scheduled processing for data streams is prioritized over manual processing to ensure that they
don't interfere with each other.
Q: Can I change the priorities of data streams within a task?
A: No. You can prioritize tasks as a whole, but you can't prioritize specific data streams within a task.
MDP Harmonize Your Data
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines offers multiple tools for harmonizing your data. Because you
can upload data from various sources, you need a way to merge the data and provide a complete view of
your marketing activity. In most cases, no single data source holds all relevant data on a marketing event
or campaign. The data is shared by the multiple platforms that digital marketers use to do things like
promoting the campaign on multiple channels. To analyze the full picture, harmonize data from all

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Harmonize Your Data
sources into one. If data isn't harmonized, you must analyze each source individually-making it a
challenge to gain insights quickly and efficiently.
Note Creating a new help portal takes time. We're in the process of creating all the necessary topics
for MDP. New content will become available over the next several months.
The MDP data model is marketing oriented and facilitates natural harmonization of marketing data from
multiple sources. Another option is to use data harmonization tools, like the harmonization center,
calculated dimensions and measurements, and Data Fusion.
MDP Harmonize Data with the Harmonization Center
The Harmonization Center in the Harmonize tab, unifies, enriches, and validates your data in one
place. In the Harmonization Center, create and manage your naming convention patterns,
classifications, and harmonized dimensions.
Parent-Child Connections in MDP
A parent-child connection describes a case where two or more data streams are linked by a matching
entity key. This connection establishes hierarchical dependencies between entities, allowing for
grouping at various granular levels such as campaign, media buy, or creative. Matching entity keys
between the data streams are required to establish a parent-child relationship.
MDP Fusing Data Between Datasets
Data fusion creates a link between dimensions that don't already have a data model relationship
between them.
MDP Harmonize Data with the Harmonization Center
The Harmonization Center in the Harmonize tab, unifies, enriches, and validates your data in one place.
In the Harmonization Center, create and manage your naming convention patterns, classifications, and
harmonized dimensions.
The main page contains an overview of your harmonized data.
The KPI status bar shows source dimensions, harmonized dimensions, and classification rules (1). Click
Fix & Maintain to monitor and remedy issues that occur with data governance directly within MDP (2).
View all your patterns (3), harmonized dimensions (4), and data classification rules and perform actions
directly from the Harmonization Center.
MDP Naming Convention Patterns
In the distraction-filled world of a digital marketer, clarity is important. Therefore, when using different
marketing platforms, digital marketers often name an activity with all the information in the name.
Proper naming allows them to differentiate between the different campaigns, and understand
campaign goals and details at a glance without having to look elsewhere.
MDP Data Classification
Classify your data without using a single line of code. For example, classify your sites by news, sports,

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Harmonize Your Data
or entertainment, and so on. Upload a classification file and create a classification rule.
MDP Validate and Maintain Your Harmonized Data in the Harmonization Center
Keeping track of your naming convention patterns, classifications, and harmonized dimensions is
overwhelming and time-consuming. Fix & Maintain offers a solution for identifying data that doesn't
adhere to your predefined naming conventions or data classifications. Validate and maintain
harmonized data with Fix & Maintain and if issues arise, fix, and adjust as needed.
MDP Naming Convention Patterns
In the distraction-filled world of a digital marketer, clarity is important. Therefore, when using different
marketing platforms, digital marketers often name an activity with all the information in the name.
Proper naming allows them to differentiate between the different campaigns, and understand campaign
goals and details at a glance without having to look elsewhere.
Digital marketers usually abide by a certain structure or naming convention for the campaign name.
Creating a naming convention pattern deconstructs your dimension, which is in a naming convention
form, and creates harmonized dimensions for each segment of your naming convention. A naming
convention usually consists of the main details and contains a delimiter.
For example, in this naming convention each color represents an attribute and an underscore is used as
the delimiter. The naming convention can be divided into four attributes: Car Name, Car Category,
Promotion Name, and Site.
MDP Create a Naming Convention Pattern
Create a naming convention pattern to deconstruct your dimension, which is in a naming convention
form, and create harmonized dimensions for each segment of your naming convention. A harmonized
dimension acts like any regular calculated dimension and can be used in the platform, for example in
reports.
MDP Manage and Monitor Harmonized Dimensions
After harmonized dimensions are created, they appear in a list so you can easily manage them. Add
classifications and validations, see previews, find and replace in bulk, and more.
MDP Multi-Delimiter Patterns and Structure Compliance
A multi-delimiter pattern contains different types of delimiters in a pattern, such as an underscore,
colon, and comma. Structure compliance determines how and when extractions take place. With a
single delimiter pattern, an extraction occurs only for dimension values that adhere to the pattern's
exact structure. For example, dimensions that have the same number of positions as the dimension
that you're currently creating this pattern for. When working with multi-delimiter patterns, the logic is
different.
MDP Create a Naming Convention Pattern
Create a naming convention pattern to deconstruct your dimension, which is in a naming convention

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Harmonize Your Data
form, and create harmonized dimensions for each segment of your naming convention. A harmonized
dimension acts like any regular calculated dimension and can be used in the platform, for example in
reports.
11.. From the Harmonize tab, select Harmonization Center, and then Patterns.
22.. Click Add New.
33.. Define the general details:
aa.. Enter a descriptive name so you can easily identify the pattern later.
bb.. Select the Source Dimension that holds the pattern you want to deconstruct.
cc.. To filter by a certain data source or data stream, click Add Filter.
dd.. Select List to include current data streams.
ee.. Select Contains to include future data streams.
ff.. Select the structure that best suits your naming convention, or select Manual Setup to manually
create a naming structure from scratch. Then, enter the number of positions and delimiters.
gg.. Click Apply.
44.. Construct your pattern.
aa.. MDP breaks down the original values of the source dimension according to the naming structure
(1).
bb.. Navigate to each position and type in the name of the new harmonized dimension or select one
from the dropdown (2).
cc.. The Extract toggle is turned on by default. A harmonized dimension is created for each position
after the pattern is created. If you don't need harmonized dimensions, for example for a serial
number, then turn off the toggle (3).
dd.. To change the delimiter, enter the required delimiter (4).
ee.. Click Add Position and continue selecting the dimensions (5). For example, if your naming
convention contains an additional position.
ff.. Structure Compliant is on by default. Extractions occur for dimension values that adhere to this
pattern's exact structure. For example, dimensions that have the same number of positions as the
dimension that you're creating this pattern for (6).
55.. Click Done.
New harmonized dimensions are created according to how you defined the extraction when creating
harmonized dimensions. For example, if we look at this naming convention:
Product_TargetAudience_Offer_PlacementType_CreativeType.
When breaking it down, you get 5 positions, where each position produces a harmonized dimension.
In this image, the naming convention pattern produced 5 different harmonized dimensions: Product,
Target Audience, Offer, Placement Type, and Creative Type.
MDP Manage and Monitor Harmonized Dimensions
After harmonized dimensions are created, they appear in a list so you can easily manage them. Add
classifications and validations, see previews, find and replace in bulk, and more.
11.. From the Harmonize tab, select Harmonization Center and then Harmonized Dimensions.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Harmonize Your Data
22.. View all of your harmonized dimensions.
33.. To review where the harmonized dimension is used and replace it with another dimension, click Find/
Replace.
44.. To view more details about a harmonized dimension, double-click it.
aa.. To add another layer of validation, such as a list of products, click Add Validation (1).
bb.. To classify your harmonized dimension by categories, click Add Classification. You're redirected to
the Data Classification page, and the classification rule is filtered according to your selected
harmonized dimension (2).
cc.. Data Preview shows what's going to be extracted. In this example, Product 1, Product 2, Product 3,
and Product 4 are extracted (3).
dd.. For a full view of your data, click Export when you want to remove a dimension and then validate
your file. Fo

chunks.heading: 06-mdp-dimensions
chunks.body:
---
title: "MDP Dimensions"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_intelligence_data_pipelines_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "345-355"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

MDP Dimensions
that case, you can use this solution for the same effect.
MDP Create a Data Fusion
Create a data fusion to link two datasets with a shared dimension together.
Note You can't select overarching entities, calculated dimensions, and Web Analytics Page Path
dimensions for a data fusion.
11.. On the Harmonize tab, click Data Fusion.
22.. Click Add New.
aa.. Give the data fusion a unique name (1) and add a description (2).
bb.. On the left side, the Dimension dropdown (3) lists all the dimensions that are mapped in your
workspace. Select the first dimension that you want to create the data fusion for. For example,
select Media Buy Key.
cc.. After you select a dimension, the Dimension dropdown on the right (4) lists all the dimensions that
can be fused with the first dimension. Select the dimension to fuse. For example, select DF Media
Buy Key. The Case-Sensitive option is selected by default (5). MDP doesn't perform data fusions on
dimensional values that don't follow the same capitalization rules.
33.. Save your work.
MDP Applying Filters to Fused Dimensions
When applying a filter to fused dimensions, the filtering impacts the datasets associated with all the
fused dimensions.
For example, when filtering on Unit Name "d" (from the generic data stream), values of the Media Buy
Name dimension from the Ads data stream are also filtered accordingly.
MDP Dimensions
In Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines, a dimension is a qualitative non-numerical attribute that
provides information about your dataset, for example, campaign name and campaign ID. A dimension
can't be counted, even when it includes a number. For instance, a campaign ID consists of numbers but
you can't calculate them.
Note Creating a new help portal takes time. We're in the process of creating all the necessary topics
for MDP. New content will become available over the next several months.
The dimension list shows which dimensions the data model uses and are available in your workspace.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Dimensions
The available dimension types are: Text, Date, Link, Image, Number, Video.
From the dimension list, you can:
•
Export the dimension list.
•
Edit dimension display names.
Note If a calculated dimension is inherited from another workspace, you can edit it only in the
original workspace.
•
Replace a dimension across your workspace.
•
View the data stream associated with the dimensions.
•
View which dimensions are mapped and unmapped.
MDP Calculated Dimensions
A calculated dimension is a non-numerical field or dimension that you can create on top of your
existing data. Calculated dimensions don't exist physically in the database. Rather, each calculated
dimension represents a query that can be referenced to from any report or pivot table.
MDP Calculated Dimension Functions
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines provides basic functions you can use to create dynamic
calculated dimensions
MDP Calculated Dimensions
A calculated dimension is a non-numerical field or dimension that you can create on top of your existing
data. Calculated dimensions don't exist physically in the database. Rather, each calculated dimension
represents a query that can be referenced to from any report or pivot table.
You can create and manage calculated dimensions from the Calculated Dimension tab within the
Calculated Fields menu under the Harmonize tab.
You can use a calculated dimension to:
•
Remove unwanted characters or spaces. You can also use it to shorten a name, such as a long website
name, without affecting the data.
•
Remove unwanted spaces from dimension values. If you have a campaign name with too many spaces
in between the words, you can remove the additional spacing, leaving a single space between words.
MDP Create a Calculated Dimension from the Calculated Dimension Tab
Create calculated dimensions on top of existing data and refer to them across your workspace.
MDP Related Measurements in Calculated Dimensions
A related measurement is linked to a calculated dimension and used to populate the dimension
values in a list. You can add a related measurement when creating a calculated dimension, in the
Advanced section.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Dimensions
MDP Create a Calculated Dimension from the Calculated Dimension Tab
Create calculated dimensions on top of existing data and refer to them across your workspace.
11.. On the Harmonize tab, expand Calculated Fields, and then expand Calculated Dimensions.
22.. Click Add New.
33.. In the General section, name the dimension, add a description, and select the type.
The type affects the format in the widget settings. In most cases, select Text.
44.. In the Advanced section, you can link a measurement to the calculated dimension's formula to
populate lists with the dimension's values. For example, you have a calculated dimension that returns
an email name with the related measurement of clicks. When the calculated dimension is used in a
pivot table, the returned email name values are only emails that had at least one click within the date
range that the pivot table is filtered on. If no related measurement is used, all email names are
returned, including those without clicks.
55.. In the Expression section, enter the expression that you want to base the calculated dimension on.
For example, if your customers' email names consist of multiple concatenated values, you can create a
calculated dimension to extract the values and create fields using the formula
EXTRACT([Email_Name],'_',1) .
aa.. Create an expression using the list of available dimensions and functions. To add a function to your
expression, click it or start typing for auto-complete.
bb.. Apply any of the options that appear at the bottom of the screen.
••
Full Screen-View the expression section in full-screen mode.
••
Auto Format-Format your code into the standard correct structure.
••
Comment-Enter a comment within your expression.
••
Uncomment-Remove your comment from the expression.
••
Code Validation-Validate that your expression is accurate.
••
Test-Test your expression.
66.. Click Save
MDP Related Measurements in Calculated Dimensions
A related measurement is linked to a calculated dimension and used to populate the dimension values
in a list. You can add a related measurement when creating a calculated dimension, in the Advanced
section.
When a calculated dimension is added to a list, such as to populate a pivot table, the dimension values
from the relevant data stream type are pulled from the database.
For example, you have a calculated dimension that uses Campaign Name, and you want to include the
calculated dimension in an interactive filter. However, you have mutually exclusive campaigns in your Ads
and Conversion data. In this case, you can use a calculated measurement that includes Impressions and
Total Conversions as the related measurement. The calculated dimension applies to both the ads and
conversion types to produce the results you want.
Calculated dimensions can generate values from different data stream types, as long as the related

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Dimensions
measurements pull measurements from other data stream types.
To use a calculated dimension that includes an entity that exists in multiple data stream types while
including all the values of the calculated dimension, create a new calculated measurement. Then use the
new calculated measurement as the related measurement. The calculated dimension applies to both
data tables and unifies the values into one list.
Selecting a related measurement with no values doesn't affect the results of the calculated dimension.
MDP Calculated Dimension Functions
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines provides basic functions you can use to create dynamic
calculated dimensions
The functions are divided into these categories:
•
Number Functions
•
Text Functions
•
Date Functions
•
Aggregation Functions
•
Logical Functions
•
Type Conversion
•
Content Functions
These tables summarize the available functions for calculated dimensions.
Number Functions

| FUNCTION NAME | SYNTAX | DESCRIPTION |
| --- | --- | --- |
| ABS | ABS(number) | Returns the absolute value of a number. |
| CEILING | CEILING(number, significance) | Rounds the number up, away from zero, to the nearest multiple significance. |
| FLOOR | FLOOR(number, significance) | Rounds the number down toward zero, to the nearest multiple significance. |
| RAND | RAND() | Gets a random number between 0 and 1. |
| ROUND | ROUND(number) | Rounds a number to the nearest integer. |
| ROUND | ROUND(number, num_digits) | Rounds a number to a specified |

| FUNCTION NAME | SYNTAX | DESCRIPTION |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  | number of digits. |

Text Functions

| FUNCTION NAME | SYNTAX | DESCRIPTION |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CHAR | CHAR(number) | Returns a character specified by a number. |
| COALESCE | COALESCE([value1, value2,...]) | Returns the first value from the list that isn't empty. |
| CODE | CODE(text) | Returns a numeric code for the first character in a text string. |
| COUNT_CHAR | COUNT_CHAR(text, charToCount) | Counts how many times a specific character appears in a text. |
| EXTRACT | EXTRACT(text, delimiter, position) | Extracts from the given text the substring, at the given position, after splitting by the given delimiter. For example, EXTRACT('a,b,c','_',1) returns 'b'. |
| FIND | FIND(findText, withinText) | Returns the starting position of findText withinText. Returns -1 if withinText doesn't contain findText. |
| FUZZYMATCH | FUZZYMATCH(valueToMatch, array, threshold) | Searches for a string in an array of values that potentially match using duplicate-detection algorithms that calculate the similarity of two streams of data. Returns the matched string from the array if there's a match or, if no matches are found, returns an empty string. For example, FUZZYMATCH("ABC Company",{"ABC Company Inc","Apple Inc","IBM Company"},0.8) returns "ABC Company Inc". The threshold parameter must be a value between 0 and 1. A threshold of |

| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  | 1 means an exact match. |
| GET_EXCHANGE_RATE | GET_EXCHANGE_RATE(date, fromISO, toISO) | Get the exchange rate for the given currencies and date. The 'date' variable in the formula can be a mapped date field or a date function For example: • GET_EXCHANGE_RATE([Day], 'USD', 'GBP') where [Day] is a mapped date field • GET_EXCHANGE_RATE(DATE(' 2015','8','17'),'USD','GBP') where DATE('YYYY','MM','DD') is a date function. • GET_EXCHANGE_RATE(YESTE RDAY(),'USD','GBP') where YESTERDAY() is a date function. |
| INDEXOF | INDEXOF(array, findText) | Searches the array for the specified item, and returns its position. Returns -1 if the item isn't found. If the item is present more than once, the INDEXOF function returns the position of the first occurrence. For example, INDEXOF(csv['row'], 'Campaign ID') == 0 returns true if 'Campaign ID' was found in the |

| FUNCTION NAME | SYNTAX | DESCRIPTION |

|  |  | first cell. Note: The first item has position 0, the second item has position 1, and so on. |
| ISEMPTY | ISEMPTY(value) | Returns if the value is empty or not. |
| LEFT | LEFT(text, numOfChars) | Returns the requested number of characters from the left. |
| LEN | LEN(text) | Returns the number

chunks.body:
---
title: "MDP Measurements"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_intelligence_data_pipelines_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "356-379"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

MDP Measurements
MDP Measurements
In Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines, measurements are quantitative numerical data that you
can count, for example, number of clicks and emails opened. Measurements are used to set KPIs and
analyze your data.
The Measurement list, found in the Harmonize tab under the Data Model menu, shows which
measurements the data model uses and the entities the measurements are associated with.
The available measurement types are: Currency, Number, Duration, and Percentage.
The available aggregation types are: AVG, COUNT, FIRST, LAST, LIFETIME, MAX, MIN, and SUM.
From the Measurement list, you can:
•
Export the measurement list.
•
Edit the measurement display names, type, and aggregation.
•
Replace a measurement across your workspace.
•
View which data streams the measurement is mapped to.
•
View which measurements are mapped and unmapped.
Note If a calculated measurement is inherited from another workspace, you can edit it only in the
original workspace.
MDP Calculated Measurements
A calculated measurement is a numerical custom calculation that you can create on top of your
existing data. The calculated measurement doesn't physically exist in the database. Rather, it
represents a query that you can reference from any report, or pivot table.
Filtered Measurements
A filtered measurement is used to customize an existing measurement to include information relating
to a specific dimension attribute.
MDP Calculated Measurements
A calculated measurement is a numerical custom calculation that you can create on top of your existing
data. The calculated measurement doesn't physically exist in the database. Rather, it represents a query
that you can reference from any report, or pivot table.
For example, you want to view your return on ad spend (ROAS) to better analyze your campaign
performance. Instead of manually calculating the ROAS per campaign, create a calculated measurement
to extract this information from your data and view it in your campaign's report.
MDP Create a Calculated Measurement
Create a calculated measurement on top of existing data that you can refer to across your workspace.
MDP Aggregation and Total Aggregation Functions

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Measurements
An aggregation function computes numerical calculations on data, allowing the query to return
summarized information about a given column or result set. You can use an aggregation function to
calculate each row at the dimension level or the total value. In MDP, you define the aggregation
function when creating a calculated measurement.
MDP Granularity in Calculated Measurements
Granularity is the level of detail considered and then represented in an analysis or query. The greater
the granularity, the deeper the level of detail. With more detail, you can better understand the smaller
components of your data and create a fuller picture.
MDP System Calculated Measurement
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines calculates popular industry measurements that you can
use as data in reports, and pivot tables. The measurements are calculated using fixed formulas using
your workspace's data. A measurement is available only if its formula's components are mapped in an
existing data stream. For example, if you uploaded Impressions and Media Cost data, CPM appears.
This table describes the available system calculated measurements and their formulas.
MDP Calculated Measurement Functions
Use functions when creating a calculated measurement to make your functions more dynamic.
MDP Create a Calculated Measurement
Create a calculated measurement on top of existing data that you can refer to across your workspace.
11.. On the Harmonize tab, expand Calculated Fields, and then expand Calculated Measurements.
22.. Click Add New.
33.. In the General section, define the calculated measurement.
When setting the decimal places, the more places you specify, the more exact the calculation.
44.. In the Advanced section define advanced settings.
aa.. For Total Aggregation Function, select the function to calculate the total value of a query. For
example, SUM, AVG, MIN, or MAX. Values that appear in separate rows of a display are all
aggregated in the Total row. The Total Aggregation Function and the Aggregation Function-set in
the expression section- should be the same.
bb.. Set the granularity-Determine the level the calculations for the measurement takes place.
cc.. To let null values be used in the formulas,, select Treat Nulls as Zeros
dd.. To include the date in the granularity of the calculation, select Include Date in Granularity
55.. In the Expression section, define the expression:
aa.. Set the aggregation function to determine the calculation result for all the produced rows. When a
granularity is set, the underlying calculation produces multiple calculation results for multiple rows.
These rows must be aggregated into a single row of the displayed dimension.
bb.. Create the expression that you want to base the calculated measurement on using the list of
available measurements and functions. For example, if you want to calculate email open rate that
uses email sends as the denominator instead of email deliveries, use this expression:
IFERROR([Email_Unique_Opens]/[Email_Sends], null) The IFERROR function results in
the calculated measurement returning null values when the email sends equal to 0.
cc.. To add a function to your expression, click it, or start typing for auto-complete.
66.. Click Save.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines MDP Measurements
MDP Aggregation and Total Aggregation Functions
An aggregation function computes numerical calculations on data, allowing the query to return
summarized information about a given column or result set. You can use an aggregation function to
calculate each row at the dimension level or the total value. In MDP, you define the aggregation function
when creating a calculated measurement.
For example, to perform a different calculation per row, such as getting the average value for each
dimension while summing all the values in the total row, set the aggregation function to AVG and the
total aggregation function to SUM to sum the values in the different rows.
Use Case
You can use both the aggregated function and the total aggregation function in cases where you have a
calculated measurement with a formula that includes a dimension with an aggregation function set to
Auto, and the total value in the query for this calculated measurement is zero. The reason there's no total
value is because the total row doesn't include the formulas' required dimension for this calculated
measurement. To solve this issue and display a total value, you can set a total aggregation function to
display a total value based on the value of each row in the query.
MDP Granularity in Calculated Measurements
Granularity is the level of detail considered and then represented in an analysis or query. The greater the
granularity, the deeper the level of detail. With more detail, you can better understand the smaller
components of your data and create a fuller picture.
For example, you have a calculated measurement that calculates cost per click (CPC) per media buy. The
granularity is set to the media buy dimension, and the calculation is done on the values of each media
buy key. By setting the granularity of the calculated measurement to a lower level, such as the creative
level, the calculation now occurs at the creative key level when slicing the calculated measurement on
any relevant dimension.
MDP System Calculated Measurement
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines calculates popular industry measurements that you can use
as data in reports, and pivot tables. The measurements are calculated using fixed formulas using your
workspace's data. A measurement is available only if its formula's components are mapped in an existing
data stream. For example, if you uploaded Impressions and Media Cost data, CPM appears. This table
describes the available system calculated measurements and their formulas.

| System Calculated Measurement | Expression |
| --- | --- |
| company count | countdistinct('company_id') |
| conversion CPA | media cost / conversion total conversions |

| conversion rate | total conversions / impressions |
| conversion ROAS | conversion revenue / delivery media cost |
| conversion ROI | (conversion revenue - delivery media cost) / delivery media cost |
| CPC | media cost / clicks |
| CPE | media cost / total engagements |
| CPM | (media cost / impressions) * 1000 |

| web analytics position weighted | web analytics position computed / search results impressions |

Depending on when your workspace was created, you could see email measurements or messaging

| email bounces rate daily | email total bounces daily / email send daily |
| email CTR | email clicked / email delivered |

| message CTR lifetime | email clicks/email delivered |
| message open rate lifetime | email opens / e

chunks.heading: 08-manage-your-data-with-mdp
chunks.body:
---
title: "Manage Your Data with MDP"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_intelligence_data_pipelines_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "380-383"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Manage Your Data with MDP
allows you to apply the filtered measurement to a specific data stream.
Note To save your filtered measurement you must select a time period or add a filter.
66.. Save your filtered measurement.
Manage Your Data with MDP
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines offers multiple tools for managing your data. In the Manage
tab, you can create workflows and actions to manage your data, and remove data that you no longer
need.
Note Creating a new help portal takes time. We're in the process of creating all the necessary topics
for MDP. New content will become available over the next several months.
Workflows in MDP
Use workflows to execute data streams, reports, and actions in your workspace. For example, create a
workflow to generate a report after specific data streams are processed.
MDP Activation Center
Use the Activation Center to connect to third-party platforms, such as Slack, Facebook Ads, and
Google Ads, to trigger alerts, collaborate, and automate workflows in other systems. For example, you
can create an action that sends a notification in Slack every time a certain campaign hits the target
value you set for it.
Delete Data Streams
When you delete data streams in your workspace within the Delete Data tab, only the measurements
are deleted. The dimension values associated with the deleted data aren't deleted. The dimension
values can still appear in the platform, for example, in VLOOKUPs. Classification data, dimension-only
data, CRM entities, and overarching entities are also not deleted. To delete all data from a data stream,
delete the data stream from the Data Stream List.
Workflows in MDP
Use workflows to execute data streams, reports, and actions in your workspace. For example, create a
workflow to generate a report after specific data streams are processed.
Create a Workflow
Create a workflow to set the order of when to execute data streams, reports, and actions. You can also
chain workflows together so that the end of the workflow initiates the next workflow.
Execute a Workflow
You can execute a workflow manually, even if it has a defined schedule. You can run up to 10
workflows per hour.
Troubleshoot a Workflow
If a workflow fails, view the log file to see what caused the failure. A workflow can run for 6 hours. If

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines Manage Your Data with MDP
the workflow doesn't finish executing in that time, it times out.
Create a Workflow
Create a workflow to set the order of when to execute data streams, reports, and actions. You can also
chain workflows together so that the end of the workflow initiates the next workflow.
11.. On the Manage tab, select Workflows.
22.. Click Add New.
33.. Name the workflow, and select Enable.
44.. To run the data streams only after the workflow is triggered, select Convert all Data Streams to run
only in Workflows. For example, if a data stream receives a file via delivery information, the file
processes after the workflow triggers.
55.. To schedule the workflow, set the frequency and time.
66.. In the Flow area, double-click an empty space.
77.. Select the type of entity to include in the workflow: data stream, action, report, system event, or
another workflow.
88.. Select which entities to include in the workflow. An entity can be in multiple workflows.
99.. Click Add.
1100.. To create more workflows, repeat the same process.
1111.. To connect between workflows, drag the cursor from the first workflow to the one you're connecting it
to.
By default, if an entity in a workflow fails, the entire workflow doesn't run. To enable the workflow to
continue even if an entity fails, hover over the entity, click Settings, and select Continue workflow on
failure.
1122.. Click Save & Execute.
Execute a Workflow
You can execute a workflow manually, even if it has a defined schedule. You can run up to 10 workflows
per hour.
11.. On the Manage tab, select Workflows.
22.. Select the workflow to execute, and click Process.
33.. To stop the execution, click Stop Run.
44.. To restart the workflow, click Status and select Enable.
Troubleshoot a Workflow
If a workflow fails, view the log file to see what caused the failure. A workflow can run for 6 hours. If the
workflow doesn't finish executing in that time, it times out.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines Manage Your Data with MDP
11.. On the Manage tab, select Workflows.
22.. Select the failed workflow, and click Show Run Status.
A window shows the run status of each element in the workflow. The information is updated while the
workflow runs
33.. To investigate why a workflow failed to run, click Log. Each instance has its own ID.
MDP Activation Center
Use the Activation Center to connect to third-party platforms, such as Slack, Facebook Ads, and Google
Ads, to trigger alerts, collaborate, and automate workflows in other systems. For example, you can create
an action that sends a notification in Slack every time a certain campaign hits the target value you set for
it.
You can create actions and customize them using Webhooks and Python. And then you can
automatically trigger actions based on key performance indicators (KPIs) you set for your campaigns,
media buys, and creatives.
Actions and Action Rules
You can react, track, and manage your most important KPIs and benchmarks by using the activation
center data optimization tools. Access the tools from the Activation Center menu in the Analyze & Act
tab.
•
Actions - Interact with your channel or campaign. For example, send a Slack message to your
reporting channel, pause a campaign on Facebook, or send a custom email.
•
Action Rules - Define the triggers for your actions. Triggers for your stored actions are automatically
executed when the event you define occurs. You can trigger multiple actions for one action rule.
Action Types
These action types are available.
•
Email - send email to a list of contacts.
•
Webhook - trigger actions over the web in other applications.
•
IFTTT Connector - trigger an appellate in IFTTT.
•
Twilio Connector - send an SMS through Twilio.
•
Slack Connector - post to Slack.
•
Python - trigger python scripts.
•
Media Actions in Google, Twitter, and Facebook - trigger media actions such as stopping or starting
campaigns, ad sets, or ads.
Create Actions in the Activation Center
Create actions for third-party providers and channel platforms such as Facebook Ads and Slack.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines Manage Your Data with MDP
Create Action Rules in the Activation Center
Action rules define how a rule is triggered. For example, the action can be triggered by time or by a
specific event. You can create a rule for several actions.
Trigger an Action Rule from the Management Screen
Triggering an action manually allows you to run an action rule at any time. If the action rule condition
is met, the rule executes the actions specified in it. For example, if your stakeholders ask for an
updated report in the middle of the week, you can trigger the action rule and see the latest
information.
Create Actions in the Activation Center
Create actions for third-party providers and channel platforms such as Facebook Ads and Slack.
11.. On the Manage tab, on the left menu, click Activation Center and then select Actions.
22.. Click Add New.
33.. Select the connector or channel that you want to create an action for. For example, select Email.
44.. Define the parameters for the action. The setup for each connector or channel is unique:
aa.. Name your action.
bb.. Set the destination. For example, a recipient's email address.
cc.. Add your content. For example, add a message or the information that you want to share.
dd.. To add a query to your action, click Query Settings. Queries make the data dynamic and interactive.
When you add a query to your content, ensure that the measurements and dimensions you
selected are available in your data.
ee.. A preview of your action rule appears in the window on the right side.
55.. To create the action, save your changes.
66.. To define a trigger for the action, click Create Rule.
Create Action Rules in the Activation Center
Action rules define how a rule is triggered. For example, the action can be triggered by time or by a
specific event. You can create a rule for several actions.
11.. On the Manage tab, click Activation Center and then Action Rules in the left panel.
22.. Click Create New.
33.. Name your rule.
44.. From the dropdown, select the action that you want to apply the rule to. For example, an email action
named Daily Update that sends daily email updates.
55.. To add another action, click the + icon, and select the action from the dropdown.
66.. Define the trigger By Time or By Event. Defining the trigger determines what prompts the action rule:
aa.. By Time - Sets daily, weekly, or monthly triggers.
bb.. By Event - Sets a KPI for a specific measurement. For example, you want to be notified when your
Message Total Clicks drops below 100.
77.. Apply filters. To narrow your results, you can apply a date range filter. For example, if you want your
action rule to stop after 1 month, you can set the date range for 1 month.

chunks.heading: 09-explore-your-data-with-mdp
chunks.body:
---
title: "Explore Your Data with MDP"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_intelligence_data_pipelines_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "384-386"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Explore Your Data with MDP
88.. To save your rule, click Create Action Rules.
Trigger an Action Rule from the Management Screen
Triggering an action manually allows you to run an action rule at any time. If the action rule condition is
met, the rule executes the actions specified in it. For example, if your stakeholders ask for an updated
report in the middle of the week, you can trigger the action rule and see the latest information.
11.. On the Manage tab, click Activation Center and then Action Rules in the left panel. A list of all the
action rules appears.
22.. Select the row of the desired action rule. Click Run.
33.. If the action rule's condition is met, the rule executes the actions specified in it. For example, if you
want to be notified when your Message Total Clicks drops below 100, you're notified when your total
drops below 100.
Delete Data Streams
When you delete data streams in your workspace within the Delete Data tab, only the measurements are
deleted. The dimension values associated with the deleted data aren't deleted. The dimension values
can still appear in the platform, for example, in VLOOKUPs. Classification data, dimension-only data,
CRM entities, and overarching entities are also not deleted. To delete all data from a data stream, delete
the data stream from the Data Stream List.
11.. On the Manage tab, click Delete Data.
22.. To delete specific data streams, click Selected data streams and select the data streams from the
dropdown.
33.. To delete all data streams, click All data streams.
44.. Select the date range that you want to delete data from.
••
To delete data within specific dates, click Dates between and select the date range.
••
To delete all data up to a specific date, click Dates until and select the date.
55.. Click Delete Selected Data.
Note Data is stored for 90 days from the last processing date. After 90 days, you can no longer
consume, query, report, or export the data.
Explore Your Data with MDP
Explore your data and ensure it is accurate before exporting it for further analysis and consumption.
Note Creating a new help portal takes time. We're in the process of creating all the necessary topics
for MDP. New content will become available over the next several months.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines Explore Your Data with MDP
For example you can use pivot tables to ensure that your data is being loaded correctly from the source
into MDP and that the values are aggregating correctly. You can even create a report from your pivot
table to upload elsewhere. You can also use the Query API Builder to perform advanced queries on your
existing data.
Pivot Tables in Salesforce MDP
Explore your data from multiple perspectives using pivot tables. You can calculate, summarize, and
analyze data by seeing comparisons, patterns, and trends in your data. You can also create custom
reports without using query formulas, such as SQL queries.
Pivot Tables in Salesforce MDP
Explore your data from multiple perspectives using pivot tables. You can calculate, summarize, and
analyze data by seeing comparisons, patterns, and trends in your data. You can also create custom
reports without using query formulas, such as SQL queries.
A common use case for using pivot tables in Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines is to validate
your data and ensure that it's loading correctly from the source into MDP and that the values are
aggregating correctly. After you create a pivot table, you can export it locally or turn it into a scheduled
report.
MDP Create a Pivot Table from Scratch
Use a pivot table to explore and extract data.
MDP Create a Pivot Table Using a Data Stream
Create a pivot table using a data stream to base it on already-defined measurements and dimensions.
MDP Manage Pivot Tables
Edit, duplicate, and delete pivot tables in Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines.
MDP Create a Pivot Table from Scratch
Use a pivot table to explore and extract data.
11.. On the Explore tab, click Pivot Tables.
22.. Click the plus (+) icon.
33.. Add the dimensions and measurements that you want to include in the pivot table by clicking + Add a
Field.
By default, dimensions are placed in rows, and measurements are placed in values. You can change
the format by manually dragging them to where you want. To hide a dimension or measurement from
the table, hover over the field name and click the eye icon.
44.. When you're done, close the Add Data window.
55.. Select the date range that you want to view data for and how to display dates.
••
Period name-Displays a given name for the time period. For example, naming the first 4 weeks in
January "W1-W4."
••
Period first day-Shows the first date of the selected date range in the pivot table. For example, 1 Jan
2022.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines Explore Your Data with MDP
••
Period first day (calendric)-If you have a custom calendar and want to display the date range
according to it, use this option.
66.. To apply a filter, in the Filters section, click the + icon and select the filters. To quickly filter your data,
click the filter icon in the relevant column in the table, and select the filters.
77.. Name the pivot table.
88.. To visualize your data in a chart, turn on Visualize.
••
To select which dimensions and measurements to show or to change the display, click Chart
Settings
••
To change how the results are sorted, click Chart Top and Sorting.
99.. To view the pivot table as a flat table, click the flat table icon.
1100.. To save, export, or download your pivot table, click More Actions.
1111.. Save your work.
A pivot table can include up to 8,000 rows, and you're notified when you reach the limit. You can
either reduce the number of rows by updating the pivot table settings or adjusting the date range to
narrow the results or save the pivot table and download the full results.
See Also
MDP Create a Pivot Table Using a Data Stream
MDP Create a Pivot Table Using a Data Stream
Create a pivot table using a data stream to base it on already-defined measurements and dimensions.
11.. On the Import tab, click Data Streams Lists in the left panel.
22.. Select a data stream.
33.. Click Pivot Table A pivot table filtered on this data stream is created using its measurements, along
with the Day field.
44.. Customize the pivot table if needed.
55.. Save your work.
MDP Manage Pivot Tables
Edit, duplicate, and delete pivot tables in Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines.
11.. On the Explore tab, click Pivot Tables in the left panel.
22.. To change the pivot table configuration, click Edit.
33.. To create a copy of the pivot table, which you can modify, click Duplicate.
44.. To delete a pivot table, click Delete.
Note When you delete a pivot table, it goes to your workspace bin. If you want to restore a pivot

chunks.heading: 10-export-your-data-with-mdp
chunks.body:
---
title: "Export Your Data with MDP"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_intelligence_data_pipelines_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "387-399"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Export Your Data with MDP
table, contact support.
55.. To copy the pivot table to another workspace, click Copy to Workspace.
Export Your Data with MDP
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines offers multiple tools for exporting your data to consume it in
your tool of choice. You can export your data into a database, report, or Tableau.
Note Creating a new help portal takes time. We're in the process of creating all the necessary topics
for MDP. New content will become available over the next several months.
Use Database Exports to Export Datasets
Export data from Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines, and connect it to an enterprise
database such as Snowflake and PostgreSQL. With Database Exports, automate the export of MDP
marketing data. Enhance enterprise-wide analytics by making marketing insights accessible, and get a
full view of your company's data.
Reports in MDP
Use reports to retrieve, view, and share a detailed analysis of the data that you've integrated into
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines. You can save them locally or share them via email or the
cloud. The data uploaded into MDP is displayed as a flat file and includes selected dimensions and
measurements within a defined time range.
Connect MDP with Tableau
Connect Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines with Tableau to easily query your data. With the
MDP Tableau integration, you get harmonization capabilities with MDP's ETL solution and Tableau's
advanced visualization tools.
Use Database Exports to Export Datasets
Export data from Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines, and connect it to an enterprise database
such as Snowflake and PostgreSQL. With Database Exports, automate the export of MDP marketing data.
Enhance enterprise-wide analytics by making marketing insights accessible, and get a full view of your
company's data.
Create a Database Export
Create a database export to export marketing data from Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines,
and import it to an external database. Select the criteria that you want to export, define scheduling to
determine when the export occurs, and map the source data fields to the database fields.
Define the Data to Export
Define the source dataset by selecting the dataset, required dimensions, and measurements and date
range.
Configure the Destination Database and Connect Data

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines Export Your Data with MDP
Set the external database where you're exporting the data to. Databases are configured in Workspace
Integrations. Configure the database first, and then you can select it.
Configure the Database Export Feed
After you connect to the database and map your fields, configure how the exported data is inserted
into your database table and how often to execute the export. This step defines what happens when
new data is loaded into the database and how to handle it.
Troubleshoot Database Exports
After a database export is created, view and manage exports execution in the History tab. If an export
fails, troubleshoot to find out what happened. You can download a report, and even rerun or
reprocess it.
Adding or Editing Database Integrations
Configure an external database to import data from Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines. You
configure the database before creating a database export. The user whose credentials are used when
connecting to the database must have INSERT and DELETE permissions for at least one table in the
database.
Create a Database Export
Create a database export to export marketing data from Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines,
and import it to an external database. Select the criteria that you want to export, define scheduling to
determine when the export occurs, and map the source data fields to the database fields.
Before creating a database export, configure the database you're connecting to. This is done in the
Integrations tab. For more information see Adding or Editing Database Integrations.
11.. From the Export tab, select Database Exports.
22.. Click Create New.
33.. Define the data that you want to export.
44.. Map the source data fields to the destination database fields.
55.. Configure the database export feed.
66.. Save your work, and run the export.
Define the Data to Export
Define the source dataset by selecting the dataset, required dimensions, and measurements and date
range.
11.. From the Export tab, select Database Exports.
22.. Create a database export, or edit one.
33.. Enter the name of the data that you're exporting.
44.. Enter a description of the data.
55.. Set the date range for the data that you're exporting. For example, set the range for the last 90 days.
66.. Choose one of these options:
••
Use data date: Take all data from the defined date range. For example, all data from the last 90

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines Export Your Data with MDP
days.
••
Modified date: Take only data that changed during the date range. If you only want the changes,
use this option.
77.. Select the dimensions and measurements that you're exporting.
88.. Set a filter. For example, you want to only export data from a specific campaign and not all the
campaigns.
99.. To move onto the next step, click Next.
Configure the Destination Database and Connect Data
Set the external database where you're exporting the data to. Databases are configured in Workspace
Integrations. Configure the database first, and then you can select it.
For more information, see Adding or Editing Database Integrations.
After you connect to the database, map the fields from your source data to the database fields to ensure
that the data is connected properly.
11.. From the Export tab, select Database Exports.
22.. Create a database export, or edit one.
33.. At the bottom of the page, select Map to Destination.
44.. In the Destination Database area, select the database that you're exporting to.
55.. After you connect to a database, all tables in the database that the user has INSERT and DELETE
permissions for are available for selection.
66.. Select the table that you're connecting to. After you select a table, all available fields appear in the
Mapping area farther down the page. The fields change according to the table that you select.
77.. In the Mapping area, map fields from the dataset to the required field in the database. For example,
map the measurement Website Clicks to Clicks.
88.. Repeat this step for all dimensions and measurements that you want to map.
99.. To move onto the next step, click Next.
Configure the Database Export Feed
After you connect to the database and map your fields, configure how the exported data is inserted into
your database table and how often to execute the export. This step defines what happens when new
data is loaded into the database and how to handle it.
11.. From the Export tab, select Database Exports.
22.. Create a database export, or edit one.
33.. At the bottom of the page, select Configure Feed.
44.. In the Load Mode section, select how you want to load new data into the database.
••
Incremental: All new data is incremented into the database and added to the existing data.
••
Total Replace: Every time new data is updated, it replaces all the data in the database table.
55.. In the Trigger section, define when to execute the export. For example, set a daily frequency, and then
set a time. For example, you can have the data update every day at 8:00 AM.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines Export Your Data with MDP
66.. If you want to apply advanced settings on the export, in the Advanced section, set the export type.
••
Entire dataset: Exports the entire dataset as one report.
••
Partitioning: Exports the dataset into separate reports. If you select this option, set the division by
day, week, month, and so on.
••
To replace empty values with a set value, select Replace empty values, and then enter a value. For
example, replace all empty values with the word NULL. All empty values are replaced, no matter if
they're dimensions or measurements.
77.. To delete the report history after a specified time, set the Purge Report History Days and then enter a
value. For example, if you enter 30, the report's history is deleted after 30 days.
88.. To create the database export, click Create.
99.. To create the database export and run the export, click Create & Run.
Troubleshoot Database Exports
After a database export is created, view and manage exports execution in the History tab. If an export
fails, troubleshoot to find out what happened. You can download a report, and even rerun or reprocess
it.
11.. From the Export tab, select Database Exports.
22.. Select the export that you want to troubleshoot.
33.. On the toolbar, click History.
44.. If an export fails, select the row of the job, click More Actions, and then select Show Detailed Log to
understand why the export failed.
55.. If the dataset was created successfully but failed to load to the database table, click Rerun. This action
takes the dataset and loads it into the database again. For example, rerun a report if the credentials
failed during loading.
66.. To reprocess the entire job according to the same date range, click Re-Process Job.
77.. To download the data to a local file, click Download.
Adding or Editing Database Integrations
Configure an external database to import data from Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines. You
configure the database before creating a database export. The user whose credentials are used when
connecting to the database must have INSERT and DELETE permissions for at least one table in the
database.
11.. From your workspace settings, on the left window, click Workspace Integration.
22.. Select the Database tab.
33.. All database integrations appear here. To create an integration, click Add Integration.
44.. Select the vendor.
55.. Enter a name.
66.. Configure the connection details, including host, port, database, warehouse, role, and account.
77.. To use a connection string instead, click Or, and then enter the string used for the connection.
88.. Define the authentication details, including user, password, and if necessary, private key.
99.. Before you create the integration, check it to make sure that it works and is valid. At the top right-hand

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines Export Your Data with MDP
side of the page, click Validate Connection.
1100.. If there are any errors, a red information icon appears that you can click to view more details. If the
integration is valid, click Create.
Reports in MDP
Use reports to retrieve, view, and share a detailed analysis of the data that you've integrated into
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Pipelines. You can save them locally or share them via email or the
cloud. The data uploaded into MDP is displayed as a flat file and includes selected dimensions and
measurements within a defined time range.
You can run reports as part of a workflow in real time, or according to a defined schedule. You can create
data reports which display data in an Excel, CSV, TSV, or TXT file.
MDP Create a Data Report
A data report is based on the data that exists in your workspace. You can save it as an Excel, CSV, TSV,
or TXT file.
MDP Configure Report Scheduling and Delivery
You can choose when and how to run and share a report. You can set up multiple schedules for a
report. For example, you can send the report to the management team on a weekly basis and to the
analyst team daily.
MDP Manage Reports
After you create a report, it appears in your reports list. From your reports list

…

|  | Marketing Cloud Intelligence |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

With Marketing Cloud Intelligence you connect, harmonize, visualize, and act on your marketing data to
optimize performance within campaigns, discover insights in real-time, and then act on them. Learn
more about each in the associated help sections.
If you're new to the Salesforce Help site and want to see how to navigate and find information, click here
to view a short instructional video.
Note Did you know there's a new way to stay up to date on Marketing Cloud Intelligence features?
Check out Updates Released by Month-your monthly guide to the latest enhancements, new
connectors, and feature updates, all in one place.
Tip Did you know there's a new and improved Marketing Intelligence? Built natively on the
Salesforce Platform, Marketing Intelligence harnesses the power of Data 360 and Agentforce to
boost campaign performance and reduce wasted spend. Check out the Marketing Intelligence
Quick Look.

chunks.body:
---
title: "Get Started with Marketing Cloud Intelligence"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_intelligence_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "12-83"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Get Started with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
options.

| Administration/Settings/Marketplace |
| --- |
| Analyze & Act |
| Connect & Mix |
| Visualize |

ee.. Enter your Workspace ID. To locate the ID, in Marketing Cloud Intelligence click your workspace
name on the top left side of the screen. Your ID appears under your workspace name. If not
applicable, you can enter 1234.
ff.. Add the URL for the page you're experiencing difficulty with. Enter N/A if not applicable.
gg.. Before creating the ticket, take a look at the potential solutions that are automatically generated for
you.
hh.. Select the Timezone where you're located or where you prefer being contacted.
ii.. Select the Severity of your case.
jj.. If you want to add other people to the case, enter the email addresses of the collaborators.
kk.. Upload files if necessary.
99.. Click Create Case.
After you create a case, you can track its progress and collaborate with support within the case.
Get Started with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
With Marketing Cloud Intelligence, you can integrate all your data from marketing and advertising
platforms, web analytics, CRM, e-commerce, and more. You can then use this unified view of your data
to optimize campaign performance and discover and act on insights in real time.
To get started with Marketing Cloud Intelligence, you set up an account and create workspaces to
integrate your data.
Marketing Cloud Intelligence offers additional functionality at an extra cost, such as Granular Data
Center, Sandbox, and Media Transparency. You can view the available features on the Add-on Features
tab in the Marketplace.
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Updates Released by Month
Learn about recent updates to Marketing Cloud Intelligence (MCI), including changes to data
coverage, new metrics, and feature enhancements.
Get Ready for Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Before we start, explore the Marketing Cloud Intelligence platform. Then check that you are using one
of Salesforce's support browsers. Finally, familiarize yourself with the user roles in Marketing Cloud
Intelligence.
Account Settings in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
A Marketing Cloud Intelligence account is divided into workspaces. An account can contain multiple
workspaces. Account settings allow you to manage and view data, user actions, and analytics for your

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Get Started with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
account. In the account menu you can find account settings, such as account name and logo, number
of allowed workspaces, and rows, and all the way to more advanced settings, such as currency, and
password permissions.
Workspace Settings in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Each Marketing Cloud Intelligence account can have multiple workspaces. Each workspace has its own
data streams, dashboards, and reports. You typically use workspaces to separate clients, business
units, channel views, internal views, or role-based views-whatever best fits your needs.
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Updates Released by Month
Learn about recent updates to Marketing Cloud Intelligence (MCI), including changes to data coverage,
new metrics, and feature enhancements.

| MArch '26 |  |  |

Non-link interactions are now classified as Engaged-Through conversions rather than Click-Through. This change may result in a decrease in reported click-through conversion rates. This reflects a change in reporting methodology, not a drop in performance. • MCI now supports the engaged-through attribution setting. This setting isn't mapped by default but is included in total conversion metrics. To report on it separately, map the engaged-through attribution setting in your configuration. |
