Get Ready for Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Before we start, explore the Marketing Cloud Intelligence platform. Then check that you are using one of
Salesforce's support browsers. Finally, familiarize yourself with the user roles in Marketing Cloud
Intelligence.
Location of all Help Topics
Beginning May 1, 2022, you can access Marketing Cloud Intelligence help documentation and training
materials in Salesforce Help. The Datorama Success Center is no longer available.
Navigate the Marketing Cloud Intelligence Platform
The Marketing Cloud Intelligence platform is divided into four tabs. Here you can explore the different
tabs.
Set Up Your First Workspace in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
After your Marketing Cloud Intelligence account is provisioned, you can now start setting up your
workspace. A Marketing Cloud Intelligence account is divided into workspaces. Each workspace is a
separate working environment with its own permissions. When you make changes, the changes are
applied only to the workspace that you're working in.
Supported Browsers
You can access Marketing Cloud Intelligence from a browser on a Windows, macOS, or Linux
computer.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Get Started with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
User Roles and Permissions
Marketing Cloud Intelligence includes three types of user roles: viewer, power user, and admin. A user
role defines which actions a user can perform. Each user role has a default set of permissions, but you
can assign users with the same role different permissions. For example, one user with the viewer role
can view unpublished pages but another user with the viewer role can't.
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Videos
Use this video resource to learn more about Intelligence features.
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Glossary
Familiarize yourself with Marketing Cloud Intelligence and other marketing terminology.
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Security
Marketing Cloud Intelligence uses a variety of industry-standard and state-of-the-art security
technologies and procedures to help protect your information from unauthorized access, use, or
disclosure. All users must have a registered MFA method in order to access the Marketing Cloud
Intelligence platform directly. You can decide on which methods best suit your needs. Session timeout
is another form of security and determines the amount of inactive time after which a user is logged
out of the platform. The session timeout period can be set in the account settings. For security
purposes there is a forced timeout after five days regardless of inactivity. Keeping up

chunks.heading: 04-data-model-for-marketing-cloud-intelligence
chunks.body:
---
title: "Data Model for Marketing Cloud Intelligence"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_intelligence_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "84-121"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Data Model for Marketing Cloud Intelligence

| REQUIRED USER ROLE |  |
| --- | --- |
| To manage sandbox dependencies | Admin |

11.. Go to Sandbox Mode.
22.. Create a calculated measurement.
33.. Click Deploy.
If there is a dependency, a message informs you of the dependency and that the second measurement
will be added to your deployment automatically. You can either remove the measurements from the
deployment action or cancel the entire deployment.
View Deployment History in the Marketing Cloud Intelligence Sandbox
All your deployment activity is logged and can be viewed in the Sandbox Deployment History dialog.

| REQUIRED USER ROLE |  |
| --- | --- |
| To view deployment history | Admin or Power User |

11.. Go to Sandbox Mode.
22.. Click the clock icon to access the Sandbox Deployment History.
Note If no entities were deployed, this icon is disabled
33.. To get more information about an item, click More Details.
Data Model for Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Marketing Cloud Intelligence pulls data from a variety of sources with different data types. Each one of
these different data types has its own unique combination of dimensions and measurements. The
dimensions are made up of entities that have a certain relationship, or hierarchy, between them. It's
important to maintain this relationship in Marketing Cloud Intelligence's database, otherwise the data
can get aggregated incorrectly.
For this reason, for each of the different data types, Marketing Cloud Intelligence has emulated the
unique relationship, which exists between these entities. This means that the corresponding Marketing
Cloud Intelligence entities within its database follow the same relationship. Modeling the data by these
relationships for the various data types is what's collectively known as the Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Data Model.
The legend used by the diagrams that follow:

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Model for Marketing Cloud Intelligence
There are two possible relationship types between entities - one-to-many and many-to-many:
One to Many - For example, the connection between Site and Media Buy. One Site can be linked to
many Media Buys, however, one Media Buy can be linked to one Site only.
Many to Many - For example, the connection between Media Buy and Creative. One Media Buy can be
linked to many Creatives and vice versa, and one Creative can be linked to many Media Buys.
The Main Entity in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
The Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Model considers one of the entities from each data type to be the
main entity. The main entity is the entity around which everything else revolves or is associated with
within a given data type. In the data stream type diagrams below, all main entities appear in a circle. For
example in the Ads data stream type, Media Buy is the main entity.
The different main entities are:
Overarching Entities in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Overarching entities exist in all data stream types and allow you to link different data streams by these
entities. This enables slicing different data types by one (or more) overarching entity. For example,
delivery and social data can both be sliced by the overarching entity 'Product' (as seen in the following
image):
In the overarching entities in the following diagram, the entities on the-right hand side are in fact
'Attributes' of the main entity. They are in a one-to-many relationship with the main entity, so they can
each only hold one value for each main entity value. For example, in the 'Ads Data Stream Type',
'Product' is an attribute of 'Media Buy' meaning that there can only be one 'Product' value for each
'Media Buy' value. The entities on the left-hand side are in a many-to-many relationship with the main
entity and as such, they can each hold multiple values for each main entity value. So for example, in the
'Ads Data Stream Type', there can be multiple 'Device Category' values for 'Media Buy' value.
Note that Channel isn't a mappable entity.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Model for Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Data Stream Custom Attributes in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Data Stream Custom Attributes behave like Attributes to the Data Stream Entity. Data Stream Custom
Attributes are updated with a slight delay, so they might not appear in your workspace data immediately
after being added.
Cross-Connectivity Between Data Stream Types in Marketing Cloud
Intelligence
A relationship between entities doesn't have to be via a direct link but can also be through a 'third party'
Entity. For example, Campaign and Site aren't linked directly one to another. However, since each one of
them is linked to Media Buy in a one-to-many relationship, the relationship between Campaign and Site
is many-to-many indirectly.
Data Stream Types in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
The following diagrams show which entities are included in each data stream type. Not all entities must
be mapped in each data stream, however it's advised that you always map the main entity. The main
entity appears in a circle so you can distinguish it from the other entities. Entities that aren't mapped by
the user are given a default value by the Marketing Cloud Intelligence platform. For example, in the
following widget (representing one data stream) the 'Media Buy Entity' is mapped, but the 'Campaign
Entity' isn't, hence the 'Campaign Entity' gets a default value.
Note If the existing Data Stream types don't match your data use case, you can use the Generic
Data Stream Type.
In the Data Models below, the largest circle highlights the Main Entity.
The main entity is determined per data stream type. For example, 'Media Buy' is the main entity in the
'Ads Data Stream Type', but it isn't the main entity in the 'Conversions Data Stream Type'. In the data
models that follow, the largest circle highlights the main entity. You can see that some data stream types
include entities from other data stream types. For example, in the data model for the 'Conversions Data
Stream Type', you can see entities from the 'Ads Data Stream Type'.
The types of data streams are:
•
Ads
•
Ads Verification Blocking
•
Buy Data
•
Buy Data Conversions
•
CRM Leads

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Model for Marketing Cloud Intelligence
•
Competitive
•
Conversion Tag
•
Conversion Tag with Keywords
•
Ecommerce
•
Messaging
•
Products
•
Search Keywords
•
Social Element Traffic Source
•
Social Listening
•
Social Objects
•
Web Analytics
•
Web Analytics Events
•
Web Analytics Pages
•
Web Analytics Tags
•
Generic Data Stream Type
Ads
In the world of digital marketing, the ads data stream type is where you can find the data for your display
ads campaigns. This data stream type contains the delivery data that comes from these campaigns. For
example, how many times an ad has been seen, clicked or how much money it cost. The ads data stream
type can be used with providers, such as Facebook Ads, Google Ads, Twitter Ads, Bing Ads and more.
Ads Verification Blocking
The ads verification blocking data stream type tracks data where ads were blocked by ad verification
platforms, and therefore weren't served. This usually pertains to ads that were blocked due to a violation
of the platform's terms and conditions and either disrupted user experience or violated brand safety. You
can use this data stream type with providers, such as IAS, and Double Verify.
Buy Data
The 'buy data' data stream type is intended for ingesting data relating to planned advertising activity,
sometimes referred to as 'planned data' or a 'media plan'. Used in Media Transparency Center
implementations, it facilitates the bulk creation of Insertion Orders (or IOs for short), which themselves
represent the scope and details of the said activity, with information such as the IO start and end dates,
IO cost type, IO rate and IO budget. It's also used to create an association between IOs and their related
delivery data, thereby unifying planned with actuals. This data stream type can be used to ingest media
plans from systems, such as Salesforce, Mediaocean, Nexelus, and Netsuite.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Model for Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Buy Data Conversions
Buy data (ingested using the 'buy data' data stream type) can contain IOs that are associated with
conversion tags. However, not all conversion tags associated with a certain delivery item under an IO, are
necessarily relevant for that IO's cost calculations. The buy data conversions data stream type allows you
to associate between existing IOs and the conversion tags of relevance. In a sense, this data stream type
can be thought of as a 'conversion tag filter' for IOs, and is only used as an add-on to an existing use of
the 'buy data' data stream type.
CRM Leads
The CRM leads data stream type is intended for ingesting data relating to the potential prospects of a
business, commonly known as 'Leads'. Designed to keep a historical record of each lead's evolution over
time, this data stream type is equipped with slowly changing type 2 dimensions, such as lead stage, lead
status and lead modified date. The CRM leads data stream type can be used to ingest data from lead
generation platforms, such as Marketing Cloud Account Engagement (Pardot), Hubspot Marketing and
Intercom, as well as from CRM platforms, such as Salesforce, Sugar CRM, and CRM Creatio.
Competitive
The competitive data stream type, which is used side by side with the social objects data stream type,
allows you to compare yourself to your competitor by displaying various social metrics side by side from
the two data stream types. The purpose of this data stream type is to allow you to view your competitors'
data separately from your own data. This data stream type is suitable for any social media platforms,
such as Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, and YouTube.
Conversion Tag
The conversion tag data stream type is intended for ingesting data related to the various 'calls to action'
tied to your campaigns, also known as conversions. You can see what particular conversion tag prompted
your audience to complete a call to action, and whether this happened after they viewed your ad or after
they clicked it. This data stream type also includes data related to the monetary aspects of your
conversions for example, how much a conversion cost or how much revenue it brought in. This data
stream type can be used with providers, such as Facebook Ads, Google Ads, Google Display&Video 360,
Twitter Ads, and more.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data Model for Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Conversion Tag with Keywords
Whenever an ad is displayed in a search engine result and drives a conversion, the search keywords that
led to this conversion are attributed to it. In these cases, the conversion tag with keywords data stream
type is used to ingest the conversion data along with the respective search keywords related to it. The
conversion tag with keywords data stream type is suitable for providers, such as Google Ads, Search Ads
360, and Bing Ads.
ecommerce
In the world of ecommerce, the ecommerce data stream type is where you can find different levels of
data for product catalogs, placed orders, number of purchased items within each order, and more. This
data stream type is intended for ingesting different data types, in their various forms, from ecommerce
platforms. The ecommerce data stream type can be used with providers, such as Salesforce OMS,
Amazon Seller Central, and Amazon Vendor Central.
For details on the ecommerce data stream type, see: Ecommerce Data Stream Type
Messag

…

chunks.heading: part-01
chunks.body:
---
title: "Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence (part 1/5)"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_intelligence_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "122-439"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Messaging Measurements
This table lists all the measurements in the messaging data stream type alongside their aggregation
method.
This data stream type has the exact same measurements in both Sum Aggregation and Lifetime
Aggregation. For example, message soft bounces from the source data can be mapped to message soft
bounces or message soft bounces (Lifetime) in Marketing Cloud Intelligence. You can decide which
aggregation method to use based on the relationship between the date breakdown in the source data.
The messaging data stream type also contains Rate Measurements in percentage terms.
Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
One of the most crucial steps when working with Marketing Cloud Intelligence, is uploading data. Since
Marketing Cloud Intelligence supports numerous types of data, you can choose from many different
ways to upload it. During the upload stage, you can map your data to determine which fields appear in
Marketing Cloud Intelligence, and you can even create custom calculated fields to get the most out of
your data. All these actions are done in the Connect & Mix tab.
Create Data Streams in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Whenever you upload data, data streams are created. These data streams can be referenced
throughout the Marketing Cloud Intelligence platform, for example in pivot tables, reports, and
dashboards. There are several methods available for creating data streams in Marketing Cloud
Intelligence. You can use an API connector to connect via API, TotalConnect to upload external files,
LiteConnect if you have data from a single source, or even Granular Data Centers if you have granular
data. You can choose which method is best for you.
Mapping Your Source Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Mapping connects the source fields in your uploaded file to the corresponding Marketing Cloud
Intelligence fields. Intelligence uses machine learning algorithms to map your data. The mapping
process is similar regardless of which method you use to upload your data, with some minor
differences. If you're using an API connector to upload your data, Marketing Cloud Intelligence
automatically maps the fields for you, but you can make changes to the mapping. If you're using
TotalConnect, Intelligence identifies the most suitable data stream type according to your data and
offers an initial mapping suggestion.
Advanced Settings in Data Streams in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Define advanced settings to your data stream to select load modes, load rules, or configure additional
settings. The available settings vary based on the type of data stream that you're creating.
Data Stream Control Center
The Data Stream Control Center is a dashboard that centralizes data stream management into a single

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
view. You can assess stream status, evaluate data quality, and identify ingestion issues. Get actionable
insights for improved data hygiene and row management. The Data Stream Control Center is
automatically filtered to show data for Today. To view the Data Stream Control Center dashboard, in
the Connect & Mix tab, expand the Control Center tab.
Data Stream List
View, manage, and create data streams from the Data Stream List, in the Connect & Mix tab.
Data Stream Templates in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Use data stream templates to create a mapping template that you can replicate to create data
streams. Store the template on the workspace or account level. You can share data stream templates
between workspaces and accounts. The data stream template saves formulas used in the original data
stream.
Data Mapping Visualizer
Use the Data Mapping Visualizer tool to view an animated display of the mapped data in your
Intelligence workspace. Data Mapping Visualizer lets you easily view and search your mapped data.
Workflows in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Use workflows to execute data streams, reports, and actions in your workspace. For example, create a
workflow to generate a report after specific data streams are processed.
Transformation Rules
Use transformation rules to set values to a specified attribute, based on a set of defined criteria. The
rules can be set at the level of the entity key or name. Transformation rules are most effective when
the entity key or name has a consistent naming convention.
Transformation Groups
Transformation groups allow you to classify attributes of entities, such as sites, campaigns, or
products, so you can filter information.
Create Data Streams in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Whenever you upload data, data streams are created. These data streams can be referenced throughout
the Marketing Cloud Intelligence platform, for example in pivot tables, reports, and dashboards. There
are several methods available for creating data streams in Marketing Cloud Intelligence. You can use an
API connector to connect via API, TotalConnect to upload external files, LiteConnect if you have data
from a single source, or even Granular Data Centers if you have granular data. You can choose which
method is best for you.
Creating Data Streams with TotalConnect in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
If you need to upload data from a source and no API connector exists, you can create a data stream
with TotalConnect. For example, if you have a proprietary dataset in Excel, CSV, or another file format,
or if a custom report is sent to you by a data partner that is beyond the scope of what their API can
provide.
Creating Data Streams with API Connectors in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
API connectors enable you to easily connect data sources such as Google Ads or Facebook to
Marketing Cloud Intelligence. When you connect an API connector, an API data stream is created that
automatically maps the data fields the vendor provides to the Marketing Cloud Intelligence data

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
model, allowing you to access your information.
Creating Data Streams with LiteConnect in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
With LiteConnect, you can upload a file into Marketing Cloud Intelligence as is and visualize the data
automatically. You don't need to consider the predefined structure and constraints of the data model
or map fields.
Create Data Streams with DirectConnect
DirectConnect is a premium feature that allows you to retrieve data directly from an external database,
without having to store the data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence's database. DirectConnect is used in
cases where you have data sensitivity issues, or you want to display and interact with your data from an
external source instead.
Pull Data 360 Data into Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Data 360 unifies your data from multiple sources so that you can build a comprehensive view of your
customer. Pull in a small amount of data with TotalConnect or try the Granular Data Center connector
for larger amounts. You can also install the Data Cloud app to get predefined insights.
Creating Data Streams with TotalConnect in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
If you need to upload data from a source and no API connector exists, you can create a data stream with
TotalConnect. For example, if you have a proprietary dataset in Excel, CSV, or another file format, or if a
custom report is sent to you by a data partner that is beyond the scope of what their API can provide.
With TotalConnect, you can either upload a flat file or use a technical vendor. You can define the retrieval
method, for example, if you want to pull your data via an HTTPS server. You can also decide what to do
when new data comes in, for example, replace the existing data with the new data or filter out data
before it's loaded.
Create a Data Stream with TotalConnect in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
You can upload data manually, automatically using FTP, or an email address, or by scheduling an
update with a technical vendor.
Preview Source Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
After you upload your data with TotalConnect, a preview of your source data lets you see how
Marketing Cloud Intelligence reads the raw data, including fields and their types. The table reflects all
the fields (columns) from your source file. Each field is defined with a type-text, number, or date-and
you change the type if needed. You can also change the date format.
Transformers in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Transformers are a set of configurations applied to files that are uploaded into Marketing Cloud
Intelligence. Transformers are used to alter the structure of the data files in order to pull the exact data
sets you need. They instruct the platform as to what data table must be extracted from your source file
allowing you to manipulate the file structure.
Mapping Your Source Data in TotalConnect
Mapping connects the source fields in your uploaded file to the corresponding Marketing Cloud
Intelligence fields. Marketing Cloud Intelligence uses machine learning algorithms to map your data.
The mapping process is similar regardless of which method you use to upload your data, with some
minor differences. If you're using TotalConnect, Intelligence identifies the most suitable data stream

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
type according to your data and offers an initial mapping suggestion.
Troubleshoot Total Connect Data Streams
Errors can occur that prevent data streams from being created or processed. You can view errors using
the Log button. The log contains important technical information and can help you diagnose issues.
Create a Data Stream with TotalConnect in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
You can upload data manually, automatically using FTP, or an email address, or by scheduling an update
with a technical vendor.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To upload data: Admin
When uploading data, be aware of the data limits:
•
Direct upload-40 MB / 15 million rows (subsequent)
• Email-30 МB / 15 million rows (subsequent)
•
Import-500 GB / 15 million rows (subsequent)
•
FTP-500 GB / 15 million rows (subsequent)
•
The timeout for a file retrieval from the provider is 55 minutes
•
The timeout for the file processing is 120 minutes
11.. From the Connect & Mix tab, select Data Streams, and then Data Streams List.

33.. Click TotalConnect.
44.. To upload a file, drag it to the center of the page.
55.. To use a technical vendor, select the technical vendor from the list.
66.. To use a template, click Use Template.
After the data uploads, a preview screen appears. For information about viewing the source data and
how to change it, see Preview Source Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Preview Source Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
After you upload your data with TotalConnect, a preview of your source data lets you see how Marketing
Cloud Intelligence reads the raw data, including fields and their types. The table reflects all the fields
(columns) from your source file. Each field is defined with a type-text, number, or date-and you change
the type if needed. You can also change the date format.
Field Types
Each field is defined with a different type (text, number, or date), and you can change the type if

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
necessary. Click the column name and select the type.
Date Type Fields
When it comes to date type fields, recognizing date formats is of utmost importance regarding data
correctness and aggregation. In order to ingest a particular row of data, Marke

…

chunks.heading: part-02

---
title: "Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence (part 2/5)"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_intelligence_4-7-2026.pdf"

purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"

This connector always retrieves datasets for both Amazon Campaign Types-Sponsored Products, and

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Headline Search Ads regardless of what is configured in the Extended Properties of the Data Stream.
•
Get Conversions - Select this checkbox to retrieve Conversion data in addition to the Delivery data
retrieved by the API by default
QA Your Data
•
Create the desired report in the Amazon DSP platform for the required date range.
•
Next, process the relevant Data Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence for the same date range.
•
Create a Report/Table Widget/Pivot Table within Marketing Cloud Intelligence containing the exact
same Dimensions and Measurements for the same report and date range.
•
Compare the Measurement Totals and make sure they're aligned.
Amazon Seller Central API Connector in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Amazon Seller Central is a web interface used by brands and merchants to market and sell their products
directly to Amazon customers.
With the Amazon Seller Central API connector, you connect your advertising and ecommerce data to
bring in sales data, such as inventory, pricing, orders, and performance analysis. This connector provides
both order and item-level data using the SKU of the products. The data stream type is ecommerce, and
data lagging is 1 day.
Important Due to Amazon's new authentication process, you must re-authenticate all Amazon
accounts once a year to ensure your data is up-to-date. For more information about this workflow,
see Amazon Authentication Workflow.
More details about the Amazon Seller Central API connector
•
The Amazon Seller Central API connector brings in the ASIN product number as shown in Amazon
Seller Central and maps it as 'Product Key' automatically.
•
You can connect more than one marketplace to the connector, however you must create a separate
data stream per each marketplace.
•
Amazon sometimes requires your approval to share data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
Create an Amazon Seller Central Data Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Connect your advertising and ecommerce data to bring in sales data.
Create an Amazon Seller Central Data Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Connect your advertising and ecommerce data to bring in sales data.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create an API data stream: Admin
To authenticate with Marketing Cloud Intelligence: Admin in Amazon Seller Central
11.. On the Connect & Mix tab, expand Data Streams.
22.. Click Add New.
33.. Select the Amazon Seller Central connector.
44.. In the General section, add an authentication.
55.. In the Authentication Properties window, from the Market Place dropdown, select the location where
you sell your products.
You can have one marketplace per data stream. If you're using more than one marketplace in your
account, create another data stream for this marketplace. You can create as many data streams as
needed.
66.. Click Next.
77.. Enter your Amazon Seller Central credentials. Amazon sometimes requests your approval to share
some of your data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
88.. To bring in revenue on product level data, under Extended Properties, select Get Product Level Data.
This means that data is brought in at the product level every day, removing the granularity of order
level data. If this checkbox isn't selected, order level data is pulled instead.
99.. To change the mapping, at the bottom of the Create New Stream page, select Map to Mode.
1100.. To apply advanced settings, at the bottom of the Create New Stream page, select Advanced Settings.
1111.. Click Create.
Quality check your data:
•
Create a report in Amazon Seller Central for the date range and then process the relevant data stream
in Marketing Cloud Intelligence for the same date range. Go to Reports and then select Fulfillment by
Amazon (FBA). Expand FBA Inventory, then FBA Business Reports, Sales Reports, and then select All
Order Report. For more information, see Amazon's Help Site.
•
Process the relevant data stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence for the same date range.
•
Create a report, table widget, or pivot table in Marketing Cloud Intelligence containing the same
dimensions and measurements for the same report and date range.
•
Compare the measurement totals and make sure that they align.
Amazon Vendor Central Inventory API Connector in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Amazon Vendor Central Inventory is a web interface used by first-party sellers, such as direct
manufacturers and distributors, for selling products directly to Amazon.
By connecting to the Amazon Vendor Central Inventory API, you get information about sales and traffic,
consumer behavior, operations, and more. You can view this data using the Amazon Standard
Identification Number (ASIN) of the products. The data stream type is ecommerce, and data lagging is 1
day.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Important Due to Amazon's new authentication process, you must re-authenticate all Amazon
accounts once a year to ensure your data is up-to-date. For more information about this workflow,
see Amazon Authentication Workflow.
Note Because of an Amazon limitation, you can fetch only the past 7 days of your data. After
connecting your data to Marketing Cloud Intelligence, the API brings in data according to the set
schedule (the default is daily), and it accumulates in Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
More details about the Amazon Vendor Central Inventory API connector:
•
The Amazon Vendor Central Inventory API connector brings in the ASIN product number as shown in
Amazon Vendor Central and maps it as 'Product Key' automatically.
•
You can connect more than one marketplace to the connector, however you must create a separate
data stream per each marketplace.
•
Amazon sometimes requires your approval to share data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
•
Amazon Vendor Central Inventory allows you to query 7 days back of data. After you connect your data
to Marketing Cloud Intelligence the API continues bringing in data according to the set cadence,
which is automatically set to daily.
•
Currently you can't retrieve direct-fulfillment data with this connector; however a new connector is
being developed to handle this use case. For more information, contact Marketing Cloud Intelligence
support.
Create an Amazon Vendor Central Inventory Data Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Connect your Amazon Vendor Central Inventory data to Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
Create an Amazon Vendor Central Inventory Data Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Connect your Amazon Vendor Central Inventory data to Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create an API data stream: Admin
To authenticate with Marketing Cloud Intelligence: Admin in Amazon Vendor Central Inventory
11.. On the Connect & Mix tab expand Data Streams.
22.. Click Add New.
33.. Select the Amazon Vendor Central Inventory connector.
44.. In the General section, add an authentication. Make sure you're logged into Amazon Vendor Central
Inventory before creating a new connection to avoid a longer authentication process.
55.. In the Authentication Properties window, from the Market place Location dropdown, select the
location of your store or marketplace.
You can have one marketplace per data stream. If you're using more than one marketplace in your
account, create another data stream for this marketplace. You can create as many data streams as

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
needed.
66.. Click Next.
77.. Enter your Amazon Vendor Central credentials. Amazon sometimes requests your approval to share
some of your data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
88.. To change the mapping, at the bottom of the Create New Stream page, select Map to Mode.
99.. To apply advanced settings, at the bottom of the Create New Stream page, select Advanced Settings.
1100.. Click Create.
Quality check your data.
•
Create a report in the Amazon Vendor Central platform for the date range and then process the
relevant data stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence for the same date range.
•
Create a report, table widget, or pivot table in Marketing Cloud Intelligence containing the same
dimensions and measurements for the same report and date range.
•
Compare the measurement totals and make sure they align.
Account Analytics API Connector in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
With the Marketing Cloud Intelligence Account Analytics connector, you can monitor engagement, page
views, audit changes, and entity creation logs. You can use this data to create customized views, add
dynamic dashboards, and pull actionable reports. The connector includes logs for both unpublished and
published pages.
To create an API data stream for Marketing Cloud Intelligence Account Analytics, you need access to the
workspace that you're requesting data for.
The Marketing Cloud Intelligence Account Analytics data stream type is Lite Connect, and data lagging is
1 day.
Users are account level entities. When selecting the user entity from the extended properties, you see
data for all users in the account and not the specific workspace.
Create an Account Analytics Data Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
To pull user activity and measure user engagement, create a Marketing Cloud Intelligence Account
Analytics data stream.
Create an Account Analytics Data Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
To pull user activity and measure user engagement, create a Marketing Cloud Intelligence Account
Analytics data stream.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create an API data stream: Admin

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
To create an API data stream for Marketing Cloud Intelligence Account Analytics, you need access to the
workspace that you're requesting data for.
11.. On the Connect and Mix tab, expand Data Streams.
22.. Click Add New.
33.. Select Intelligence [Datorama] Account Analytics.
44.. In the General section, select the account and workspaces from the dropdown.
55.. Set the time period for the initial data fetch. You can process data up to the past 180 days.
66.. In Extended Properties, select the entities that you want to audit from the dropdown.
77.. To change the mapping, click Mapping. You can also access mapping by selecting Map to Model at
the bottom of the Create New Stream page.
88.. To define advanced settings for the data stream, at the bottom of the Create New Stream page, click
Advanced Settings.
99.. Click Create.
1100.. Quality check your data. Compare the data pulled from the connector to the data in Marketing Cloud
Intelligence Account Analytics.
AOL One Video API Connector
The ONE by AOL API connector retrieves data related to Video Ads that are displayed on mobile devices.
In using our API you'll able to retrieve standard Ads and Conversions data, such as Campaigns, Media
Buys, Impressions, Clicks, and Video Measurements.
Additional Measurements and Dimensions, including Viewability Measurements, are also retrieved and
are available unmapped. Their relevance depends on your data configuration.
•
Channel: Display
•
Data Lag: 1 Day
Prerequisites
•
Valid Client ID & Secret - Contact your ONE by AOL representative to receive these credentials. Ask for
a Production Client ID and Secret. The Aut

chunks.heading: part-03
chunks.body:
---
title: "Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence (part 3/5)"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_intelligence_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "122-439"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Compare the Measurement Totals and make sure they are aligned.
Google Display and Video 360 TrueView API Connector
TrueView line items allow you to create video campaigns with a range of formats to engage customers on
YouTube and across the web on partner sites and apps.
•
Channel: Display
•
Data Stream Type: Ads
•
Data Lag: 1 day
Prerequisites
In order to connect to Display & Video 360 TrueView, you need a valid DV360 TrueView username and
password with at least 'Read Only' permissions or higher.
Details
•
Marketing Cloud Intelligence pulls data from the 'TrueView Report' in DV360 TrueView.
General Setup
•
After entering your credentials, select the relevant Partner you want to retrieve data for.
•
To retrieve all Advertisers, leave the Advertiser box empty.
•
If you want to retrieve data for a specific Partner or Advertiser then select the relevant Partner or
Advertiser from the respective list. If either list doesn't populate automatically, or you can't find the
Partner or Advertiser that you want to retrieve data for, enter the Partner and/or Advertiser IDs into
the Override Partner Id and/or Override Advertiser Ids section under Extended Properties (see
Extended Properties).

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Extended Properties
•
Override Partner Id - Enter the Partner Ids you want to pull data for. Use this section if the Partner list
didn't populate automatically, or you can't find the Partner that you want to retrieve data for under the
General Settings section. (see General Setup).
•
Override Advertisers Ids - Enter the Advertiser Ids you want to pull data for. Use this section if the
Advertiser list didn't populate automatically, or you can't find the Advertiser that you want to retrieve
data for under the General Settings section. (see General Setup). Insert multiple Advertisers IDs by
separating them with a comma.
•
Additional Breakdown - Select one of the available options to add further breakdowns to the data
retrieved by the API, resulting in a higher level of granularity on which the Measurements can be
sliced. You can only select one type of Breakdown in any given Google Display & Video 360 TrueView
API Data Stream. The following Additional Breakdowns are available when pulling data from the
DV360 TrueView API in addition to the standard data being pulled:
- TrueView Ad - Select to pull TrueView ad data and add the DimensionsTrueView Ad ID and TrueView
Ad to the dataset.
- Device Type - Select to pull device data and add the Dimension 'Device Type' to the dataset.
Known Issues
•
Due to a limitation with the DV360 API some data might not load as fast as it does with other
connectors, which could cause the Data Stream to time out and fail.
QA Your Data
•
Create the desired report in the Google Display & Video 360 platform for the required date range.
•
Next, process the relevant Data Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence for the same date range.
•
Create a Report/Table Widget/Pivot Table within Intelligence containing the exact same Dimensions
and Measurements for the same report and date range.
•
Compare the Measurement Totals and make sure they're aligned.
Google My Business API Connector
The Google My Business API connector retrieves data related to listings of businesses that are advertised
on Google.
By using this API, you are able to retrieve both your Business details, including your business' name,
photos, phone number and more, and your listings performance - such as the following actions that
were made, including calls, driving directions requests and more.
Note According to a recent update from Google, their API now provides a more precise
representation of data, showcasing solely the number of unique impressions and engagements.
This can cause a discrepancy in your data.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
•
Channel: Social Earned
•
Data Lag: 1 day
Prerequisites
Valid User & Password
Details
•
When using Generic Data Stream Type, it is recommended to map the 'locationName' field as the
Main Entity Key for the Insights report, and the 'reviewID' for the Reviews report.
•
The newly added 'Location Selector' will allow you to add up to 20 locations by which the data will be
broken down by.
•
You can only process data up to 18 months back from the requested date.
General Setup
In the Extended Properties section, choose one of the two available reports then choose the appropriate
Data Stream Type. If you decide to map the data using the 'Generic' data stream type, please refer to the
'Details' section of this article for a mapping best practice.
The available reports are:
•
Location Insights - This report will retrieve the data that's displayed under the 'Insights' tab in Google
My Business platform, including the following views:
- "Where customers view your business on Google"
- "Customer actions"
- "Phone calls"
•
Reviews -This report will retrieve the data that's displayed under the 'Reviews' tab in Google My
Business platform.
Google Search Console API Connector
Google Search Console is a free service offered by Google that helps you monitor, maintain, and
troubleshoot your site's presence in Google Search results.
•
Channel: Search
•
Data Stream Type: Web Analytics
•
Data Lag: 3 days
Prerequisites
To connect to Google Search Console, you need a valid username and password with access to a Google
Search Console account.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Details
•
There can be a discrepancy in Total values when querying the Top Results entity. This is because the
Google Search Console only displays 1000 results in their platform, whereas Marketing Cloud
Intelligence retrieves all results. The value per row will be identical, but the Total value could be
different.
•
The sliding window for the Google Search Console API connector is 3.
•
Click here to learn about the differences in methodology used to calculate the Query Report (site
aggregation) vs. the Page Report.
General Setup
After entering your credentials, select the relevant website you want to retrieve data for.
Extended Properties
•
Breakdown- Select one of the available options to add further breakdowns to the data retrieved by the
API, resulting in a higher level of granularity on which the Measurements can be sliced. The following
Breakdowns are available when pulling data from the Google Search Console, besides the standard
data being pulled:
- Country - Select to pull country data and add the Dimension 'Country' to the dataset.
- Device - Select to pull device data and add the Dimension 'Device' to the dataset.
- Device & Country - Select to pull both Country and Device data and add the Dimensions 'Country'
and 'Device' to the dataset.
•
Report Entity- Select the entities you want to display your data by. The following options are available:
- Page and Query - Display your data by both your page and query entities.
- Page - Display your data by your page entities.
- Query - Display your data by your query entities.
- None - Display your data not aggregated by any reporting entity.
QA Your Data
•
Create the desired report in the Google Search Console platform for the required date.
•
Next, process the relevant Data Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence for the same date range.
•
Create a Report/Table Widget/Pivot Table within Intelligence containing the exact same Dimensions
and Measurements for the same report and date range.
•
Compare the Measurement Totals and make sure they're aligned.
Has Offers by Tune API Connector
Has Offers by Tune is a Performance Marketing Platform and an Affiliate Network.
•
Channel: Affiliate
•
Data Stream Type: Ads

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
•
Data Lag: 1 day
Prerequisites
API key and Network ID. To retrieve your API Key and Network ID:
11.. Log in to your Has Offers Account
22.. Click on 'Support' to the left of the screen
33.. Select 'API'. You should now see your Network ID, API Key and API Domain.
44.. Has Offer require you to list all IPs that will access the API. Enter the following IP address to add the IP
Addresses relevant to your region:
••
US Environment:
54.83.10.143
54.83.10.155
54.83.57.26
54.163.226.11
54.83.31.176
54.225.94.111
54.225.94.116
52.4.199.170
34.231.53.216
34.237.132.144
35.169.235.99
34.225.211.155
34.194.224.126
54.157.67.145
••
EU Environment:
52.28.211.106
52.58.90.172
52.28.190.64
52.59.78.109
18.196.162.52
18.196.154.206
Setup Process
•
After authentication, the Account field will be automatically populated, but you will need to manually
select the Advertisers for which you wish to retrieve data.
•
You can select up to 3 Advertisers or leave the field empty if you wish to retrieve all data for all
Advertisers. Note that if you have hundreds of Advertisers, pulling all of them may be heavy and time
out, if this happens split the streams and get only active Advertisers.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Details
Marketing Cloud Intelligence pulls the following Dimensions and Measurements via this API:

| Has Offers Field | Intelligence Field | Type |
| --- | --- | --- |
| All Stream Types |  |  |
| Date_id | Day | Dimension |
| Browser | Device Browser | Dimension |
| Offer_id | Campaign Key | Dimension |
| Offer | Campaign Name | Dimension |
| Advertiser_id | Campaign Advertiser ID | Dimension |
| Advertiser | Campaign Advertiser | Dimension |
| Offer_id + '_' + affiliate_id | Media Buy Key | Dimension |
| Offer | Media Buy Name | Dimension |
| Payout_type | Media Buy Cost Model | Dimension |
| Affiliate_id | Site Key | Dimension |
| Affiliate | Site Name | Dimension |
| Affiliate_manager | Site Custom Attribute 01 | Dimension |
| Currency | Currency | Attribute |
| Ads Data Stream |  |  |
| Impressions | Impressions | Measurement |
| Gross_clicks | Clicks | Measurement |
| Cost | Media Cost | Measurement |
| Conversion Tag Data Stream |  |  |
| Conversions | Total Conversions | Measurement |
| Revenue | Revenue | Measurement |
| Unmapped |  |  |

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence

| Has Offers Field | Intelligence Field | Type |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Clicks |  |  |
| Affiliate_reference_id |  |  |
| Affiliate_source |  |  |
| Campaign |  |  |
| Creative |  |  |
| Goal |  |  |
| Goal_id |  |  |
| Offer_url |  |  |
| Revenue Type |  |  |

Some Dimensions within the Has Offers API are mapped twice - for example, 'Offer' is mapped to both
Media Buy Name and to Campaign Name.
How To QA Your Data
Whenever you are creating a report in the UI in order to compare it with the data in Marketing Cloud
Intelligence, be sure that you are using the same Dimensions as to what has been defined for the
relevant Data Stream in Intelligence.
11.. Click on 'Reports' to the left of the page
22.. Select any Report from the list ('Daily Report' and 'Advertisers' is recommended)
33.. Ensure all the relevant Dimensions and Measurements are selected and match the data you are
retrieving via the Has Offers Data Stream in Intelligence.
44.. Next create a Pivot Table in Intelligence that has the same Date Range.
55.. Compare the Measurement Totals between the Has Offers Report and the Intelligence Pivot Table -
applying relevant filters to both views and making sure the Totals still match.
Hubspot API Connector
Hubspot is a marketing automation tool, Marketing Cloud Intelligence

…

chunks.heading: part-04
chunks.body:
---
title: "Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence (part 4/5)"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_intelligence_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "122-439"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Details
•
In the CRM Data Stream Type the Marketing Cloud Intelligence platform relies on the Modified Date,
Stage, and Status fields to display the data correctly, therefore it's important you retrieve these fields.
•
Under Retrieval Settings, choose to exclude deleted or archived Entities by selecting the appropriate
checkbox.
General Settings
•
From the Data Stream Type dropdown list, select the Data Stream Type you're pulling data for.
•
From the Data Stream Source dropdown list, select the source you're pulling data for.
•
From the Default Channel Name dropdown list, select the Channel Name you're pulling data for.
Advanced Settings
This section allows you to define advanced settings for your API connector and are the same for all API
connectors.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Extended Properties
None.
Known Issues
•
Marketing Cloud Intelligence doesn't support the Sandbox connection.
How to QA Your Data
This section describes how to make sure your Data is working in Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
•
Create the desired report in the Salesforce SOQL UI for the required date range.
•
Next, process the relevant Data Stream in Marketing Cloud Intelligence for the same date range.
•
Create a Report/Table Widget/Pivot Table within Marketing Cloud Intelligence containing the exact
same Dimensions and Measurements for the same report and date range.
•
Compare the Measurement Totals and make sure they're aligned.
Search Ads 360 API Connector
Search Ads 360
•
Channel: Search
•
Data Lagging: 1 Day
Prerequisites
Since Search Ads 360 derives its permissions from Google Campaign Manager, be sure to enable Search
Data.
Extended Properties
•
Checkboxes - Enables you to upload further attributes according to your Search Ads 360 account
definitions:
- 'Get device breakdown'- This option is checked by default, and pulls an additional Dimension-
'devicesegment', which is mapped to 'Device Category'
- 'Get Conversions Data'- This option adds the Conversion bucket to the Data Stream, which contains
the following Dimensions and Measurements:
- Conversion Tag Category ('foodlightgroup')
- Conversion Tag Key ('foodlightactivityid')
- Conversion Tag Name ('foodlightactivity')
- Total Conversions ('dfaTotalConversions')
- Post Impression Conversions ('dfaTransactions')
- Post Click Conversions ('dfaActions')

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
- Revenue (dfaRevenue)
- 'Include Removed Entities' - This option will not add new Dimensions to the Data Stream. This
option means that removed Entities will be pulled as well.
- 'Get Keyword Data'- This option adds the Search Keywords bucket to the Data Stream, which
contains the following Dimensions and Measurements:
- Search Keyword Key ('keywordId')
- Search Keyword (keywordText)
- Search Keyword Match Type (keywordMatchType)
- Search Keyword Status (status)
- Search Keyword Quality Score (qualityScoreCurrent)
- Search Keyword URL (keywordLandingPage)
- Impressions ('impr')
- Clicks ('clicks')
- Media Cost ('cost')
- Position ('avgPos')
Search Ads 360 Custom API Connector
Search Ads 360
•
Channel: Search
•
Data Lagging: 1 Day
Prerequisites
Search Ads 360 credentials with at least Advertiser viewer permissions
When Should I use the Google Search Ads 360 Custom Connector?
The Search Ads 360 API Connector is used to retrieve a predefined set of Dimensions and
Measurements, supplied by Search Ads 360's API. These Dimensions are mapped within the Ads,
Conversion Tag, Conversion Tag with Keywords, Search Keywords or Conversion Tag Data Stream Types,
depending on the Extended Properties that are selected within the Data Stream. The pre-defined nature
of this API means that creating a Data Stream containing any additional fields other than the predefined
ones is limited and would require a Feature Request.
The Search Ads 360 Custom API Connector allows you to retrieve fields that are not available through
the Standard API, such as Floodlight Dimensions.
Keep in mind that unless you are intending to retrieve a custom set of fields, the standard GSA 360 API
Connector would be a better choice, as it is preset to be accurately mapped to the Data Model, while the
Custom Connector requires manual setup.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
General Setup
Once authenticated you will need to select the Account and the Advertiser you would like to retrieve. The
General, Data Stream Custom Attributes, Advanced and Initial Data Fetch sections in the Data Stream
Settings have the same configuration as the regular GSA 360 Connector.
Extended Properties
•
Report Type- Select the Report Type that you wish to pull. The following Reports are available for
retrieval:
- Account
- Campaign
- AdGroup
- Keyword
- Ad
•
Dimensions - Specify the list of Dimensions you wish to retrieve for the relevant Report as they appear
in the GSA 360 documentation (see above). Ensure that you seperate each Dimension using a comma.
•
Measurements - Specify the list of Measurements you wish to retrieve for the relevant Report as they
appear in the GSA 360 documentation (see above). Ensure that you seperate each Measurement
using a comma.
•
Data Stream Type- Select the Data Stream Type into which the data will be mapped. You can choose
from the following Data Stream Types:
- Ads
- Conversion Tag
- Conversion Tag with Keywords
- Search Keywords
- Generic
SEMrush API Connector
SEMrush is a competitive research platform that allows you to analyze your competitors' domains,
looking into their traffic, relevant keyword, and so on, SEMrush offers different types of data so you can
set up the connector to your liking.
•
Data Lagging: 0 Days
Prerequisites
SEMrush API key. An API key is available as part of the SEMrush API package. When purchasing a
package, note that Marketing Cloud Intelligence uses 'line' requests for live data only.
Details
•
You can validate the data by comparing the Marketing Cloud Intelligence view to the table view under

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
each report type, according to the report you chose to retrieve.
•
SEMrush displays in its platform either live data or historical data aggregated to a month granularity.
Marketing Cloud Intelligence retrieves only live data from SEMrush, so data will start to aggregate from
the stream's creation date moving forward. Note, however, that unlike SEMrush, that doesn't enable
viewing the data in a daily granularity, Marketing Cloud Intelligence enables this view.
•
Since the report available in SEMrush displays only the current date's data, you won't be able to
reprocess past dates in SEMrush streams.
•
The SEMrush data is different in structure from the 'Search Keyword' data model. For example, the
'Position' field is defined as a Dimension in SEMrush, but as a Measurement in Marketing Cloud
Intelligence. Furthermore, in SEMrush the 'Keyword Name' might be linked to several different
'Position' values. Mapping the data 'as is' might cause discrepancies. The best practice for mapping
SeEMrush data is to make sure the 'Keyword Name' and 'Position' fields are linked in a 'many to many'
relationship. Another option is to concatenate the two fields together to create a unique 'Keyword Key'
for every pairing instance.
csv['keyword_name']+ csv['position']
Then map the 'Keyword Name' to the 'Keyword Name' field in the 'Search Keyword' data model, and
map the 'Position' to the 'Keyword Custom Attribute' field.
General Setup
•
Choose the Report you want to retrieve:
- Domain_organic_search_Keywords - corresponding with the 'Positions' report in Semrush Buyer
untargeted Segment Performance report - corresponding with the 'Competitors' report in Semrush.
•
Enter the domain you want to retrieve data for. If no domain is entered, the stream fails .
•
Choose the Database Type. This refers to the Databases available at the top of the report.
•
Select the appropriate Data Stream Type.
SimilarWeb API Connector
SimilarWeb is a digital marketing platform offering a competitive analysis of site traffic.
•
Channel: Social Listening
•
Data Stream Type: Competitive
•
Data Lagging: Retrieves the last full month
Prerequisites
To connect to the SimilarWeb API, you need a valid API Key. Contact your SimilarWeb representative to
receive an API Key.
Details
•
SimilarWeb displays data up to the last month. For example, if the date is May 4 SimilarWeb displays

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
data up until April.
•
When reprocessing a SimilarWeb Data Stream, the reprocess retrieves only whole months, even if you
entered only part of a month. For example, if you chose to reprocess Jan 8 to Feb 14, the whole month
of January and February will be retrieved in this reprocess.
•
Data can only be retrieved as far back as 16 months. All the Measurements pulled by the SimilarWeb
connector aren't summable, except for Visits. The Measurements retrieved are either Rate
Measurements for example, Pages Per Visit or Average Measurements for example, Avg visit duration.
- You might experience inconsistencies when pulling these types of Measurements, as they're
retrieved from SimilarWeb with calculated values, and not as 'native' Measurements.
- It's recommended to view these Measurements in the lowest granularity available.
•
The Avg Visits Duration Measurement is retrieved from SimilarWeb as seconds. To ensure that it's
displayed properly in Marketing Cloud Intelligence, change the Measurement type to Duration by
completing the following steps:
- Navigate to Connect & Mix > Measurements > Measurement List
- Filter your Measurements to Avg Visit Duration
- Under Type select Duration
- Click Save
•
Marketing Cloud Intelligence Transposes the data based on the Device entity - SimilarWeb metrics
have the Device Value and Metric Name merged into one. For example, Mobile Visits contains Mobile,
which is a Dimension value and Visits, which is a Measurement. Transposing the data separates the
two fields so that the Device value is mapped to the relevant Dimension, and the Measurement value
is mapped to the relevant Measurement.
- This process adds more rows depending on the number of Device values, but it's the best way to
process the data so that you can filter the data by device, and so you can easily update any new
devices that are added in the future, without having to update the mapping each time.
•
Marketing Cloud Intelligence pulls worldwide data (if it's available in the account) by default. If
worldwide data is not available, Marketing Cloud Intelligence pulls data only for the countries available
within the account.
General Setup
In this section you can define the general details. After entering your credentials, select the relevant
Account you want to retrieve data for.
Advanced Settings
This section allows you to define advanced settings for your API connector. These settings are the same
for all API connectors. For more information about the different settings, see API Connector - Advanced
Settings.
Extended Properties
•
Domain- Insert up to five domains you want to pull data for. The domains should be separated by a
comma.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
- Ensure that the domain entered doesn't include the 'www' prefix, for example, salesforce.com and
not www.salesforce.com. Inserting invalid domains results in an error.
How to QA Your

chunks.heading: part-05
chunks.body:
---
title: "Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence (part 5/5)"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_intelligence_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "122-439"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

date column. The platform automatically identifies and pulls date information. If a date isn't included the
file can't be uploaded.
In some cases, the date appears in the file name. For example, a file named 'File_06-01-2022' contains a
date in the file name but not as a date column in the data. To use the file name as a date column, you
can apply a formula to the file and extract the date from the file name.
Extracting a Date From the File Name
If your file includes a date in the file name but not in the file itself, you can extract the date field from
the file name and use it as the date field.
Adding a Date in the Mapping Process
If your file doesn't include a date in any of the fields, you can add a date in the mapping process,
without having an existing date field in your file.
Extracting a Date From the File Name
If your file includes a date in the file name but not in the file itself, you can extract the date field from the
file name and use it as the date field.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To extract a date from a file name: Admin
11.. From the Connect & Mix tab, select Data Streams, and then Data Streams List

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
22.. Click Create New, and upload your file.
33.. In the Map to Model window click Date to add a date field.
44.. Click the formula editor icon across from the date field and apply a formula.
Here are some examples of formulas you can use to extract a date from the file name.

| PARSEDATE(RIGHT(env['filename'],10),'M M-dd-yyyy'). | The RIGHT function takes the indicated number of characters starting on the right of the text referenced. |
| --- | --- |
| PARSEDATE(EXTRACT(env['filename'],'_', 1),'MM-dd-yyyy'). | The EXTRACT function obtains a substring from the string you referenced, according to a delimiter and position. The delimiter is the separator between the parts of the string, and the positions are the different parts sectioned by the delimiter. |
| PARSEDATE(SELECT(env['filename'],'\\d{ 2}-\\d{2}-\\d{4}'),'MM-dd-yyyy') | The SELECT function obtains a specific sub string from the string you referred to based on specific characters or patterns. The function supports the use of multiple regular expressions. In this example, a regular expression is used and this pattern is retrieved: ##-##-####-2 digits, hyphen(-), 2 digits, hyphen, 4 digits. The 'd' in the syntax refers to digits, and the number in the brackets {} indicates the number of digits. The delimiter is stated according to its location in the pattern. You can use the select function syntax and change the delimiter and the number of digits in every position in the pattern according to your data. Only use the select formula if you're familiar with the use of regular expressions. |

Adding a Date in the Mapping Process
If your file doesn't include a date in any of the fields, you can add a date in the mapping process, without
having an existing date field in your file.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To add a date in the mapping process: Admin
11.. From the Connect & Mix tab, select Data Streams, and then Data Streams List.
22.. Click Create New, and upload your file.
33.. In the Map to Model window click Date to add a date field, and apply a formula in the formula editor.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
There are several date formulas you can apply.

| TODAY() | Adds today's date to the file. The uploading date is applied to the file. |
| --- | --- |
| DATEADD(interval,number,date) | Adds or subtracts time from a given date according to the entity you entered as the interval. For example, DATEADD('d',-1,TODAY()) retrieve yesterday's date and DATEADD('m',-1,TODAY()) retrieves last months date. |
| DATE(year,month,day) | Enter a date of your choice, for example DATE(2022,06,01). |

Note When mapping files with the same date you may lose data as time goes by. Data entered for
a certain date replaces existing data for the selected date. To prevent losing your data, enter a
new date for each file uploaded without reprocessing the data.
Mapping Formulas
Use the mapping formula editor to apply formulas directly in your mapping fields. Using functions in
your mapping formulas, such as text and date functions, allows you to perform more complex
manipulations to your data.
For example, applying the COALESCE() function when there's a change in your file column headers.
(csv['Media Buy Key']) -file name is uppercase, to (csv['media buy key']) -file name is lowercase. CSV
fields are case-sensitive, the new header doesn't match the name specified in the mapping, causing the
new file process to fail.
After applying this formula COALESCE([csv['Media Buy Key'], csv['media buy key']]), reprocess the data
stream-changes are applied allowing any failed files to go through successfully.
The formulas are intuitive and use a similar syntax as Excel. More complex manipulations can be done
with MVEL, a Java-based expression language.
VLOOKUP
Use the VLOOKUP function to retrieve specific data from a table. You can use it when you're mapping
fields from your source file to Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
FUZZYVLOOKUP
The FUZZYVLOOKUP mapping formula helps identify two elements of text, strings, or entries that are
similar but not identical.
PARSEDATE and FORMATDATE Functions
You can convert a date to text format, for example converting a date to a string format. The conversion
is called formatting and you can use the FORMATDATE function. You can also do the opposite-convert
a string into a date format to obtain a date object, the conversion is called parsing and the PARSEDATE

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
function is used
REPLACE
Use the REPLACE function to replace characters or patterns in a string.
GROUPCONCAT
Use the GROUPCONCAT function to merge data from multiple rows into one field. GROUPCONCAT is
a GROUP BY function and returns a string if the group contains at least 1 non-null value, if no non-null
values exist, it returns a null value.
Using Special Characters in Java Regular Expressions
In Marketing Cloud Intelligence, you can use the Java Regular Expression (RegEx) API to search for and
modify text strings, for example, in a mapping formula or a calculated dimension. RegEx API uses
special characters called metacharacters. A metacharacter is a character that has a special meaning
during pattern processing. You can use a metacharacter as a regular character by escaping.
VLOOKUP
Use the VLOOKUP function to retrieve specific data from a table. You can use it when you're mapping
fields from your source file to Marketing Cloud Intelligence.
VLOOKUP isn't limited to the data stream where it's applied, and it uses all dimensions to match data
columns.
VLOOKUP takes three parameters:
•
ValueToSearch (the CSV field)-The value used to match the two data columns. It can be csv[''] ,
dat[''] , or string.
•
searchDimension (correlating dat field)-The name of the dimension where the value is being
searched. If the dat[''] field is used as a value to search, it can't contain VLOOKUP. For example, you
can insert csv ['Field_1']+ csv ['Field_2'] in the ValueToSearch parameter, and the
formula searches for strings like 'AAA111' and 'CCC333'. However, the returnDimension must contain
only one field.
•
returnDimension-The value that is returned. Both the searchDimension and the returnDimension
must be related to the same entity. The search and return values must be dimensions.
You can also use these optional parameters:
•
workspaceLevel (true/false)-If true, VLOOKUP searches for the value in the entire workspace. When
set to false, the function applies to the current data stream only. This option is useful when looking for
a link between two dimensions by using an attribute or name field rather than a key as the common
denominator.
•
concatvalue (true/false)-If true, VLOOKUP concatenates multiple returned dimension values
alphanumerically or chronologically. Because VLOOKUP can return only one value for each

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
searchDimension, this option treats multiple dimension values as one value. You can then extract the
values using a mapping formula or calculated dimension. If concatvalue is false, VLOOKUP presents
the first value found.
•
{'streamfilter1', 'streamfilter2',...}-You can limit the search to specific data streams. This parameter
overrides the workspaceLevel parameter. You can also use the sign '%%' as a placeholder for 1 or
more characters, before, after or in the middle of the string. For example, type {'%%Facebook%%'} in
order to apply the formula on any data stream that includes the string "Facebook"
•
searchByConstantId (true/false)-if true, VLOOKUP searches the data stream by the constant ID only.
When false or empty, VLOOKUP searches by data stream name. If set to true and a data stream name
was entered by mistake, the function searches by name. To search by ID, insert your ID in the
{'streamfilter1', 'streamfilter2',..} parameter. The new data stream constant ID dimension remains
constant and is not updated when recreating the data stream. To see the constant ID value, either add
the dimension to the data stream list or query it in your tables.
You can use VLOOKUP to pull attributes from a classification data stream into another data stream in
your workspace. A classification data stream often holds attributes that aren't retrieved as part of the
usual data upload. VLOOKUP makes it easy to link these attributes to their entity, harmonizing the
attributes with the rest of your data.
To return multiple dimension values and insert the values into separate rows, use VLOOKUP_UNNEST.
VLOOKUP_UNNEST must be mapped to a dimension that holds a many-to-many relationship with the
searched value, because the resulting returnDimension value holds a many-to-many relationship with the
searched value. If the returned dimension is mapped to the entity's key, a many-to-many relationship
isn't required. You can apply only one VLOOKUP_UNNEST formula per data stream.
VLOOKUP Example
You have two data streams-Data Stream 1 (Ads) and Data Stream 2 (Ads)-and each data stream has a
file. The first file has two fields, Media Buy Key and Media Buy Size (1), and the other file has the same
values for Media Buy Key, but includes two different dimensions: Media Buy Name and Media Buy
Attribute (2). Both files have matching Media Buy Key values, allowing you to use VLOOKUP to display
values for Media Buy Size with the dimensions from the other data stream.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
In Data Stream 1 (Ads), your Media Buy Key is mapped to Media Buy Key, and Media Buy Size is mapped
to Media Buy Size. (1)
In Data Stream 2 (Ads), all three dimensions are mapped to Media Buy Size, where you can add the
VLOOKUP function (2).
In the Media Buy Size dimension, add the following formula: VLOOKUP(csv['Media Buy
Key'],[Media_Buy_Key],[Media_Buy_Size], true,true)
csv['Media Buy Key'] (1) contains the Media Buy Key values from the raw data in the second file.
[Media_Buy_Key] (2) contains the Media Buy Key values from the first file. VLOOKUP looks for matching
values between these two parameters and returns [Media_Buy_Size] (3)-the corresponding Media Buy
Size values from the first file.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Uploading Data in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
After the formula returns the values you can se

…
