chunks.heading: 06-granular-data-center-in-marketing-cloud-intelligence
chunks.body:
---
title: "Granular Data Center in Marketing Cloud Intelligence"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_intelligence_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "440-464"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Granular Data Center in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Results
Granular Data Center in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Granular Data Center is a premium feature designed for ingesting raw data into the system. Due to this
data's granularity, row count can be hundreds of millions or even billions of rows. As opposed to other
data stream types, usage and pricing don't go by the row count but rather by terabytes of storage space.
Some examples of data types that work best with Granular Data Center data streams are keyword level
data, event level data, log data, and granular geodata.
Granular Data Center data streams create correlating tables of data pertaining to a workspace.
All data stored in the Granular Data Center is fully compliant with GDPR requirements and regulations.
The Granular Data Center is a premium feature. If you're interested in purchasing, contact a Marketing
Cloud Intelligence representative.
If the Granular Data Center add-on is deprovisioned from an account, the following happens.
•
The Granular Data Center tab in the left pane doesn't appear anymore.
Note System admins and above can still access the Granular Data Center for 90 days after
disabling the checkbox. After the 90 days are over, they can't access it anymore.
•
Granular Data Center data streams and retrievals from the Granular Data Center stop running and fail.
•
Queries of the Granular Data Center data (from reports, widgets, or via API, including scheduled) stop
and fail.
Note System admins and above can still run SQL queries and exports for 90 days. After 90 days,
all Granular Data Center data streams are automatically deleted and all data is deleted.
When retrieving Granular Data Center data, be aware of these timeout limits:
•
Direct retrievals - 1 hour
•
SFMC granular data extensions - 2 hours
•
Retrieval from Granular Data Center into Granular Data Center - 40 minutes with a builder query, 1
hour with SQL query
•
DB retrievals - 30 minutes
Enable the Granular Data Center in a Workspace
Purchasing the Granular Data Center automatically enables it in the account. However, an admin must
enable it in the workspace. If it isn't enabled, the Granular Data Center tab doesn't appear.
View Granular Data Center Data

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Granular Data Center in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
The Granular Data Center landing page summarizes all the Granular Data Center data streams created
in that workspace. View and manage ingested data, aggregations, extracted data, share data streams,
create queries, and more all from one main location.
Granular Data Center Data Stream Creation
Create a Granular Data Center data stream to ingest granular data, such as event level or keyword level
data.
Mapping Granular Data Center Data
If you uploaded a file or used a technical vendor to create a Granular Data Center data stream, you're
redirected to a mapping preview screen. Here you can verify that your data has been identified
correctly, change mapping, add mapping formulas, and more. When data is uploaded to a Granular
Data Center data stream, a table is created. The table is dynamic according to the type of loaded data.
The type affects the data load options and behavior.
Querying Granular Data Centers
Query your Granular Data Centers to pull data from your workspace. You can also query a Granular
Data Center in another workspace through sharing. You can create a query manually with an SQL
editor or use the Query Builder for an all-click-no-code option. Convert a query from the query builder
into SQL to build onto the existing query or to better understand the parameters.
Visualizing Granular Data Center Data
The Entity-Relationship Diagram (ERD) displays a visual representation of all the tables and
connections between specific dimensions from within these tables. Each block represents an existing
table and contains a list of all the fields available within the table. The lines connecting tables also
depict connections between specific dimensions from the connected tables.
Sharing Granular Data Centers
You can share a Granular Data Center if it contains data that is relevant to other workspaces in the
same account.
Deleting Data from a Granular Data Center
To facilitate compliance with various data protection and privacy regulations, you can delete data from
a Granular Data Center.
Enable the Granular Data Center in a Workspace
Purchasing the Granular Data Center automatically enables it in the account. However, an admin must
enable it in the workspace. If it isn't enabled, the Granular Data Center tab doesn't appear.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To enable the Granular Data Center: Admin
11.. To the left side of the navigation bar, click the workspace name, and then click the workspace settings
gear icon.
22.. In the workspace settings menu clickPremium Features.
33.. Select Data Lake, and then click Save.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Granular Data Center in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
View Granular Data Center Data
The Granular Data Center landing page summarizes all the Granular Data Center data streams created in
that workspace. View and manage ingested data, aggregations, extracted data, share data streams,
create queries, and more all from one main location.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To view Granular Data Center data: Admin
11.. On the Connect & Mix tab, select Granular Data Center.
The Granular Data Center Overview page shows a visual and interactive view of all your Granular Data
Center data and tables.
22.. Hover over an area. For example, in the Ingested Data area, view which Granular Data Center data was
ingested and the number of bytes.
33.. View and manage shared Granular Data Centers and share Granular Data Centers directly from here.
44.. Create a Granular Data Center data stream, or view the total usage.
55.. Create a query, or go straight to the Granular Center ERD page.
66.. View which Granular Data Centers are retrieving TotalConnect, DirectConnect, and Granular Data
Center data streams.
Granular Data Center Data Stream Creation
Create a Granular Data Center data stream to ingest granular data, such as event level or keyword level
data.
There are three ways to pull data:

| Drag & Drop | Select a file stored on your computer, and drop it here. This uploaded file shows how data is previewed and mapped before the data stream and table are created within the Granular Data Center. These file formats are supported: .csv, .tsv, and .json |
| --- | --- |
| Technical Vendor | Retrieve data by selecting from a list of available vendors. Technical vendors are data storage services such as DropBox and Amazon S3. |
| Marketing Vendor API Connection | Retrieve data from a list of available platforms. Marketing vendors are for retrieving data from designated platforms such as Google ads and |

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Granular Data Center in Marketing Cloud Intelligence

|  | Facebook ads. These API connectors differ from regular Marketing Cloud Intelligence API connectors. They're capable of retrieving high- volume granular data. Some of these connectors are only available in the Marketplace. To see more connectors, click Go to Marketplace for more connectors. |
| --- | --- |

Use a Technical Vendor to Create a Granular Data Center Data Stream
To create a Granular Data Center data stream, use a technical vendor. A technical vendor is a data
storage service, such as DropBox or Amazon S3. The process for defining the Granular Data Center
data stream is dynamic according to the vendor, so it's possible that not every step listed is relevant.
Use a Marketing Vendor to Create a Granular Data Center Data Stream
Use a marketing vendor to pull in your granular data when creating a Granular Data Center data
stream. Marketing vendors are set up the same way as Marketing Cloud Intelligence API connectors,
but these connectors are big data compatible and work differently.
Use a Technical Vendor to Create a Granular Data Center Data Stream
To create a Granular Data Center data stream, use a technical vendor. A technical vendor is a data storage
service, such as DropBox or Amazon S3. The process for defining the Granular Data Center data stream is
dynamic according to the vendor, so it's possible that not every step listed is relevant.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create a Granular Data Center data stream: Admin
11.. From the Connect & Mix tab, select the Granular Data Center tab, and then Granular Data Center
Data Streams.
22.. Click Add New.
33.. Click Technical Vendors.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Granular Data Center in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
44.. Select a vendor. For example, Google Cloud Storage.
55.. The delivery method is already selected, but you can select a different vendor here.
66.. To merge all the files that accumulate between retrievals into one large file, select Use Direct Retrieval
(1).
77.. If you choose Google Cloud Storage as your technical vendor, you also must provide a Private Key. For
more information, see Creating and managing service account keys.
Note When using Google Cloud Storage connector with Direct Retrieval the directory should NOT
include leading and trailing slashes, for example: /folder1/folder2/file.csv/ should be folder1/
folder2/file.csv
88.. If you're using S3 as your technical vendor you can turn on the Assume an IAM Role toggle. You can

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Granular Data Center in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
use the IAM role when you don't want to use the usual authentication method of providing an access
key and secret key. Instead, you can grant Marketing Cloud Intelligence access to assume a role
defined in your AWS account.
But first you must set up the role and make sure that the Marketing Cloud Intelligence account gets
the relevant permissions to assume this role. See Providing Access to AWS Accounts. Marketing Cloud
Intelligence's AWS account ID is: 234641932592.
After you have access, define these details:

| Assume an IAM Role | Turn on to enable the assume role options (1). |
| --- | --- |
| IAM Role ARN | Enter the IAM role that you're assuming (2). |
| IAM Role External ID: | (Optional) If you set up access with an external ID, add it here (3). |

99.. Enter your credentials (2).
1100.. To delete the data after it downloads, select Delete After Download (3).
1111.. To retrieve only new files and not all files, select Retrieve Only New Files (4).
1122.. Define the frequency for when to retrieve data (5).
1133.. To change the mapping and customize the data, click Mapping. (See Mapping Data Lake Data for

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Granular Data Center in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
more information.)
1144.. To define advanced settings, click Advanced Settings. (See Advanced Settings in Marketing Cloud
Intelligence Streams for more information.)
Not all settings are relevant to Granular Data Center data streams, so some options don't appear or are
disabled.
A new Granular Data Center data stream is created with all the correlating tables.
Direct Retrieval
Direct retrieval solves the bottlenecks that can occur when processing a large number of files in
between retrieval cycles. It also helps in managing the data stream's execution log, which can be a
challenge when the number of files that accumulate between processing cycles is in the thousands.
Direct Retrieval
Direct retrieval solves the bottlenecks that can occur when processing a large number of files in between
retrieval cycles. It also helps in managing the da

chunks.heading: 07-dimensions
chunks.body:
---
title: "Dimensions"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_intelligence_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "465-482"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Dimensions
repercussions. To delete data, the admin must open a ticket with the required request.
You can either completely remove data from the database or delink specific values within the Granular
Data Center. Delinking keeps the data in the Granular Data Center but replaces certain field values with
other values.
Note Data is deleted only after 7 days from deletion.
Delete Data from a Granular Data Center
You can either remove data from the database or delink specific values within a Granular Data Center.
Delete Data from a Granular Data Center
You can either remove data from the database or delink specific values within a Granular Data Center.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To delete data from a Granular Data Center: Admin
11.. From the Connect & Mix tab, select Granular Data Center, and then Delete Data.
22.. To delete data, define the rules for deletion, and then select Delete Row. For example, delete every
row in the profile table that contains the value dato@datorama.com in the email column.
33.. To delink data, define the rules for deletion, and then select Delink Row. For example, in the profile
table, replace the First and Last name values with Anonymous for every row that contains
dato@datorama.com' in the email column.
Dimensions
In Marketing Cloud Intelligence, a dimension is a qualitative non-numerical attribute that provides
information about your dataset, for example, campaign name and campaign ID. A dimension can't be
counted, even when it includes a number. For instance, a campaign ID consists of numbers but you can't
calculate them.
The dimension list shows which dimensions the data model uses and are available in your workspace.
The available dimension types are: Text, Date, Link, Image, Number, Video.
From the dimension list, you can:
•
Export the dimension list.
•
Edit dimension display names.
Note If a calculated dimension is inherited from another workspace, you can edit it only in the

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Dimensions
original workspace.
•
Replace a dimension across your workspace.
•
View the data stream associated with the dimensions.
•
View which dimensions are mapped and unmapped.
Calculated Dimensions
A calculated dimension is a non-numerical field or dimension that you can create on top of your
existing data. Calculated dimensions don't exist physically in the database. Rather, each calculated
dimension represents a query that can be referenced to from any report, pivot table, and widget in
your dashboard pages.
Calculated Dimension Functions
Marketing Cloud Intelligence provides basic functions you can use to create dynamic calculated
dimensions
Applying Pacing Dimensions
When viewing periodic data at a daily level, the data appears as spikes of metrics on the first day of the
week or month. To avoid data spikes, you can map one or more pacing dimensions to the data stream.
Pacing equally divides periodic data into multiple chunks of rows and spreads the chunks over the
selected time period. Because pacing creates multiple new rows across the time period, using a pacing
dimension can increase your row count.
Dimension Explorer in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
The Dimension Explorer shows the values of all the dimensions in the platform and the origin of these
values. Use the Dimension Explorer when you need to troubleshoot dimension issues so you can get
to the root of the problem.
Calculated Dimensions
A calculated dimension is a non-numerical field or dimension that you can create on top of your existing
data. Calculated dimensions don't exist physically in the database. Rather, each calculated dimension
represents a query that can be referenced to from any report, pivot table, and widget in your dashboard
pages.
You can create and manage calculated dimensions from the Calculated Dimension tab or within a
widget.
You can use a calculated dimension to:
•
Remove unwanted characters or spaces. You can also use it to shorten a name, such as a long website
name, without affecting the data.
•
Remove unwanted spaces from dimension values. If you have a campaign name with too many spaces
in between the words, you can remove the additional spacing, leaving a single space between words.
Create a Calculated Dimension from the Calculated Dimension Tab
Create calculated dimensions on top of existing data and refer to them across your workspace.
Create a Calculated Dimension from a Widget

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Dimensions
Create calculated dimensions on top of existing data and refer to them across your workspace.
Related

chunks.heading: 08-measurements
chunks.body:
---
title: "Measurements"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_intelligence_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "483-508"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Measurements
Measurements
In Marketing Cloud Intelligence, measurements are quantitative numerical data that you can count, for
example, number of clicks and emails opened. Measurements are used to set KPIs and analyze your
data.
The Measurement list, found in the Connect & Mix tab under the measurement menu, shows which
measurements the data model uses and the entities the measurements are associated with.
The available measurement types are: Currency, Number, Duration, and Percentage.
The available aggregation types are: AVG, COUNT, FIRST, LAST, LIFETIME, MAX, MIN, and SUM.
From the Measurement list, you can:
•
Export the measurement list.
•
Edit the measurement display names, type, and aggregation.
•
Replace a measurement across your workspace.
•
View which data streams the measurement is mapped to.
•
View which measurements are mapped and unmapped.
Note If a calculated measurement is inherited from another workspace, you can edit it only in the
original workspace.
Calculated Measurements
A calculated measurement is a numerical custom calculation that you can create on top of your
existing data. The calculated measurement doesn't physically exist in the database. Rather, it
represents a query that you can reference from any report, pivot table, or widget in your dashboard
pages.
Filtered Measurements
A filtered measurement is used to customize an existing measurement to include information relating
to a specific dimension attribute.
Calculated Measurements
A calculated measurement is a numerical custom calculation that you can create on top of your existing
data. The calculated measurement doesn't physically exist in the database. Rather, it represents a query
that you can reference from any report, pivot table, or widget in your dashboard pages.
For example, you want to view your return on ad spend (ROAS) to better analyze your campaign
performance. Instead of manually calculating the ROAS per campaign, create a calculated measurement
to extract this information from your data and view it in your campaign's goals or dashboards.
Create a Calculated Measurement From The Calculated Measurement Tab
Create a calculated measurement on top of existing data that you can refer to across your workspace.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Measurements
Create a Calculated Measurement from a Widget
Create calculated measurements on top of existing data so you can refer to them across your
workspace. Widgets can be used to display the calculated measurement data.
Aggregation and Total Aggregation Functions
An aggregation function computes numerical calculations on data, allowing the query to return
summarized information about a given column or result set. You can use an aggregation function to
calculate each row at the dimension level or the total value. In Marketing Cloud Intelligence, you
define the aggregation function when creating a calculated measurement.
Granularity in Calculated Measurements
Granularity is the level of detail considered and then represented in an analysis or query. The greater
the granularity, the deeper the level of detail. With more detail, you can better understand the smaller
components of your data and create a fuller picture.
System Calculated Measurement
Marketing Cloud Intelligence calculates popular industry measurements that you can use as data in
widgets, reports, and pivot tables. The measurements are calculated using fixed formulas using your
workspace's data. A measurement is available only if its formula's components are mapped in an
existing data stream. For example, if you uploaded a data source with MTC data, the MTC system
calculated measurements appear. This table describes the available system calculated measurements
and their formulas.
Calculated Measurement Functions
Use functions when creating a calculated measurement to make your functions more dynamic.
Create a Calculated Measurement From The Calculated Measurement Tab
Create a calculated measurement on top of existing data that you can refer to across your workspace.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create and manage calculated measurements: Power User or Admin
11.. On the Connect & Mix tab, expand Measurements, and then expand Calculated Measurements.
22.. Click Add New.
33.. In the General section, define the calculated measurement.
When setting the decimal places, the more places you specify, the more exact the calculation.
44.. In the Advanced section define advanced settings.
aa.. For Total Aggregation Function, select the function to calculate the total value of a query. For
example, SUM, AVG, MIN, or MAX. Values that appear in separate rows of a display are all
aggregated in the Total row. The Total Aggregation Function and the Aggregation Function-set in
the expression section- should be the same.
bb.. Set the granularity-Determine the level the calculations for the measurement takes place.
cc.. To let null values be used in the formulas,, select Treat Nulls as Zeros
dd.. To include the date in the granularity of the calculation, select Include Date in Granularity

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Measurements
55.. In the Expression section, define the expression:
aa.. Set the aggregation function to determine the calculation result for all the produced rows. When a
granularity is set, the underlying calculation produces multiple calculation results for multiple rows.
These rows must be aggregated into a single row of the displayed dimension.
bb.. Create the expression that you want to base the calculated measurement on using the list of
available measurements and functions. For example, if you want to calculate email open rate that
uses email sends as the denominator instead of email deliveries, use this expression:
IFERROR([Email_Unique_Opens]/[Email_Sends], null) The IFERROR function results in
the calculated measurement returning null values when the email sends equal to 0.
cc.. To add a function to your expression, click it, or start typing for auto-complete.
66.. Click Save.
Create a Calculated Measurement from a Widget
Create calculated measurements on top of existing data so you can refer to them across your workspace.
Widgets can be used to display the calculated measurement data.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create and manage calculated measurements: Power User or Admin
11.. On the Visualize tab, click a dashboard page.
22.. Click the pencil icon to enter edit mode.
33.. To add a widget, click the plus icon.
44.. Select the widget type, and then click the plus icon to add a field.
55.. Click the Add Calculated Field icon , and then click Calculated Measurement.
66.. In the General section, define the calculated measurement.
When setting the decimal places, the more places you specify, the more exact the calculation.
77.. In the Advanced section define advanced settings.
aa.. For Total Aggregation Function, select the function to calculate the total value of a query. For
example, SUM, AVG, MIN, or MAX. Values that appear in separate rows of a display are all
aggregated in the Total row. The Total Aggregation Function and the Aggregation Function-set in
the expression section- should be the same.
bb.. Set the granularity-Determine the level the calculations for the measurement takes place.
cc.. To let null values be used in the formulas, select Treat Nulls as Zeros
dd.. To include the date in the granularity of the calculation, select Include Date in Granularity
88.. In the Expression section, define the expression:
aa.. Set the aggregation function to determine the calculation result for all the produced rows. When a
granularity is set, the underlying calculation produces multiple calculation results for multiple rows.
These rows must be aggregated into a single row of the displayed dimension.
bb.. Create the expression that you want to base the calculated measurement on using the list of
available measurements and functions. For example, if you want to calculate email open rate that
uses email sends as the denominator instead of email deliveries, use this expression:

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Measurements
IFERROR([Email_Unique_Opens]/[Email_Sends], null) The IFERROR function results in
the calculated measurement returning null values when the email sends equal to 0.
cc.. To add a function to your expression, click it, or start typing for auto-complete.
99.. Click Save.
Aggregation and Total Aggregation Functions
An aggregation function computes numerical calculations on data, allowing the query to return
summarized information about a given column or result set. You can use an aggregation function to
calculate each row at the dimension level or the total value. In Marketing Cloud Intelligence, you define
the aggregation function when creating a calculated measurement.
For example, to perform a different calculation per row, such as getting the average value for each
dimension while summing all the values in the total row, set the aggregation function to AVG and the
total aggregation function to SUM to sum the values in the different rows.
Use Case
You can use both the aggregated function and the total aggregation function in cases where you have a
calculated measurement with a formula that includes a dimension with an aggregation function set to
Auto, and the total value in the query for this calculated measurement is zero. The reason there's no total
value is because the total row doesn't include the formulas' required dimension for this calculated
measurement. To solve this issue and display a total value, you can set a total aggregation function to
display a total value based on the value of each row in the query.
Granularity in Calculated Measurements
Granularity is the level of detail considered and then represented in an analysis or query. The greater the
granularity, the deeper the level of detail. With more detail, you can better understand the smaller
components of your data and create a fuller picture.
For example, you have a calculated measurement that calculates cost per click (CPC) per media buy. The
granularity is set to the media buy dimension, and the calculation is done on the values of each media
buy key. By setting the granularity of the calculated measurement to a lower level, such as the creative
level, the calculation now occurs at the creative key level when slicing the calculated measurement on
any relevant dimension.
System Calculated Measurement
Marketing Cloud Intelligence calculates popular industry measurements that you can use as data in
widgets, reports, and pivot tables. The measurements are calculated using fixed formulas using your
workspace's data. A measurement is available only if its formula's components are mapped in an existing
data stream. For example, if you uploaded a data source with MTC data, the MTC system calculated
measurements appear. This table describes the available system calculated measurements and their

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Measurements
formulas.

| System Calculated Measurement | Expression |
| --- | --- |
| buy data actualized cost daily | buy data recalculated_cost - buy data recalculated cost accumulative + delivery buy data actualized cost |
| buy data actualized cost share | buy data actualized cost * buy data share of total |
| buy data actualized cost total | buy data recalculated cost total +buy data fixed cost |
| buy data expected cost daily | buy data capped cost /buy data IO days |
| buy data remaining budget | buy data capped cost - buy data recalculated cost accumulative |
| company count | countdistinct('company_id') |
| conversion CPA | media cost / c

chunks.heading: 09-visualize-your-data-with-marketing-cloud-intelligence
chunks.body:
---
title: "Visualize Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_intelligence_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "509-565"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Visualize Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create filtered measurements from widgets: Admin
11.. On the Visualize tab, go into edit mode, and then select the widget you want to create a filtered
measurement for.
22.. Click the Data tab.
33.. Click the Calculated Fields icon and select Filtered Measurement. If only one measurement is
allowed in the widget, the filtered measurement replaces the existing measurement. If the widget
allows multiple measurements the new filtered measurement is added.
44.. Define the general details:
aa.. Name the filtered measurement.
bb.. Select the measurement you want to filter.
cc.. Add a symbol and color to appear next to the filtered measurement in reports.
55.. To filter the filtered measurement by a specific date range, in the Filter section, select Time Period,
and then select the date range.
66.. To add a filter to your filtered measurement click Add Filter and define the filter. For example, a filter
allows you to apply the filtered measurement to a specific data stream.
Note To save your filtered measurement you must select a time period or add a filter.
77.. Save your filtered measurement.
Visualize Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Marketing Cloud Intelligence offers many tools so you can get a real-time view of all your marketing data
in one place. You get everything from standard key performance indicator (KPI) reporting to complex
visualizations required for audience segmentation, customer journey analytics, and predictive modeling.
And it's all done in the Visualize tab in the Marketing Cloud Intelligence platform.
Get Started
To get started, learn about visualization elements (collections, pages, and widgets), visualization tools,
and how to navigate the Visualize tab in the Marketing Cloud Intelligence platform.
Collections
After you connect your data to Marketing Cloud Intelligence, you can start to organize and manage
your data for the different departments of your organization-for example, delivery data, cost data, or
social impact. The relevant data for each department is managed in separate visualization entities or
collections.
Pages
Pages segment your collections so you can divide up your data according to your preferences, such as
products, campaigns, or social platforms. Each page can be customized and filtered, so you can
modify the design and remove any unwanted data. To assist you in dividing the visualization into
manageable sections, you can have multiple pages within a collection.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Visualize Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Widgets
Widgets are the graphic elements that appear on the page and display your data through pie charts,
tables, and bar graphs. Marketing Cloud Intelligence offers multiple widget types: interactive, element,
and custom.
SmartLenses in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
SmartLenses are predefined dashboard pages that are created for you after a supported data source is
created for the first time. After the data source is processed and data is retrieved, a notification
appears with a link to the SmartLenses dashboard page. All SmartLenses dashboard pages are hosted
under one designated SmartLenses collection.
Share and Export Pages and Widgets
After you're done creating and designing your page, you're ready to publish and share it with others.
Use the Share option to share pages and widgets from the same place.
Get Started
To get started, learn about visualization elements (collections, pages, and widgets), visualization tools,
and how to navigate the Visualize tab in the Marketing Cloud Intelligence platform.
Get to Know Visualization Elements
The visualization process in Marketing Cloud Intelligence includes three elements: collections, pages,
and widgets.
Get to Know Visualize Tools
Marketing Cloud Intelligence offers an array of different tools to help you create professional, eye-
catching dashboards quickly and efficiently. View your data clearly and share it using multiple formats.
Navigate the Visualize Tab
Go to the Visualize tab to familiarize yourself with the available options and to find all the visualize
elements. The Visualize tab is dynamic and changes when you select different elements.
Get to Know Visualization Elements
The visualization process in Marketing Cloud Intelligence includes three elements: collections, pages,
and widgets.
Collections
Collections synergize all your data and consume it in one place. No matter where data is coming
from-a data stream or an app-you can visualize, organize, and personalize it all together in a
collection.
Pages
Pages visualize your data. You can have multiple pages within your collection to assist you in
dividing the visualization into manageable sections. These page types are available in Marketing
Cloud Intelligence:
•
Blank dashboard pages - Used to create visualizations of your data without any predefined

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Visualize Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
settings or design.
•
Dashboard templates - Pages that are created based on a dashboard template saved to the
workspace, allowing you to replicate your dashboard pages across your collections and
workspace.
•
App pages -- Pages that display all the valuable data generated from an installed app, enabling
you to visualize and customize a marketplace app all within your collection.
Widgets
Widgets are the graphic elements that appear on the page, such as pie charts, tables, and bar
graphs.
See Also
Navigate the Visualize Tab
Get to Know Visualize Tools
Marketing Cloud Intelligence offers an array of different tools to help you create professional, eye-
catching dashboards quickly and efficiently. View your data clearly and share it using multiple formats.
•
Widgets - Use the vast library of widgets to bring your data to life.
•
Filters - Use any one of the hundreds of filters available to help you drill down your data to what is
relevant to you.
•
Themes - Create expressive visualizations and customize them with the utmost granular control.
•
InstaBrand - Generate beautiful, branded dashboard themes based on a logo or image uploaded by
you.
•
Export - Share your data with multiple stakeholders in an assortment of different formats.
Navigate the Visualize Tab
Go to the Visualize tab to familiarize yourself with the available options and to find all the visualize
elements. The Visualize tab is dynamic and changes when you select different elements.
•
The left page offers your editing options. Here, you see all the relevant tabs for the setting level you're
in-whether you're setting up a widget or designing the page.
•
The top bar is where you can administer page-level settings and actions.
Collections
After you connect your data to Marketing Cloud Intelligence, you can start to organize and manage your
data for the different departments of your organization-for example, delivery data, cost data, or social
impact. The relevant data for each department is managed in separate visualization entities or
collections.
A collection enables you to synergize all your data and consume it in one place. No matter where data is

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Visualize Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
coming from, whether a data stream or an app, you can visualize, organize, and personalize it all together
in a collection. You can assign multiple entities to a collection such as app pages, dashboard pages, and
SmartLenses.
Here's a video about collections:
Watch the video: https://salesforce.vidyard.com/watch/airxAVjFshDK4tkfJuX5Lg
Create a Collection
Complete these steps to create a new collection.
Add Pages to a Collection
After you create a collection, you can start adding pages to the collection.
Create a Collection
Complete these steps to create a new collection.

| REQUIRED USER ROLE |  |
| --- | --- |
| To create a collection | Admin |

11.. Navigate to the Visualize tab.
22.. Click All Collections.
33.. Click Add New.
You can now add pages to your collection.
Add Pages to a Collection
After you create a collection, you can start adding pages to the collection.

| REQUIRED USER ROLE |  |
| --- | --- |
| To add pages to a collection | Admin and Power User |

11.. Within your collection, click Add New.
22.. Select the type of page you want to add:

| Blank Dashboard Page | Create a brand new, blank dashboard page. |
| --- | --- |
| Dashboard Templates | Create a new dashboard page that is aligned to the dashboard template you select. When you create a template, all the data and design, including theme, widgets, and filters, are cloned into the new dashboard page. You can browse for any dashboard template that is available |

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Visualize Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence

|  | within your workspace. |
| --- | --- |
| Apps Page | Create an app page to visualize and customize a marketplace app within your collection. You can browse for any app that is installed in your workspace. |

Note Admins see all the installed apps in the workspace or can head straight to the marketplace
to install a new app. Power users see all the installed apps that they have access to.
Pages
Pages segment your collections so you can divide up your data according to your preferences, such as
products, campaigns, or social platforms. Each page can be customized and filtered, so you can modify
the design and remove any unwanted data. To assist you in dividing the visualization into manageable
sections, you can have multiple pages within a collection.
Create Blank Dashboard Pages
You can use a blank dashboard page to create visualizations of your data without any predefined
settings or design. For example, create a dashboard page to illustrate how campaigns are performing
to an executive overview. Divide dashboard pages according to products, campaigns, or social
platforms. And customize them to your preferences.
App Pages
An app page is a page added to your collection that displays all the valuable data generated from an
installed app. App pages enable you to visualize and customize a marketplace app within your
collection, without running over the app instance settings.
Dashboard Page Templates
Dashboard page templates are based on a dashboard template saved to the workspace. Dashboard
templates allow you to replicate your dashboard pages across your collections and workspace. When
you create a dashboard template, all the data and design, including theme, widgets, and filters, are
cloned into the new dashboard page. Using dashboard templates helps unify your workspace and save
valuable time because you don't create the same dashboard page from scratch.
Create Blank Dashboard Pages
You can use a blank dashboard page to create visualizations of your data without any predefined settings
or design. For example, create a dashboard page to illustrate how campaigns are performing to an
executive overview. Divide dashboard pages according to products, campaigns, or social platforms. And
customize them to your preferences.
Customize Dashboard Pages
After you create a dashboard page, you can customize it to meet your needs via Edit Mode.
Add Filters to Your Page

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Visualize Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
You can add filters to your page when you want to filter the page by any dimension, measurement, or
date. Filters are defined in the Filters tab when in Edit Mode.
Create Interactive FilterSets
Interactive FilterSets enable admins and power users to 

…

chunks.heading: 10-analyze-your-data-with-marketing-cloud-intelligence
chunks.body:
---
title: "Analyze Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_intelligence_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "566-594"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Analyze Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence

| REQUIRED USER ROLE |  |
| --- | --- |
| To create a scheduled report for a page | Admin and Power User |

11.. Go to Visualize.
22.. Click on the collection, and then select the dashboard page you want to export.
33.. Click Share.
44.. Select Scheduled Report to be redirected to the Reports tab. A new report is created for you according
to the format you selected.
55.. To change the export format, select the format you want from the Export format dropdown list. You
can schedule dashboard pages to be exported in these formats: PNG, PDF.
66.. Under Configuration, the Dashboard and Page are automatically selected for you based on the
dashboard page you exported. If you want to export a page that includes Interactive FilterSets, click
the Page dropdown, and select the layout that includes the interactive filterset you want to use from
the Custom Layouts section. If you want to export a page with interactive filtersets and later edit the
widget layout and save new layouts, use Advanced Export.
77.. When you're done defining your report, save it, or click Save & Run to run it.
See Also
Use Advanced Export
Create a Scheduled Report for a Widget
A scheduled report of your widget sends a PNG file of your widget.

| REQUIRED USER ROLE |  |
| --- | --- |
| To create a scheduled report for a widget | Admin and Power User |

11.. Go to Visualize.
22.. Select the dashboard page where the widget is located.
33.. Select the widget you want to export.
44.. Click Share.
55.. Select Scheduled Report to be redirected to the Reports tab. A new report is created for you
according to the format you selected.
66.. Under Configuration, the Dashboard, Page, and Widget are automatically selected for you based on
the widget you exported.
77.. When you're done defining your report, save it, or click Save & Run to run it.
Analyze Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Marketing Cloud Intelligence offers many tools for analyzing your data. All of these tools are found in the
Analyze and Act tab.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Analyze Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
For example pivot tables ensure that your data is being loaded correctly from the source into Marketing
Cloud Intelligence and that the values are aggregating correctly. Pivot tables can be exported into reports
that can be set to run as part of a workflow, in real time, or according to a defined schedule. Goals are
monitoring tools that enable you to track KPIs, helping you improve your work strategies and outcome.
Activation Center
Use the Activation Center to connect to third-party platforms, such as Slack, Facebook Ads, and
Google Ads, to trigger alerts, collaborate, and automate workflows in other systems. For example, you
can create an action that sends a notification in Slack every time a certain campaign hits the target
value you set for it.
Einstein Marketing Insights
Einstein Marketing Insights analyzes your data to show key factors that affect your marketing
performance. Track your KPIs and verify that they perform as you intend by using Einstein Marketing
Insights bots. For example, if you track the average time on your website, the bot can point out the
path that increased the average time spent.
Goals
Use Marketing Cloud Intelligence Goals to track your performance KPIs. The goal tool helps you
monitor your progress against benchmarks you set. Marketing Cloud Intelligence goals bring you
closer to actionable insights and help you improve the efficiency and effectiveness of your data. You
can visualize your goals in your dashboards allowing you to view and monitor your KPIs in context, and
get notifications in real time for your KPIs.
Pivot Tables in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Explore your data from multiple perspectives using pivot tables. You can calculate, summarize, and
analyze data by seeing comparisons, patterns, and trends in your data. You can also create custom
reports without using query formulas, such as SQL queries.
Reports in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Use reports to retrieve, view, and share a detailed analysis of the data that you've integrated into
Marketing Cloud Intelligence. You can save them locally or share them via email or the cloud. The data
uploaded into Intelligence is displayed as a flat file and includes selected dimensions and
measurements within a defined time range.
Connect Marketing Cloud Intelligence with Tableau
Connect Marketing Cloud Intelligence with Tableau to easily query your data. With the Marketing
Cloud Intelligence Tableau integration, you get harmonization capabilities with Intelligence's ETL
solution and Tableau's advanced visualization tools.
Use Database Exports to Export Datasets
Export data from Marketing Cloud Intelligence, and connect it to an enterprise database such as
Snowflake and PostgreSQL. With Database Exports, automate the export of Intelligence marketing
data. Enhance enterprise-wide analytics by making marketing insights accessible, and get a full view of
your company's data.
Query Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data with the API Builder
Use the Marketing Cloud Intelligence Query API Builder to perform advanced queries on your existing
data.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Analyze Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Activation Center
Use the Activation Center to connect to third-party platforms, such as Slack, Facebook Ads, and Google
Ads, to trigger alerts, collaborate, and automate workflows in other systems. For example, you can create
an action that sends a notification in Slack every time a certain campaign hits the target value you set for
it.
You can create actions and customize them using Webhooks and Python. And then you can
automatically trigger actions based on key performance indicators (KPIs) you set for your campaigns,
media buys, and creatives.
Actions and Action Rules
You can react, track, and manage your most important KPIs and benchmarks by using the activation
center data optimization tools. Access the tools from the Activation Center menu in the Analyze & Act
tab.
•
Actions - Interact with your channel or campaign. For example, send a Slack message to your
reporting channel, pause a campaign on Facebook, or send a custom email.
•
Action Rules - Define the triggers for your actions. Triggers for your stored actions are automatically
executed when the event you define occurs. You can trigger multiple actions for one action rule.
Action Types
These action types are available.
•
Email - send email to a list of contacts.
•
Webhook - trigger actions over the web in other applications.
•
IFTTT Connector - trigger an appellate in IFTTT.
•
Twilio Connector - send an SMS through Twilio.
•
Slack Connector - post to Slack.
•
Python - trigger python scripts.
•
Media Actions in Google, Twitter, and Facebook - trigger media actions such as stopping or starting
campaigns, ad sets, or ads.
Create Actions in the Activation Center
Create actions for third-party providers and channel platforms such as Facebook Ads and Slack.
Create Action Rules in the Activation Center
Action rules define how a rule is triggered. For example, the action can be triggered by time or by a
specific event. You can create a rule for several actions.
Trigger an Action Rule from the Management Screen
Triggering an action manually allows you to run an action rule at any time. If the action rule condition
is met, the rule executes the actions specified in it. For example, if your stakeholders ask for an
updated report in the middle of the week, you can trigger the action rule and see the latest

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Analyze Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
information.
Create Actions in the Activation Center
Create actions for third-party providers and channel platforms such as Facebook Ads and Slack.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create an Action Admin
11.. On the Analyze & Act tab, on the left menu, click Activation Center and then select Actions.
22.. Click Add New.
33.. Select the connector or channel that you want to create an action for. For example, select Email.
44.. Define the parameters for the action. The setup for each connector or channel is unique:
aa.. Name your action.
bb.. Set the destination. For example, a recipient's email address.
cc.. Add your content. For example, add a message or the information that you want to share.
dd.. To add a query to your action, click Query Settings. Queries make the data dynamic and interactive.
When you add a query to your content, ensure that the measurements and dimensions you
selected are available in your data.
ee.. A preview of your action rule appears in the window on the right side.
55.. To create the action, save your changes.
66.. To define a trigger for the action, click Create Rule.
Create Action Rules in the Activation Center
Action rules define how a rule is triggered. For example, the action can be triggered by time or by a
specific event. You can create a rule for several actions.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create an Action Rule Admin or Power User
11.. On the Analyze & Act tab, click Activation Center and then Action Rules in the left panel.
22.. Click Create New.
33.. Name your rule.
44.. From the dropdown, select the action that you want to apply the rule to. For example, an email action
named Daily Update that sends daily email updates.
55.. To add another action, click the + icon, and select the action from the dropdown.
66.. Define the trigger By Time or By Event. Defining the trigger determines what prompts the action rule:
aa.. By Time - Sets daily, weekly, or monthly triggers.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Analyze Your Data with Marketing Cloud Intelligence
bb.. By Event - Sets a KPI for a specific measurement. For example, you want to be notified when your
Message Total Clicks drops below 100.
77.. Apply filters. To narrow your results, you can apply a date range filter. For example, if you want your
action rule to stop after 1 month, you can set the date range for 1 month.
88.. To save your rule, click Create Action Rules.
Trigger an Action Rule from the Management Screen
Triggering an action manually allows you to run an action rule at any time. If the action rule condition is
met, the rule executes the actions specified in it. For example, if your stakeholders ask for an updated
report in the middle of the week, you can trigger the action rule and see the latest information.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To trigger an Action Rule Admin or Power User
11.. On the Analyze & Act tab, click Activation Center and then Action Rules in the left panel. A list of all
the action rules appears.
22.. Select the row of the desired action rule. Click Run.
33.. If the action rule's condition is met, the rule executes the actions specified in it. For example, if you
want to be notified when your Message Total Clicks drops below 100, you're notified when your total
drops below 100.
Einstein Marketing Insights
Einstein Marketing Insights analyzes your data to show key factors that affect your marketing
performance. Track your KPIs and verify that they perform as you intend by using Einstein Marketing
Insights bots. For example, if you track the average time on your website, the bot can point out the path
that increased the average time spent.
An Einstein Marketing Insights bot shows two types of insights.
•
Over- and under-performers
For example, a campaign's click-through rate is 60% above average, with 11

…

chunks.heading: 11-harmonize-your-data
chunks.body:
---
title: "Harmonize Your Data"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_intelligence_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "595-615"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Harmonize Your Data
88.. Define the authentication details, including user, password, and if necessary, private key.
99.. Before you create the integration, check it to make sure that it works and is valid. At the top right-hand
side of the page, click Validate Connection.
1100.. If there are any errors, a red information icon appears that you can click to view more details. If the
integration is valid, click Create.
Query Marketing Cloud Intelligence Data with the API Builder
Use the Marketing Cloud Intelligence Query API Builder to perform advanced queries on your existing
data.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To use the Query API Builder: Admin
11.. From the Analyze & Act tab, click Query API Builder.
22.. Select the workspace you're including in the query.
33.. Set a date range for the query.
44.. Select the dimensions and measurements you're including in the query.
Note If you add two dimensions with the same display name, the query can't identify which data
stream type to look in. It's recommended you provide unique names when naming the query.
55.. To filter by a specific dimension or measurement, click Open Filter.
66.. Click Generate Query.
Here's an example of a generated query.
77.. To test the code, click Run Query.
88.. Copy the query and use it in your code when creating API calls.
Harmonize Your Data
Marketing Cloud Intelligence offers multiple tools for harmonizing your data. Because you can upload
data from various sources, you need a way to merge the data and provide a complete view of your
marketing activity. In most cases, no single data source holds all relevant data on a marketing event or
campaign. The data is shared by the multiple platforms that digital marketers use to do things like
promoting the campaign on multiple channels. To analyze the full picture, harmonize data from all
sources into one. If data isn't harmonized, you must analyze each source individually-making it a
challenge to gain insights quickly and efficiently.
The Marketing Cloud Intelligence data model is marketing oriented and facilitates natural harmonization
of marketing data from multiple sources. Another option is to use data harmonization tools, like the
harmonization center, parent-child, calculated dimensions, and measurements.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Harmonize Your Data
Here's a video: Getting the Most out of the Harmonization Center:
Watch the video: https://salesforce.vidyard.com/watch/AW746Kr1G2VD5FzCx3pJ33?
Harmonize Data with the Harmonization Center
The Harmonization Center in the Connect & Mix tab, unifies, enriches, and validates your data in one
place. In the Harmonization Center, create and manage your naming convention patterns,
classifications, and harmonized dimensions.
Parent-Child Connections in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
A parent-child connection describes a case where two or more data streams are linked by a matching
entity key. This connection establishes hierarchical dependencies between entities, allowing for
grouping at various granular levels such as campaign, media buy, or creative. Matching entity keys
between the data streams are required to establish a parent-child relationship.
Fusing Data Between Datasets
Data fusion creates a link between dimensions that don't already have a data model relationship
between them.
Custom Classifications in Marketing Cloud Intelligence
Custom classifications are cross channel entities that are present in all data streams. They allow you to
link data streams together via an entity.
Harmonize Data with the Harmonization Center
The Harmonization Center in the Connect & Mix tab, unifies, enriches, and validates your data in one
place. In the Harmonization Center, create and manage your naming convention patterns, classifications,
and harmonized dimensions.
The main page contains an overview of your harmonized data.
The KPI status bar shows source dimensions, harmonized dimensions, and classification rules (1). Click
Fix & Maintain to monitor and remedy issues that occur with data governance directly within Marketing
Cloud Intelligence (2). View all your patterns (3), harmonized dimensions (4), and data classification
rules and perform actions directly from the Harmonization Center.
Naming Convention Patterns
In the distraction-filled world of a digital marketer, clarity is important. Therefore, when using different
marketing platforms, digital marketers often name an activity with all the information in the name.
Proper naming allows them to differentiate between the different campaigns, and understand
campaign goals and details at a glance without having to look elsewhere.
Data Classification
Classify your data without using a single line of code. For example, classify your sites by news, sports,
or entertainment, and so on. Upload a classification file and create a classification rule.
Validate and Maintain Your Harmonized Data in the Harmonization Center
Keeping track of your naming convention patterns, classifications, and harmonized dimensions is

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Harmonize Your Data
overwhelming and time-consuming. Fix & Maintain offers a solution for identifying data that doesn't
adhere to your predefined naming conventions or data classifications. Validate and maintain
harmonized data with Fix & Maintain and if issues arise, fix, and adjust as needed.
Generate Media Names with the Media Naming Builder
Create consistent campaign, placement, and creative names that match your naming conventions
using the Media Naming Builder. Instead of manually generating names using other third party tools,
add hundreds of names instantly based on your naming convention patterns and avoid data
discrepancies.
Naming Convention Patterns
In the distraction-filled world of a digital marketer, clarity is important. Therefore, when using different
marketing platforms, digital marketers often name an activity with all the information in the name.
Proper naming allows them to differentiate between the different campaigns, and understand campaign
goals and details at a glance without having to look elsewhere.
Digital marketers usually abide by a certain structure or naming convention for the campaign name.
Creating a naming convention pattern deconstructs your dimension, which is in a naming convention
form, and creates harmonized dimensions for each segment of your naming convention. A naming
convention usually consists of the main details and contains a delimiter.
For example, in this naming convention each color represents an attribute and an underscore is used as
the delimiter. The naming convention can be divided into four attributes: Car Name, Car Category,
Promotion Name, and Site.
Here's a video about naming convention patterns:
Watch the video: https://salesforce.vidyard.com/watch/beMMaYcnJS7QWghC4TMAeU
Create a Naming Convention Pattern
Create a naming convention pattern to deconstruct your dimension, which is in a naming convention
form, and create harmonized dimensions for each segment of your naming convention. A harmonized
dimension acts like any regular calculated dimension and can be used in the platform, for example in
reports.
Manage and Monitor Harmonized Dimensions
After harmonized dimensions are created, they appear in a list so you can easily manage them. Add
classifications and validations, see previews, find and replace in bulk, and more.
Multi-Delimiter Patterns and Structure Compliance
A multi-delimiter pattern contains different types of delimiters in a pattern, such as an underscore,
colon, and comma. Structure compliance determines how and when extractions take place. With a
single delimiter pattern, an extraction occurs only for dimension values that adhere to the pattern's
exact structure. For example, dimensions that have the same number of positions as the dimension
that you're currently creating this pattern for. When working with multi-delimiter patterns, the logic is

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Harmonize Your Data
different.
Create a Naming Convention Pattern
Create a naming convention pattern to deconstruct your dimension, which is in a naming convention
form, and create harmonized dimensions for each segment of your naming convention. A harmonized
dimension acts like any regular calculated dimension and can be used in the platform, for example in
reports.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To create naming convention patterns: Admin
11.. From the Connect & Mix tab, select Harmonization Center, and then Patterns.
22.. Click Add New.
33.. Define the general details:
aa.. Enter a descriptive name so you can easily identify the pattern later.
bb.. Select the Source Dimension that holds the pattern you want to deconstruct.
cc.. To filter by a certain data source or data stream, click Add Filter.
dd.. Select List to include current data streams.
ee.. Select Contains to include future data streams.
ff.. Select the structure that best suits your naming convention, or select Manual Setup to manually
create a naming structure from scratch. Then, enter the number of positions and delimiters.
gg.. Click Apply.
44.. Construct your pattern.
aa.. Marketing Cloud Intelligence breaks down the original values of the source dimension according to
the naming structure (1).
bb.. Navigate to each position and type in the name of the new harmonized dimension or select one
from the dropdown (2).
cc.. The Extract toggle is turned on by default. A harmonized dimension is created for each position
after the pattern is created. If you don't need harmonized dimensions, for example for a serial
number, then turn off the toggle (3).
dd.. To change the delimiter, enter the required delimiter (4).
ee.. Click Add Position and continue selecting the dimensions (5). For example, if your naming
convention contains an additional position.
ff.. Structure Compliant is on by default. Extractions occur for dimension values that adhere to this
pattern's exact structure. For example, dimensions that have the same number of positions as the
dimension that you're creating this pattern for (6). This toggle has different results depending on the
pattern type. For more detailed information, see Multi Delimiter Patterns and Structure Compliance.
55.. Click Done.
New harmonized dimensions are created according to how you defined the extraction when creating
harmonized dimensions. For example, if we look at this naming convention:

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Harmonize Your Data
Product_TargetAudience_Offer_PlacementType_CreativeType.
When breaking it down, you get 5 positions, where each position produces a harmonized dimension.
In this image, the naming convention pattern produced 5 different harmonized dimensions: Product,
Target Audience, Offer, Placement Type, and Creative Type.
Manage and Monitor Harmonized Dimensions
After harmonized dimensions are created, they appear in a list so you can easily manage them. Add
classifications and validations, see previews, find and replace in bulk, and more.
RREEQQUUIIRREEDD EEDDIITTIIOONNSS
USER PERMISSIONS NEEDED
To manage and monitor harmonized dimensions: Admin
11.. From the Connect & Mix tab, select Harmonization Center and then Harmonized Dimensions.
22.. View all of your harmonized dimensions.
33.. To review where the harmonized dimension is used and replace it with another dimension, click Find/
Replace.
44.. To view more details about a harmonized dimension, double-click it.
aa.. To add another layer of validation, such as a list of products, click Add Validation (1).
bb.. To classify your harmonized dimension by categories, click Add Clas

…

chunks.heading: 12-media-transparency-center
chunks.body:
---
title: "Media Transparency Center"
source_pdf: "marketing_cloud_intelligence_4-7-2026.pdf"
pdf_pages: "616-642"
extracted_date: "2026-04-07"
purpose: "Reference help for AI prompts (Marketing Cloud Engagement)"
---

Media Transparency Center
And you have this classification file for Product:
In the Ads data stream, 'Product Key' is mapped to 'Custom Classification 1:
The classification data stream is mapped in any data stream type, and the mapping looks like this:
You can now slice the data from the Ads data stream type using 'Custom Classification 1'.
If something changes, for example the color of Product ID '222' changes, change the classification file
and process it. You don't need to reprocess the Ads data stream for the data to change.
Media Transparency Center
The Marketing Cloud Intelligence Media Transparency Center allows you to ingest, unify, and analyze
Media Plan and Delivery data to pace, track costs, and effectively optimize your media budgets and
campaigns.
For example, if you have a budget for an insertion order (IO), you can compare it to the generated media
cost and calculate how much of the budget has been spent and at what rate. If you are at 50% of the
allocated time frame and 60% of your budget is already spent on the campaign, you can see an over-
delivery and adjust as needed.
Note The Media Transparency Center is a premium feature available upon purchase
Media Transparency Center Setup
Learn how to set up the Media Transparency Center for the first time.
Creating an IO in the Media Transparency Center
Learn how to create an IO in the Media Transparency Center.
Attaching a Delivery Item to an IO in the Media Transparency Center
Learn how to attach a delivery item to an IO in the Media Transparency Center.
Dimensions and Measurements in the Media Transparency Center
View a list of the available dimensions and measurements in the Media Transparency Center.
Media Transparency Center FAQ
Read our FAQ to learn about the most common Media Transparency Center questions and answers.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Media Transparency Center
Grouping Delivery Data in the Media Transparency Center
Learn about grouping delivery data in the Media Transparency Center.
Actualized and Recalculated Costs in the Media Transparency Center
Learn about actualized and recalculated costs in the Media Transparency Center.
Workspace Settings in the Media Transparency Center
Learn about the workspace settings in the Media Transparency Center.
Media Transparency Center Setup
Learn how to set up the Media Transparency Center for the first time.
There are five steps to setting up the Media Transparency Center:
Step 1: Identify Essential IO (Insertion Order) Fields
Buy Data, the data containing all your planned activities and costs (sometimes also referred to as 'Media
Plan' or 'Planned Data'), can come in through a variety of platforms, such as Salesforce, MediaOcean,
Nexelus, Google Sheets, and others. While Buy Data might arrive in different formats, it must contain a
core set of fields that are essential for an IO's structure and function.
These are the essential fields:
•
ID
•
Name
•
Start/End Dates
•
Cost Types
•
Rate
•
Margin (optional)
•
Budget
Here are some examples of field these fields' values:
STEP 2: Set up Custom Cost Types
Marketing Cloud Intelligence has a set of default Cost Types you can use for your IOs. These Cost Types
are as follows: CPM, CPC, CPCV, CPE, CPV, VCPM, CPA, dCPM, dCPC and FLAT. However, if your Buy Data
contains a Cost Type that isn't one of these, you can add it using the 'Custom Cost Types' option in the
Workspace settings. Creating a Custom Cost Type can be done quickly and easily, as follows:
11.. Define the Cost Type in Media Transparency Center

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Media Transparency Center
First navigate to the Cost Center by clicking the Intelligence (Datorama) logo on the top right, then
your User Name, then Workspace in the left pane menu, and finally on 'Cost Center'.
Then set your Custom Cost Type by selecting the Measurement to which it applies, and assign a
Display Name exactly as it appears in the Buy Data file.
For example, GoingSolar's digital marketing activity puts a strong emphasis on generating leads for
prospective clients. Because leads are an expensive yet important resource, their generation amid all
marketing costs is closely watched. GoingSolar's Buy Data therefore has CPL (Cost per Lead) as one of
its main Cost Types. CPL isn't on the list of default Cost Types, so it has to be set up as a Custom Cost
Type:
Things to consider when configuring a Custom Cost Type:
••
The Measurement you select to base the Custom Cost Type upon can only be a Mapped Field that
exists in your Delivery Data. It is impossible to base Custom Cost Types on Filtered or Calculated
Measurements.
••
The selected Measurement can only be Mapped in one of the following Data Stream Types: Ads,
Conversion Tags, or Ads Verification'.
••
Custom Cost Type for a given IO is calculated as follows: <Selected Measurement> X IO Rate.
So in our example, if a given IO has generated a total of 211 Leads within a certain date range, and
the IO Rate for CPL for that IO is $2.5, the calculation the system would perform is: 211 X $2.5 =
$527.50:
••
Since the calculation performed by the Media Transparency Center for a Custom Cost Type is always
a direct multiplication of the <Selected Measurement> by the 'IO Rate', calculations that require
additional steps, must have these steps applied from within the mapping of the Selected
Measurement. For example, if the Custom Cost Type required is the 'Cost per 1,000 Leads', then the
Leads Generated value has to be divided by 1,000 from within the mapping of the Leads Generated
measurement:
This way, when the Multiplication in the Transparency Center occurs, the Selected Measurement is
already pre-divided by 1,000.
••
Apply a Cost Type function in the Buy Data data stream mapping
22.. Apply a Cost Type function in the Buy Data data stream mapping.
For any Custom Cost Type created, in order for it to work, it has to be referenced from the Mapping of
the Buy Data Stream using a dedicated Custom Cost Type Function:
COSTTYPE(<Name of Cost Type Field in the Buy Data File>)
This function searches for the Cost Type value in the Cost Center, obtains the Selected Measurement
and uses that to multiply by the IO Rate.

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Media Transparency Center
Since Custom Cost Types are generally used in addition to Standard Cost Types, could handle both
cases in the Mapping formula of 'IO Cost Type'. In such a case, the formula would be as follows:
if(csv['Cost Type']=='CPL')
{
return COSTTYPE(csv['Cost Type']);
}
else
{
return csv['Cost Type'];
}
STEP 3: Mapping your Buy Data
The following screenshot shows how each of the essential IO Fields should be mapped:
The IO Key or ID can either be found in the source file itself as the key that uniquely identifies each row
in question, as shown below:
If it isn't present in the file as a unique identifier, you can create an IO Key using a mapping formula to
concatenate a group of fields that together identify each row uniquely:
The IO Name is the name given to the marketing activity in question. A common practice is to name the
row after the Campaign that this IO represents. Like the IO Key, the IO Name can also be created or
modified using a mapping formula
The IO Start Date and IO End Date indicate the date range during which an IO is active. Recalculations
aren't carried out for dates on which an IO is NOT active.
The next fields refer to the IO Measurements:
•
IO Cost Type denotes the type of Recalculation that will be performed for a given IO. For example,
CPM means that if a certain IO has generated 20,000 Impressions, the Recalculated Cost is: (20,000 /
1000) * IO Rate = 20 * IO Rate
•
IO Rate is the Planned Cost or 'price tag' for every unit (or 1000 units). This is the rate that is used for
cost recalculations for a given IO. For example if a certain IO's Cost Type is CPC and its IO Rate is
$0.20, then the Recalculated Cost would be: Total Number of Clicks X $0.2
•
IO Margin is optional. It denotes the Margin that is added to the Recalculated Cost (or to the Media

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Media Transparency Center
Cost in cases of Dynamic Cost recalculations).
•
IO Capped Cost indicates the maximum value for Actualized Cost and its subsequent calculations,
capping them out after this value has been reached.
STEP 4: Connecting your Buy Data to your Delivery Data
You can connect your buy data to your delivery data using one of the following four methods:
11.. Associating each individual IO with its Delivery Items from the UI:
Start from the IO management tab, locate the relevant IO from the list and double-click it in order to
edit. Then, scroll down to the tab at the bottom entitled 'Items' and expand it in order to enter
Delivery items that this IO is associated with. In the example below, the Delivery items added are
identified by their Media Buy Keys:
You can also link IOs to Delivery data using a combination of Site and Campaign Keys, or by
'Classification' as explained separately in method 4. Linking your IOS to your Delivery data from within
each individual IO however, isn't the best method to use when having to create this association at
scale.
22.. Mapping your Delivery data as part of your Buy Data:
Your Delivery Item Keys might appear in the Buy Data file, as shown here:
In this case, you can associate the IO Keys with the Delivery Item Keys by simply mapping the relevant
Delivery Field to the matching Intelligence Field in the Buy Data data stream:
The availability of the Delivery Items in your Buy Data however is rare and in most cases you won't be
able to use this solution.
33.. Using an additional Buy Data Stream
You can associate your IOs with your Delivery Data by creating an additional Buy Data, data stream (in
addition to the original Buy Data Stream you already have) which includes your IO Keys alongside your
Delivery Item Keys:
Since MTC data is at the workspace level rather than at the data stream level, all 'buy data' data
streams created under the same workspace are part of the same pool of buy data. Therefore, any

Marketing Cloud Intelligence Media Transparency Center
matching IO Key values ingested by different 'buy data' data streams are effectively considered as the
same IO entity, thus creating an association between IO keys and Media Buy Key Delivery Items in the
above example.
44.. Using the 'MTC Classification' method
IOs can be associated with Delivery Items by virtue of a shared MTC classification. In the example
below, IO Key cvb123 in the buy data and three media buy keys within the delivery data, all share the
same classification value 'term1'. The result is that IO key cvb123 is associated with the three delivery
items that have the same classification:
When using the MTC Classification method, in order to assign a classification value within the Buy
Data data stream, use the 'IO Item Classification Term' and in order to assign a classification value
within the Ads (Delivery) data, use the 'Media Buy IO Classification'
STEP 5: QA Your Data
The first step is to ensure that the uploaded Buy Data has been converted successfully into IOs. To do
this, navigate to the IO Management tab, where you should see all the IOs displayed:
Next, access a few IOs randomly to make sure the IO Attributes such as IO Cost Type, IO Start Date, IO
End Date, IO Rate etc. are correct.
Additionally, verify that the IO's associated Delivery Keys are correct:
You can now visualize the connection between the Buy Data and the Delivery Data by creating a Table
Widget or a Pivot Table that has the IO Name with some of its Main Attributes of Interest. Als

…
